systemPrompt:
You are Arivoam AI, a sharp and highly specialised assistant for TNPSC (Tamil Nadu Public Service Commission — Groups 1, 2, 4) and SSC (Staff Selection Commission — CGL, CHSL) competitive exam preparation.

## Core Knowledge Areas
• **Indian Polity & Constitution** — Preamble, Articles 1–395, 12 Schedules, 106 Amendments, Fundamental Rights (Art 12–35), DPSP (Art 36–51), Fundamental Duties (Art 51A), Parliament (Art 79–123), President, Vice-President, PM, Council of Ministers, Governor, State Legislature, Judiciary, CAG, Election Commission, Finance Commission, UPSC, local self-government
• **Indian History** — Ancient: Indus Valley, Vedic, Jainism/Buddhism, Mauryas, Guptas, Harsha; Medieval: Delhi Sultanate (1206–1526), Mughals, Vijayanagara Empire; Modern: British rule, 1857 Revolt, Indian National Congress, Gandhi, Nehru, Subhas Chandra Bose, partition, social reform movements
• **Tamil Nadu History** — Sangam Age, Pallavas (Mahabalipuram), Cholas (Thanjavur, bronze art), Pandyas, Nayakars; Social reformers: Periyar EVR, B.R. Ambedkar, Justice Party; Freedom struggle: V.O. Chidambaram Pillai, Subramania Bharathi, Thirupur Kumaran; Post-independence TN
• **Indian Geography** — Physical divisions, Himalayan ranges, Peninsular plateau, Northern plains, Coastal plains, Islands; Monsoon types (SW/NE), major rivers (Himalayan vs Peninsular), soil types, vegetation, national parks, agriculture, minerals, population census 2011
• **Indian Economy** — Colonial economy, Five Year Plans (1–12), NITI Aayog vs Planning Commission, Union Budget structure, RBI functions, Monetary Policy Committee, types of banks, inflation measures (CPI/WPI), GDP/GNP/NDP/NNP, poverty lines, FRBM Act, GST, Finance Commission, economic reforms 1991
• **Central Government Schemes** — PM-KISAN (₹6000/yr, 100% central), Ayushman Bharat PMJAY (₹5L cover, 10.74Cr families), Jal Jeevan Mission (tap water to all rural HH by 2024), MGNREGS (100 days guaranteed work), PMAY-G (60:40, ₹1.2L plains), PMAY-U (4 verticals), SBM (Oct 2, 2014), Smart Cities (100 cities), AMRUT (500 cities), PM Garib Kalyan Anna, PMGKAY, JJM, PM Mudra, PM SVANidhi, DDU-GKY, NEP 2020, Skill India, Digital India, Make in India, Stand Up India, Startup India, Sagarmala, Bharatmala, UDAN, FAME, PM Vishwakarma, Naan Mudhalvan, Pudhumai Penn, Magalir Urimai Thittam, CMCHIS (TN), Illam Thedi Kalvi, Innuyir Kaaval and all TN state schemes
• **General Science** — Biology: cell biology, human physiology, genetics, ecology, diseases; Physics: mechanics, heat, light, electricity, magnetism, nuclear; Chemistry: periodic table, chemical bonds, acids/bases, carbon chemistry; all at 10th/12th level
• **Current Affairs** — Monthly themes: economy (Budget/RBI), environment (COP/biodiversity), polity (amendments/elections), science (ISRO/DRDO), international (summits/awards), TN-specific current events
• **Tamil Language** — Thirukkural (133 chapters, 1330 couplets), Sangam literature (Ettuthokai, Pattupattu), Tamil grammar basics, Tolkappiyam, prominent Tamil authors
• **English Grammar** — Parts of speech (Nouns, Pronouns, Verbs, Adjectives, Adverbs, Prepositions, Conjunctions, Interjections), tenses, active/passive, direct/indirect speech, sentence transformation, vocabulary, idioms
• **Quantitative Aptitude** — Number system, HCF/LCM, percentage, ratio-proportion, averages, profit-loss, SI/CI, time-work, time-distance, data interpretation
• **Logical Reasoning** — Series (number/letter/figure), analogies, classification, coding-decoding, blood relations, direction sense, calendar, clock, arrangements

## Response Rules
1. Keep answers **150–280 words** — punchy, exam-focused, no fluff
2. **Bold** every key fact, name, year, article number, rupee amount, percentage
3. Use "• " bullet points for lists; use **Label:** headings only when helpful
4. Mark exam traps: ⚠ **Trap:** [common confusion students face]
5. Add memory aids: 💡 **Trick:** [mnemonic or shortcut]
6. Tag past questions: 📘 **PYQ [year]:** when relevant
7. For Government Schemes always state: Launched (year), Ministry/Dept, Beneficiary, Key amount/benefit, Funding ratio
8. For History topics always state: Period, Dynasty/Ruler, Capital, Key contribution, TNPSC exam angle
9. Use the retrieved context (provided below) as primary source; supplement from training if needed
10. If asked in Tamil, reply in Tamil (Tamil script) with key terms also in English for clarity

chunks.text: PART I The Union and its Territory Art. 1–4 🔴 CRITICAL All Groups 🏆 PYQ Art. 1–4 Name, Territory, Admission & Formation of States ▾ Show Details ✅ KEY CONTENT Art. 1 — India, that is Bharat , shall be a Union of States . Territory = States + UTs (1st Sched) + acquired territories. 2 — Parliament may admit new states (not yet in India) by law. 3 — Parliament can form new states, alter boundaries/names of existing states. Needs President's recommendation + state legislature consultation (NOT consent).

chunks.text: 4 — Laws under Art. 2 & 3 are not constitutional amendments (pass by simple majority). 🔴 CRITICAL India = Union of States NOT Federation. 2 = new states from outside. 3 = reorganise existing states. " → Union of States. 🪤 TRAP NOTE Art. 4 says laws under Art. 2 & 3 are NOT deemed to be amendments under Art. 368 → they pass by simple majority .

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 9 — Voluntary acquisition of foreign citizenship = loss of Indian citizenship Art. 10 — Continuance of citizenship rights Art. 11 — Parliament regulates citizenship by law → Citizenship Act 1955 🔴 CRITICAL India = Single Citizenship . USA = Double Citizenship. Citizenship Act 1955 was amended by CAA (Citizenship Amendment Act) 2019. 🏆 PYQ "Double citizenship exists in? " → Parliament to regulate citizenship.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: PART III Fundamental Rights Art. 12–35 🔴 CRITICAL All Groups 🏆 PYQ Art. 12–13 Definition of State + Laws Inconsistent with FRs are Void ▾ Show Details ✅ KEY CONTENT Art. 12 — "State" = Govt of India + Parliament + State Govts/Legislatures + all local/other authorities within India or under Govt control. 13 — All laws inconsistent with FRs = void . Includes pre-constitutional laws (void to extent of inconsistency). "Law" includes ordinances, orders, bye-laws, rules, notifications, customs, usages.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 🔴 CRITICAL Art. 13 = guardian of FRs. Courts can strike down any law violating FRs. 13(2) = State shall not make laws abridging FRs. 💜 GEM — Doctrine of Eclipse Pre-constitutional laws inconsistent with FRs are NOT dead — eclipsed . They revive if the FR is later amended away. Doctrine of Severability = only inconsistent part is void, not entire law. 🔴 CRITICAL All Groups 🏆 PYQ Art. 14–18 Right to Equality — 5 Articles ▾ Show Details ✅ KEY CONTENT Art.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 14 — Equality before law + Equal protection of laws (British + American concept) Art. 15 — No discrimination on religion, race, caste, sex, place of birth. 15(3)/(4)/(5)/(6) = exceptions for women, children, OBC/SC/ST, EWS Art. 16 — Equal opportunity in public employment. 16(4) = reservation for backward classes Art. 17 — Abolition of Untouchability; practice = punishable offence Art. 18 — Abolition of Titles (military/academic distinctions allowed; Bharat Ratna/Padma = civilian honours, not titles) 🔴 CRITICAL Art.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 14 = "Equality before law" (negative/British) + "Equal protection of laws" (positive/American). Two different concepts in ONE article. 🏆 PYQ ALERT Art. 17 (Untouchability) and Art. 18 (Titles) = most asked pair. Protection of Civil Rights Act 1955 operationalises Art. 15(3) allows State to make special provision for women and children — this is an exception to non-discrimination. 15(6) added by 103rd Amend 2019 for EWS. 🟢 TIP 14=Equal | 15=No Discrimination | 16=Equal Jobs | 17=No Untouchability | 18=No Titles 🔴 CRITICAL All Groups 🏆 PYQ Ar

chunks.title: Indian Constitution

RS: Permanent, never dissolved, 1/3 retire every 2 yrs, VP is Chairman, min age 30. Special RS powers: Art. 249 (legislate on State subject, 2/3 majority) and Art. 312 (new All-India Services). 🏆 PYQ Joint Session used only 3 times: Dowry Prohibition Bill 1961, Banking Commission Bill 1977, POTA 2002.

chunks.text: 130 — Seat of SC is Delhi (but CJI can appoint other places) Art. 131 — Original Jurisdiction: Centre-State, State-State disputes Art. 132 — Appellate jurisdiction in constitutional cases from HCs Art. 133 — Appellate jurisdiction in civil matters from HCs Art. 134 — Appellate jurisdiction in criminal matters from HCs Art. 136 — Special Leave Petition (SLP) — widest; discretionary; from any court/tribunal Art. 137 — SC can review its own judgments Art.

SC = final interpreter of Constitution. 141 makes SC law binding on ALL courts including HCs. 136 (SLP) = broadest appellate power — can even admit criminal cases.

chunks.text: Parliament can amend any part including FRs. 🆕 25th — 1971 Art. 31C — DPSP Override Added Art. 31C — laws implementing Art. 39(b)(c) (DPSP) not void even if they abridge Art. Response to Champakam case 1951. Minerva Mills 1980 partially struck down Art. 🆕 26th — 1971 Abolition of Privy Purses Abolished privy purses and special privileges of rulers of former Indian states. Indira Gandhi government's major move. 🆕 36th — 1975 Sikkim becomes 22nd State Added Art.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: Sikkim merged with India after a referendum (1975). Changed from a protectorate to full statehood. Added to 1st Schedule. 🆕 39th — 1975 Art. 329A — PM/Speaker Election Shield Added Art. 329A — elections of PM and Speaker not challengeable in courts. STRUCK DOWN by SC in Indira Gandhi v. Raj Narain 1975. Landmark case on Basic Structure. 42nd — 1976 "Mini Constitution" — Biggest Ever Amendment Added Socialist, Secular, Integrity to Preamble. Added Part IVA (FDs).

chunks.title: Indian Constitution

Janata Party government response after Emergency. 44th — 1978 Emergency Safeguards + Right to Property Removed Right to Property removed from FRs (Art. 31) → Legal Right (Art. 20 & 21 cannot be suspended during Emergency.

Disqualification of members for defection from party. Merger rule: originally 1/3 (later changed to 2/3 by 91st Amend 2003).

Largest single increase in voter base (approximately 5 crore new voters). 🆕 65th — 1990 National Commission for SC/ST (Art. 338) Established a National Commission for SC/ST with constitutional status under Art.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: LANDMARK CASES Famous Constitutional Cases — TNPSC Must Know 1950 — SC Case A. State of Madras SC held Art. 21 "procedure established by law" meant any law enacted by Parliament, even if unfair. 21 were seen as mutually exclusive. 🏆 PYQ: First major FR case. Later overruled by Maneka Gandhi 1978. 1951 — SC Case Champakam Dorairajan v. State of Madras First case on fundamental rights vs DPSP. SC held FR prevails over DPSP. Led to 1st Constitutional Amendment 1951 and addition of Art.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 🏆 PYQ: "Which case led to 1st Amendment? " → Champakam Dorairajan. 1958 — SC Case State of Madras v. Row SC laid down "reasonableness" test for restrictions on Art. Restrictions must be proportionate and reasonable, not arbitrary. 🏆 Important for Art. 19 restriction jurisprudence. 1967 — SC Case Golaknath v. State of Punjab SC held Parliament CANNOT amend Fundamental Rights. FRs are sacrosanct. Parliament has no power under Art. 368 to abridge FRs.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 🏆 PYQ: Led to 24th Amendment 1971. Overruled by Kesavananda 1973. 1970 — SC Case R. Union of India (Bank Nationalisation Case) SC struck down Bank Nationalisation Act 1969. Held that deprivation of property must meet Art. Led to 25th Amendment. 🏆 PYQ: Indira Gandhi's bank nationalisation challenged. Landmark on property rights. 1973 — SC Case (Most Important) Kesavananda Bharati v. State of Kerala Largest ever SC bench (13 judges), decided 7:6.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: Parliament CAN amend the Constitution including FRs but CANNOT destroy the BASIC STRUCTURE. Basic Structure = supremacy of Constitution, democratic republic, secularism, separation of powers, federalism, unity & integrity, judicial review, FR, rule of law, parliamentary system. 🏆 PYQ: Most important constitutional law case ever. "Basic Structure doctrine = ? " → Kesavananda Bharati 1973. Date = April 24, 1973. 1975 — SC Case Indira Gandhi v. Raj Narain SC struck down 39th Amendment that shielded PM's election from judicial review.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: Held "free and fair elections" and "judicial review" are part of Basic Structure. 329A struck down. Led to declaration of Emergency 1975. 🏆 PYQ: "Which case struck down 39th Amendment? " → Indira Gandhi v. Raj Narain 1975. 1978 — SC Case Maneka Gandhi v. Union of India SC overruled AK Gopalan. 21 "procedure established by law" must be fair, just and reasonable. 14, 19, and 21 are interconnected (golden triangle). Procedure must pass test of reasonableness under Art.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 14 and test of freedoms under Art. Expanded right to life enormously. 🏆 PYQ: "Golden Triangle of FRs? 14, 19, 21 (Maneka Gandhi 1978). "Procedure in Art. " → Fair, just, reasonable. 1980 — SC Case Minerva Mills v. Union of India SC struck down 42nd Amendment provisions that gave DPSPs absolute precedence over FRs (Art. 31C as amended). Held that balance between FRs and DPSPs is part of Basic Structure. Also struck down provisions curtailing judicial review power.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 🏆 PYQ: "Minerva Mills struck down which amendment? " → Parts of 42nd Amendment. 1980 — SC Case Bachan Singh v. State of Punjab SC upheld constitutional validity of death penalty. Established "rarest of rare cases" doctrine for awarding death penalty. 21 does not prevent death penalty if procedure is fair and just. 🏆 PYQ: "Rarest of rare doctrine? " → Bachan Singh 1980. Death penalty constitutional in India. 1981 — SC Case Waman Rao v. Union of India SC held that 9th Schedule laws added before April 24, 1973 (Kesavananda date) cannot be challe

Union of India (Mandal Case) SC upheld 27% reservation for OBCs (Mandal Commission) but struck down reservation in promotion for OBCs. Held 50% is the outer limit for reservations (SC/ST + OBC combined).

Speaker's decision reviewable by HC under Art.

Secularism is a Basic Structure element — states doing anti-secular activities can be put under President's Rule. 🏆 PYQ: Most important case on Art.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: India = Union of States (Art. 1) — NOT Federation. 28 States + 8 UTs. 3 allows state reorganisation without state's consent (only consultation). Single Citizenship — India (Art. USA = Double Citizenship. Citizenship Act 1955 + CAA 2019. 6 Fundamental Rights (Art. 12–35) — from USA. 32 = Heart & Soul (Ambedkar). 5 Writs: H-M-P-C-Q. 226 (HC) = wider scope than Art. 14–18 (Equality) : Art. 14 = British + American concept. 17 = No Untouchability. 15(6)/16(6) = EWS 10% (103rd Amend 2019).

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 19 = 6 Freedoms (originally 7). 21 = most expansive (privacy, livelihood, health). 20 & 21 CANNOT be suspended during Emergency (44th Amend 1978). 36–51) — Non-justiciable. From Irish Constitution. 3 types: Socialistic/Gandhian/Liberal-Intellectual. 39(equal pay), Art. 40(Panchayats), Art. 48A(Environment). 11 Fundamental Duties (Art. 51A) — From USSR. 42nd Amend 1976 (10 duties) + 86th Amend 2002 (11th duty: educate child). Non-justiciable. 7th Schedule (3 Lists) : Union (97+), State (66), Concurrent (47).

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: Education + Forests moved to Concurrent by 42nd Amend 1976. Residuary = Parliament. 8th Schedule = 22 Languages . Tamil in original 14. Last 4 added: 92nd Amend 2003. India has NO national language (only official language = Hindi in Devanagari). 3 Emergencies : National (Art. 352) — declared 3 times (1962, 1971, 1975); President's Rule (Art. 356) — SR Bommai 1994 limits misuse; Financial (Art. 360) — NEVER declared. Amendment (Art. 368) — 3 methods.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 370 abrogated Aug 5, 2019 (SC upheld Dec 11, 2023). 106th Amend 2023 = Women 33% reservation. 102nd Amend 2018 = NCBC constitutional. Sub-classification SC/ST upheld by SC 2024. Landmark Cases Must Know : Champakam (1st Amend) → Golaknath → Kesavananda (Basic Structure) → Indira Gandhi (39th Amend struck down) → Maneka Gandhi (Art. 21 expanded) → Minerva Mills → SR Bommai (Art. 356) → Indra Sawhney (50% ceiling) → Puttaswamy (Privacy) → Art.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Mapping Details Prelims Unit-V: Indian Polity (Parliament, Constitutional Amendments) Unit-II: Current Events (Awards, Important Days) Unit-VI: Indian Economy (Infrastructure, EV) Mains GS Paper-II (Unit-I: Indian Parliament Structure, Women's Reservation) GS Paper-III (Unit-I/II: Industrial Corridors, Environment)

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Technology & Infrastructure: The inauguration of a new Semiconductor plant in Gujarat and upcoming ones in Tamil Nadu signals India's push for electronic self-reliance. Polity: Discussions on the Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam (106th CAA) and the transition of the Andhra Pradesh capital to Amaravati highlight significant legislative shifts. Awards: Key literary and state awards were announced, including the Sahitya Akademi Yuva and Bal Puraskars for 2025.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Category Extracted Data Points & Details Awards (Literary & State) Sahitya Akademi Yuva Puraskar 2025: Lakshmikhar (Book: Koothondru Koodichu ). Sahitya Akademi Bal Puraskar 2025: Vishnu Puram Saravanan (Book: Ottrai Siragu Oviya ). Thagaisal Thamizhar Award 2021: Khader Mohideen (IUML). Kottai Ameer Award (Communal Harmony): Kalimullah. Sports Awards Hockey Player of the Year 2025: Hardik Singh (Male), Navneet Kaur (Female). Geography & Environment Trishna Wildlife Sanctuary: Located in Tripura.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Andhra Pradesh Capital: Amaravati (Replaces Hyderabad per AP Reorganization Act 2014, ending its 10-year shared status). Science & Technology Semiconductor Plant: Inaugurated in Sanand, Gujarat. Vikram 3201: Microprocessor chip developed indigenously by ISRO. International & Days World Youth Parliament: Stuti Pradhan (Sikkim) represented India. Important Days: April 2 - World Autism Awareness Day. (Informal: USA Liberation Day observed due to tariff shifts).

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: 106th Constitutional Amendment Act (2023): Known as Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam . Mandates 33% reservation for women (incl. SC/ST) in Lok Sabha and State Assemblies. Validity: 15 years. Relevant Articles: 239AA (Delhi), 330A (Lok Sabha), 332A (State Assembly), 334A (Sunset Clause). Article 79: Defines the Parliament of India as consisting of the President, Lok Sabha, and Rajya Sabha. The Hindi names were adopted in 1954. India Semiconductor Mission (ISM): Launched in 2021 to build a vibrant semiconductor and display ecosystem.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: PM E-DRIVE Scheme: Launched in October 2024 to accelerate EV adoption and build electric vehicle charging infrastructure.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Semiconductor Parks: Two new parks are to be established in the Kongu region — Sulur (Coimbatore) and Palladam (Tiruppur). State Honors: Regular citations of the Thagaisal Thamizhar Award and Kottai Ameer Award highlight the state's focus on social justice and communal harmony. TN Shore Neithal Meetchi Iyakkam: An ecological restoration project involving 14 coastal districts of Tamil Nadu.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Social Impact The 106th CAA (Women's Reservation Bill) guarantees a critical mass of female representation (33%) in policy-making, radically altering the patriarchal structure of Indian legislatures. Economic Impact The India Semiconductor Mission (2021) and the setup of plants in Sanand (Gujarat), Sulur, and Palladam (TN) will drastically reduce India's import dependency on critical tech components, fostering a robust electronics ecosystem. Political Impact The official transition of Andhra Pradesh's capital to Amaravati, following the 10-year

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Environmental Impact The PM E-DRIVE Scheme (launched Oct 2024) drives infrastructure growth for EV charging, mitigating fossil-fuel reliance amid rising global fuel prices. Pros & Cons Analysis

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: EV Push (PM E-DRIVE): Insulates the Indian economy from volatile international oil prices and limits carbon footprints in urban centers. Semiconductor Independence: Domestic chips like ISRO's Vikram 3201 ensure national security in strategic defense and space sectors.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Women's Reservation Bottlenecks: Implementation relies on delimitation and census completion, potentially delaying actual electoral parity. EV Infrastructure Lag: Rapid commercial EV rollout is hindered by grid capability and the lack of high-density charging stations in older residential clusters.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) 2021: The nodal framework under Digital India Corporation guiding the establishment of semiconductor fabs and testing centers like those in Sanand and Tamil Nadu.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: “ Fostering true self-reliance demands a dual approach: securing technological sovereignty through domestic semiconductor manufacturing and transitioning to clean energy via electric mobility frameworks like PM E-DRIVE. Concurrently, strengthening democratic foundations via the 106th CAA ensures that equitable development is steered by inclusive representation. 106th CAA SDG 5: Gender Equality SDG 9: Industry & Innovation

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Consider the following statements regarding the Parliament of India as per the Constitution: Which of the statements given above is/are correct? Article 79 defines the Parliament as consisting of the President, the Lok Sabha, and the Rajya Sabha. The names "Lok Sabha" and "Rajya Sabha" were adopted in the year 1954, replacing their English names. The Vice President of India is considered a constituent part of the Parliament under Article 79. A 1 and 2 only B 2 and 3 only C 1 and 3 only D 1, 2, and 3 Statement 1: Correct ✓ Statement 2: Correct ✓

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Article 79 only includes the President and the two Houses (Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha) as parts of the Parliament. The Vice President is the ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha but is not listed as a distinct component of Parliament in Article 79. With reference to the 106th Constitutional Amendment Act (Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam), consider the following statements: Which of the statements given above is/are correct? It mandates a 33% reservation for women in the Lok Sabha under the newly inserted Article 330A.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: The reservation applies to State Legislative Assemblies through Article 332A. The validity of this reservation is initially set for a period of 15 years as per Article 334A. A 1 and 2 only B 2 and 3 only C 1 and 3 only D 1, 2, and 3 Statement 1: Correct ✓ Statement 2: Correct ✓ Statement 3: Correct ✓ ✔ Answer: D All statements are correct. The 106th CAA (2023) inserts specific articles to ensure 33% representation for women (including SC/ST) across Lok Sabha and State Assemblies with a 15-year sunset clause.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Polity: The newly formed Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly elected its Speaker and Deputy Speaker, invoking critical discussions surrounding Constitutional Articles 178–181. Economy: The Union Government announced the 'VPG RAMJI' scheme, structurally overhauling and replacing the iconic MGNREGA. Macroeconomics: The Prime Minister advised citizens to minimize gold imports for a year to stabilize India's depleting Foreign Exchange (Forex) reserves and defend the Rupee.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Category Extracted Data Points & Details TN Assembly Speaker: J. Prabhakar (1000 Lights). Deputy Speaker: Ravi Shankar (Thuraiyur). Environment Sariska Tiger Reserve: Located in Rajasthan. Litchi State of India: Bihar (facing a crisis due to poor rainfall and pest attacks). Infrastructure PM MITRA Park: The first Mega Integrated Textile Region and Apparel Park was inaugurated in Warangal, Telangana. Sports (Olympics) 2028 Los Angeles Olympics: IOC lifted the ban on Belarus, allowing participation (Russia remains banned).

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Important Days May 12: International Nurses Day (Commemorating Florence Nightingale, "The Lady with the Lamp").

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Article 178: Mandates the offices of the Speaker and Deputy Speaker for the State Legislative Assembly. Article 179: Vacation, resignation, and removal. Crucial Note: The Speaker submits their resignation to the Deputy Speaker, and vice versa. Article 180: Deputy Speaker's power to perform Speaker's duties in their absence. Article 181: The Speaker cannot preside over the Assembly while a resolution for their removal is pending. They cannot cast a deciding/tie-breaking vote.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: VPG RAMJI: Viksit Bharat Guarantee for Rojgar and Ajeevika Mission Gramin. Replaces MGNREGA. Increases guaranteed workdays from 100 to 125. Implemented from July 1, 2026. Annapurna Bhandar: West Bengal scheme announced to provide ₹3,000/month to eligible women (replacing Lakshmi Bhandar). PM RAHAT: Launched Feb 13, 2026 (MoRTH). Free medical treatment up to ₹1. 5 Lakhs for 7 days for accident victims.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Political Transition: C. Joseph Vijay assumed office as the Chief Minister. Key cabinet inductees include Bussy Anand, Venkat Ramanan (School Education), and Arun Raja (Ex-IRS). Fiscal Strain of VPG RAMJI: The new scheme alters the central funding pattern to a 60:40 ratio for major states. For a project worth ₹10,000 Crores, the TN exchequer must now bear ₹4,000 Crores. Civic Admin (PYQ Drill): In the Greater Chennai Corporation, the executive wing is administratively headed by the Commissioner (IAS), not the Mayor.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Social Impact Extending employment guarantee to 125 days bolsters rural safety nets against agrarian distress. State schemes like West Bengal's Annapurna Bhandar (₹3000/month) reflect a nationwide pivot towards direct cash transfers for women, aiming to recognize unpaid domestic labor. chunks.text: FAO (Food and Agriculture Organization): Established on October 16, 1945 (World Food Day). Headquartered in Rome, Italy. Statutory authority mandated to issue Aadhaar, under the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology. Indo-Sri Lanka Accord (1987): Bilateral agreement resulting in the deployment of the Indian Peace Keeping Force (IPKF) to Sri Lanka.

chunks.text: TN Cabinet Expansion: 10 ministers inducted. Congress joins the TN Cabinet after 59 years with 2 ministers: Rajesh Kumar (Killiyur) and Viswanathan (Melur). CTS Exam: Combined Technical Services notification officially released on May 20, with exams projected for August.

chunks.text: Social Impact The return of the Chola Copper Plates from Leiden University corrects a historical cultural void, reviving tangible links to Tamil antiquity and the inclusive religious policies (Buddhist Vihara grants) of the Chola dynasty. Economic Impact The appointment of a new UIDAI CEO highlights the ongoing consolidation of India's digital public infrastructure, crucial for eliminating leakages in direct benefit transfers and welfare schemes.

The observance of Anti-Terrorism Day (May 21) serves as a stark reminder of the geopolitical complexities and domestic repercussions stemming from the 1987 Indo-Sri Lanka Accord. Environmental Impact Recognitions like the UNESCO World Heritage status for the Sundarbans (West Bengal) highlight global mandates to prote

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Space Diplomacy (Shukrayaan): Sweden's inclusion in India's Venus mission reduces financial burdens, facilitates technology transfer, and elevates ISRO's status as a global hub for cost-effective planetary exploration. Soft Power: High-profile awards (FAO, Sweden, Norway) and diplomatic gestures (Year of Culture 2027) heavily bolster India's geopolitical leverage.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Regional Geopolitics: Historical interventions (like IPKF in Sri Lanka) leave lingering trust deficits, complicating current diplomatic and electoral narratives within Tamil Nadu regarding Sri Lankan Tamil welfare.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note

Albania: Appointed an AI named "Dia" as a minister. India's 1st State AI Ministry: Kerala (Minister: PK Kunhalikutty). India's 2nd State AI Ministry: Tamil Nadu (Minister: Kumar, Velachery MLA). Luxembourg: First country to make all public transport completely free. Sports Asian Weightlifting Championship: Held in Gandhinagar, Gujarat.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: One Station One Product (OSOP): Launched during the 2022-23 Union Budget. Vision: "Vocal for Local". It aims to provide indigenous and local products a specialized market at railway stations. Tobacco and Vapes Act: Recently introduced in the UK to protect future generations from smoking and vaping addictions. PHEIC: Public Health Emergency of International Concern. A formal declaration by the WHO signalling an extraordinary public health event that constitutes a global risk.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: AI in State Cabinet: Tamil Nadu became the second state in India to establish a separate Artificial Intelligence portfolio, led by Velachery MLA Kumar. Namma Arasu Chatbot: An AI-driven WhatsApp chatbot launched by the TN Government offering around 50 digital services directly to citizens. Public Health Alert: Health officials (like Minister Arunraj) issued reassuring statements to prevent public panic over the 'Glanders' bacterial disease affecting horses.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Social Impact Free public transport models (Luxembourg) and targeted transit welfare for children (UK) vastly improve social mobility and equalize access to education and essential services across income divides. Economic Impact The 'One Station One Product' scheme leverages the vast Indian Railways network to economically empower local artisans, directly bridging the gap between grassroots micro-economies and national markets. Political Impact The rapid rise of phenomenon like the "Cockroach Janata Party" underscores the volatile power of soci

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Environmental Impact Experimenting with AI-driven artificial rain (as seen in Jaipur) points to emerging technological interventions aimed at combating severe heatwaves and erratic monsoon patterns caused by climate change. Pros & Cons Analysis

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: AI Integration in Governance: Establishing dedicated AI ministries (Kerala, TN) and deploying tools like 'Bhashini' and 'Kisan e-Mitra' streamlines welfare delivery, breaks language barriers, and ensures data-driven policymaking. Global Health Monitoring: The WHO's prompt PHEIC declaration for Ebola mobilizes international resources, preventing localized outbreaks from becoming catastrophic global pandemics.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Zoonotic Spillover: The rise in diseases like Glanders (bacterial/horses) and Hantavirus (viral/rodents) exposes the fragile human-animal interface and systemic gaps in veterinary public health infrastructure. Social Media Weaponization: Unregulated social media movements can easily be hijacked by anti-state actors or foreign entities to provoke domestic unrest under the guise of grassroots activism.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: IndiaAI Mission (2024): Approved by the Union Cabinet to establish a comprehensive AI ecosystem, including sovereign AI compute infrastructure, to catalyze innovation while ensuring ethical AI guidelines are met.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: “ As India rapidly integrates emerging technologies into its administrative fabric through dedicated AI Ministries and digital public goods like Bhashini, it must simultaneously fortify its public health infrastructure against emerging zoonotic threats. Balancing technological adoption with stringent safeguards against social media polarization will be the defining hallmark of resilient, future-ready governance. IndiaAI Mission SDG 3: Good Health SDG 9: Innovation SDG 16: Strong Institutions

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: TNUHDB Chairman: Koopa Krishnan appointed as Chairman of Tamil Nadu Urban Habitat Development Board. TNUHDB History: Started as Tamil Nadu Slum Clearance Board in Sept 1970 (Kalaignar period). Act passed in 1971. Renamed to TNUHDB in Sept 2021 (MK Stalin). Key TN Boards Established: TIIC (1949), TNHB (1961), TIDCO (1965), SIPCOT (1971). Cabinet Reshuffle: Vanniyarasu (VCK) gets Social Welfare; Shahjahan (IUML) gets Minorities Welfare, marking coalition representation.

chunks.text: Political Impact The inclusion of VCK and IUML members in the TN cabinet after roughly 60 years illustrates a maturation of coalition politics, balancing power-sharing with electoral alliances at the regional level. Internal Security Impact The seizure of "Captagon" under Operation Rage Pill exposes the transnational networks of narco-terrorism, necessitating advanced border intelligence to cut off funding for extremist groups. Political Appointments: Using welfare boards (like TNUHDB) primarily to accommodate un-elected political loyalists can sometimes compromise technocratic efficiency.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Global Leadership: Establishing the IBCA headquarters in New Delhi enhances India's soft power and environmental diplomacy. Legislative Scrutiny: Dedicated financial committees (Estimates, PAC) with proportional representation from all parties effectively check public expenditure.

chunks.text: Category Extracted Data Points & Details Art, History & Culture Kumbhalgarh Fort: Located in Rajasthan (Aravalli Range). Built by Rajput King Rana Kumbha (Mewar Province) in the 15th Century. It features the second longest continuous wall globally (36 km). Note: Great Wall of China is the longest (~21,000 km). Conferred UNESCO World Heritage status in 2013. Parliamentary Facts New Parliament Building: Named "Sansad Bhavan" . Lok Sabha has 888 seats (Peacock theme).

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Rajya Sabha has 384 seats (Lotus theme). Old Parliament: Renamed to "Samvidhan Sadan" . Election Commission: Headquarters named "Nirvachan Sadan". Awards Sansad Ratna Award 2026: Instituted in 2010 based on the suggestion of Dr. Given by Chennai-based NGO Prime Point Foundation to top-performing MPs. This year, 12 MPs received the award. Bureaucratic Appointments: Arun IPS (former Chennai Commissioner) was recently appointed as the Director of the Directorate of Vigilance and Anti-Corruption (DVAC).

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Agriculture Relief: The TN crop loan waiver immediately alleviates the debt burden of marginal farmers, preventing distress sales and supporting foundational agrarian economies. Strategic Defense: Successful testing of the Agni-1 (700-900 km SRBM) continues to secure India's regional deterrence capability.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Social Issues & Demographics: Andhra Pradesh's contentious cash incentive for families having a fourth child highlights the looming crisis of falling Total Fertility Rates (TFR) and potential delimitation impacts. Environment & AI: The establishment of India's first Human-Elephant Conflict Research Center in Palamu underscores the growing role of Artificial Intelligence in wildlife conservation. Space & Industry: Approval for space manufacturing clusters in Tamil Nadu and Gujarat marks a pivot toward decentralized, localized aerospace industria

chunks.text: Defense Technology: Indian Army's integration of AI platforms like 'Q-Force' and 'Kautilya' signals a rapid modernization of military logistics and command intelligence.

Kalpasar Project: A dam construction project across the Gulf of Khambhat in Gujarat. Palamu Tiger Reserve: Located in Jharkhand . Hosted India's first tiger census. Now hosts India's first AI-driven Human-Elephant Conflict Research Center. Demographics & Policies Andhra Pradesh Policy: Announced ₹40,000 cash incentive for a 4th child (₹30,000 for 3rd child) to combat falling TFR.

chunks.text: Total Fertility Rate (TFR): Replacement level is 2. India's average is ~1. Tamil Nadu is ~1. Science, Tech & Space Space Manufacturing Facilities: Approved in 2 states. Gujarat (Goraj) will get a Space Manufacturing Park. Tamil Nadu (Thoothukudi) will get a Space Vehicles Cluster. Defense AI: Q-Force (Logistics/freight transport AI) and Kautilya (Command Intelligence Platform) launched by the Indian Army. Institutions & Governance CHIRAG Center: Indian Railways' Human Resource Management and Research center established in Hyderabad .

chunks.text: First Private Museum: Established in Thiruvananthapuram (Kerala). Project Zero: An anti-corruption initiative introduced by the Kerala Government. Important Days World Population Day: July 11. World Elephant Day: August 12.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: PM SETU Scheme: Launched on October 4, 2025, under the Ministry of Skill Development. The scheme aims to upgrade 1,000 Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs) across India. Delimitation Commission: Tasked with redrawing constituency boundaries. Fears exist that southern states with successful population control (low TFR) might lose political representation compared to northern states.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Thoothukudi Space Cluster: The Department of Space approved the establishment of a dedicated Space Vehicles Cluster in Thoothukudi, transforming the region into an aerospace hub. Gajraj Suraksha: An AI-based system implemented in the Madukkarai area (Coimbatore-Palakkad border) to alert loco pilots about elephants crossing railway tracks. DVAC Appointments: Administrative appointments, such as the new Chairman of the Directorate of Vigilance and Anti-Corruption (DVAC), continue to spark political debate compared to anti-corruption measures in n

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Social Impact The sharp drop in Total Fertility Rate (TFR) to ~1. 4 in southern states threatens to create an "aging population trap. " This demographic shift increases the dependency ratio and strains future public healthcare resources. Incentivizing large families purely via cash (like AP's 4th child policy) risks pushing below-poverty-line families into a cyclical debt trap and forces women out of the organized workforce. Economic Impact Real economic growth and demographic stabilization require robust, systemic investments in free healthcar

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Political Impact The impending political delimitation exacerbates friction between the North (high population) and South (low population), driving regional leaders to make controversial population policies to safeguard parliamentary representation. Environmental Impact The deployment of AI-driven systems like "Gajraj Suraksha" and the new Palamu research center represents a crucial shift from reactive culling to proactive, tech-enabled coexistence in human-wildlife conflict zones.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Defense Modernization: The integration of specific AI tools like Kautilya (Command Intelligence) and Q-Force (Logistics) vastly enhances the operational readiness and data-driven decision-making of the Indian Army. Decentralized Space Economy: Establishing specialized space clusters in Thoothukudi and Goraj democratizes aerospace manufacturing, bringing high-tech jobs directly to regional corridors.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Flawed Population Policies: Cash-for-children policies often fail to address the root causes of declining birth rates (cost of living, career disruption) and disproportionately exploit economically vulnerable groups. Political Defections: The continuous trend of engineering political defections (across national and regional parties) severely undermines electoral mandates and democratic institutional integrity.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: National Family Health Survey (NFHS): Provides the vital statistical backbone regarding TFR across states, establishing the stark North-South demographic divide. Delimitation Commission Directives: The constitutional freeze on seat allocation based on the 1971 census (extended till 2026) is nearing its end, prompting urgent debates on population vs. representation.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Standardized Justice: The implementation of a UCC ensures that civil liberties and property rights, especially for women, are uniform and not dictated by diverse, sometimes patriarchal, personal laws. Local Governance: Schemes like RGSA directly empower grassroots administration (Panchayati Raj), making policy execution highly localized and transparent.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Cultural Friction: Enforcing a UCC without vast consensus risks alienating minority communities who view personal laws as an intrinsic part of their religious identity. Privacy Concerns: Mandating the registration of live-in relationships under state surveillance raises serious ethical questions regarding an individual's right to privacy and state overreach.

chunks.text: Polity & Governance: The appointment of Mahesh Kumar Aggarwal as Tamil Nadu's new DGP brings attention to the UPSC-led empanelment process for state police chiefs. Social Justice: Kerala Government's progressive announcement to grant a 3-day menstrual leave for school girls aims to normalize biological realities and reduce educational dropout rates. Economy: India evaluates printing ₹10 and ₹20 denominations as polymer (plastic) notes to drastically improve currency lifespan and reduce recurring printing costs.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note

Famous for its extensive Mangrove forests (அலையாத்தி காடுகள்) and as a crucial nesting site for Olive Ridley Turtles . Appointments TN DGP: Mahesh Kumar Aggarwal (1994 IPS batch, Punjab origin). He previously headed the Border Security Force (BSF) and replaces Sandeep Rathore. chunks.text: Plastic Money: Australia was the first country globally to introduce polymer/plastic currency notes. AI Ranking: India currently ranks 4th globally in Artificial Intelligence. Sports & Awards Vinesh Phogat: Indian Wrestling champion and newly elected MLA from the Julana constituency (Haryana). Supreme Court allowed her to participate in Asian Games trials. National Spelling Bee (USA): Won by Indian-origin boy Shrey Parikh . Important Days May 30: Goa Statehood Day & International Potato Day.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: PURA Scheme: Providing Urban Amenities in Rural Areas. Launched as a pilot project in 2004 under the Ministry of Rural Development. It was the brainchild of former President Dr. 12th Constitutional Amendment Act (1962): Incorporated Goa, Daman, and Diu as Union Territories following Operation Vijay (1961). 56th Constitutional Amendment Act (1987): Elevated Goa from a Union Territory to become the 25th State of India on May 30, 1987.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: DGP Appointment Procedure: The appointment of Mahesh Kumar Aggarwal highlights the structural protocol where the State Government submits a list of eligible IPS officers to the UPSC . The UPSC recommends a shortlist of three names (this time: Mahesh Kumar Aggarwal, Sandeep Rathore, Rajiv Kumar), from which the State selects the DGP (Director General of Police for Law & Order).

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Social Impact Kerala's introduction of a 3-day menstrual leave for school girls is a radical step in normalizing biological realities, destigmatizing women's health, and preventing educational dropout rates during puberty. Economic Impact Transitioning lower denomination currency (₹10, ₹20) to Polymer/Plastic notes dramatically reduces wear-and-tear. This increases the circulation lifespan and curtails the recurring macroeconomic costs of printing replacement currency.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Political Impact The history of Goa's integration via Operation Vijay (1961) and subsequent Constitutional Amendments (12th and 56th CAAs) showcases India's post-independence territorial consolidation and constitutional flexibility. Environmental Impact Safeguarding vital ecological hotspots like the Coringa Wildlife Sanctuary ensures the survival of climate-resilient Mangrove forests, acting as natural barriers against coastal erosion while protecting Olive Ridley nesting sites.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Gender Equity: Mandated menstrual leaves (supported by compensatory weekend classes) create an equitable educational environment, previously pioneered by Karnataka for working women. Currency Durability: Polymer notes are significantly cleaner, harder to counterfeit, and waterproof compared to traditional cotton-paper blends.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: Implementation Hurdles: Menstrual leaves without strict academic catch-up frameworks risk girls missing out on core curriculum, potentially creating an unintended academic disparity. Ecological Costs of Plastic: While polymer notes last longer, their initial production costs are higher, and their end-of-life disposal poses specialized environmental recycling challenges.

chunks.title: Senior Current Affairs Study Note
chunks.text: UN Human Rights Frameworks: The addition of nations to the UN Blacklist on Sexual Violence acts as a global diplomatic pressure tool to enforce the Geneva Conventions during armed conflicts. Police Reforms (Prakash Singh Guidelines): The UPSC empanelment process for selecting the state DGP ensures merit-based tenure stability, insulating police administration from arbitrary political shuffling.

chunks.section: TN 10th Standard Public Exam Results Declared: 94.31% Pass
chunks.text: The Tamil Nadu School Education Department announced the 10th standard public exam results for 2026. The state recorded an overall pass percentage of 94. 31%, which is an increase of 0. 51% from the previous year (2025: 93. Girls outperformed boys significantly (Girls: 96. Pudukkottai district secured the first position state-wide with a 97. 57% pass rate, followed closely by Sivaganga (97. A total of 1,931 Government schools achieved a 100% pass rate.

chunks.section: TN 10th Standard Public Exam Results Declared: 94.31% Pass
chunks.text: Chennai district stood at the 29th position with 92. பள்ளிக் கல்வித்துறை 2026-ஆம் ஆண்டுக்கான 10-ஆம் வகுப்பு பொதுத்தேர்வு முடிவுகளை வெளியிட்டது. ஒட்டுமொத்த தேர்ச்சி 94. 31% ஆக பதிவாகியுள்ளது, இது முந்தைய ஆண்டை (2025: 93. மாணவர்களை விட மாணவிகள் அதிக அளவில் தேர்ச்சி பெற்றுள்ளனர் (மாணவிகள்: 96. 47%, மாணவர்கள்: 92. புதுக்கோட்டை மாவட்டம் 97. 57% தேர்ச்சியுடன் மாநில அளவில் முதலிடம் பிடித்துள்ளது, சிவகங்கை (97. 54%) இரண்டாமிடம் பிடித்துள்ளது. மொத்தம் 1,931 அரசுப் பள்ளிகள் 100% தேர்ச்சி பெற்றுள்ளன.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: Historic Cabinet Expansion: Coalition Government Formed in TN
chunks.text: Chief Minister Vijay (TVK) expanded his cabinet by inducting 21 TVK MLAs and 2 Congress MLAs (Rajeshkumar and P. Viswanathan), bringing the total cabinet strength to 33. This marks the first time in Tamil Nadu's political history that a coalition government has been formed where allied parties share cabinet berths. Notably, after 59 years, Congress ministers are part of the TN cabinet. Viswanathan's induction is highlighted as a milestone for social justice, as he won from a general constituency (Melur) despite belonging to the SC community.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: Historic Cabinet Expansion: Coalition Government Formed in TN
chunks.text: விஸ்வநாதன் பட்டியலின சமூகத்தைச் சேர்ந்தவராக இருந்தாலும் பொதுத் தொகுதியில் (மேலூர்) வென்றதால், அவரது பதவியேற்பு சமூக நீதிக்கான மைல்கல்லாகக் கருதப்படுகிறது. ▸ TNPSC Enrichment Constitutional Article: Article 164 (1A) - The total number of Ministers, including the Chief Minister, in the Council of Ministers in a State shall not exceed 15% of the total number of members of the Legislative Assembly. PYQ 91st Constitutional Amendment Act, 2003 established this 15% ceiling.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: "One Nation, One Election" JPC Review; Expected to Save ₹7 Lakh Crore
chunks.text: சவுத்ரி தலைமையிலான நாடாளுமன்றக் கூட்டுக்குழு (JPC) தற்போது ஆய்வு செய்து வருகிறது. மக்களவை மற்றும் மாநில சட்டமன்றங்களுக்கு ஒரே நேரத்தில் தேர்தல் நடத்துவதன் மூலம் ₹7 லட்சம் கோடி மிச்சமாகும் என்றும், தேசிய மொத்த உள்நாட்டு உற்பத்தி 1. 6% அதிகரிக்கும் என்றும் கணிக்கப்பட்டுள்ளது. ▸ TNPSC Enrichment Constitutional Context: Requires amendments to Article 83 (Duration of Houses of Parliament), Art 85 (Dissolution of Lok Sabha), Art 172 (Duration of State Legislatures), Art 174 (Dissolution of State Assemblies), and Art 356 (President's Rule).

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: "One Nation, One Election" JPC Review; Expected to Save ₹7 Lakh Crore
chunks.text: TIP Remember the committee heads: Main report = Ram Nath Kovind. JPC review = P. Welfare Schemes · Economy HIGH YIELD

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: 125-Day Rural Employment Scheme (VP-GRAM) Replaces MGNREGA from July 1
chunks.text: The Central Government has announced the rollout of the new 125-day rural employment scheme (VP-GRAM) replacing the 100-day MGNREGA scheme starting July 1. 25 states have already allocated funds. Under the new act passed last December, the fund-sharing ratio is 60:40 (Centre:State) for normal states, and 90:10 for North-Eastern and Himalayan states. The scheme introduces smart job cards with facial recognition for transparency, with an estimated central wage budget of ₹95,692 crore.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: 125-Day Rural Employment Scheme (VP-GRAM) Replaces MGNREGA from July 1
chunks.text: ▸ TNPSC Enrichment Constitutional Hook: DPSP Article 41 (Right to work, to education and to public assistance in certain cases) and Article 43 (Living wage, etc. Previous Scheme: MGNREGA was enacted in 2005. The shift from 100 to 125 days is a significant policy shift. TRAP Pay attention to the funding ratio: It is NOT 100% centrally funded. 60:40 for general states, 90:10 for special category states.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: PM Modi visits Italy; Strengthens Bilateral Ties
chunks.text: Concluding his 5-nation tour, Prime Minister Narendra Modi visited Rome, Italy, where he held bilateral talks with Italian PM Giorgia Meloni. The leaders reaffirmed the "India-Italy Joint Action Plan 2025-2029" to multi-fold their bilateral relations. Currently, over 400 Italian companies operate in India, contributing to a bilateral trade nearing 20 billion Euros. The two nations also discussed defense cooperation, AI, and countering terrorism financing.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: PM Modi visits Italy; Strengthens Bilateral Ties
chunks.text: ▸ TNPSC Enrichment Static Hook: Italy's capital is Rome. Currency: Euro. Current PM: Giorgia Meloni. GEM In recent years, Italy has been a key European partner for India, especially regarding the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEC).

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: UN Forecasts India's GDP Growth at 6.4% for 2026
chunks.text: The United Nations has revised India's economic growth forecast for the current year (2026) to 6. 4%, slightly down from its earlier projection of 6. The downgrade is attributed to the global economic shock and supply chain disruptions caused by the US-Iran conflict in the Middle East. Despite the slight dip, India remains one of the fastest-growing major economies, far outpacing the global growth average, which is projected at a mere 2. நடப்பு ஆண்டிற்கான (2026) இந்தியாவின் பொருளாதார வளர்ச்சி குறித்த கணிப்பை ஐக்கிய நாடுகள் சபை 6.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: UN Forecasts India's GDP Growth at 6.4% for 2026
chunks.text: 4% ஆக திருத்தியுள்ளது, இது முந்தைய கணிப்பான 6. 6% இலிருந்து சற்றே குறைக்கப்பட்டுள்ளது. மத்திய கிழக்கில் அமெரிக்க-ஈரான் மோதலால் ஏற்பட்ட உலகளாவிய பொருளாதார அதிர்ச்சி மற்றும் விநியோக சங்கிலி சீர்குலைவுகளே இந்த குறைப்புக்கு காரணமாகும். உலகளாவிய வளர்ச்சி சராசரியான 2. 5% ஐ விட இந்தியா மிக வேகமாக வளரும் முக்கிய பொருளாதாரங்களில் ஒன்றாகத் தொடர்கிறது. ▸ TNPSC Enrichment Trend: Previous year (2025) growth was 7. The projected global growth is 2. TRAP TNPSC often asks for specific GDP forecasts by different bodies (UN, IMF, World Bank, RBI).

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: Core Sectors Record 1.7% Growth in April
chunks.text: India's eight core infrastructure sectors grew by 1. 7% in April, showing signs of recovery after slipping to 1. The growth was primarily supported by the cement (9. 4%) and steel (6. 2%) sectors, which offset heavy contractions in coal (-8. 7%), crude oil, natural gas, refinery products, and fertilizers (-8. The conflict in the Middle East has heavily impacted energy supply chains. மார்ச் மாதத்தில் 1. 2% ஆக சரிந்த பிறகு, இந்தியாவின் எட்டு முக்கிய உள்கட்டமைப்புத் துறைகள் ஏப்ரல் மாதத்தில் 1.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: Core Sectors Record 1.7% Growth in April
chunks.text: 7% வளர்ச்சியைப் பதிவு செய்துள்ளன. 4%) மற்றும் எஃகு (6. 2%) துறைகளின் வளர்ச்சியே இதற்கு முக்கிய காரணமாக அமைந்தது, இது நிலக்கரி (-8. 7%), கச்சா எண்ணெய், இயற்கை எரிவாயு மற்றும் உரங்கள் (-8. 6%) ஆகியவற்றின் வீழ்ச்சியை ஈடுசெய்தது. ▸ TNPSC Enrichment GEM Static GK: The 8 core sectors (Refinery Products, Electricity, Steel, Coal, Crude Oil, Natural Gas, Cement, Fertilizers) comprise 40. 27% of the weight of items included in the Index of Industrial Production (IIP).

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: Bangladesh sweeps Pakistan 2-0 in Test Series; Arsenal wins Premier League
chunks.text: Bangladesh defeated Pakistan by 6 wickets in the 2nd Test at Rawalpindi to secure a historic 2-0 series whitewash. Mushfiqur Rahim was named Player of the Series. With this win, Bangladesh climbed to the 5th spot in the World Test Championship (WTC) points table. In football, Arsenal won the English Premier League title for the current season, lifting the trophy after a 22-year drought (last won in 2004), edging out Manchester City. ராவல்பிண்டியில் நடந்த 2-வது டெஸ்டில் பாகிஸ்தானை 6 விக்கெட் வித்தியாசத்தில் வீழ்த்தி வங்கதேசம் 2-0 என வரலாற்றுச் ச

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: Q: According to the 91st Constitutional Amendment Act, the total number of Ministers in a State Council of Ministers sha
chunks.text: View Answer & Explanation Correct Answer: 15% Context: Seen in the current affairs context as CM Vijay expanded the TN cabinet to 33. Tamil Nadu has 234 assembly seats, so the constitutional maximum limit is 35 (15% of 234). Article 164 (1A) mandates this. பின்னணி: முதலமைச்சர் விஜய் தமிழக அமைச்சரவையை 33 ஆக விரிவுபடுத்திய நடப்பு நிகழ்வுகள் சூழலில் காணப்படுகிறது. தமிழகத்தில் 234 சட்டமன்ற இடங்கள் உள்ளன, எனவே அரசியலமைப்பு உச்சவரம்பு 35 (234 இல் 15%) ஆகும்.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: Q: Under which Article of the Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSP) is the State directed to endeavour to bring ab
chunks.text: View Answer & Explanation Correct Answer: Article 47 Context: Madras High Court upheld the TN Govt's decision to close 717 TASMAC shops near educational institutions. பின்னணி: கல்வி நிறுவனங்கள் அருகே 717 டாஸ்மாக் கடைகளை மூடும் தமிழக அரசின் முடிவை சென்னை உயர்நீதிமன்றம் உறுதி செய்தது.

Global economic parameters showed UN projections for India's GDP dropping to 6. 4%, and domestic core sector growth ticking up to 1. 7% in April despite declines in coal and crude oil.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: TN Cabinet Reaches Maximum Strength (35); VCK and IUML Join Govt
chunks.text: In a major political development, two more MLAs—Vanni Arasu from Viduthalai Chiruthaigal Katchi (VCK) and Shahjahan from Indian Union Muslim League (IUML)—took oath as Ministers in Chief Minister Joseph Vijay's cabinet. Ravi administered the oath of office and secrecy. With this addition, the total strength of the Tamil Nadu Council of Ministers has reached its constitutional maximum limit of 35. Vanni Arasu was allocated the Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare portfolio, while Shahjahan was given Minorities Welfare and Wakf Board.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: TN Cabinet Reaches Maximum Strength (35); VCK and IUML Join Govt
chunks.text: ▸ TNPSC Enrichment Constitutional Article: Article 164 (1A) states that the Council of Ministers in a State shall not exceed 15% of the total MLAs. For TN (234 seats), 15% equals 35. 1, making the max limit 35. PYQ The 91st Constitutional Amendment Act (2003) introduced this ceiling limit for cabinet size. This is a very frequently asked question. GEM Exam Angle: Note the portfolios—Vanni Arasu (Adi Dravidar & Tribal Welfare) and Shahjahan (Minorities Welfare).

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: TN Cabinet Reaches Maximum Strength (35); VCK and IUML Join Govt
chunks.text: Matching type questions are highly probable for new cabinets. Administration · Police MODERATE

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: A. Amalraj Appointed as 112th Commissioner of Police, Greater Chennai
chunks.text: Senior IPS officer A. Amalraj took charge as the 112th Commissioner of Police (CoP) of Greater Chennai City. He replaces Sandeep Rai Rathore, who was transferred by the Election Commission during the recent assembly polls. Amalraj, a 1996 batch IPS officer from Kanyakumari district, has authored several books on police management and success traits. மூத்த ஐபிஎஸ் அதிகாரி ஏ. அமல்ராஜ் சென்னை பெருநகர காவல்துறையின் 112-வது காவல் ஆணையராகப் பொறுப்பேற்றுக் கொண்டார்.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: A. Amalraj Appointed as 112th Commissioner of Police, Greater Chennai
chunks.text: சமீபத்திய சட்டமன்றத் தேர்தலின் போது தேர்தல் ஆணையத்தால் இடமாற்றம் செய்யப்பட்ட சந்தீப் ராய் ரத்தோர் இடத்திற்கு இவர் நியமிக்கப்பட்டுள்ளார். கன்னியாகுமரி மாவட்டத்தைச் சேர்ந்த 1996 பேட்ச் ஐபிஎஸ் அதிகாரியான அமல்ராஜ், காவல் மேலாண்மை மற்றும் வெற்றிக்கான பண்புகள் குறித்து பல புத்தகங்களை எழுதியுள்ளார். ▸ TNPSC Enrichment Context: High-level bureaucratic reshuffles happen after a new government is formed. Knowing the heads of major state forces is crucial for Unit 9.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: A. Amalraj Appointed as 112th Commissioner of Police, Greater Chennai
chunks.text: TRAP Sandeep Rai Rathore was replaced; Amalraj is the *current* 112th Commissioner. Don't confuse the names if both appear in MCQs. Inter-State Issues HIGH YIELD

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: TN Leaders Condemn Karnataka Dy CM's Remarks on Mekedatu Dam
chunks.text: Political leaders across Tamil Nadu, including Edappadi K. Palaniswami (AIADMK), Anbumani Ramadoss (PMK), and Vaiko (MDMK), strongly condemned Karnataka Deputy Chief Minister D. Shivakumar's assertion that Tamil Nadu has no right to oppose the construction of the Mekedatu dam across the Cauvery river. Leaders urged the TN government to take immediate legal action and highlighted that any construction without TN's consent violates the Cauvery Water Disputes Tribunal (CWDT) and Supreme Court rulings.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: TN Leaders Condemn Karnataka Dy CM's Remarks on Mekedatu Dam
chunks.text: ▸ TNPSC Enrichment Constitutional Link: Article 262 deals with the adjudication of disputes relating to waters of inter-state rivers or river valleys. Geography Hook: Mekedatu is a deep gorge situated at the confluence of the river Cauvery and its tributary Arkavathi in Ramanagara district, Karnataka. GEM Exam Angle: Questions on River dispute tribunals (Inter-State Water Disputes Act 1956) and sharing formulas are highly expected.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: Centre Alerts States Over Ebola Virus Outbreak in Africa
chunks.text: The Union Health Ministry has instructed all states and UTs to intensify medical surveillance following an outbreak of the Ebola virus in Congo and Uganda, which has affected over 600 people and caused 100 deaths. The WHO has declared it a Public Health Emergency of International Concern. Health Secretary Punya Salila Srivastava directed authorities to monitor passengers from affected countries. The National Institute of Virology (NIV) in Pune is ready for sample testing.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: Centre Alerts States Over Ebola Virus Outbreak in Africa
chunks.text: ▸ TNPSC Enrichment Science Hook: Ebola is a viral hemorrhagic fever. It is transmitted to people from wild animals and spreads in the human population through human-to-human transmission. TIP Remember the premier testing institute: National Institute of Virology (NIV) is located in Pune, Maharashtra. Economy · Finance HIGH YIELD

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: RBI to Transfer Record ₹2.87 Lakh Crore Dividend to Central Govt
chunks.text: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) announced it will transfer a record surplus (dividend) of ₹2. 87 lakh crore to the Central Government for the financial year 2025-26. 7% higher than the previous year's transfer. The RBI’s Contingency Risk Buffer was maintained at 6. This massive dividend will help the government strengthen its fiscal position, especially amidst rising crude oil import costs due to Middle East conflicts. 2025-26 நிதியாண்டிற்கான உபரித் தொகையாக (ஈவுத்தொகை) ரூ.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: RBI to Transfer Record ₹2.87 Lakh Crore Dividend to Central Govt
chunks.text: 87 லட்சம் கோடியை மத்திய அரசுக்கு மாற்றுவதாக இந்திய ரிசர்வ் வங்கி (RBI) அறிவித்துள்ளது. இது முந்தைய ஆண்டை விட 6. ரிசர்வ் வங்கியின் அவசரக்கால இடர் காப்பு நிதி 6. 5% ஆகப் பராமரிக்கப்பட்டது. இந்த மாபெரும் ஈவுத்தொகை, குறிப்பாக மத்திய கிழக்கு மோதல்களால் கச்சா எண்ணெய் இறக்குமதி செலவுகள் உயர்ந்து வரும் நிலையில், அரசின் நிதி நிலையை வலுப்படுத்த உதவும். ▸ TNPSC Enrichment Economy Concept: RBI generates surplus from its operations (interest on domestic/foreign bonds, currency trading).

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: RBI to Transfer Record ₹2.87 Lakh Crore Dividend to Central Govt
chunks.text: Section 47 of the RBI Act mandates transferring this surplus to the Govt after making provisions for bad debts and depreciation. GEM Exam Angle: This helps in reducing the Fiscal Deficit. Bimal Jalan Committee (2019) recommended the Economic Capital Framework that dictates how much reserve RBI should keep vs transfer.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: Kalpakkam Fast Breeder Reactor Achieves Criticality; Historic Milestone
chunks.text: The 500 MW Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor (PFBR) at the Indira Gandhi Centre for Atomic Research (IGCAR) in Kalpakkam has successfully initiated a continuous nuclear chain reaction (achieved criticality). With this, India becomes the second country in the world, after Russia, to operate a commercial fast breeder reactor. This reactor generates more fuel than it consumes and uses a Mixed Oxide (MOX) fuel of Uranium and Plutonium. It represents the second stage of India's three-stage nuclear power programme.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: Kalpakkam Fast Breeder Reactor Achieves Criticality; Historic Milestone
chunks.text: இது இந்தியாவின் மூன்று கட்ட அணுசக்தி திட்டத்தின் இரண்டாம் கட்டத்தைக் குறிக்கிறது. ▸ TNPSC Enrichment Static GK Hook: India's 3-stage nuclear program was formulated by Homi Bhabha. Stage 1: Pressurized Heavy Water Reactors (PHWRs). Stage 2: Fast Breeder Reactors (FBRs). Stage 3: Thorium-Based Reactors. TRAP Do not confuse it with Kudankulam. Kalpakkam is home to the PFBR, built by BHAVINI (Bharatiya Nabhikiya Vidyut Nigam Ltd). PYQ FBR uses liquid Sodium as a coolant, unlike normal reactors which use water.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: DRDO Successfully Tests Agni-1 Ballistic Missile
chunks.text: The Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) successfully test-fired the Agni-1 short-range ballistic missile from Dr APJ Abdul Kalam Island off the coast of Odisha (Chandipur). The missile can strike targets with high precision at a range of 700 km to 1,200 km and can carry nuclear payloads weighing up to 2,500 kg. பாதுகாப்பு ஆராய்ச்சி மற்றும் மேம்பாட்டு நிறுவனம் (DRDO) குறுகிய தொலைவு ஏவுகணையான அக்னி-1 ஐ ஒடிசா கடற்கரையில் உள்ள டாக்டர் ஏபிஜே அப்துல் கலாம் தீவிலிருந்து (சந்திப்பூர்) வெற்றிகரமாகச் சோதித்தது.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: Federation Cup: Animesh Kujur Breaks National Record in 100m
chunks.text: At the Federation Cup National Senior Athletics Championships in Ranchi, Jharkhand, the men's 100m national record was broken twice in a single day. First, Gurindervir Singh clocked 10. 17 seconds in the first semi-final. Shortly after, Animesh Kujur clocked 10. 15 seconds in the second semi-final, establishing a new national record. ஜார்க்கண்ட் மாநிலம் ராஞ்சியில் நடைபெறும் கூட்டமைப்புக் கோப்பை தேசிய தடகள சாம்பியன்ஷிப்பில், ஆண்களுக்கான 100 மீ ஓட்டத்தில் ஒரே நாளில் இரண்டு முறை தேசிய சாதனை முறியடிக்கப்பட்டது.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: Q: Consider the following statements regarding the Fast Breeder Test Reactor (FBTR) at Kalpakkam: 1. It uses water as a
chunks.text: View Answer & Explanation Correct Answer: 2 only Explanation: Fast breeder reactors use liquid sodium as a coolant, not water. They are called 'breeders' because they produce more nuclear fuel (Plutonium-239) than they consume. விளக்கம்: வேக ஈனுலைகள் நீருக்குப் பதிலாக திரவ சோடியத்தைக் குளிரூட்டியாகப் பயன்படுத்துகின்றன. இவை உட்கொள்ளும் அணு எரிபொருளை (புளூட்டோனியம்-239) விட அதிகமாக உற்பத்தி செய்வதால் 'ஈனுலைகள்' என்று அழைக்கப்படுகின்றன.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: World Tamil Research Conference to be Held in Toronto in October
chunks.text: The World Tamil Research Conference is scheduled to be held in Toronto, Canada, from October 15 to 18, 2026. The theme for this year's conference is "Neyyarivu Ulagil Semmozhit Thamizhum Thamizharum" (Classical Tamil and Tamils in the World of Artificial Intelligence). The conference will invite 60 researchers globally, and will focus on the intersection of Tamil history, AI, economics, and cultural development. உலகத் தமிழ் ஆராய்ச்சி மாநாடு 2026 அக்டோபர் 15 முதல் 18 வரை கனடாவின் டொராண்டோவில் நடைபெற உள்ளது.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: Mekedatu Dam: Left Parties and MDMK Announce Protests
chunks.text: Condemning the Karnataka government's persistent efforts to build a dam across the Cauvery river at Mekedatu, the Left parties (CPI, CPM) have announced a protest in Thanjavur on May 29. Similarly, MDMK general secretary Vaiko has announced a protest in Chennai on June 2. They urged the Central Government not to grant permission for the project, warning it would severely affect the delta districts and bypass the Cauvery Water Disputes Tribunal's final order.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: Mekedatu Dam: Left Parties and MDMK Announce Protests
chunks.text: காவேரி ஆற்றின் குறுக்கே மேகதாதுவில் அணை கட்டும் கர்நாடக அரசின் தொடர் முயற்சிகளை கண்டித்து, மே 29-ஆம் தேதி தஞ்சாவூரில் போராட்டம் நடத்த இடதுசாரிகள் (சிபிஐ, சிபிஎம்) அறிவித்துள்ளன. அதேபோல், மதிமுக பொதுச்செயலாளர் வைகோ ஜூன் 2-ஆம் தேதி சென்னையில் போராட்டம் நடத்தப் போவதாக அறிவித்துள்ளார். இத்திட்டத்திற்கு மத்திய அரசு அனுமதி அளிக்கக் கூடாது என அவர்கள் வலியுறுத்தியுள்ளனர். ▸ TNPSC Enrichment Geographical Context: Mekedatu is a gorge where the river Cauvery merges with its tributary Arkavathi.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: Draft Rules Released for the New 'VP-GRAM' 125-Day Employment Scheme
chunks.text: The Union Government has published the draft rules for the new "VP-GRAM" rural employment scheme, set to roll out on July 1. This scheme replaces the 100-day MGNREGA with a 125-day work guarantee. Under the new funding formula, the financial burden will be shared in a 60:40 ratio (Centre:State) for normal states, and 90:10 for North-Eastern, Himalayan states, and Jammu & Kashmir. The public is invited to provide feedback on these rules. ஜூலை 1-ஆம் தேதி தொடங்கப்பட உள்ள புதிய "விபி-கிராம்" ஊரக வேலைவாய்ப்பு திட்டத்திற்கான வரைவு விதிகளை மத்திய அரசு

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: Draft Rules Released for the New 'VP-GRAM' 125-Day Employment Scheme
chunks.text: இத்திட்டம் 100 நாள் மகாத்மா காந்தி தேசிய ஊரக வேலை உறுதித் திட்டத்திற்குப் பதிலாக 125 நாள் வேலை உத்தரவாதத்தை வழங்குகிறது. புதிய நிதிப் பகிர்வு சூத்திரத்தின் கீழ், சாதாரண மாநிலங்களுக்கு 60:40 (மத்திய:மாநில) விகிதத்திலும், வடகிழக்கு, இமயமலை மாநிலங்கள் மற்றும் ஜம்மு காஷ்மீருக்கு 90:10 விகிதத்திலும் நிதிச்சுமை பகிரப்படும். ▸ TNPSC Enrichment Constitutional Angle: Article 41 (Right to Work). PYQ The funding pattern (60:40 vs 90:10) is a highly repeated TNPSC question format for centrally sponsored schemes.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: India's Forex Reserves Decline by $2 Billion to $688.89 Billion
chunks.text: India's foreign exchange (forex) reserves dropped by $8. 09 billion (₹68,889 crore) to settle at $688. 89 billion for the week ending May 15. The decline is attributed to global economic fluctuations stemming from Middle-Eastern conflicts. The RBI has been actively intervening in the market to stabilize the Rupee, which recently hit an all-time low against the US Dollar. மே 15-ஆம் தேதியுடன் முடிவடைந்த வாரத்தில் இந்தியாவின் அந்நிய செலாவணி (forex) கையிருப்பு $8.

chunks.text: 09 பில்லியன் (ரூ. 68,889 கோடி) குறைந்து $688. 89 பில்லியனாக சரிந்தது. மத்திய கிழக்கு மோதல்களால் ஏற்படும் உலகளாவிய பொருளாதார ஏற்ற இறக்கங்களே இந்த சரிவுக்குக் காரணமாகும். அமெரிக்க டாலருக்கு எதிரான ரூபாயின் மதிப்பு சமீபத்தில் வரலாறு காணாத சரிவைச் சந்தித்த நிலையில், ரூபாயை நிலைநிறுத்த ரிசர்வ் வங்கி சந்தையில் தீவிரமாகத் தலையிட்டு வருகிறது. ▸ TNPSC Enrichment Components of Forex: Foreign Currency Assets (FCA), Gold reserves, Special Drawing Rights (SDRs), and Reserve Position in the IMF.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: Centre Plans 50 New Industrial Parks in 40 Days
chunks.text: Union Minister Piyush Goyal announced that the Central Government has asked states to submit proposals within 40 days to set up 50 new industrial parks. This is the first phase of a larger scheme approved to create 100 world-class industrial parks under the PM Gati Shakti plan. An allocation of ₹33,600 crore has been made for the first 50 parks, offering ₹1 crore per acre for infrastructure development. 50 புதிய தொழில் பூங்காக்களை அமைப்பதற்காக 40 நாட்களுக்குள் கருத்துருக்களை சமர்ப்பிக்குமாறு மத்திய அரசு மாநிலங்களைக் கேட்டுக்கொண்டுள்ளதாக மத்திய

chunks.text: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh inaugurated a new ammunition and bullet manufacturing facility in Shirpur (Ahilyanagar district, Maharashtra). Emphasizing indigenization, he stated that no force can stop India from becoming the world's largest arms exporter in the next 25 to 30 years. chunks.text: உள்நாட்டு உற்பத்தியை வலியுறுத்திய அவர், அடுத்த 25 முதல் 30 ஆண்டுகளில் உலகின் மிகப்பெரிய ஆயுத ஏற்றுமதியாளராக இந்தியா உருவெடுப்பதை எந்த சக்தியாலும் தடுக்க முடியாது என்று கூறினார். பாதுகாப்பு உற்பத்தியில் தனியார் துறையின் பங்களிப்பு தற்போதைய 25-30% இலிருந்து 50% ஆக அதிகரிக்க இலக்கு நிர்ணயிக்கப்பட்டுள்ளது. ▸ TNPSC Enrichment Static GK Hook: Ahmednagar district in Maharashtra was officially renamed as Ahilyanagar. chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: Lens Wins French Cup; IPL Centurion Shreyas Iyer
chunks.text: In European football, Lens FC secured the French Cup title for the first time by defeating Nice FC 3-1 in the final held in Paris. In the IPL, Punjab Kings defeated Lucknow Super Giants to keep their playoff hopes alive, bolstered by an explosive unbeaten century (101*) from their captain Shreyas Iyer in just 51 balls. ஐரோப்பிய கால்பந்தில், பாரிஸில் நடைபெற்ற இறுதிப் போட்டியில் நைஸ் எஃப்சி அணியை 3-1 என்ற கோல் கணக்கில் வீழ்த்தி லென்ஸ் எஃப்சி அணி முதல் முறையாக பிரெஞ்சு கோப்பை பட்டத்தை வென்றது.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: Supreme Court Rejects TN's Review Petition on Mekedatu Dam Project
chunks.text: The Supreme Court has dismissed the review petition filed by the Tamil Nadu government challenging the order that allowed the Karnataka government to prepare a Detailed Project Report (DPR) for the Mekedatu balancing reservoir project across the Cauvery river. A three-judge bench headed by Chief Justice Surya Kant rejected the plea, stating there were no valid grounds to review the earlier 2018 judgment. The court also denied TN's request to hear the matter in an open court, deciding it instead in the judges' chambers.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: Supreme Court Rejects TN's Review Petition on Mekedatu Dam Project
chunks.text: ▸ TNPSC Enrichment Constitutional Article: Article 262 handles adjudication of disputes relating to waters of inter-state rivers. Article 137 grants the Supreme Court the power to review its own judgments. Static GK Hook: The Mekedatu project proposes a balancing reservoir at the confluence of the Cauvery and Arkavathi rivers. GEM Exam Angle: Review petitions are usually decided in chambers by circulation (without oral arguments) unless an open court hearing is explicitly allowed.

The Supreme Court in Balaji Raghavan Vs Union of India (1996) upheld the constitutional validity of Padma Awards stating they are not 'titles' under Article 18.

Rubio assured that the US does not intend to target India specifically and hopes to resolve issues mutually.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: TN Govt Waives Crop Loans Up to ₹50,000 for Small & Marginal Farmers
chunks.text: Chief Minister Joseph Vijay has ordered the waiver of crop loans up to ₹50,000 obtained by small and marginal farmers from cooperative banks between May 1, 2025, and February 28, 2026. For marginal farmers with loans up to ₹50,000, the entire amount is waived. For loans between ₹50,001 and ₹1,00,000, staggered waivers apply (e. , ₹40,000 for marginal and ₹20,000 for small farmers). The scheme will benefit 14,22,555 farmers and cost the exchequer ₹2,044.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: "Singappen" Special Task Force for Women's Safety to be Inaugurated on May 27
chunks.text: Following up on his election promise, CM Joseph Vijay will officially inaugurate the "Singappen" Special Task Force on May 27 at Rajarathinam Stadium, Egmore. Headed by IG K. Bhavaneeswari, the force aims to curb crimes against women. In the first phase, over 30 police personnel (including 1 SP, 2 DSPs, 4 Inspectors) have been appointed, and 30 patrol vehicles will be provided. The force will operate under the direct supervision of the Chief Minister.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: Assam Introduces Uniform Civil Code (UCC) Bill in Assembly
chunks.text: Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma introduced the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) Bill in the state assembly. The bill aims to streamline and unify laws regarding marriage, divorce, succession, and live-in relationships. Key features include banning polygamy, setting the legal marriage age to 21 for men and 18 for women, and making the registration of marriages and live-in relationships mandatory. Notably, the bill exempts tribal populations from its purview.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: Assam Introduces Uniform Civil Code (UCC) Bill in Assembly
chunks.text: ▸ TNPSC Enrichment Constitutional Article: Article 44 of the Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSP) states that the State shall endeavor to secure for the citizens a Uniform Civil Code throughout the territory of India. Context: Uttarakhand was the first state post-independence to pass a UCC bill earlier. Assam follows suit. TRAP Tribal exemption: Remember that UCC in Assam *exempts* the tribal population, respecting their customary laws under the Sixth Schedule.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: President Murmu Confers Padma Awards to 66 Individuals
chunks.text: President Droupadi Murmu conferred the Padma Awards for 2026 to 66 awardees in the first phase at Rashtrapati Bhavan. Among the recipients from Tamil Nadu/Puducherry were Dr. Palanisamy (Padma Bhushan - Medicine), former TN Health Minister H. Hande (Padma Shri), writer Sivasankari (Padma Shri), Oduvar N. Swaminathan, and Silambam trainer Palanivel from Puducherry. Posthumous Padma Vibhushan was awarded to actor Dharmendra, and Uday Kotak received the Padma Bhushan.

chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: President Murmu Confers Padma Awards to 66 Individuals
chunks.text: ▸ TNPSC Enrichment PYQ Article 18 abolishes titles, but the Supreme Court has ruled that Padma awards are honors for merit, not titles. The leak in Rajasthan forced the cancellation of the exam for 22 lakh students, with a re-exam scheduled for June 21. chunks.title: Current Affairs Artifact
chunks.section: Lack of Sanitary Napkins & Toilets Must Not Affect Girls' Education: SC
chunks.text: The Supreme Court emphasized that girls' education should not suffer due to the unavailability of sanitary napkins or separate toilets in schools. The Court directed the Centre to strictly monitor states and UTs to ensure the implementation of its earlier order (passed in January 2025) mandating the free distribution of sanitary pads and the construction of separate toilets in all government, aided, and private schools. The Centre must submit compliance reports every three months.
