chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.section: Live Session Summary — SP Academy (SP Velen Sir)
chunks.text: 11 Questions Covered 2 Units (Unit 5 + Unit 6) ~40 Expected GS Qs from these units 8+ Live Gems Captured Topics: Archaeological Excavation (Korkai), Anna & Periyar timeline, PM Fasal Bima Yojana, OBC Commissions (India & TN), Constitutional Amendments (EWS, GST), MPI, Rural Electrification, Tamil Nadu Rice Research Institute, Tamil Literature (Sangam), Velu Nachiyar, TNSWAN, Navarai Pattam, Periyar on education/examination.

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 🏺 Section 1: Archaeological Excavation — Tamil Nadu Q1 An excavation revealed a structure of 6 rows and 9 bricks at a depth of 75 cm below surface level, with 3 large ring-wells stacked one above another. This is associated with which place? MEDIUM Factual ✅ Answer Korkai (கொற்கை) — Thoothukudi District, Tamil Nadu Context: Korkai was one of the two major seaports of the Pandya Kingdom (the other being Kayal/Kayalpattanam), both located in present-day Thoothukudi district.

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: The excavation uncovered: A brick structure — 6 rows × 9 bricks — found at 75 cm below surface 3 large ring-shaped structures (stacked) below — likely a ring-well / uraikkinaru (soaking jar / drainage system) 💎 LIVE GEM — Current Affairs Alert At the time of this session, Tamil Nadu Chief Minister announced the new Cabinet. The Minister for Archaeology & Excavations is Rajmohan , who also holds School Education and Tamil Development portfolios. This is exam-ready current affairs — note it!

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 💎 LIVE GEM — Pandya Ports Memory Trick Two Pandya ports in Thoothukudi district: (1) கொற்கை = Korkai | (2) காயல் = Kayal / Kayalpattanam . Both are recurring excavation spots in TNPSC Unit 6. Know the district for each! 🏆 PYQ ALERT Archaeological excavation sites + their districts have been asked repeatedly in TNPSC exams. Vasavsamudram (previously asked in TNPSC — it is in Krishnagiri / Dharmapuri region) is another major site. Know: Site → District mapping.

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 💡 TIP — Key Sites to Know Panchalankurichi (freedom struggle site), Vasavsamudram (excavation), Anaimalai , Korkai — all are TNPSC Unit 6 favourite spots. Match each to its district!

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 📜 Section 2: Tamil Nadu History — Anna & Periyar Timeline Q2 Choose the correct statement(s): (I) Anna joined the Justice Party (Neetikatchi) in 1937. (II) Anna met Periyar for the first time in 1934. HARD Trap/Trick ✅ Answer Only Statement II is correct. (Answer Option B) Statement I — WRONG: Anna joined the Justice Party (Neetikatchi) in 1935 (NOT 1937). The Justice Party's principle of creating a caste-free society appealed to Anna, who joined it in 1935.

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Statement II — CORRECT: Anna met Periyar for the first time in 1934 at the Sengundar Conference held in Tiruppur . This was the beginning of Anna's discipleship under Periyar. 🪤 TNPSC TRAP Examiners deliberately swap "1934 meet" and "1935 join" dates. Anna met Periyar in 1934 → joined Justice Party in 1935 → Later founded DMK in 1949 (September 17) — Periyar's birthday. Do not confuse these three key dates! 💎 LIVE GEM — Anna & Periyar Relationship Anna considered Periyar his guru and greatest leader.

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: When Anna founded DMK (1949), he declared: "As long as Periyar is alive, he is the Leader; I am only the General Secretary. " DMK emerged from Dravidar Kazhagam, but Anna explicitly said it walks in Periyar's footsteps. — Teacher: September 17 = Periyar's Birthday = DMK Formation Date (1949) 🏆 PYQ ALERT Anna's political timeline is a perennial TNPSC Unit 6 topic. Dates tested: 1934 (met Periyar), 1935 (joined Neetikatchi/Justice Party), 1949 (founded DMK).

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 🌾 Section 3: PM Fasal Bima Yojana (Crop Insurance Scheme) Q3 Which State had the highest percentage of insurance coverage under the Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY) in 2016–17? MEDIUM Factual ✅ Answer For 2016–17 specifically: Rajasthan topped registration (92%) under PMFBY. Tamil Nadu had achieved high coverage but Rajasthan led in % registration. PMFBY Registration Rank (2016-17): 1st → Rajasthan (~92%) | 2nd → Nagaland | 3rd → Himachal Pradesh TNPSC Exam Shortcut (Teacher's Note): In TNPSC exams, if Tamil Nadu is one of the options

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: However, year-specific questions like "2016-17" may differ — always check the year carefully! 💎 LIVE GEM — Hindi Scheme Names Decoded Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana: • Fasal = Harvest / Crop (அறுவடை) • Bima = Insurance / Protection (காப்பீடு) • Yojana = Scheme / Plan (திட்டம்) Teacher promises a separate video on Hindi terms for all central government schemes. Learning these roots helps decode any new scheme name in exams. 💡 TIP — TNPSC Tamil Nadu Bias For agriculture, infrastructure, welfare schemes — Tamil Nadu is frequently the correct a

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: The state has pioneered many "firsts. " But for questions tied to specific years or percentages, verify. When Tamil Nadu is an option and no year is specified, choose Tamil Nadu.

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: ⚖️ Section 4: OBC / Backward Class Commissions Q4 Choose the incorrect statement: (I) The First Backward Class Commission was constituted under the chairmanship of Kaka Kalelkar under Article 340. (II) It submitted its report in 1955. HARD Trap/Trick ✅ Answer (Incorrect Statement) Statement II is incorrect. (Answer Option A — only Statement I is correct) Statement I — CORRECT: The First OBC Commission (India) was constituted in 1953 under Kaka Kalelkar under Article 340 of the Constitution.

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: ✓ Statement II — INCORRECT: The commission submitted its report in 1956 , NOT 1955. The report was submitted but never implemented — no one acted on it. 🪤 TNPSC TRAP — Classic Year Confusion The exam tests 1955 vs 1956 . The Kaka Kalelkar Commission report was submitted in 1956 . Choosing 1955 costs you the mark. The report was famously shelved — never implemented. This is why Dr. Ambedkar resigned as Law Minister over OBC reservation not being implemented.

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 💎 LIVE GEM — All 4 OBC Commissions (India + Tamil Nadu) Commission Year Chairperson India — 1st OBC Commission 1953 Kaka Kalelkar (Report: 1956) India — 2nd OBC Commission 1979 VP Mandal (Mandal Commission) Tamil Nadu — 1st OBC Commission During Karunanidhi CM Sattanathan Commission Tamil Nadu — 2nd OBC Commission During MGR CM Ambasankar Commission Teacher noted: Dr. Ambedkar resigned as India's first Law Minister over the failure to implement OBC reservations — he was the most prominent champion of reservation rights.

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 🏆 PYQ ALERT This exact question (Kaka Kalelkar + Article 340 + year of report) has appeared multiple times in TNPSC exams. Mandal Commission (1979) is equally important — its implementation in 1990 under PM VP Singh triggered national protests.

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 📜 Section 5: Constitutional Amendments Q5 Which Constitutional Amendment Act provides 10% reservation in government jobs and educational institutions for Economically Weaker Sections (EWS)? MEDIUM Factual ✅ Answer 103rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 2019 EWS Reservation Details: 10% reservation for those who are NOT in SC/ST/OBC categories Annual family income below ₹8 lakh Enacted by: 103rd Amendment Act, 2019 Note: This 10% EWS quota does NOT apply in Tamil Nadu (TN has its own reservation structure) 💎 LIVE GEM — Key Amendments Cheat List (

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Amendment Key Change 93rd (2006) RTE — Free education for children (6-14 yrs) 99th (2014) National Judicial Appointments Commission (NJAC) 100th (2015) Land boundary agreement with Bangladesh 101st (2016) GST — Goods & Services Tax 103rd (2019) EWS — 10% reservation 104th (2020) Extension of SC/ST reservation in Parliament/Assemblies (25 more years) 105th (2021) State OBC lists — power restored to States 🏆 PYQ ALERT — GST Amendment GST = 101st Amendment Act (2016) — this is asked repeatedly in TNPSC and SSC.

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Also know: Finance Commission, Income Tax — these are repeat Economics topics.

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 📉 Section 6: Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI) Q6 When did the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) launch / introduce the Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI)? MEDIUM Factual ✅ Answer 2010 (Answer Option D) The Multidimensional Poverty Index was introduced in 2010 by the Oxford Poverty and Human Development Initiative (OPHI) in association with UNDP. It measures poverty across 3 dimensions — Health, Education, and Standard of Living .

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 💎 LIVE GEM — Key Development Index Dates (Exam Table) Index / Report Year Introduced By Whom PQLI (Physical Quality of Life Index) 1970s Morris D. Morris HDI (Human Development Index) 1990 UNDP Gross National Happiness Index 2012 (mainstreamed) Bhutan / UN MPI (Multidimensional Poverty Index) 2010 OPHI + UNDP Teacher Note: PQLI and HDI are in the Class 10 Social Science syllabus — study from that book for this concept. 🏆 PYQ ALERT A question on MPI appeared in TNPSC Group 2 exam recently.

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: OPHI (Oxford Poverty and Human Development Initiative) is the publishing body — know the full form!

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: ⚡ Section 7: Rural Electrification Q7 Which State/UT had NOT achieved 100% rural electrification by March 2017? (Options: Sikkim, Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, Odisha) HARD Trap/Trick ✅ Answer Karnataka and Odisha had NOT achieved 100% by March 2017. (Correct options: D — Karnataka & Odisha) Sikkim — had achieved 100% ✓ Tamil Nadu — had achieved 100% (since 1989 ) ✓ Karnataka — had NOT achieved ✗ Odisha — had NOT achieved ✗ The Pradhan Mantri UJALA Scheme / Saubhagya Scheme was launched to bring 100% electrification across all of India.

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 🚨 CRITICAL — Do NOT say Tamil Nadu failed! Tamil Nadu achieved 100% electrification in 1989 under CM Kalaignar Karunanidhi. Never select Tamil Nadu as the state that "failed" in electrification questions! 💎 LIVE GEM — India's FIRST 100% Electrified State Haryana was the FIRST state in India to achieve 100% electrification — in 1970 . Tamil Nadu followed in 1989 (under CM Karunanidhi). This is a frequently asked "first" in TNPSC. Memory: Haryana (1970) → Tamil Nadu (1989)

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 🌾 Section 8: Tamil Nadu Rice Research Institute (TNRRI) Q8 In which year was the Tamil Nadu Rice Research Institute (TNRRI) established? (Options: 1995 / 1985 / 1960 / 1970) EASY Factual ✅ Answer 1985 — Located at Aduthurai, Nagapattinam district (near Kumbakonam) The Tamil Nadu Rice Research Institute (TNRRI) was established in 1985 at Aduthurai , which is on the Kumbakonam–Mayiladuthurai road in Nagapattinam district. It conducts research on paddy varieties suited for Tamil Nadu's climate.

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 💎 LIVE GEM — Location Memory Trick Teacher personally visited Aduthurai when meeting a student there: "On the road from Kumbakonam to Mayiladuthurai — that's where TNRRI is located at Aduthurai. " Visualise the route: Kumbakonam → Aduthurai (TNRRI) → Mayiladuthurai .

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 📖 Section 9: Sangam Tamil Literature Q9 Select the literary work that does NOT mention the "Velan Veriyattu" (Velan's ritual festival / trance dance festival). MEDIUM Conceptual ✅ Answer Mullaipattu (முல்லைப்பாட்டு) — Answer Option D Velan Veriyattu = A ritual trance-dance performed by the Velan (priest of Murugan). • Thirumurugatrupadai — about Murugan → mentions Velan Veriyattu ✓ • Pattinappalai — mentions Velan Veriyattu ✓ • Kurinjipattu — Kurinji = Murugan's land → mentions it ✓ • Mullaipattu — Mullai = forest/pastoral → Lord is Mayon (Vis

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: ) In Sangam literature, each of the 5 Tinais (landscapes) has a presiding deity: Tinai (நிலம்) Deity (தலைவன்) Theme Kurinji (Hills) Murugan / Velan Love union Mullai (Forest) Mayon (Vishnu/Krishna) Waiting Marudam (Farmland) Indiran Infidelity Neithal (Seashore) Varuna Longing Palai (Desert) Korravai (Durga) Separation Key rule: Velan Veriyattu is ONLY associated with Murugan → Only Kurinji & Murugan-related texts. Mullaipattu's god is Mayon, so NO Velan connection!

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 💎 LIVE GEM — "Velan" in Sangam Literature In all of Sangam literature, only TWO names refer to Murugan: முருகன் (Murugan) and வேலன் (Velan) . Sanskrit names like Kandan, Kartikeyar, Subramaniyar are NOT found in Sangam texts. This is a unique exam gem — know it! 🏆 PYQ ALERT Tinai system + associated deities have appeared in TNPSC multiple times. Also tested: which Sangam text belongs to which tinai, and which customs are described in which text.

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 🏴 Section 10: Velu Nachiyar — Sivaganga Rani Q10 Choose the incorrect statement about Velu Nachiyar: (I) With the help of Maruthu brothers, Velu Nachiyar was crowned queen. (II) She was the first female ruler in India to fight against British colonial power. MEDIUM Factual ✅ Answer Both statements are CORRECT. (Answer Option D — Neither is incorrect) Statement I: Velu Nachiyar, with the help of Maruthu Pandiyar brothers (Chinna Maruthu & Periya Maruthu), recaptured Sivaganga and was crowned queen in 1780 .

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: ✓ Statement II: She IS the first female ruler in India to successfully fight and expel the British — unique because unlike others, she won and reclaimed her kingdom. ✓ 💎 LIVE GEM — Why Velu Nachiyar is UNIQUE in Indian History Every freedom fighter ultimately lost to the British — they were hanged, killed, or defeated. Velu Nachiyar is the ONLY ruler in Indian history who: Lost her kingdom to the British Built an army (including India's first woman suicide bomber — Kuyili ) Fought back and defeated the British Recaptured her kingdom and was cr

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Velu Nachiyar did all of this before Jhansi Rani was even born ! 🏆 PYQ ALERT Velu Nachiyar is a high-frequency TNPSC Unit 6 topic. Remember: Sivaganga Rani, Maruthu brothers support, crowned 1780, first female ruler to resist British in India.

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 💻 Section 11: e-Governance — TNSWAN Q11 What is the full form of TNSWAN? EASY Factual ✅ Answer Tamil Nadu State Wide Area Network (Answer Option A) TNSWAN = Tamil Nadu State Wide Area Network Established in 2007 under CM Kalaignar Karunanidhi — connects all government offices across Tamil Nadu for e-governance and data transfer. 💎 LIVE GEM — TN e-Governance Acronyms (All Must Know) Acronym Full Form Purpose TNSWAN TN State Wide Area Network Govt office connectivity (2007) TNDTPS / ELCOT TN Govt Cable TV / Electronics TN IT body Nambikkai Inay

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 🌱 Section 12: Agriculture — Navarai Pattam Q12 What is the sowing period of "Navarai Pattam" (நவரிப்பட்டம்)? MEDIUM Factual ✅ Answer November to December (Answer Option C) Navarai Pattam is one of Tamil Nadu's three traditional crop cultivation seasons (Pattams): • Sowing: November–December • Harvesting: February–March • Crops: Watermelon (தர்பூசணி), Cucumber (வெள்ளரிக்காய்), and other short-duration crops grown in cool-to-warm season transitions Memory Shortcut: "Nava = November" → Navarai starts in November!

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 💎 LIVE GEM — Tamil Nadu's 3 Pattams (Crop Seasons) Pattam Sowing Harvest Season Sornavarri (சொர்ணவாரி) April–May Aug–Sep SW Monsoon / Adi month Samba / Thaladi Aug–Sep Jan–Feb NE Monsoon Navarai (நவரிப்பட்டம்) Nov–Dec Feb–Mar Cool/dry season Teacher mentioned the classic novel "Ponniyin Selvan" begins with Aadi Pattam (Sornavarri season) — a great literary-agriculture connection to remember!

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 💬 Section 13: Periyar — Views on Education & Examination Q13 Choose the incorrect statement about Periyar: (I) Periyar said education should be given to all members of society. (II) Periyar did not give any opinion/comment about the examination system. MEDIUM Conceptual ✅ Answer Statement II is incorrect. (Answer Option B — only Statement I is correct) Statement I — CORRECT: Periyar believed education is the only tool for human advancement — regardless of caste, religion, race, or language.

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: ✓ Statement II — INCORRECT: Periyar DID comment on the examination system. He said the mark/rank-based examination system itself is wrong and should be abolished. He argued that ranking students by marks creates discrimination and inequality. 💎 LIVE GEM — Periyar's Vision on Education (Deep Insight) Periyar's views (as explained by teacher): Universal education : Every person — regardless of caste/religion — deserves education Against mark system : Periyar opposed ranking students by marks because it creates new forms of discrimination (first-

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Q14 Assertion–Reason: "Puli pasiththanna melivulla tharanuudaiyaar mannai menmaiyodu eynththa vagaiyil ulavokiyaro" — The Assertion states that those with small efforts (like mice) should not befriend those with great power (like tigers). The Reason explains this principle. Are both statement and reason correct and the reason properly explains the assertion? HARD Conceptual ✅ Answer Both Statement and Reason are correct, and Reason properly explains the Statement.

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: (Answer Option A) Verse meaning: "Those who have the humility of a mouse (small effort) living alongside those with the might of a tiger — is that wise? " → The verse advises: befriend equals or those more powerful, but do not try to maintain friendship with those far below your capability (as it leads to imbalance). The explanation (reason) given — that small-effort people should not befriend great-power people — correctly interprets the verse. This verse is from Purananuru or a Sangam-era ethical poem (the teacher indicated it's from Tamil cl

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 💎 LIVE GEM — Assertion-Reason Strategy for Literature Questions When the assertion is a Tamil literary verse and the reason is its meaning: First verify if the meaning given IS the correct meaning of the verse If meaning is correct, both assertion and reason are usually right These questions appear in TNPSC Unit 6 under Tamil Literature and Ethics Q15 15. Which year has been decided to be observed as the International Yea of millets by World Food and Agriculture Organization ?

chunks.title: Crack Sheet
chunks.text: EASY Factual ✅ Answer 2023 Background: The resolution was proposed by India, supported by more than 70 nations, and officially adopted by the UN General Assembly. Purpose: To raise global awareness about the nutritional and health benefits of millets, and to advocate for their sustainable, climate-resilient cultivation in arid regions.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: 📿 Today's Thirukkural (Session Opener) "காலத்தினால் செய்த நன்றி சிறிதெனினும் ஞாலத்தின் மாணப் பெரிது" English Translation: "A favour done at the right time, however small it may be, is in reality greater than the world itself. " Exam Context: This Kural appears in the chapter on Gratitude (நன்றி). The teacher's example: giving ₹10 to a hungry person at the right moment is worth more than any grand gesture. Applied to teaching: even a single question shared at the right time can change an exam outcome.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q1 MEDIUM FACTUAL 🏆 PYQ — TNPSC Gr. 2 When was the Madras Native Association (Chennai Vasikal Sangam / சென்னை வாசிகள் சங்கம்) founded, and by whom? ▾ ✅ Answer 26 February 1852 — Founded by Gazulu Lakshminarasu (காஜுல லட்சுமி நரசு) 📌 Explanation The Madras Native Association was the first major (largest) association formed in all of India (முதல் பெரிய சங்கம்). Its effort led to the passage of the Abolition of Torture Act (சித்திரவதை தடைச் சட்டம்), which abolished the practice of torture by authorities in the Madras Presidency.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: 🏆 PYQ ALERT This question has been asked in TNPSC Group 2 exam (recent year) . The association's name, founding date, and the law it helped pass are all commonly tested. 🪤 TNPSC TRAP Examiners swap "First Political Association" and "First Major Association" to confuse you: • First MAJOR/LARGEST Association in India → Madras Native Association (1852) • First POLITICAL Association in India → Madras Mahajana Sabha (1884) Both have been tested together in Group 4 exams with swapped answer options!

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: 💎 Live Gem Teacher's exact words: "Studying is important, but studying with clarity is far more important. You can study and still fail. Only those who study with clarity will pass. " This distinction between rote reading and conceptual clarity is the core exam strategy.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q2 HARD TRAP/TRICK 🏆 PYQ — TNPSC Gr. 4 Choose the CORRECT statements about the Madras Mahajana Sabha (1884): I. Its inaugural meeting was held on May 16, 1884. Subramaniam, Veeraraghavachariar, and Ananthaselu Rangaiah were key figures who participated. ▾ ✅ Answer Both statements are correct → Answer Option C (Both I and II) ⚠️ CORRECTION NOTE — DATE TRAP The question in the live session initially showed "March 16" — this was a deliberate trap. The teacher confirmed: the correct date is May 16, 1884 (not March 16).

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: The teacher used this to check if students were paying attention. Several students missed it. 📌 Key Facts About Madras Mahajana Sabha Founded: May 16, 1884 First President: P. Rangaiah Naidu (பி. ரங்கையா நாயுடு) Significance: India's first political association (முதல் அரசியல் ரீதியான சங்கம்) Precursor to Congress: Indian National Congress (founded Dec 28, 1885 in Bombay) adopted all of Sabha's demands Demand 1: ICS exams should be held BOTH in London AND in India (not India alone) Demand 2: Separate the Judiciary from the Executive — later ens

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: The teacher called this a "shortcut": remember 50-50 → Article 50 = separation of judiciary from executive. 🏆 PYQ ALERT TNPSC has asked: "Who was the first President of Madras Mahajana Sabha? Rangaiah Naidu . Also asked in Group 4 as a "choose correct statement" question.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q3 MEDIUM FACTUAL Choose the CORRECT statement(s) about the early Indian National Congress's demands: I. The ICS exam should be conducted ONLY in India. Executive and Judiciary administration must be completely separated. ▾ ✅ Answer Only Statement II is correct → Answer Option B (II only) Statement I is WRONG — Congress demanded ICS exams in BOTH London AND India, not India alone. 📌 Explanation The early Congress (rooted in Madras Mahajana Sabha's agenda) demanded simultaneous ICS examinations in London and India — so Indian candidates didn't

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Statement I incorrectly says "only in India. " This is a classic TNPSC trap — already asked in an actual TNPSC exam. 🪤 TNPSC TRAP The word "only" (மட்டும்) is the trap word. The actual demand was "ALSO in India" — not "ONLY in India.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q4 MEDIUM FACTUAL 🏆 PYQ Where was the All India Khilafat Conference held in November 1919? ▾ ✅ Answer Delhi → Answer Option D 📌 Key Facts: Khilafat Movement Khilafat (خلافت) = Arabic word meaning "Opposition/Caliphate" — refers to the movement to restore the Ottoman Caliph (spiritual head of all Muslims) Founded in India by: Maulana Mohammad Ali and Maulana Shaukat Ali (Ali Brothers) Gandhi's role: He supported the movement and chaired the All India Khilafat Conference in Delhi (November 1919) Tamil Nadu Khilafat Conference: Held at Erode (no

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: The Khilafat Conference in Tamil Nadu was held at Erode — the birthplace of Periyar (E. Erode is in the Kongu region (western TN), not near Vaniambadi (Vellore district). 💎 Live Gem — Student Mnemonic A student (Leodas) shared: "Valentine's Day → REST? " → to remember Indian Unrest was written by Valentine Chirol . The teacher loved this and confirmed it for use.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q5 HARD FACTUAL 💎 GEM Who is the author of the book "Indian Unrest" (1910)? And who is known as the "Father of Indian Unrest"? ▾ ✅ Answer Author of "Indian Unrest": Valentine Chirol (published 1910) "Father of Indian Unrest": Bal Gangadhar Tilak 📌 Explanation "Indian Unrest" documents the rise of revolutionary nationalism among Indian youth against British rule in the early 1900s. Valentine Chirol, a British journalist, wrote it. He described how Indian youth's anger toward the British was growing.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: He called Bal Gangadhar Tilak the "Father of Indian Unrest" — meaning Tilak was the one who ignited this revolutionary fire. As a result, Tilak was arrested and sent to jail. 💎 Live Gem — Memory Trick "Valentine → REST" : Valentine's Day gives REST, but Valentine Chirol wrote about Indian UN-REST. This mnemonic was shared live and confirmed by the teacher as an effective memory tool. Concept reminder: When studying History, years are extremely important .

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: The teacher emphasized: "One concept from this topic — knowing the year + the connection between events — is what separates toppers from others.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q6 EASY FACTUAL India's first Trade Union (Labour Union): The Madras Labour Union was founded by whom, and on what date? Wadia — founded on April 13, 1918 📌 Details The Madras Labour Union (Madras Thozhirsangam) was India's first labour union. Other important Tamil leaders associated with it include Singaravelar (சிங்காரவேலர்) and Thiru. Kalyanasundaram (திருவி கல்யாணசுந்தரம்). 💡 TIP Remember April 13, 1918 — same year as Jallianwala Bagh massacre (April 13, 1919 is Jallianwala).

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q7 HARD TRAP/TRICK 💎 GEM What was the Tinkathia System (தின்கதியா முறை) practiced in Champaran, and why did Gandhi oppose it? ▾ ✅ Answer The Tinkathia System forced farmers to cultivate Indigo (நீலச்சாயச் செடி / அவுரி செடி) on 3 out of every 20 acres of their land — compulsorily for the British. 📌 Complete Explanation Tinkathia = "Three Kattha" — farmers had to plant indigo on 3 katthas out of every 20 (i. , 3/20 of land) Indigo = blue dye plant (Avuri plant/அவுரி செடி) — Europeans needed it for textile dyeing; Indians had no use for it Probl

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Champaran = First Satyagraha. Kheda = First Civil Disobedience. 💎 Live Gem — Student Question Answered Student asked: "Why did the British listen to Gandhi when he went on a hunger strike? Couldn't they just ignore it? " Teacher's answer: Gandhi's power was Ahimsa (non-violence). The British strategy was to arrest leaders and use police to break up protests. But Gandhi's non-violent approach meant people wouldn't fight back — so the British couldn't justify arrests or use force.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: They couldn't figure out how to suppress it. His hunger strikes created moral and public pressure that the British couldn't easily counter with violence.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q8 MEDIUM FACTUAL Gandhi's first Civil Disobedience Movement in India — which Satyagraha was it, in which state, and in what year? ▾ ✅ Answer Kheda (Kaira) Satyagraha, 1918 — in Gujarat Month: March–April 1918 📌 Why Kheda = Civil Disobedience? In Kheda, there was a law (Famine Code) that said: if crop yield falls below 25%, farmers are exempt from paying taxes. But British officers were still forcibly collecting taxes during a famine year. Gandhi called on farmers to disobey this unjust enforcement of the law — hence Civil Disobedience (சட்டமற

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q9 MEDIUM CONCEPTUAL 💎 GEM Where was the First Round Table Conference held? Who chaired it? Was it successful? ▾ ✅ Answer Held in London → All three Round Table Conferences were held in London. Chaired by Ramsay MacDonald (British Prime Minister) Result: NOT successful — Gandhi did not attend the First RTC. 📌 Round Table Conference Summary Conference Year Location Gandhi? Outcome 1st RTC 1930 London ❌ Absent Failed — no Congress participation 2nd RTC 1931 London ✅ Present Gandhi–Ambedkar clash on separate electorates 3rd RTC 1932 London ❌ Abs

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: The Simon Commission was boycotted by most Indians because it had no Indian members. 💎 Live Gem — Simon Commission Context The 1919 Government of India Act was valid for only 10 years → due for review by 1929. The British sent the Simon Commission to review it. Indians boycotted it. The RTCs were set up to analyse the Simon Report. All 3 were held in London (not Delhi, not Bombay). A "round table" means equal seating — everyone sat at a round table to symbolize equal status.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q10 EASY FACTUAL On January 8, 1933 — which day was observed / celebrated? ▾ ✅ Answer Temple Entry Day (கோவில் நுழைவு நாள்) — announced by Mahatma Gandhi 📌 Explanation Gandhi announced January 8, 1933 as "Temple Entry Day" to fight against untouchability and promote the right of lower-caste Hindus to enter temples. This was part of his broader Harijan upliftment campaign.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q11 MEDIUM FACTUAL 💎 GEM Choose the correct statement(s) about Annie Besant: I. She started the newspaper "Commonwealth" in 1914. In 1917, she published the book "How India Wrought for Freedom. " ▾ ✅ Answer Both statements are correct → Answer Option C (Both I and II) 📌 Annie Besant — Key Facts for TNPSC Nationality: Irish/British — she worked for India's freedom Newspaper: "New India" (1914) — British banned "Commonwealth," so she started "New India" Book: "How India Wrought for Freedom" (1917) Home Rule League: Founded in September 1916 (Ti

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Study her thoroughly. " Key exam points: nationality, newspaper names (Commonwealth → banned → New India), book title, Home Rule League founding, and first woman INC president. 💡 HOMEWORK QUESTION (Session End) Q: Who founded Banaras Hindu University (BHU)? Options: (A) Mahatma Gandhi (B) Mohandas (C) Madan Mohan Malaviya (D) B. Wadia Answer to be posted in comments. Hint: It's NOT Gandhi.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q12 MEDIUM FACTUAL Choose the correct statement about the Indian Rupee symbol (₹): I. It was designed by D. Udaya Kumar, a graduate of IIT Kharagpur. The new currency symbol came into use on July 15, 2010. ▾ ✅ Answer Only Statement II is correct → Answer Option B (II only) ⚠️ CORRECTION NOTE — IIT Campus Statement I says "IIT Kharagpur" — this is WRONG . Udaya Kumar studied at IIT Bombay (Mumbai) , not IIT Kharagpur. This was a deliberate trap in the question.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Several students missed it. 📌 ₹ Symbol Facts An open competition was held — over 3,000+ designs submitted Indian Government selected D. Udaya Kumar's design His father was a former DMK member India became the 5th country to have a unique currency symbol Others before India: USA ($), UK (£), Japan (¥), EU (€) The 4th country (before India) was the EU 🪤 TNPSC TRAP — "Laddoo or Nuttu" Teacher's phrase: "Hidden inside the question like a laddoo (sweet) — look carefully!

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: " The IIT campus name is the trap. "IIT Kharagpur" vs "IIT Bombay" — both are real IITs, making this a high-quality distractor.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q13 HARD CONCEPTUAL 💎 GEM If the rate of inflation is in single digits (3%–9%), what type of inflation is it called? ▾ ✅ Answer Walking Inflation (நடக்கும் பணவீக்கம்) 📌 Complete Inflation Classification Table Type Rate Tamil Name Nature Creeping Inflation Below 3% தவலும் பணவீக்கம் Very slow, manageable Walking Inflation 3% – 9% நடக்கும் பணவீக்கம் Moderate, concerning Running Inflation 10% – 20% ஓடும் பணவீக்கம் Dangerous, fast Galloping / Hyper Inflation 20% – 100%+ தாவும் பணவீக்கம் Catastrophic Inflation = When the value of money (currency) d

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: The more money in circulation, the higher inflation. 💎 Live Gem — Understanding Economics Teacher's advice on Economics study strategy: "Don't memorize economics terms. UNDERSTAND them. If you understand what inflation is — money value down, prices up — then all the sub-types make logical sense. Knowledge wins competitive exams, not notes. " Key terms to understand (not memorize): Inflation, Deflation, Stagflation, GDP, GNP, NNI, CRR, SLR, Repo Rate.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: 💡 REAL-LIFE CONNECTION (Teacher's Example) "Even today, petrol prices were raised 4 times this week. That IS inflation — the value of your rupee is buying less petrol than before. " — Teacher used current events to make the concept tangible.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q14 EASY FACTUAL Who manages / controls Paper Currency (காகித பண முறை) in India? ▾ ✅ Answer Central Monetary Authority = Reserve Bank of India (RBI) 📌 Key Point RBI = Central Monetary Authority. No other body controls paper currency. The Class 12 textbook uses the term "Central Monetary Authority" — this IS the RBI. Don't confuse it with State Government or Central Government.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q15 MEDIUM FACTUAL A Debit Card is an example of which type of money? ▾ ✅ Answer Plastic Money (நெகிழி பணம்) → Answer Option B 📌 Types of Money Type Example Tamil Paper Money / Fiat Money Currency notes (₹500, ₹100) காகித பணம் Plastic Money Debit Card, Credit Card நெகிழி பணம் Credit Money / Loan Money Cheques, Loans கடன் பணம் Important life tip from teacher: "You can use anything in life, but NEVER get a credit card. It will make your life very difficult.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Debit card = fine, it's your own money. Credit card = borrowed money with high interest. " 🪤 TRAP Some students answered "Paper Currency" because a debit card looks like it's related to paper money transactions. But the physical material of the card is plastic → Plastic Money.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q16 HARD TRAP/TRICK Choose the correct statement(s): I. The Central Bank of China was established in 1928. The Reserve Bank of New Zealand was established in 1934. ▾ ✅ Answer Only Statement II is correct → Answer Option B (II only) ⚠️ CORRECTION — Central Bank of China Statement I says 1928 — this is WRONG. The Central Bank of China was established in 1924 (not 1928). The current "Central Bank of China" is actually headquartered in Taiwan — because after the split between China and Taiwan, the original bank remained in Taiwan.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: 💎 Live Gem — Historical Context The teacher explained the China-Taiwan split: China and Taiwan were once one nation. After WWII, when the People's Republic of China was formed, the original Nationalist government fled to Taiwan. The Central Bank of China (1924) stayed in Taiwan. So today, the "Central Bank of China" is in Taiwan, while mainland China has the "People's Bank of China. " Key fact: RBI was recommended by the Hilton Young Committee and established in 1934 (same year as Reserve Bank of New Zealand).

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q17 MEDIUM FACTUAL 💎 GEM Who controls / is responsible for managing counterfeit / fake currency (போலியான பணம்) in circulation? ▾ ✅ Answer Reserve Bank of India (RBI) — Answer: Central Monetary Authority / RBI 📌 RBI's Role in Fake Currency The RBI monitors, identifies, and controls fake currency in India. It also controls the volume of currency in circulation — too much money in circulation causes inflation; too little causes deflation. This is why the RBI (not the Government of India) controls money supply.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: The 2016 Demonetisation (Nov 8, 2016) — when ₹500 and ₹1000 notes were banned — was partly aimed at removing fake currency (reportedly being pumped by Pakistan into India to destabilize the economy). 💎 Live Gem — Demonetisation Context Teacher's complete explanation: Pakistan had allegedly prepared a large volume of fake Indian currency to flood India's economy. To counter this, PM Modi announced Demonetisation on November 8, 2016. The film "Dhurandhar" (recently released) depicts this story.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: RBI's normal annual job is to check and replace fake notes — Demonetisation was an extraordinary measure. Even 10 years later (November 2026 will be the 10th anniversary), fake currency still exists, managed by the RBI.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q18 EASY FACTUAL 🏆 PYQ In which year was the Imperial Bank of India converted to the State Bank of India (SBI)? ▾ ✅ Answer 1955 (July 1, 1955) 📌 History of SBI Stage Bank Name Year Stage 1 Bank of Bengal (first of the three) 1806 Stage 2 Bank of Madras + Bank of Bombay + Bank of Bengal merged 1921 Stage 3 (merged name) Imperial Bank of India 1921 Stage 4 (nationalized) State Bank of India (SBI) 1955 July is the key month for Economics facts: GST launched July 1, SBI nationalized July 1, etc.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q19 MEDIUM CONCEPTUAL 💎 GEM Does the Anti-Defection Law (கட்சி தாவல் தடை சட்டம்) apply when an elected MLA resigns from their party and joins another party? ▾ ✅ Answer NO — If the MLA resigns from their post FIRST and then joins another party, the Anti-Defection Law does NOT apply. 📌 When Does Anti-Defection Apply? The Anti-Defection Law (10th Schedule, 1985) applies when a person holds an elected post (MLA/MP) and simultaneously supports or joins another party without resigning .

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: If they resign first → free to join any party → no defection. Recent example: AIADMK MLAs who resigned their positions and then joined another party — Anti-Defection law did NOT apply. 💎 Live Gem — Clear Conceptual Explanation Teacher's analogy: "If you are working in Company X and while still employed you start supporting Company Y, that is defection. But if you resign from Company X and then join Company Y, that is your free choice — no defection.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: " The public post is like employment — you can't serve two masters simultaneously.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q20 EASY FACTUAL 📝 HOMEWORK Who founded Banaras Hindu University (BHU)? Options: (A) Mahatma Gandhi (B) Mohandas (C) Madan Mohan Malaviya (D) B. Wadia ▾ ✅ Answer (Homework — to be verified) Madan Mohan Malaviya — founded BHU in 1916 📌 BHU Facts Banaras Hindu University (BHU) was founded by Pandit Madan Mohan Malaviya in 1916 in Varanasi (Banaras), Uttar Pradesh. Gandhi inaugurated the foundation stone-laying ceremony but did NOT found it. This is a classic TNPSC trap — Gandhi's presence at the ceremony leads students to choose Gandhi as the f

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: 🪤 CLASSIC TNPSC TRAP Gandhi gave a famous speech at BHU's founding event — but Madan Mohan Malaviya was the founder. The speech itself became historically significant (Gandhi criticized the Indian princes and wealthy at the event).

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q: The "First Political Association" and "First Major Association" of India — match them correctly with their names, years, and founders. ⚡ REASON: This distinction has been tested in Group 2 and Group 4. Examiners deliberately swap the two. With 2026 exams covering 19th century nationalism, this is near-certain. Q: What is the Tinkathia System? OR: Gandhi's first Satyagraha in India was in connection with which system? ⚡ REASON: Champaran Satyagraha is a standard question.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: The Tinkathia System (3/20 acres indigo) is the conceptual follow-up that differentiates Group 1 level candidates. Q: Which committee recommended the establishment of the Reserve Bank of India? ⚡ REASON: RBI questions appear in almost every TNPSC exam cycle. The Hilton Young Committee is a specific, testable fact that many students overlook. Q: When inflation rate is between 20%–100%, it is called ___? (Walking / Running / Galloping / Creeping) ⚡ REASON: Inflation classification from Class 12 Economics is high-frequency TNPSC material.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: The percentages are frequently tested as matching or fill-in questions. Q: Annie Besant was the first woman president of the Indian National Congress. ⚡ REASON: Teacher explicitly stated Annie Besant always has ≥1 question in 200-question TNPSC exams. Her 1917 INC presidency + Home Rule League are core facts.

(1st Major) 1884 → Madras Mahajana Sabha (1st Political) 1885 → Indian National Congress ✊ Gandhi's Firsts in India Satyagraha → Champaran 1917 Civil Disobedience → Kheda 1918 Hunger Strike → Ahmedabad 1918 📊 Inflation Types <3% → Creeping 3–9% → Walking 10–20% → Running 20–100%+ → Galloping/Hyper 🏦 Banking History 3 banks (1921) → Imperial Bank Imperial Bank (1955) → SBI RBI established: 1934 RBI recommended: Hilton Young Committee 💰 Money Types Notes/coins → Fiat/Paper Money D

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
