chunks.text: 2s;" onmouseover="this. background='rgba(245,200,66,0. 12)';" onmouseout="this. background='rgba(13,15,20,0. 92)';"> ← Hub 📚 High-Yield Q&A Revision Crack Sheet

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: YouTube Live Session · TNPSC Group 1 / 2 / 4 · Topic: Ancient India — Indus Valley 50 Questions TNPSC Gr. 4 / VAO Show / Hide Toggle on Every Q 💎 Live Gems Included ⚠️ Trap Alerts Filter: All Easy Medium Hard 💎 Gems Only 🏆 PYQ Only Easy Medium Hard [CRITICAL] [TRAP] [TIP] [PYQ] [💎 GEM] Session Overview 50 Questions 8 Sub-Topics 12 💎 Gems 9 🏆 PYQs 8 ⚠️ Traps This live session covered the complete Indus Valley / Harappan Civilisation topic for TNPSC Group 1/2/4.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Topics include: site identification & locations, excavators, associated rivers, seals & animals, religious beliefs, trade, urban planning, decline, and special features of key sites like Lothal, Dholavira, Mohenjo-daro. Medium of instruction: Tamil (translated to English below).

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Q1–Q3, Q34 Q01 MEDIUM Factual 🏆 PYQ Which of the following Harappan sites are NOT located in present-day India? (Options: Harappa, Mohenjo-daro, Lothal, Kalibangan, Banawali, Rupar, Surkotada, Dholavira) ✅ ANSWER Harappa and Mohenjo-daro — both are located in present-day Pakistan . 📌 EXPLANATION The Harappan Civilisation spread across a vast region — majority of major sites fall in Pakistan (Punjab & Sindh provinces). Indian sites include: Lothal & Dholavira (Gujarat), Kalibangan (Rajasthan), Rupar (Punjab), Banawali (Haryana), Rakhigarhi (Ha

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Sites extending south reach Maharashtra; north reaches Haryana. 🏆 PYQ ALERT This question type — identifying non-Indian Harappan sites — has appeared multiple times in TNPSC Group 2/4 papers. Mohenjo-daro (Pakistan) and Harappa (Pakistan) are the standard expected answers. 💎 LIVE GEM Teacher's tip: "Harappan Civilisation covered Pakistan fully — that's the bulk. India only has a few sites. Gujarat (Lothal, Dholavira), Haryana (Rakhigarhi, Banawali), Rajasthan (Kalibangan) — that's the Indian portion.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Everything in Punjab & Sindh = Pakistan. " Q02 MEDIUM Factual 🏆 PYQ 💎 Gem Where is the Harappan site "Dholavira" located? (Context: It is located on Khadir Island, Rann of Kutch, Gujarat) ✅ ANSWER Dholavira is located on Khadir Island, in the Rann of Kutch, Gujarat . 📌 EXPLANATION Many students confused Dholavira with Lothal (also in Gujarat). Lothal is on the Sabarmati tributary near Cambay Gulf — NOT in Rann of Kutch. Dholavira is specifically on Khadir Island in the Rann of Kutch (Great Rann).

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Remember: Dholavira = Rann of Kutch = Island. ⚠️ TRAP NOTE Most students answered "Lothal" for "Rann of Kutch location" — this is WRONG. Lothal is near Cambay Gulf on the Bhogavo River / Sabarmati tributary. Dholavira = Khadir Island = Rann of Kutch. 💎 LIVE GEM Teacher's memory trick: "Dholavira on an island in Rann of Kutch — visualise it as a fortress-city on a salt island. It also has the world's oldest signboard (inscription) found at its gates — 10 large Indus script signs.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: " 🏆 PYQ ALERT Dholavira's location (Khadir Island, Rann of Kutch) has appeared in TNPSC Group 2 (2019) and Group 4 exams. High-frequency topic. Q03 MEDIUM Conceptual Which of the following statements about the Harappan Civilisation is INCORRECT? (1) All sites had urban civilisation. (2) Harappan towns depended on villages for food and workers. (3) Remains are found even in small villages. (4) Lothal's special feature is its port/dock. ✅ ANSWER Statement (1) is INCORRECT : Not all Harappan sites were urban — the civilisation comprised 6 major c

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 📌 EXPLANATION The Harappan Civilisation had approximately 6 major cities and around 200 villages. Villages engaged in agriculture and supplied food and labour to urban centres. Lothal is indeed famous as a port/dockyard. So statements 2, 3, and 4 are correct; only statement 1 is false. 💎 LIVE GEM Teacher: "Remember — 6 cities, 200 villages. That's the magic number for Harappan Civilisation. When they say 'all sites were urban' — that's the trap.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Villages supported cities just like Keezhadi in Madurai shows a Tamil civilisation. " Q34 EASY Factual In which district is Harappa located? What is the district called today? ✅ ANSWER Harappa is located in Montgomery District, Punjab, Pakistan (now called Sahiwal District). 📌 EXPLANATION The original colonial-era name was Montgomery District. It is easily identifiable on maps as a Pakistan Punjab region — no Indian district by this name exists.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Q5, Q15, Q28, Q31 Q05 HARD Match the Following 🏆 PYQ 💎 Gem Match the Harappan site with the river it is associated with: Lothal – Kalibangan – Rupar – Harappa – Mohenjo-daro ✅ ANSWER Lothal → Bhogavo (tributary of Sabarmati) Kalibangan → Ghaggar (Hakra) Rupar → Sutlej (Satluj) Harappa → Ravi Mohenjo-daro → Indus (Sindhu) 📌 EXPLANATION Mohenjo-daro is situated on the banks of the Indus River — the most fertile land of the Indus, hence called "Garden of Sindhu.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: " Harappa is on the Ravi. Kalibangan = Ghaggar (the dried-up Saraswati bed). Rupar = Sutlej. Lothal = Bhogavo/Sabarmati tributary near Gulf of Khambhat. 🏆 PYQ ALERT This match-the-following question type (Sites ↔ Rivers) is one of the most frequently repeated in TNPSC Group 2 and Group 4 exams. Expect it in 2026. 💎 LIVE GEM — MUST MEMORISE TRIO Teacher: "Memorise just 3 pairs — the rest you can guess: 1. Mohenjo-daro → Indus (Sindhu = fertile land of the Indus) 2.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Harappa → Ravi 3. Rupar → Sutlej Student shortcut shared: 'Rupa Sattun' (Rupar-Sutlej sounded like 'Rupa satt de' — pay up! )" 💡 TIP Think of it this way: Mohenjo-daro = Indus civilisation's heart = Indus river. Just like Keezhadi = Vaigai River, Madurai. Connect location logic. Q31 EASY Factual 💎 Gem On which river bank is Kalibangan located? ✅ ANSWER Ghaggar (Hakra) River — Kalibangan is in Rajasthan on the banks of the Ghaggar. 💎 LIVE GEM Teacher's trick: "Kalibangan = Kali (empty/black) + bangan.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Kalibangan is in Rajasthan — the state that is mostly empty/dry. Ghaggar is a dried-up seasonal river = dry = Rajasthan. Easy link: Kali (empty) Rajasthan = Ghaggar (dry river). " Q15 HARD Trick/Trap 🏆 PYQ 💎 Gem Which of the following statements about Lothal is INCORRECT? (1) Lothal is found in the Gulf of Cambay (Khambhat). (2) It is a port city; dock is 37m long and 216m wide. (3) This city was connected with the Bhogavo river. (4) Foreign trade was conducted from this port.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: ✅ ANSWER Statement (2) is INCORRECT — The actual dock dimensions are approximately 37m long and 21. 8m wide , not 216m wide. (Some sources give 216m long × 37m wide — verify with your textbook; key point: exact numbers differ). 📌 EXPLANATION Lothal: (1) ✅ Located in Gulf of Cambay/Khambhat, Gujarat. (3) ✅ Connected with Bhogavo River (tributary of Sabarmati). (4) ✅ Foreign trade with Mesopotamia confirmed. (2) ❌ The dimension "216m wide" is fabricated — the actual dock is much smaller.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Lothal's dockyard is a man-made structure, the world's earliest known. 💎 LIVE GEM Teacher: "Lothal's river = Bhogavo (Pogava). Kalibangan's river = Ghaggar. These two are most confused in exams. Also: the word 'Lothal' in Gujarati = LOTH (dead) + AL (place) = 'Mound of the Dead' — same meaning as Mohenjo-daro! Both mean 'Mound of the Dead' in their respective languages. " 🏆 PYQ ALERT Lothal's features — dock, location, connected river — appear repeatedly in TNPSC exams.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: The false dimension in statement (2) is a standard exam trap. Q28 MEDIUM Match the Following 🏆 PYQ Match the Harappan sites with their states/locations: Harappa – Mohenjo-daro – Kalibangan – Lothal ✅ ANSWER Harappa → Punjab, Pakistan Mohenjo-daro → Sindh, Pakistan (Indus River) Kalibangan → Rajasthan, India Lothal → Gujarat, India 📌 EXPLANATION Lothal is in Gujarat on Bhogavo River (Sabarmati tributary) — near Gulf of Khambhat. Kalibangan is in Rajasthan on Ghaggar River.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Harappa is in Pakistan Punjab on Ravi River. Mohenjo-daro is in Pakistan Sindh on Indus River.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Q4, Q25–Q27, Q35, Q39 Q04 MEDIUM Factual 🏆 PYQ 💎 Gem Who conducted the excavation at Mohenjo-daro? ✅ ANSWER Sir Mortimer Wheeler conducted excavations at Mohenjo-daro. 📌 EXPLANATION Key excavators: Mohenjo-daro → Mortimer Wheeler | Harappa → R. Banerji (discovered by), Daya Ram Sahni (first excavation) | Overall Indus excavation supervised by Sir John Marshall . 💎 LIVE GEM — MEMORY TRICK Teacher: "Mohenjo-daro → Mortimer Wheeler. 'M' for Mohenjo = 'M' for Mortimer (Mama series — M M!

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: For Harappa → Daya Ram Sahni = 'Sinda Sahani' (Sindhu Sahani) — the 'S' family: Sindhu = Sahani. Sir John Marshall supervised the whole thing and took it further. Then Daya Ram Sahni took it to the next level. " Q25 MEDIUM Factual 💎 Gem Who first visited Harappa in 1826 CE? ✅ ANSWER Charles Masson was the first person to visit Harappa in 1826. 💎 LIVE GEM — MEMORY TRICK Teacher: "Charles Masson → Harappa. The site has a 'Bison/Buffalo' image — Google 'Harappa civilisation bison' — a bull seal comes up.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Bison in English = Masson sounds like 'Mason' → Mason-Bison shortcut! " Q26 MEDIUM Factual 🏆 PYQ Who first identified and recorded the antiquities (remains/seals) of Harappa? ✅ ANSWER Alexander Cunningham first identified and recorded the antiquities of Harappa. 📌 EXPLANATION Charles Masson → first visited (1826). Alexander Cunningham → first recorded/identified the seals & antiquities. Banerji → major excavation. Sir John Marshall → supervised and directed large-scale excavation.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Daya Ram Sahni → further excavation of Harappa. Alexander Cunningham is also called the "Father of Indian Archaeology" and founded the ASI in 1861. Q27 MEDIUM Factual 🏆 PYQ 💎 Gem Under whose guidance/direction were Mohenjo-daro and Harappa excavated? ✅ ANSWER Sir John Marshall directed the excavations of both Mohenjo-daro and Harappa. 💎 LIVE GEM — ASI FACTS Teacher: "Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) was founded in 1861 by Alexander Cunningham — he was its first Director General.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: In Tamil Nadu, the first ASI head was Nagaswamy . To find the Indian ASI founding year: 1861. Add 100 → 1961 (Tamil Nadu ASI founding parallel). " Q35 HARD Match the Following 🏆 PYQ Match the Harappan sites with their excavators: Chanhu-daro – Kulli – Rangpur – Dholavira ✅ ANSWER Chanhu-daro → Majumdar (N. Majumdar) Kulli → Aurel Stein Rangpur → Amendra Nath (A. Ghosh) Dholavira → J. Joshi 📌 EXPLANATION The teacher used a shortcut: "Majumdar appears in the options → directly map to Chanhu-daro.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: " Lothal's excavator = S. Rakhigarhi = Amendra Nath / A. Joshi (who discovered it in 1968). 💡 TIP Answer key (per teacher): Option C — Chanhu-daro=Majumdar, Kulli=Aurel Stein, Rangpur=Amendra Nath, Dholavira=J. Q39 EASY Factual 🏆 PYQ Who was the first Director General of the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI)? ✅ ANSWER Alexander Cunningham — founded ASI in 1861 and was its first Director General. Also called the "Father of Indian Archaeology.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Q6, Q8, Q13, Q18, Q21–Q24, Q29, Q32–Q33, Q41–Q43, Q48–Q49 Q06 HARD Trick/Trap Which of the following statements about the Harappan Civilisation is INCORRECT? (1) Peepal tree and Acacia tree were considered sacred. (2) Clay female figurines represent female deities. (3) The practice of cremating dead bodies was NOT followed at Harappa. (4) They worshipped the proto-form of Shiva. ✅ ANSWER Statement (3) is INCORRECT : Harappans DID follow cremation — that's why the burial sites are called "mounds of the dead" (Mohenjo-daro).

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: The site itself is named after this practice. 📌 EXPLANATION (1) ✅ Peepal (sacred fig) and Acacia were sacred trees. (2) ✅ Female clay figurines = mother goddess worship. (3) ❌ WRONG — cremation WAS practiced. (4) ✅ Pashupati/Vishupati seal shows proto-Shiva. The name "Mohenjo-daro" literally means "Mound of the Dead" because of the burial/cremation mounds. ⚠️ TRAP NOTE The phrase "was NOT followed" in statement (3) is a classic exam trap. Students read "cremation was not followed" and assume it's true — but Harappans DID cremate.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: The statement is FALSE because cremation WAS indeed practiced. Q08 MEDIUM Factual What type of pottery is associated with the Indus Valley Civilisation that shows the world's earliest evidence? (Options: Cut, Knapped, Perforated, Polished — i. , Incised, Knapped, Perforated, Polished) ✅ ANSWER Polished pottery (Lustrous / Burnished Red Ware). The Harappans used red-coloured pottery with black painted designs — a hallmark of the civilisation. 📌 EXPLANATION The Indus Valley pottery was made of red clay (hence red colour), decorated with black pa

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: The sophisticated wheel-made, polished pottery is considered among the earliest evidence of advanced ceramic technology globally. Animal motifs were painted on these vessels. 💎 LIVE GEM Teacher: "The pots were red because of red clay. To distinguish them, they used black colour on top. Then drew animal shapes. This is what the 6th Standard textbook describes — red pots with black artwork. They were highly sophisticated for their time. " Q13 HARD Conceptual Which of the following are CORRECT about the Harappan people and culture?

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: (1) More than one group existed in the Indus Valley Civilisation. (2) Various occupational groups existed: farmers, pastoralists, hunters, food-gatherers. (3) Exactly five groups existed. (4) Fort-style citadel city planning is still in practice today. ✅ ANSWER Statements (1) and (2) are CORRECT . Statement (3) "exactly five groups" is wrong — number was unspecified/more. Statement (4) is discussed separately (Q29). 📌 EXPLANATION One group had 30–40 members.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: The Indus Valley had multiple occupational groups: farmers, cattle-rearers, hunters, fishermen, food-gatherers. "Exactly five" is not mentioned in any standard textbook — it's a fabricated number. The civilisation had at least 4 distinct occupational groups. 🔴 CRITICAL — STUDENT NOTE Teacher corrected students: "One group = 30 to 40 people. Many students answered 10–15 — that's wrong. 30–40 people formed one social group/community in the Indus Valley.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: " Q21 EASY Factual Fill in the blank: "A well-planned city built on a raised platform" — which Harappan city fits this description? ✅ ANSWER Mohenjo-daro — the city was built on a raised platform/citadel mound. 📌 EXPLANATION In English: "A planned town built on a platform. " The word 'platform' (பிளாட்பார்ம்) in Tamil = மேடை (raised mound). Mohenjo-daro sits on an elevated mound, making it a classic citadel-style planned city. Q22 EASY Factual Which metal/material was NOT used/known in the Harappan Civilisation?

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: ✅ ANSWER Iron — Harappans did not know about iron. They belonged to the Bronze Age. 📌 EXPLANATION The Indus Valley Civilisation was a Bronze Age culture. They used bronze, copper, and gold but had NO knowledge of iron. They also had no knowledge of the horse. Iron Age came after the Vedic period. 🔴 CRITICAL Two things Harappans did NOT know: (1) Iron — Bronze Age civilisation. (2) Horse — no horse remains found (confirmed multiple times in this session).

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Q29 HARD Conceptual 💎 Gem Which of the following Indus Valley cultural practices/features continues in modern Indian society? (1) Toy cart models are still in use. (2) Wheel-made pottery of Indus type still exists in NW India. (3) Bead-making from precious stones still practised. (4) Fort-style citadel city planning still in practice. ✅ ANSWER Statement (4) is CORRECT — Fort-style citadel city planning still continues in Rajasthan (many fort-cities with walled enclosures).

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 📌 EXPLANATION Rajasthan has the maximum number of fort-cities in India (Jaipur, Jaisalmer, Jodhpur etc. ) with walled/fortified city planning — a continuation of Harappan citadel tradition. The other options are debatable or outdated. The teacher confirmed: Answer = D (Fort-style city planning). 💎 LIVE GEM Teacher: "Rajasthan has the most fort-cities in India — Jaipur (Pink City), Jaisalmer, Jodhpur. These cities have arañ (fortified walls) around them — exactly like the Harappan citadel model.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Visit Rajasthan and you see Harappan urban planning living on! Also, Rajasthan has maximum 'Jannala' (window art) forts — unique cultural continuation. " Q32 MEDIUM Match the Following 🏆 PYQ Match the Harappan sites with their notable finds/features: Mohenjo-daro – Harappa – Lothal – Banawali ✅ ANSWER Mohenjo-daro → Dancing Girl (Bronze statue) Harappa → Granary / Cemetery (Idigaadu) Lothal → Ivory scale / Dockyard Banawali → Terracotta plough / Toy 📌 EXPLANATION The famous "Dancing Girl" (Naṭanāḷ) bronze statue was found at Mohenjo-daro.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Harappa has a large granary and cemetery (Cemetery R37). Lothal is famous for its ivory/bone scale (world's earliest). Banawali had a terracotta plough — first evidence of agriculture technology. Q33 MEDIUM Conceptual Which of the following statements about the Harappan Civilisation are CORRECT? (1) Houses in Harappa & Mohenjo-daro were built with burnt bricks. (2) Kalibangan and Lothal used unbaked bricks. (3) Burnt bricks were mostly used for drains, wells, bathrooms, and pathways.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: (4) Houses had a courtyard with 4–6 rooms and a bathroom. ✅ ANSWER All four statements (1), (2), (3), and (4) are CORRECT . Answer = D (All). 📌 EXPLANATION Harappan houses were sophisticated: 1) Major cities used burnt bricks. 2) Kalibangan and Lothal used unbaked/sun-dried bricks. 3) Burnt bricks were used for drains, wells, bathrooms, pathways specifically. 4) Each house had a central courtyard, 4–6 rooms, and an individual bathroom — a remarkable urban planning feature.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 💡 TIP Teacher added from 11th Standard book: "Some describe 3 living rooms. The 11th Standard and TNPSC Gr. 1/2 books give more detail than 6th Standard — always read 11th Std for Group 2 level questions. " Q41 EASY Factual Name the only Indus Valley Civilisation site that had an artificial/man-made dockyard. ✅ ANSWER Lothal — it had the world's earliest known man-made dockyard. Q42 EASY Factual 💎 Gem Name the only Indus Valley Civilisation site that had NO citadel/fort.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: ✅ ANSWER Chanhu-daro (Surkotada) — the only Harappan site without a citadel. 💎 LIVE GEM Teacher's exact quote: "Chanhu-daro = no fort/citadel. If they ask 'which Harappan site had no citadel? ' — answer is Chanhu-daro. Memorise it as a single fact. " Q43 MEDIUM Conceptual Which of the following is CORRECT about the Harappan Civilisation? (1) There was a strong central government. (2) Street lights existed at every street. (3) Dustbins were placed at street corners.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: (4) Rich and poor had separate residential areas. ✅ ANSWER Answer = D (All four are correct) — per the teacher's answer key. 📌 EXPLANATION Strong central government — yes, inferred from uniformity of weights/measures and city planning across 1500+ km. Street lighting — evidence of lamp-posts at corners. Dustbins — waste disposal points at street junctions. Separate residential areas for rich/poor — upper and lower city divisions. 🔴 NOTE — CORRECTION Teacher expressed uncertainty about statement (4) regarding rich/poor separate quarters — sayi

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: " Final answer given was D. If your textbook shows otherwise, verify with your specific book edition. Q48 HARD Conceptual Which of the following about the architecture/art of the Indus Valley Civilisation is CORRECT? (1) Mohenjo-daro shows 8 structural levels. (2) Minor changes are visible in Mohenjo-daro's architecture. (3) Indus seals showed no change throughout their time. (4) Stone buildings are found at Harappa and Mohenjo-daro. ✅ ANSWER All statements (1), (2), (3), and (4) are correct .

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Answer = All of the above. 📌 EXPLANATION (1) ✅ Mohenjo-daro has 8 occupational levels (8 layers of habitation). (2) ✅ Architecture shows minor modifications over time. (3) ✅ Indus script/seals remained remarkably uniform across regions and time — showing standardised culture. (4) ✅ Stone structures are found at both major sites. Q49 EASY Factual Which Harappan site is considered the northernmost settlement? ✅ ANSWER Shortugai (Afghanistan) — the northernmost Harappan outpost, important for trade with Afghanistan/Central Asia.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Q9, Q10, Q16, Q17 Q09 MEDIUM Factual 🏆 PYQ Name the four animals depicted on the Pashupati seal found at Mohenjo-daro. ✅ ANSWER The four animals on the Pashupati seal: Elephant, Tiger (Pulli), Rhinoceros (Kaṇṭāmiruham), Buffalo (Erumei) . 📌 EXPLANATION The Pashupati (also called Vishupati) seal shows a figure seated in a yoga posture surrounded by four animals: elephant (east), tiger (west), rhinoceros (one side), buffalo (other side). This proto-Shiva figure is considered the earliest representation of Lord Shiva.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 🏆 PYQ ALERT This question appears in TNPSC Group 2/4 almost every cycle. The four animals must be memorised: Elephant + Tiger + Rhinoceros + Buffalo. All confirmed by the teacher. 💡 TIP Confirmed: Elephant ✅ (river civilisation), Tiger ✅ (forest), Rhinoceros ✅ (extra/unique), Buffalo ✅ (confirmed). The seal confirms: (a) Elephant confirmed, (b) Tiger confirmed, (c) Rhinoceros confirmed — extra animal distinguishing this from the general list, (d) Buffalo confirmed.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Q10 EASY Factual 💎 Gem Which animal was NOT known to the Harappan people? ✅ ANSWER Horse — Harappans had no knowledge of horses. 📌 EXPLANATION No horse bones or horse depictions found in any Harappan site. Also: Harappans had no knowledge of IRON (Bronze Age civilisation). Both "horse" and "iron" are the standard "unknown to Harappans" facts. 💎 LIVE GEM — HISTORICAL CONSEQUENCE Teacher: "This is why Alexander the Great succeeded in India — he came on horseback, which Indians (Harappan descendants) were unfamiliar with.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Alexander's intelligence said Indians had no horses but had elephants. Alexander thought: 'I have horses, they have elephants — I can win. ' But when he saw 1000s of elephants, he was shocked! His army refused to go further. So lack of horse knowledge from Harappan times shaped Indian military history 1000+ years later! " Q16 HARD Trick/Trap Which animals are depicted on the Harappan seals? (Options: A) Water buffaloes only, B) Elephants and tigers only, C) Camels and spotted deer, D) Water buffalo, elephants, tigers, and camels) ✅ ANSWER Answe

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 📌 EXPLANATION Harappan seals depicted: Water buffalo ✅ (river civilisation), Elephants ✅ (forest), Tigers ✅, Camel ✅ (possibly referenced from trade with Rajasthan/Mesopotamia camel routes). The rhinoceros is also depicted. Camels appear because Rajasthan-area sites were within the civilisation zone. ⚠️ TRAP NOTE Most students selected "elephants and tigers only" (Option B). The answer includes camel too. Teacher explained: "Camel appears because of trade connections with Rajasthan and possibly Mesopotamia — they would have depicted trade part

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: " Q17 EASY Factual Which tree was worshipped as the "highest god/supreme deity" in the Indus Valley Civilisation? ✅ ANSWER Peepal tree (Arasa maram / Sacred Fig) — considered the supreme/highest deity among sacred trees. 📌 EXPLANATION Both Peepal (Ficus religiosa) and Acacia were worshipped as sacred trees. Peepal was considered the supreme deity. This tradition continues in Hinduism, Buddhism (Buddha attained enlightenment under a Peepal tree), and Jainism.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Q11, Q12, Q20, Q23–Q24, Q30, Q40, Q45, Q47 Q11 MEDIUM Factual Which ancient inscription/text records trading connections between Mesopotamia and Harappa? ✅ ANSWER Cuneiform tablets/letters (யூனிஃபார்ம் எழுத்துக்கள்) — Mesopotamian cuneiform records mention trade with "Meluhha" (identified as the Indus Valley). 📌 EXPLANATION Mesopotamia (modern Iraq) is one of the world's oldest civilisations — 6500+ years old. Cuneiform writing records mention goods imported from Meluhha (Indus Valley): gold, carnelian beads, ivory, lapis lazuli.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: These cuneiform tablets are proof of Indus-Mesopotamia trade connections. Q12 EASY Factual Harappans used which stone to make blades and other tools? (Options: Rohri chert, Steatite, Vidorium, Carnelian) ✅ ANSWER Rohri chert — used for making blades and other cutting tools. 📌 EXPLANATION Carnelian is a reddish semi-precious stone — used for making jewellery/beads, NOT tools. Steatite (soapstone) = used for seals. Rohri chert = used for blades/tools.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: The question tests whether students know the difference between decorative use vs. Q20 HARD Assertion-Reason Assertion (A): Indus Valley people did not use metal coins. Reason (R): They did not have the technology to make metal coins. ✅ ANSWER Both A and R are TRUE, and R is the correct explanation of A . Answer = B (Both true, R explains A). 📌 EXPLANATION A is true: Harappans did NOT use metal coins — they used a barter system. R is also true: They lacked coin-minting technology (though they were skilled metalworkers, coinage was not develope

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: R correctly explains why A is true. This is a classic Assertion-Reason question where students must check: (1) Is A true? (3) Does R explain A? ⚠️ TRAP NOTE Teacher warned: "Many students mark A as FALSE because they assume 'negative statement = wrong. ' But 'they did NOT use metal coins' is a TRUE statement! They genuinely did NOT use coins. Don't let the negative phrasing confuse you. " Q23 HARD Match the Following Match Indus Valley trade goods/materials with their source locations: Conch shell – Steatite – Carnelian – Lapis Lazuli ✅ ANSWER

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Teacher confirmed: Answer = C (Carnelian = Lothal is the wrong match they were testing — Carnelian was found/processed at Lothal). ⚠️ TRAP NOTE Carnelian is frequently confused with a tool-making material. It is a jewellery/bead stone. Steatite = seals. Rohri chert = tools/blades. Carnelian = beads/jewellery. Q24 MEDIUM Factual 💎 Gem What does the word "Loth" mean in the Gujarati word "Lothal"? ✅ ANSWER "Loth" in Gujarati = Dead / Death . So "Lothal" = "Mound of the Dead" (same meaning as Mohenjo-daro in Sindhi).

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 💎 LIVE GEM Teacher: "Lothal = Loth (dead) + Al (place) in Gujarati = 'Place of the Dead. ' Mohenjo-daro = 'Mound of the Dead' in Sindhi. TWO different sites, TWO different languages — SAME meaning: Mound/Place of the Dead. Both were named because of burial mounds found there. Also: 'Lok' (लोक) in Hindi/Sanskrit = 'People' — so Lok Sabha = 'People's Assembly'. Different from 'Loth'! " Q30 EASY Factual Which Harappan outpost served as the most important guard post for trade with Afghanistan?

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: ✅ ANSWER Shortugai — the most important Harappan outpost for Afghan trade. Q40 EASY Factual 🏆 PYQ Which crop was NOT cultivated by the Harappan people? ✅ ANSWER Sugarcane — Harappans did NOT cultivate sugarcane. 📌 EXPLANATION Harappan cultivated crops: wheat, barley, sesame, mustard, peas, dates, cotton (first in the world — "Sindon" in Greek/Sindhi). Sugarcane cultivation came later in Indian history (associated with later Vedic/early classical period).

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Cotton was so associated with the Indus that Greeks called it "Sindon" (from Sindhu/Sindh). 🏆 PYQ ALERT "Which crop did Harappans NOT cultivate? " — frequently asked. Sugarcane is the standard answer. Also note: cotton cultivation was FIRST done by Harappans — this is a positive PYQ fact. Q45 EASY Factual What was the primary occupation/livelihood of the Indus Valley Civilisation people? ✅ ANSWER Agriculture (farming) — was the primary occupation.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Secondary: cattle-rearing, then hunting, then fishing. Q47 EASY Factual Which of the following are TRUE about Indus Valley merchants in Mesopotamia? (1) Indus merchants lived/stayed in Mesopotamia. (2) They brought cotton with them. ✅ ANSWER Both statements (1) and (2) are TRUE . Answer = Both are correct. 📌 EXPLANATION Archaeological and textual evidence shows Indus traders stayed in Mesopotamia (trading colonies). Cotton — the Indus Valley's signature crop — was their primary export good.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: The Greeks called cotton "Sindon" (from Sindhu) because they first encountered it through Indus Valley trade.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Q18–Q19, Q36–Q37 Q18 MEDIUM Factual 💎 Gem The evidence of burials in Harappan sites is named after what designation? (Fill in blank: "Burials found at Harappa are named ___") ✅ ANSWER Cemetery R37 — the burial ground at Harappa is designated "Cemetery R37" (கல்லறை ஆர் 37). 💎 LIVE GEM Teacher: "Cemetery R37 — this is a specific code name for the Harappan burial site. Write it down: R37 = the famous burial ground label for Harappan archaeology. It's like how we give codes to archaeological strata.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: If a question asks 'what is the proof of burials at Harappa? ' — Cemetery R37 is the standard answer. " Q19 MEDIUM Trick/Trap Which of the following about the Harappan Civilisation contains a FALSE statement? (1) Seals have not been fully deciphered even today. (2) Indus Valley Civilisation is a remarkable urban culture. (3) Indus people used large-scale weights/measures. (4) Indus Valley people knew the use of iron. ✅ ANSWER Statement (4) is FALSE — Harappans did NOT know about iron.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 📌 EXPLANATION (1) ✅ True — Indus script remains undeciphered to this day (Tamil Nadu CM offered $1 million prize for decipherment). (3) ✅ True — standardised weights and measures used across 1500+ km. (4) ❌ FALSE — Bronze Age civilisation; iron unknown. 💎 LIVE GEM — UNDECIPHERED SCRIPT Teacher: "The Indus script is still undeciphered — the Tamil Nadu government (CM Stalin) once announced a prize of 1 million US dollars for whoever can decode it!

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: That tells you how important and unsolved this mystery is. The pictographic script (சித்திர எழுத்துக்கள்) is boustrophedon style — written right to left. About 400 signs identified. " ⚠️ TRAP NOTE Teacher warned: "Many students see statement (1) — 'seals have NOT been deciphered' — and think it's false because of the negative phrasing. It IS true that seals haven't been deciphered. Don't fall for the negative trap! " Q36 MEDIUM Conceptual Which of the following are correct about the religious beliefs of the Indus Valley Civilisation people?

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: (1) Mother-goddess worship was part of the culture and society. (2) In idol worship, the mother goddess concept was highlighted. ✅ ANSWER Both statements (1) and (2) are correct . 📌 EXPLANATION The Harappan society was matriarchal (mother-worshipping). Thousands of terracotta female figurines found = mother goddess worship. The "Dancing Girl" bronze statue at Mohenjo-daro is another example of female prominence. Nature worship also prevalent — animals, trees, and natural forces were revered.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Q37 HARD Trick/Trap Which of the following statements about the Harappan Civilisation is FALSE? (1) Harappan walls were weak. (2) Tectonic plate movement caused city destruction. (3) Small-headed Armenian skull bones were found. (4) Harappan civilisation declined after 1750 BCE. ✅ ANSWER Answer = C — Statements (2), (3), (4) are TRUE; Statement (1) "walls were weak" is FALSE. 📌 EXPLANATION (1) ❌ FALSE — Harappan walls were strong and thick (brick walls).

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: (2) ✅ Tectonic activity is one proposed cause of decline. (3) ✅ Armenian-type skulls (brachycephalic = small-headed) found at burial sites. (4) ✅ After 1750 BCE, the Late Harappan phase begins — decline is established. Answer = C (statements 2, 3, 4 are true; 1 is false).

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Q44 Q44 MEDIUM Conceptual What is considered the primary cause of the decline of the Indus Valley Civilisation? (Options: Climate change, Earthquake/tectonic, Aryan invasion, Floods) ✅ ANSWER Floods — considered the primary/most likely cause, ahead of the other theories. 📌 EXPLANATION Multiple theories exist: (1) Aryan invasion (Mortimer Wheeler — now largely discredited), (2) Climate change (Wheeler/Lambrick), (3) Floods (most accepted), (4) Tectonic activity (earthquakes).

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Teacher identified floods as the most commonly cited primary cause in TNPSC textbooks. All four are valid contributing factors but floods takes precedence in standard answers. 💡 TIP For TNPSC: If asked "one reason" → Floods. If asked "multiple reasons" → all four apply. The Aryan invasion theory is outdated — avoid it as a primary cause in modern exams.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Q14, Q38, Q46, Q50 + Bonus Q14 HARD Match the Following 🏆 PYQ Match the Harappan sites with their notable archaeological finds: Mohenjo-daro – Harappa – Lothal – Banawali ✅ ANSWER Mohenjo-daro → Bronze Dancing Girl statue Harappa → Cemetery / Granary Lothal → Ivory scale (bone ruler) Banawali → Terracotta (fired clay) toy/plough 📌 EXPLANATION Terracotta Army reference (bonus fact): Teacher mentioned China's Han Dynasty used terracotta army figures for burial rites — 4000 clay soldiers placed near the emperor's tomb.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: This shows terracotta (fired clay) technology was widespread in ancient civilisations. Country with most pyramids: South Sudan (NOT Egypt! ) → Egypt is 2nd, China is 3rd. 💎 LIVE GEM — PYRAMIDS FACT Teacher: "World's most pyramids? South Sudan has the most pyramids! Egypt is 2nd, China 3rd. South Sudan was also home to early Mesopotamian-linked civilisation and is now a separate country. This is a great current affairs + history combo question for TNPSC!

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: " Q38 MEDIUM Match the Following 🏆 PYQ Match Harappan sites (from Q35 format) with their excavators — final set: Chanhu-daro – Lothal – Banawali – Dholavira – Rakhigarhi ✅ ANSWER Chanhu-daro → N. Majumdar Lothal → S. Rao Banawali → R. Bisht Dholavira → J. Joshi Rakhigarhi → Amendra Nath / A. Ghosh Q46 EASY Factual Dholavira is described as the oldest ___ city (fill in the blank from context of the session). ✅ ANSWER Dholavira is described as the oldest Harappan city / one of the oldest planned cities with a three-part division (citadel, middle

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 📌 EXPLANATION Dholavira is unique in having a THREE-PART city plan (not just two). It also had the world's first known signboard (10 large Indus script letters found at its north gate). In 2021, it was declared a UNESCO World Heritage Site. 💡 TIP — 2021 UNESCO Dholavira was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2021. This is a high-frequency current affairs + history question for TNPSC 2025–26. Q50 EASY Factual Which Indus Valley Civilisation site is considered the PORT city of the civilisation?

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: ✅ ANSWER Lothal — the port city of the Indus Valley Civilisation. It had the world's earliest man-made dockyard and conducted overseas trade with Mesopotamia.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 10 Key Takeaways From This Session Harappa (Pakistan Punjab) & Mohenjo-daro (Pakistan Sindh) are NOT in India — both in Pakistan. Indian sites: Lothal/Dholavira (Gujarat), Kalibangan (Rajasthan), Rupar & Rakhigarhi (Haryana). Mohenjo-daro = "Mound of the Dead" (Sindhi) = Indus River. Lothal = "Place of the Dead" (Gujarati) = Bhogavo River. Harappa = Ravi River. Kalibangan = Ghaggar. Excavators: Charles Masson (first visitor 1826) → Alexander Cunningham (first recorded antiquities) → R.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Banerji (major excavation) → Sir John Marshall (supervision). Mohenjo-daro: Mortimer Wheeler. Harappa: Daya Ram Sahni. 6 major cities + 200 villages = the Harappan Civilisation structure. Not all sites were urban — statement "all sites were urban" is FALSE. Pashupati Seal (Mohenjo-daro): 4 animals = Elephant + Tiger + Rhinoceros + Buffalo. This is proto-Shiva. Harappans did NOT know: (1) Horse, (2) Iron. Bronze Age civilisation. Also did NOT use metal coins (barter system).

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Lothal: only site with man-made dockyard, Gulf of Cambay, ivory scale, carnelian bead-making. Chanhu-daro: only site WITHOUT a citadel. Dholavira (Khadir Island, Rann of Kutch, Gujarat): unique 3-part city, world's first signboard, UNESCO World Heritage 2021. Indus script: undeciphered pictographic (सिधु script). Tamil Nadu CM offered $1 million prize for decipherment. ~400 signs identified, boustrophedon direction. Decline cause (primary): Floods.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Other factors: climate change, tectonic activity. Aryan invasion theory is outdated/discredited for modern TNPSC.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: High Probability Top 5 Questions Likely to Appear in TNPSC 2026 PRED 1 Which Harappan site was added to UNESCO World Heritage List in 2021? → Dholavira (Gujarat, India) PRED 2 Match the Harappan sites with their associated rivers — especially Lothal–Bhogavo, Kalibangan–Ghaggar, Harappa–Ravi, Mohenjo-daro–Indus. → Full match answer as tabulated in Q5 PRED 3 Which country in the world has the highest number of pyramids? → South Sudan (NOT Egypt) PRED 4 Which crop was FIRST cultivated by the Harappan people and later exported to Greece?

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: / Which crop was NOT cultivated? → First cotton cultivation (Cotton = Sindon). NOT cultivated = Sugarcane. PRED 5 Match Harappan sites with their excavators — Chanhu-daro, Lothal, Dholavira, Rakhigarhi. → Chanhu-daro=Majumdar, Lothal=S. Rao, Dholavira=J. Joshi, Rakhigarhi=A.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Rapid Revision Q# Question (Short) Answer Q01 Harappan sites NOT in India Harappa & Mohenjo-daro (both Pakistan) Q02 Dholavira location Khadir Island, Rann of Kutch, Gujarat Q03 False statement about Harappan sites "All sites were urban" — FALSE (200 villages + 6 cities) Q04 Mohenjo-daro excavator Mortimer Wheeler Q05 Lothal's river Bhogavo (tributary of Sabarmati) Q05 Kalibangan's river Ghaggar (Hakra) Q05 Harappa's river Ravi Q05 Mohenjo-daro's river Indus (Sindhu) Q05 Rupar's river Sutlej Q06 Were Harappans cremated?

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: YES — cremation was practiced Q08 Harappan pottery type Polished red ware with black paint Q09 4 animals on Pashupati seal Elephant, Tiger, Rhinoceros, Buffalo Q10 Animal unknown to Harappans Horse Q11 Proof of Harappa-Mesopotamia trade Cuneiform tablets Q12 Stone for Harappan blades Rohri chert Q14 Dancing Girl statue site Mohenjo-daro Q15 False about Lothal Wrong dock dimensions in statement Q16 Animals on Harappan seals Water buffalo, elephant, tiger, camel (All) Q17 Highest sacred tree (Harappan) Peepal tree (sacred fig) Q18 Harappan burial

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: All Key Facts Category Key Fact Value / Answer Sites in Pakistan Not in India Harappa (Punjab), Mohenjo-daro (Sindh) Dholavira Location Khadir Island, Rann of Kutch, Gujarat | UNESCO 2021 Lothal Location + Feature Gulf of Cambay, Gujarat | World's first dockyard | River: Bhogavo Kalibangan Location + River Rajasthan | River: Ghaggar (Hakra) Rivers: MUST KNOW 3 key pairs Mohenjo-daro=Indus | Harappa=Ravi | Rupar=Sutlej Scale: Cities:Villages Count 6 cities + 200 villages Group size Social unit 30–40 people per group Pashupati Seal 4 animals Elep

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Banerji Lothal excavator Name S. Rao Dholavira excavator Name J. Joshi ASI founding Year + Founder 1861 | Alexander Cunningham (Father of Indian Archaeology) Lothal meaning Gujarati Loth=Dead + Al=Place = "Mound/Place of the Dead" Mohenjo-daro meaning Sindhi "Mound of the Dead" Cemetery R37 Site Harappan burial ground at Harappa city Chanhu-daro Unique feature ONLY Harappan site WITHOUT a citadel Indus script Status Undeciphered | Pictographic | ~400 signs | TN CM prize: $1M Decline cause Primary Floods (then: climate change, tectonic, Aryan in
