chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: 01 What is History? Q1 The word "History" is derived from which language and what does it mean? வரலாறு என்ற சொல் எந்த மொழி சொல்லிலிருந்து பெறப்பட்டது? அதன் அர்த்தம் என்ன? EASY Factual Gr. 4/VAO ✅ Answer The word "History" is derived from the Greek word "Historia" , which means "learning through inquiry / learning by investigation. " 📌 Explanation Historia (Greek) → learning by asking, investigating, and finding out through questioning . This reflects that knowledge of history is built by questioning sources and witnesses.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: 💡 TIP — Memory Think of History = Investigation. You investigate the past to learn it. Q2 Statement I: History is a chronological record of past events. Statement II: Time in history is measured in years (AD/BC). Are both correct? கூற்று 1: வரலாறு என்பது கடந்த கால நிகழ்வுகளின் காலவரிசை பதிவு. கூற்று 2: வரலாற்றின் காலம் ஆண்டுகளில் கணக்கிடப்படுகிறது. EASY Factual ✅ Answer Both statements are correct. 📌 Explanation History records the past in chronological order.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: Time is counted in years — traditionally as BC (Before Christ) and AD (Anno Domini). Modern secular usage replaces these with BCE (Before Common Era) and CE (Common Era). 💎 LIVE GEM — Modern Calendar Language BC/AD were based on Jesus Christ's birth year. Modern historians now use BCE/CE (Common Era) to remove religious bias while referring to the same years.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: 02 Stone Age — Map-Based Questions Q3 Which of the following places yielded Neolithic (New Stone Age) tools? (Options include: Mehrgarh, Maakara, Attirampakkam, Athichanallur, Daojali Hading) புதிய கற்கால கருவிகள் கண்டுபிடிக்கப்பட்ட இடங்கள் எவை? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 1/2 ✅ Answer Mehrgarh, Maakara, and Daojali Hading (Options 1, 2, 5) 📌 Explanation Mehrgarh (Baluchistan, Pakistan) — pioneer settlement, precursor to Indus Valley Civilisation. Maakara — adjacent region also yielding Neolithic tools.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: Daojali Hading (Northeast India) — also confirmed Neolithic site. Attirampakkam = Palaeolithic. Athichanallur = Bronze/Iron Age. 🏆 PYQ ALERT Map-based questions on Stone Age sites appear in TNPSC Group 2 (2018, 2022). Mehrgarh is the most-tested answer here. 💡 MAP TIP (Textbook p. 108) Open the map and study the symbols: square = Palaeolithic, triangle = Bronze Age. Mehrgarh is in Pakistan (Baluchistan). Hathnagar/Hading is in West Bengal. Q4 Which sites yielded Iron Age tools?

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: இரும்பு கால கற்கால கருவிகள் கண்டுபிடிக்கப்பட்ட இடம் எது? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 1/2/4 ✅ Answer Hallur and Athichanallur (Thoothukudi district, Tamil Nadu) 💎 LIVE GEM — Memory Trick Iron Age → Hallur . "Iron" is heavy (அல்லு = heavy in Tamil slang). So: Iron is heavy → Hallur . Both Hallur and Athichanallur = Iron Age. Athichanallur is in Thoothukudi, TN. Q5 Which sites yielded Bronze Age tools? வெண்கல காலத்தில் கண்டுபிடிக்கப்பட்ட கருவிகள் கண்டுபிடிக்கப்பட்ட இடங்கள் எவை?

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: MEDIUM Factual Gr. 1/2 ✅ Answer Lothal (Gujarat) and Athichanallur (Tamil Nadu). Answer: Options 1 and 4. 📌 Explanation Lothal is an IVC site with a natural harbour on a tributary of the Sabarmati river, Gujarat. Athichanallur yields both Bronze and Iron Age artifacts — remember: South India (especially Athichanallur and Keeladi) has Bronze + Iron Age. 💎 LIVE GEM — Quick Table Palaeolithic (Old Stone Age) → Chennai (Attirampakkam, Pallavaram), Bheembetka (MP), Hunsagi Valley (near Goa) Neolithic (New Stone Age) → Mehrgarh, Maakara, Daojali Ha

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: 🪤 TNPSC TRAP Athichanallur is often placed only in Iron Age. But it appears in both Bronze and Iron Age in the textbook. Watch options carefully. Q6 Which sites yielded Palaeolithic (Old Stone Age) tools? பழைய கற்கால கருவிகள் கண்டுபிடிக்கப்பட்ட இடங்கள் எவை? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2/4 ✅ Answer Bheembetka (Madhya Pradesh), Attirampakkam (Chennai), Hunsagi Valley (near Goa). Answer: Options 2, 3, 5. 📌 Explanation Bheembetka (MP) has famous cave paintings of a woman holding a child with a bow aimed at a fleeing animal — sculpted ~2000 years ago.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: Attirampakkam is near Chennai. Hunsagi Valley is near Goa. 💎 LIVE GEM — Tamil Nadu Stone Age Logic Chennai / Pallavaram / Attirampakkam = Palaeolithic (Old Stone Age) Athichanallur / Keeladi (Thoothukudi/Madurai) = Bronze + Iron Age So: if Chennai → Old Stone; if deep South TN → Bronze/Iron. 💡 TIP — Bheembetka (Group 4 Frequent) Bheembetka is in Madhya Pradesh . It features cave paintings from the Palaeolithic era. Often tested in Group 4 as "which state has Palaeolithic cave art?

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: 03 Proto-History, Dating & Historical Sources Q7 What is the period between the use of stone tools and the discovery of writing called? கற்கருவிகளை பயன்படுத்தியதற்கும் எழுதும் முறைகளை கண்டுபிடித்ததற்கும் இடைப்பட்ட காலம் என்ன? 1 ✅ Answer Proto-History (வரலாற்று தொடக்க காலம் — Proto-historic Period) 📌 Explanation Three periods: • Prehistoric = Before stone tools and writing (no record at all) • Proto-historic = Stone tools used but writing not yet discovered (between the two) • Historic = Writing discovered and used (records exist) 🔴 CRITICAL —

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: The correct answer is Proto-historic / Proto-History . "Pre-historic" = before tools. "Proto-historic" = between tools and writing. 🏆 PYQ ALERT — Group 1 Level This distinction (Prehistoric / Proto-historic / Historic) is a confirmed Group 1 concept. Expect it as a Statement-based question. Q8 Statement I: The period between History and Pre-history is called Proto-Historic period. Statement II: In the Proto-historic period, written records exist but we cannot understand their meaning.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: கூற்று 1: வரலாற்றுக்கும் வரலாற்றுக்கு முந்தைய காலத்திற்கும் இடைப்பட்ட காலம் வரலாற்று தொடக்க காலமாகும். கூற்று 2: வரலாற்று தொடக்க காலத்தில் எழுதப்பட்ட பதிவுகள் உள்ளன — தற்போது அவற்றின் பொருளை நாம் புரிந்து கொள்ள முடியவில்லை. 1 ✅ Answer Statement I is correct. Statement II is correct. → Answer: Both are correct. 📌 Explanation Wait — textbook says: records exist in the Proto-historic period, BUT we cannot understand their meaning (they are pictographic/undeciphered scripts like IVC script).

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: So Statement II is also correct. Answer: A (both correct) if such option exists, or "Statement I correct, Statement II correct. " 🔴 CRITICAL — Teacher Correction During Live Class Many students answered "A" (both correct) initially but were told to re-check. The teacher confirmed: Statement I ✅ correct. Statement II ✅ correct. The Indus Valley script is a real example — records exist but are undeciphered. Group 1 students who marked "A wrong" were making an error.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: 💎 LIVE GEM — IVC Script Connection The Indus Valley script = perfect example of Proto-historic writing: it EXISTS on seals, but no one can read it yet. This is why the IVC period is classified as Proto-historic, not Historic. Q9 Statement I: The science related to the history of coins is Numismatics. Statement II: The branch dealing with inscriptions on stone/copper plates is Epigraphy. நாணவியல் — நாணயங்கள் பற்றிய அறிவியல். கல்வெட்டியல் — கல்வெட்டுகளில் பதிவு செய்யப்பட்ட செய்திகள் ஆராய்வதற்கான துறை.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: EASY Factual ✅ Answer Both statements are correct. Basic definitions — Numismatics (நாணவியல்) = coins. Epigraphy (கல்வெட்டியல்) = inscriptions. Both are standard historical sources. Q10 Statement I: "Dhamma" is a Sanskrit word. Statement II: In Prakrit it is called "Dharma. " தம்மா என்பது சமஸ்கிருத மொழிச்சொல். இது பிராகிருதத்தில் தர்மா எனப்படுகிறது. 1/2 ✅ Answer Both statements are WRONG. 📌 Explanation " Dhamma " is a Prakrit word (not Sanskrit).

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: The Sanskrit equivalent is " Dharma . " In English, it is called "Dharma. " So Statement I is wrong (Dhamma ≠ Sanskrit) and Statement II is wrong (Prakrit does not call it "Dharma" — Prakrit calls it "Dhamma"; Sanskrit calls it "Dharma"). 🔴 CRITICAL — Almost everyone got this wrong Teacher said: "If you marked 'A' (both correct) you are wrong! " The trap is assuming Dhamma = Sanskrit. Dhamma = Prakrit. Dharma = Sanskrit. Dharma = English too. 127 — check glossary terms.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: 💎 LIVE GEM — Quick Fix Asoka's edicts used Prakrit language (not Sanskrit) to reach common people. The word "Dhamma" in his edicts = Prakrit. Later scholars transliterated it to "Dharma" (Sanskrit/English). 🏆 PYQ ALERT Dhamma vs Dharma language distinction has appeared in TNPSC Group 1 Prelims (2019).

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: 04 Prehistoric Human Life — Tools & Animals Q11 How did ancient (Palaeolithic) man gather his food? பழங்கால மனிதன் தனது உணவை சேகரிக்க மேற்கொண்ட நடவடிக்கை எது? EASY Factual ✅ Answer By Hunting (வேட்டையாடுதல்) Q12 Statement (Assertion): Palaeolithic man took dogs with him while hunting. Reason: While living in caves, dogs sensed approaching animals using their smell-sense and warned him. கூற்று: பழைய கற்கால மனிதர்கள் வேட்டையாட செல்லும்போது நாய்களை உடன் சென்றனர்.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: காரணம்: குகைகளில் தங்கியிருந்த போது விலங்குகள் வருவதை நாய்கள் தமது மோப்ப சக்தியினால் அறிந்து உணர்த்தின. MEDIUM Conceptual Gr. 2/4 ✅ Answer Both Assertion and Reason are correct — and the Reason explains the Assertion. 💎 LIVE GEM — Evolution of the Dog 2 lakh (200,000) years ago: no dog breed existed . The ancestor was the wolf (ஓநாய்) . Early humans gradually domesticated wolves → over thousands of years wolves evolved (through human selection) into dogs.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: The dog is one of the fastest-evolving domesticated animals in evolutionary history. This is a Science + History crossover fact. 🔴 CRITICAL FACT — First Domesticated Animal The dog was the first domesticated animal in human history. Humans have kept dogs for ~10,000+ years — from cave-dwelling days to the present day. Q13 Find the wrong pair: (a) Palaeolithic – stone tools & cave paintings; (b) Copper plates – historical source; (c) Cats – first domesticated animal.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: தவறான இணையை கண்டுபிடி: பழைய கற்காலம் – கற்கருவிகள் / பாறை ஓவியங்கள் / குகை சுவர்கள் | செப்பு தகடுகள் – வரலாற்று ஆதாரம் | பூனைகள் – முதலில் பழக்கப்படுத்தப்பட்ட விலங்கு MEDIUM Trap ✅ Answer Wrong pair: Cats – first domesticated animal. The correct answer is Dogs (not Cats). First domesticated animal = Dog. Cats came much later in agricultural societies (to protect grain stores). Option (c) is wrong. Q14 Find the wrong statement about cave paintings of Palaeolithic man.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: பழைய கற்கால மனிதனின் குகை சுவர் ஓவியங்கள் பற்றிய தவறான கூற்று எது? MEDIUM Conceptual ✅ Answer "They painted in many / multiple colours" — this is WRONG. 📌 Explanation Correct facts: Paintings were on rocks/cave walls; hunting scenes were drawn; painted to show family members how to hunt. Wrong statement: They used many colours. Palaeolithic man could only use whatever natural pigment was available (from plants, minerals) — they did NOT have a full colour palette.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: 💎 LIVE GEM — Practical Logic Teacher asked students to think practically: "If you want green paint 2000 years ago, you'd crush a leaf. That's all you get. " So they used what nature gave them — NOT multiple designer colours. This eliminates the "many colours" option easily. Q15 Match the following: Rock paintings / Written records / Asoka (famous ruler) / Religious literature MEDIUM Application ✅ Answer Rock paintings → Way of life information (4) | Written records → Copper plates (1) | Asoka → Great king (2) | Religious literature → Thevaram

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: Thevaram is a Saiva (Shaiva) text — Shaivism follows Lord Shiva. Teacher noted: Saiva = relating to Shiva (not to be confused with vegetarian "saivam"). 💎 LIVE GEM — Saivam vs Vainavam சைவம் (Saivam) = worshippers of Shiva · வைணவம் (Vainavam) = worshippers of Vishnu. Common student error: confusing "சைவம்" with vegetarianism ("saiva food"). In historical context, Saivam = a Hindu sect, NOT food preference. Thevaram and Periya Puranam are Saiva texts.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: Q16 Statement I: Palaeolithic stone tools were found at Attirampakkam, Chennai. Statement II: Ancient artefacts excavated by archaeologists are preserved by the Archaeological department. கூற்று 1: பழைய கற்கால கருவிகள் சென்னை அத்திரம்பாக்கத்தில் கண்டுபிடிக்கப்பட்டுள்ளன. கூற்று 2: பழங்கால மனிதர்கள் பயன்படுத்திய பொருட்கள் தொல்லியல் துறையினரால் பாதுகாக்கப்படுகின்றன. EASY Factual ✅ Answer Both correct. Reason also correctly explains the assertion. Pallavaram and Attirampakkam (Chennai) are confirmed Palaeolithic sites in Tamil Nadu.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) preserves such finds. Q17 The objects excavated from ancient sites to understand past lifestyles are called what? பண்டைய காலத்தில் வாழ்ந்த மனிதர்கள் பயன்படுத்திய பொருட்கள் அகழாய்வு மூலம் தோண்டி எடுக்கப்படுகின்றன — இவை என்னென்ன? EASY Factual ✅ Answer புதைப்படிமங்கள் / Fossils and Artefacts (answer option: Buried remains/artefacts — புதைப்படிவங்கள் ) The correct textbook answer is "Fossils/Buried artefacts" (புதைப்படிவங்கள்).

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: Not "museums" or "bones" alone — the full category of excavated objects is called buried artefacts or fossils. Q18 Where did Palaeolithic man mainly live? பழைய கற்கால மனிதன் பெரும்பாலும் வாழ்ந்த இடங்கள் எது? EASY Factual ✅ Answer Caves (குகைகளில்) Palaeolithic man lived in caves. He had no permanent settlements. He used the dog (domesticated from wolf) to guard the cave entrance and warn of approaching animals.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: 05 Asoka & Maurya Empire Q19 Under whose reign did Buddhism spread to various parts of Asia? யாருடைய ஆட்சியில் புத்த மதம் ஆசியாவின் பல்வேறு பகுதிகளுக்கு பரவியது? EASY Factual All groups ✅ Answer Emperor Asoka (the Great) Asoka sent his son Mahinda to Sri Lanka and his daughter Sanghamitra to Japan and other Asian countries to spread Buddhism. This was a key policy after his conversion following the Kalinga War. Q20 After which war did Asoka embrace Buddhism and dedicate his life to spreading peace and righteousness?

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: _____ போருக்கு பின் அசோகர் புத்த மதத்தை தழுவி அமைதியையும் அறத்தையும் பரப்புவதற்கான தன் வாழ்வை அர்ப்பணித்தார். EASY Factual All groups ✅ Answer Kalinga War (கலிங்க போர்) 💎 LIVE GEM — Kalinga's Other Names Kalinga is present-day Odisha . Ancient name for the plains of Odisha: Utkala (உத்கல்). This appears in the National Song "Jana Gana Mana": "Dravida Utkala Banga" → Dravida = South India | Utkala = Odisha | Banga = Bengal/Bangladesh Utkala = Odisha is a confirmed Geography question in TNPSC Group 1.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: 💎 LIVE GEM — Kamarupa Kamarupa = ancient name for present-day Assam . This is a PYQ from Geography section. 🏆 PYQ ALERT Utkala = Odisha appeared in TNPSC Group 1 Geography and History paper. Kamarupa = Assam also tested. Q21 The 24-spoked wheel in the Indian national flag — from whose emblem / pillar was it taken? திரைவட குடியில் இடம் பெற்றுள்ள 24 ஆரக்கால் சக்கரம் அசோகர் நெறிவே தூண முத்திரையிலிருந்து பெறப்பட்டது. MEDIUM Factual Gr. 1/2 ✅ Answer From Asoka's Dharma Chakra Pillar at Sarnath (சார்நாத் தூண).

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: Sarnath is in Uttar Pradesh . 💎 LIVE GEM — Sarnath Sarnath (Uttar Pradesh) has the Asoka Pillar and a Shanti Stupa . The Asoka Chakra (24 spokes) on our national flag comes from the Sarnath Lion Capital of Asoka. 🏆 PYQ ALERT "Where is the Asoka Pillar from which the national flag emblem is taken? " = Sarnath, UP. Appeared in TNPSC Group 2 (2021). Q22 Through whose historical research did Asoka's inscriptions become known to the world? கீழ்கண்ட உற்றுள் யாருடைய வரலாற்று ஆய்வுகள் மூலம் அசோகரின் குறிப்புகள் வெளிவந்தன?

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: (Options: William Jones, James Prinsep, Alexander Cunningham, Charles Allen) HARD Factual Gr. 1 ✅ Answer William Jones, James Prinsep, and Alexander Cunningham (Options 1, 2, 3) 📌 Explanation • William Jones — orientalist scholar, linguistic research • James Prinsep — deciphered Brahmi script, enabling reading of Asoka's edicts • Alexander Cunningham — first Director-General of ASI (Archaeological Survey of India), founded 1861 • Charles Allen — wrote the book "The Search for the Lost Emperor" about Asoka (he is NOT primarily a research schola

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: "If he hadn't written it, Asoka might have remained a 'lost emperor' even today," said the teacher. 🪤 TNPSC TRAP Charles Allen is IN the textbook but is NOT part of the "research scholars" group. He wrote a popular book. The three who made Asoka's inscriptions public = Jones + Prinsep + Cunningham. Q23 Who wrote "The Search for the Lost Emperor" (about Asoka)? சர்ச் ஆப் த இந்தியன் லாஸ்ட் எம்பரர் — இந்தியாவின் தொலைந்த ஒரு அரசர் — யாருடைய புத்தகம்?

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: MEDIUM Factual Gr. 1/2 ✅ Answer Charles Allen 💡 TIP — Memory "Lost Emperor" → Charles Allen. If it were not for Charles Allen's book, Asoka would have truly remained "lost" to popular history. Q24 What did Asoka consider would happen to his history if Charles Allen had not written/publicised it? _____ செய்யவிட்டிருந்தால் அசோகரின் வரலாறு மறைக்கப்பட்ட வரலாறாகவே இருந்திருக்கும். EASY Factual ✅ Answer If Charles Allen had not documented/written about Asoka, Asoka's history would have remained hidden (lost) history.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: Answer = Charles Allen. Quick shortcut: "Lost Emperor" → Charles Allen. Q25 Find the WRONG pair: (Fort – Vellore) | (Temple – Thanjavur) | (Stupa – Sanchi) | (Palace – Thirumalai Nayakar Mahal) தவறான இணையை தேர்ந்தெடு: கோட்டை-வேலூர் | கோவில்-தஞ்சாவூர் | ஸ்தூபி-சாஞ்சி | அரண்மனை-திருமலை நாயக்கர் மகால் MEDIUM Trap ✅ Answer There is no wrong pair — all four are correct. OR: If one option is "Monastery – Thirumalai Nayakar" that would be wrong (it's a Palace not a monastery).

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: 💎 LIVE GEM — What is a Stupa? Stupa (ஸ்தூபி) = A dome-shaped structure built over the relics (physical remains) of the Buddha's body. After Buddha's cremation, his bodily remains were distributed and stupas were built over them. The most famous = Sanchi Stupa (Madhya Pradesh). Note: Stupa ≠ Place of death. Stupa = structure built over relics. Q26 What are the evidence / proofs that speak of Asoka's greatness? அசோகனுடைய பெருமையை எடுத்துச் சொல்லும் சான்றுகள் எவை?

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: EASY Factual Gr. 2/4 ✅ Answer Sarnath Pillar (Asoka Pillar) and Sanchi Stupa . Answer: Options 1 and 4. 💡 TIP Asoka → Sarnath Pillar + Sanchi Stupa. Both are in Central/Northern India. Buddhism + Asoka always go together.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: 06 World Civilisations — Timeline Q27 Name the four earliest civilisations in the world and their approximate time periods. உலகத்திலே இருக்கக்கூடிய நான்கு முதல் நாகரிகங்கள் என்ன? HARD Factual Gr. Mesopotamia (3500–2000 BCE) — Tigris & Euphrates (Iraq) 2. Indus Valley / Sindhu (3300–1900 BCE) — Indus River 3. Egypt (3100–1100 BCE) — Nile River 4. China (1700–1100 BCE) — Yellow River 💎 LIVE GEM — Mnemonic + Comparison Shortcut Mosi-eci (மொசி எசி) = Mo sopotamia → Si ndu → E gypt → Ci na (China) Key comparison: • Mesopotamia (3500–2000) and Indus

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: • Egypt continued longer: 3100–1100 (≈2000 years) • China came last: 1700–1100 BCE • "China always comes last" in world history lists — remember this! 🏆 PYQ ALERT The sequence/timeline of world civilisations appeared in TNPSC Group 1 Mains (2022) and Group 2 Prelims (2020). Q28 Why did ancient civilisations settle near rivers? நதிக்கரையில் குடியேறியதற்கான காரணம் என்ன? MEDIUM Conceptual ✅ Answer 1. Fertile soil for agriculture 2. Fresh water easily available 3.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: Transport — rivers as waterways for trade 4. Natural defence on one side The Bronze Age (Venkala Kalam) is when civilisations and religions first emerged — they needed river-based agriculture to sustain large populations.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: 07 Indus Valley — Discovery & Excavation Q29 Who first described the ruins of Harappa? ஹரப்ப நகரத்தின் இடிபாடுகளை முதன் முதலில் விவரித்தவர் யார்? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 1/2 ✅ Answer Charles Masson (1826) 💎 LIVE GEM — Memory Trick for Charles Masson Harappa ruins → Mason (who builds/notices broken structures). "Masson spotted the ruins like a mason notices broken bricks! " The textbook shows a Zebu bull (ஜெபு மாடு) as the Harappan symbol — remember the bull when you think Harappa.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: 🏆 PYQ ALERT "Who first identified/described Harappan ruins? " = Charles Masson. Appeared in TNPSC Group 4 and Group 2. Q30 In which year was the Lahore-to-Karachi railway laid? What law was passed that year? லாகூர் கராச்சி ரயில் பாதை அமைந்த ஆண்டு எது? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 1/2 ✅ Answer 1856 📌 Explanation 1856 — the year of the Lahore-Karachi railway. In the same year: Indian Military (Enlistment) Act 1856 and the Widow Remarriage Act 1856 were also passed.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: Bricks from Harappa were used as railway ballast during construction — Harappan ruins were accidentally discovered this way. 💎 LIVE GEM — 1856 Connection 1856 → Railway + Widow Remarriage Act + Military Act → all in one year. The railway workers noticed Harappan bricks and reported the ruins. This led to formal excavation starting in 1920. Q31 In which year were Harappa and Mohenjodaro formally excavated? ஹரப்பா முகஞ்சதாரோ நகர்களை அகலாய்வு செய்த ஆண்டு எது?

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: EASY Factual All groups ✅ Answer 1920 (approximately 1920–1924) 💎 LIVE GEM — Director General of ASI in 1920 Sir John Marshall was the Director-General of the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in 1920. He oversaw the excavation of Harappa and Mohenjodaro. He also identified the 10 common features between the two cities. Q32 Who identified the 10 common features between Harappa and Mohenjodaro? HARD Factual Gr. 1 ✅ Answer Sir John Marshall Marshall (1920–1924 period) = ASI Director General who excavated both sites and identified 10 points of

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: 💡 TIP John Marshall → 1920–1924 → 10 common features → first to declare IVC as a major civilisation. Q33 When was the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) established? Where is its headquarters? Who helped establish it? இந்திய தொல்பொருள் ஆய்வு துறை நிறுவப்பட்ட ஆண்டு, தலைமையகம் எங்கு? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2/4 ✅ Answer ASI founded: 1861 | Headquarters: New Delhi | Helped by: Alexander Cunningham (land surveyor / first DG) 💎 LIVE GEM — 1856 → 1861 Connection 1856: Railway laid → ruins noticed 1861: ASI founded (5 years later) to formally study the

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: Tamil Nadu Archaeological Department (TNASI) founded 100 years later: 1961 , by R. Q34 Which civilisation is older — Harappa or Mohenjodaro? EASY Factual ✅ Answer Harappa is older. Mohenjodaro came later. Harappa (first site identified → gives the civilisation its name). Mohenjodaro (discovered later, but is the larger/more studied city). Q35 What does the word "Civilisation" (நாகரிகம்) come from? What does it mean? EASY Factual ✅ Answer From Latin word "Civis" — meaning "one who lives in a city" (citizen / urban dweller).

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: 💎 LIVE GEM — Language Root Connections Civis → Civilisation → Citizen → City. The Harappan civilisation = urban civilisation = "Civis" fully applies. It was a planned urban civilisation .

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: 08 Harappa & Mohenjodaro — City Planning & Features Q36 What were the two planned divisions of the IVC cities? ஹரப்ப நாகரிகத்தின் திட்டமிட்ட இரண்டு பகுதிகள் என்ன? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 1/2 ✅ Answer Upper (Western) part — elevated, for the ruler/elite | Lower (Eastern) part — for the common people 💎 LIVE GEM — Layout Logic West = Upper = King. East = Lower = People. "The king always lives on high ground — military advantage and prestige. " Q37 In which direction did IVC streets run?

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: தெருக்கள் எந்த திசையிலிருந்து எந்த திசையில் அமைந்திருந்தன? EASY Factual ✅ Answer North to South and East to West (grid pattern / சட்ட வடிவமைப்பு) 🪤 TRAP — Direction Order Primary direction: North–South. Secondary: East to West. Grid layout = the defining feature of IVC town planning. Q38 What were IVC houses made of? How many storeys? EASY Factual ✅ Answer Houses: Burnt bricks (சுட்ட செங்கற்கள்) — kiln-fired, not sun-dried | Storeys: 1 or 2 floors Also used: lime mortar for joining bricks.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: Roofs were flat (சமதலமாக). Q39 What shape were the roofs of IVC houses? EASY Factual ✅ Answer Flat (samethalama — சமதலமாக) Drains were covered with bricks or stone slabs. Houses had their own drainage connected to the city drainage system. Q40 What is the Great Bath? Where is it located? What shape is it? What was used to prevent water seepage? பெருங்குளம் வடிவம் மற்றும் அமைந்திருக்கப்பட்ட இடம் என்ன? HARD Factual Gr. 1 ✅ Answer Mohenjodaro | Shape: Rectangular (செவ்வக வடிவம்) | Water-proofing: Bitumen/tar (பிசின் / tar) was applied to the walls

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: The living people bathed there; the grain was stored in Harappa. " 💎 LIVE GEM — Bitumen Logic Bitumen/tar was applied to the outer walls of the Great Bath (not inside). Reason: Tar on the inside would contaminate the water. Tar on outside = waterproofing from outside. Same tar used in Harappan granary — on outside walls to keep moisture out. 🏆 PYQ ALERT "World's first public bath" = Great Bath of Mohenjodaro. This is a TNPSC Group 1 and Group 2 confirmed question.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: Q41 The largest assembly hall in Mohenjodaro had how many pillars in how many rows? முகஞ்சதாரவில் இருந்த மிகப்பெரிய பொதுக்கூட்டரங்கு — எத்தனை தூண்கள் எத்தனை வரிசை? HARD Factual Gr. 1 ✅ Answer 20 pillars in 4 rows (= 80 total pillars) 🔴 CRITICAL — Do NOT Forget 20 pillars × 4 rows = 80 total. "If options say 80, that's the derived answer. But the factual answer is 20 pillars, 4 rows. " Group 1 may ask 20 or 4 or 80 separately — know all three. 🏆 PYQ ALERT This exact question (20 pillars, 4 rows) appeared in TNPSC Group 1 paper.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: Q42 Where was the granary (grain store) found? Where was the last/recent granary discovered? செங்கற்களால் கட்டப்பட்ட தானிய களஞ்சியம் கண்டுபிடிக்கப்பட்ட இடம் எது? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 1/2 ✅ Answer Classic answer: Harappa | Most recent discovery: Rakhigarhi (Haryana district) 💎 LIVE GEM — Rakhigarhi Rakhigarhi (Haryana) — the largest known IVC site in India. Recently confirmed granary found here too. This site is emerging as a major PYQ target for 2025–2026 exams.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: 09 Indus Valley — Trade, Seals, Tools & Culture Q43 In which foreign countries were Indus Valley seals found? சிந்துவெளி முத்திரைகள் கிடைத்துள்ள இடம் எது? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 1/2 ✅ Answer Iraq, Kuwait, Mesopotamia, and Sumeria (all in the Persian Gulf / Middle East region) IVC seals found abroad prove international trade. They traded with Mesopotamia / Sumerian civilisation. Q44 Sumerian King Naram-Sin purchased ornaments/goods from which Indus Valley location called "Meluhha"?

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: சின் என்பவர் சிந்துவலி பகுதியில் உள்ள _____ என்னும் இடத்தில் அணிகலன்கள் வாங்கியதாக குறிப்பு உள்ளது. HARD Factual Gr. 1 ✅ Answer He bought goods from a place called Meluhha — which scholars identify as the Indus Valley region. 💎 LIVE GEM — Cylinder Seals Cylinder-shaped seals found in Mesopotamia and Persian Gulf are ALSO found in the Indus Valley — proving direct trade contact between IVC and Sumerian civilisation. This is unique evidence of cross-civilisation exchange.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: Q45 What is Lothal? What is it famous for? கப்பல் கட்டும் தளம் மற்றும் செப்பினிடும் தளம் — உலகத்திலே இயற்கையாக அமைந்த துறைமுகம் எது? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 1/2 ✅ Answer Lothal (Gujarat) — world's first naturally located harbour , also India's first ship-building yard . Located on a tributary of the Sabarmati River . 💎 LIVE GEM — Lothal Facts • Located in Gujarat , on Sabarmati tributary • 2000+ years old harbour — naturally formed • India's first documented shipbuilding yard • Gandhi's Dandi March: Dandi is also near Sabarmati!

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: Connection to remember Sabarmati. 🏆 PYQ ALERT "First shipbuilding yard / harbour in India" = Lothal. Appeared in SSC CGL (2017) and TNPSC Group 1 (2020). Q46 A male statue in a seated position was found at which IVC site? What material was it made of? அமர்ந்த நிலையில் உள்ள ஆண் சிலை கண்டுபிடிக்கப்பட்ட இடம் எது? MEDIUM Factual ✅ Answer Mohenjodaro | Material: Limestone / Steatite (சுண்ணாம்பு கல்) The famous "Priest-King" statue from Mohenjodaro is made of steatite/limestone.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: It shows a man in a seated posture with a shawl decorated with trefoil patterns. Q47 The famous "Dancing Girl" statue — what is it made of? Where was it found? நடனமாடும் பெண் சிலை எதனால் செய்யப்பட்டது? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 1/2 ✅ Answer Bronze (வெண்கலம் / Bronze) | Found at: Mohenjodaro 💎 LIVE GEM — Gender Significance The bronze Dancing Girl is so detailed and well-crafted that it shows women had great importance in IVC society. Bronze casting at this level (4000 years ago) = extraordinary metallurgical skill.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: 🏆 PYQ ALERT "Dancing Girl statue material" = Bronze (not stone). This is a frequent wrong-answer trap in TNPSC — students confuse it with the limestone Priest-King. Q48 A measuring scale made of ivory (tusk/தந்தம்) was found in Gujarat. How many millimetres long was it? குஜராத் மாநிலத்தில் கண்டுபிடிக்கப்பட்ட தந்தத்தினால் ஆன சிறிய அளவீடு — எத்தனை மில்லிமீட்டர்? HARD Factual Gr. 704 mm (1704 millimetres = the smallest unit marked on it) 🔴 CRITICAL — Exact Number 1.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: 704 mm = the smallest graduation on the ivory measuring rod found in Lothal (Gujarat). This proves advanced standardised measurements in IVC — 4000 years ago. Q49 What was the first metal used by humans? மனிதர்களால் முதன் முதலில் கண்டுபிடிக்கப்பட்டு உபயோகப்படுத்தப்பட்ட உலோகம் எது? EASY Factual Gr. 2/4 ✅ Answer Copper (செம்பு) Copper → then Bronze (copper + tin alloy) → then Iron. IVC period = Bronze/Copper Age. Iron came much later. Q50 IVC people used carts with solid wheels (no spokes).

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: What does this mean? ஹரப்பா மக்கள் சக்கர வண்டிகளை பயன்படுத்தினார் — இடிட திடமான சக்கரங்களை பயன்படுத்தினார் (ஆரக்கால் இல்லாதது). MEDIUM Conceptual ✅ Answer IVC carts had solid wheels without spokes (ஆரக்கால் இல்லாதது) . They were heavier and slower than spoked wheels. 💎 LIVE GEM — Asoka Chakra Connection The Asoka Chakra on our flag has 24 SPOKES (ஆரக்கால்) — this represents a more advanced wheel design (post-IVC). IVC people had no spokes = solid wheel = Aarakkaal illatha chakram.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: Asoka's era = spokes present! Q51 IVC people used red pottery painted with black designs. What was the pottery made on? What colour first, then decorated with what? மண்பாண்டங்கள் எதன் மூலம் உருவாக்கினார் — எந்த வண்ணத்தில் அறிஞ்சு? MEDIUM Factual ✅ Answer Made on a potter's wheel (சக்கரம்). Base colour: Red . Decorated with Black designs (animals etc. 💎 LIVE GEM — Why Red? Clay (செம்மண்) = naturally reddish. Spin it on a wheel → shape it → fire it → it comes out reddish.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: To differentiate designs, they painted black on top. "Think practically — if you make a clay pot today, it will be reddish. That's all you get without modern pigments. " Q52 What gemstone did IVC people use for jewellery? What was it called? சிந்துவெளி மக்கள் ஆபரணம் செய்ய பயன்படுத்தப்பட்டது எது? MEDIUM Factual ✅ Answer Carnelian (கார்நெலியன்) — a red semi-precious stone Carnelian beads were a major IVC export. Found in Mesopotamia too — proves trade.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: The stone is reddish-orange in colour. Q53 Which of these was IVC people NOT aware of? சிந்துவெளி மக்களுக்கு எதன் பற்றி தெரியாது? EASY Factual ✅ Answer IVC people did NOT know about: Iron (they lived in the Bronze/Copper age) and Horses (horses were introduced later by Aryans) IVC people knew copper and bronze but not iron. Horses were absent from IVC — no horse bones or horse-related artefacts found. This is significant for the Aryan Invasion debate.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: Q54 In which direction was the IVC script written? (Left to Right or Right to Left? ) சிந்துவெளி நாகரிக எழுத்து முறை — இடமிருந்து வலம் இல்லை வலமிருந்து இடம்? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 1 ✅ Answer Left to Right (இடமிருந்து வலம்) 💎 LIVE GEM — Semitic vs IVC Script Direction Semitic religions (Judaism, Islam, Christianity) use scripts written Right to Left (e. , Arabic, Hebrew). IVC script = Left to Right — same as English, Tamil, and most modern languages.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: Memory: Semitic = Right to Left = backward (unusual). IVC/us = Left to Right = normal direction. 🪤 TRAP Students often confuse this with Semitic scripts. IVC = Left to Right. Don't reverse it. Q55 Who declared Mohenjodaro as a World Heritage Site? (UNESCO) முகஞ்சதாரவில் தொல்பொருள் ஆராய்ச்சி உலக பாரம்பரிய தளமாக எந்த நிறுவனம் தேர்ந்தெடுக்கப்பட்டது? EASY Factual All groups ✅ Answer UNESCO declared Mohenjodaro a World Heritage Site.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: 10 IVC — Geography, Extent & Decline Q56 What was the precursor (forerunner) of the Indus Valley Civilisation? சிந்துவெளி நாகரிகத்தின் முன்னோடி எது? EASY Factual Gr. 2/4 ✅ Answer Mehrgarh Civilisation — located in Baluchistan (Kaiber-Pakhtunkhwa), Pakistan Mehrgarh is the Neolithic settlement that predates and leads into the IVC. Located in the Kaiber-Bolam valley (Baluchistan). This is where the first Neolithic communities emerged before IVC. 57 What was the geographical extent of the Indus Valley Civilisation — North-East, South, East, and We

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: சிந்துவெளி நாகரிகத்தின் பரப்பு — வடகிழக்கு, தெற்கு, கிழக்கு, மேற்கு எல்லைகள்? HARD Factual Gr. 1 ✅ Answer North-East: Afghanistan West: Baluchistan (Pakistan) — Makran coast East: Kakkar-Kakra river region (Haryana/Rajasthan) South: Maharashtra (Daimabad site) 💎 LIVE GEM — KV-KV Shortcut for East East boundary: Kakkar to Kakra river (Haryana) "KV-KV" = Kakkar valley to Kakra valley These two rivers mark the eastern limit of IVC spread. Also: Rivers "Kaveri" and "Porunai" are mentioned in ancient Sangam texts and are found in Afghanistan — prov

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: 🪤 TNPSC TRAP — River Names "Kaveri/Porunai river" as a place name = Afghanistan (ancient trade route river names) "Kaveri/Porunai" as a Tamil Nadu river = Tamil Nadu If asked as a river (nathi) = Afghanistan | If asked as a city location = Tamil Nadu 🏆 PYQ ALERT IVC extent direction questions appear regularly in Group 1 Geography-History crossover. Q58 What are the probable causes of the decline of IVC? சிந்துவெளி நாகரிகம் அழிவுக்கான காரணங்கள் என்ன?

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: HARD Conceptual Gr. Climate change / Environmental change 2. Aryan Invasion (Aariya padaiyeduppu) 3. Natural calamities (floods, earthquakes) 4. Epidemic / Disease (no medical facilities) 5. Deforestation (forest clearing for agriculture) 6. Change of river course (Saraswati river dried up) 💎 LIVE GEM — Aryan Invasion Theory The Aryan Invasion Theory (AIT) = Aryans invaded from Central Asia, defeated IVC people, who fled south → became Dravidians.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: This is still debated by historians. The teacher noted: "Aryan invasion is one possible theory — it is in the textbook, but it remains controversial. " Q59 What was the total area of IVC in square kilometres? How many large cities? How many villages? சிந்துவெளி நாகரிகத்தின் பரப்பு — எத்தனை லட்சம் சதுர கிலோமீட்டர்? எத்தனை பெரிய நகரம்? எத்தனை கிராமங்கள்? HARD Factual Gr. 1 ✅ Answer Area: 13 lakh sq km (1. 3 million sq km) | Large cities: 6 | Villages: 200+ 💎 LIVE GEM — Six Major Cities The 6 major cities of IVC: Harappa, Mohenjodaro, Lothal, Sho

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: "Remember Lothal and Rakhigarhi especially — they are frequently tested. " Q60 What time period does the IVC belong to? What was the overall duration? ஹரப்பா நாகரிகம் சார்ந்த காலம் எது? சிந்துவெளி நாகரிக காலம் எந்த காலம்? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 1/2 ✅ Answer IVC period: 3300 BCE to 1900 BCE | Age: Bronze Age / Copper Age (also called Chalcolithic) 💡 TIP — Period Comparison Mesopotamia: 3500–2000 BCE IVC: 3300–1900 BCE → They were contemporaries and declined together!

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: Egypt: 3100–1100 BCE (lasted longer) China: 1700–1100 BCE (came last) Q61 The "Dancing Girl" statue of IVC was estimated to be how many years old? நடனமாடும் பெண் சிலை 3000 ஆண்டுகளுக்கு முற்பட்டது என கணித்தவர் யார்? HARD Factual ✅ Answer Estimated at over 3000 years old — statement made by John Marshall Marshall reportedly said: "Looking at this, it feels like it could not have been made 3000 years ago — it looks so modern. " This quote is in the textbook context box.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: 11 Miscellaneous & World History Connections Q62 What is a Ziggurat? ஜிகரட் என்றால் என்ன? யார் கட்டினார்? எந்த கடவுளுக்கு? HARD Factual Gr. 1 ✅ Answer Ziggurat = A stepped/tiered temple-tower built in Mesopotamia | Built by King Ur-Nammu in the city of Ur | Dedicated to the Moon God (Sin / Chandra Kadavul) 💎 LIVE GEM — Ziggurat = Twin Temple Ziggurat is sometimes called "twin temple" or "double temple" in textbook context. Compare with: Abu Simbel (Egypt) = also twin temples.

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: Ziggurat (Mesopotamia) ↔ Abu Simbel (Egypt) = common pair question. Q63 The Great Pyramid of Giza — how many tons does a single block weigh? கிசே பிரமிடு — எத்தனை டன் எடை? HARD Factual Gr. 1 ✅ Answer Each block of the Great Pyramid of Giza (Cheops Pyramid) weighs approximately 15 tons . Built by King Khufu (Cheops). Located at Giza, Egypt. One of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World. Q64 Who were the first to invent writing in the world? முதல் எழுத்து வடிவம் யாரால் உருவாக்கப்பட்டது?

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: MEDIUM Factual Gr. 1/2 ✅ Answer Sumerians (Mesopotamia) invented the first writing system — called Cuneiform script (~3200 BCE) 💎 LIVE GEM — Anunnaki Theory The teacher mentioned as a fun aside: "Search for 'Anunnaki Tamil' — some say ancient Sumerians were connected to Tamil civilisation. It's a fascinating rabbit hole for your free time — not exam material, but historically interesting! " Q65 What archaeological tool/technique is used to survey land for buried sites?

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: What carbon-based dating method is used? MEDIUM Factual ✅ Answer Land survey: Magnetic Scanner (Magnetic Survey / காந்தப்புல வருடி) | Dating method: Carbon-14 (C-14) dating / Radiocarbon dating Q66 What animal designs/patterns appear on IVC pottery? பானைகளில் காணப்படக்கூடிய வடிவம் எது? EASY Factual ✅ Answer Animal shapes / geometric animal designs (விலங்குகளின் வடிவியல் அமைப்பு) — dogs, bulls, birds etc. were painted on pots. Q67 What is the special/unique feature of the Indus Valley Civilisation?

chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: சிந்துவெளி நாகரிகத்தினுடைய சிறப்பு அம்சம் என்ன? EASY Factual All groups ✅ Answer IVC is a Planned Urban Civilisation (திட்டமிடப்பட்ட நகர நாகரிகம்) — meaning cities were designed with a plan before construction, with grid streets, drainage, and public buildings. 🔴 CRITICAL — One-Line Answer to Memorise "IVC = World's first PLANNED URBAN CIVILISATION. " This is the most important single-line description of IVC. Include this in every essay/short answer.

This is why IVC is classified as ____ period. " (Fill in blank) Answer: Proto-Historic Period. This connects Q7+Q8 from this session. Expect as a statement-correction or fill-in-blank. PREDICTION 02 · TNPSC Group 1/2 · HIGH PROBABILITY "Match the following — Harappa : Granary :: Mohenjodaro : ___" or "Great Bath was found at ___" Answer: Great Bath / Mohenjodaro.

" Answer: Ashutosh Mukherjee (not associated). This is a negative-type exclusion question based on Q22. chunks.title: QA_History_StoneAge_IndusValley_LiveRevision
chunks.text: " OR "Dharma in Sanskrit = ___ in Prakrit" Answer: Dhamma = Prakrit. Dharma = Sanskrit/English. Q10 was the most-missed in this session — expect it in exam. " Answer: Afghanistan (NE) → Maharashtra (South). The four-direction extent question based on Q57.

chunks.text: TNPSC Group 1 / 2 / 4 / VAO Tamil Nadu History Indian Polity 12 Q&A Extracted 6 Live Gems Live Session — SP Velan Academy FILTER: All Questions Easy Medium Hard 💎 Gems Only 🏆 PYQ Only Easy Medium Hard ■ [CRITICAL] ■ [TRAP] ■ [TIP] ■ [PYQ] ■ [GEM]

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: 12 Questions 6 Live Gems 2 Main Topics 4 PYQ Alerts Topic 1 — Justice Party (Neethi Katchi / Dravidar Kazhagam Origin): History, founding, key leaders, schemes, conversion to DK, key books & personalities. Topic 2 — Local Self-Government (73rd & 74th Amendments): Panchayati Raj committees, constitutional status, key year-matched facts. Thirukkural quote opened the session: "Protect humility — nothing is greater for a person. " (Adakkam / Chapter) Session by SP Velan Academy .

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: 🍱 Section 1 — Noon Meal Scheme (மதிய உணவு திட்டம்) Q 01 MEDIUM Factual Choose the correct statement(s) about the Justice Party / Neethi Katchi : Statement 1: In 1921, the Noon Meal Scheme was first implemented in Chennai (Madras) schools for the first time. Statement 2: This scheme was first implemented in the Adayar (Adyar) area. ▼ ✅ Answer Option A — Only Statement 1 is correct. Statement 1 is TRUE. Statement 2 is FALSE (Adyar is wrong — it was implemented at a school in Ayiram Vilakku / Thousand Lights area ).

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: 📌 Explanation In 1921 , the Justice Party government under P. Thyagarayar (Pitthi Thyagarayar) introduced the Noon Meal Scheme for the first time — in a single school in the Ayiram Vilakku (Thousand Lights) area of Madras, not Adyar. This predates Kamarajar's statewide implementation. The scheme was later discontinued due to lack of funds and was revived and expanded by Kamarajar across Tamil Nadu. 💎 Live Gem — Noon Meal Evolution Timeline The teacher gave a brilliant one-shot timeline of the Noon Meal scheme evolution: 1921 P.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Thyagarayar — First implementation, Thousand Lights, Madras (1 school only, later stopped) 1950s–60s Kamarajar — Expanded Noon Meal Scheme across all Tamil Nadu to encourage school attendance MGR era M. Ramachandran — Added nutritional value → called "Satthunavoo Thittam" (Nutritious Food Scheme) Jayalalithaa era — Added potatoes (urulaikizhangu) to the meal Kalaignar era — Added egg (muttai) along with potato Recent CM Stalin — Introduced Morning Breakfast Scheme (Kalai Unavu Thittam) 🏆 PYQ Alert The origin location of the Noon Meal Scheme (T

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Adyar) has been used as a trap in TNPSC Group 2 & Group 4 exams. The "Adyar" option is a classic distractor. 🪤 TRAP NOTE Most students associate Noon Meal Scheme only with Kamarajar. The exam tests whether you know P. Thyagarayar started it in 1921 — 26 years before Indian Independence!

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: 🏛️ Section 2 — Justice Party Government Schemes & Years Q 02 HARD Factual Match the following — choose the correct year for each scheme/institution established by the Justice Party government: (i) State Industries Assistance Act (Thozhil Valarchikkaana Maanila Arasu Udavi Thittam) (ii) Public Service Commission (Pothu Thervaanayam — TNPSC) ▼ ✅ Answer Option B — Only Statement 2 (Public Service Commission) is correct. • State Industries Act: 1922 (NOT 1923) • Staff Selection Board: 1924 → became Public Service Commission in 1929 📌 Explanation

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: During this tenure they passed several landmark Acts. The Madras Public Service Commission was the first PSC established in India , initially as a Staff Selection Board in 1924, formally upgraded in 1929. 💎 Live Gem — India's First PSC The teacher emphasized: Tamil Nadu's TNPSC is the first Public Service Commission established in India — not just Tamil Nadu. It originated from the Justice Party's Staff Selection Board (1924), which became the PSC in 1929.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: This is a frequently tested fact. 🪤 TRAP NOTE The year 1923 is a common distractor for the State Industries Act — the correct year is 1922 .

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: 📜 Section 3 — Justice Party History, Origin & Key Leaders Q 03 MEDIUM Factual In 1944, the Justice Party was converted into Dravidar Kazhagam . At which city's conference was this conversion resolution passed? Who proposed the resolution? ▼ ✅ Answer City: Salem Date: 27th August 1944 Resolution proposed by: Periyar E. Ramasamy (Thanthai Periyar) — merged Justice Party + Self-Respect Movement into Dravidar Kazhagam The resolution (theermanam) was moved by: Peraarignar Anna (C.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Annadurai) 📌 Explanation At the Salem Conference on 27 August 1944 , Periyar announced the merger of the Justice Party and the Self-Respect Movement (Suyamariyathai Iyakkam) to form the Dravidar Kazhagam (DK) . He declared that DK would not contest elections — its goal was to spread rationalism and self-respect among the masses, not seek votes. Anna (Annadurai) moved the formal resolution for this conversion. Dravidar Kazhagam is the parent of all Dravidian parties in Tamil Nadu — DMK, AIADMK, and others trace their roots here.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: 💎 Live Gem — Critical Distinction Two different persons, two different acts: Periyar — announced & effected the conversion (changed Justice Party → Dravidar Kazhagam) Peraarignar Anna (C. Annadurai) — moved the formal resolution (theermanam) at the Salem conference This distinction has been specifically tested in TNPSC Group 2 exam . Don't confuse the two roles! 🏆 PYQ Alert According to the teacher, this exact question — " Who moved the resolution to convert Justice Party to Dravidar Kazhagam?

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: " — was asked in a previous TNPSC Group 2 paper. Answer: Anna (Annadurai) . Q 04 MEDIUM Factual Under the Justice Party government, women were given the right to participate in elections (vote and contest) for the first time in Madras. In which year was this granted? Options: 1920 / 1921 / 1922 / 1924 ▼ ✅ Answer Option B — 1921 Women were granted voting rights AND the right to contest elections in Madras in 1921 , under the Justice Party government.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: 📌 Explanation The first direct elections in India (under the Montagu-Chelmsford reforms) were held in November 1920 . Initially, only men with property qualifications could vote, and women had no electoral rights. In 1921 , the Justice Party government passed two landmark laws: (1) the Noon Meal Scheme and (2) Women's Voting Rights and the right to contest elections — making Madras Presidency the first in India to grant women this right, 26 years before Indian Independence (1947) .

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: 💡 TIP — Connection to 106th Amendment The teacher mentioned the 106th Constitutional Amendment (Women's Reservation Bill, 2023) in this context. Note: this amendment provides 33% reservation for women in Parliament and State Assemblies. However, it has not yet come into force (pending delimitation). Keep this updated fact in mind for current affairs questions. Q 05 HARD Factual Who was the first unofficial (non-official) Mayor / Chairman of the Madras Corporation?

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: He was also known as "Vellai Udai Venthar" (Lord of White Robes) because of his love for white clothes. Thyagarayar (Pitthi Thyagarayar) One of the founding triumvirate of the Justice Party. Also the person who introduced the Noon Meal Scheme. 📌 Explanation P. Thyagarayar was called "Vellai Udai Venthar" (King of White Attire) for always wearing white. He was a key founding figure of the Justice Party — one of three principal leaders: Dr. Nair (Tharavath Madhavan Nair) P.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Thyagarayar Raja of Panagal (Bobbili Raja / P. Ramarayaningar) Note: P. Thyagarayar ≠ P. Rajan was a different person who served as Chief Minister of Madras Presidency from April–August 1936. 💎 Live Gem — Three Pillars of Justice Party The three most important leaders of the Justice Party (exam-critical): Leader Key Role / Title Dr. Nair Founding leader; given "Kaiser-i-Hind" title by British; called "Dravidam Lenin" by Periyar P. Thyagarayar "Vellai Udai Venthar"; First unofficial Madras Mayor; Started Noon Meal Scheme (Thousand Lights) Raja

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Ramarayaningar 🪤 TRAP NOTE Don't confuse P. Thyagarayar with P. Rajan (later Chief Minister). They are two different people from the same party but different eras and roles. Q 06 HARD Factual The Madras Non-Brahmin Association was founded by two lawyers. Who were they, and in which year was it established? ▼ ✅ Answer Founders: P. Subramaniam and Purushothama Naidu Year: 1909 📌 Explanation — The Origin Story of Justice Party In 1908 , Justice Sankar Nair (a Madras High Court Judge) gave a speech at the Madras University function calling for th

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Inspired by this, two lawyers — P. Subramaniam and Purushothama Naidu — founded the Madras Non-Brahmin Association in 1909. The evolution chain: 1909 — Madras Non-Brahmin Association ~1912 — Became Madras United League ~1914 — Became Madras Dravidian Association ~1916 — Became South Indian Liberal Federation 1917 — Registered as Justice Party (Neethi Katchi) — for electoral purposes 💎 Live Gem — Why Justice Party Was Needed The teacher explained the social context brilliantly: In the pre-Justice Party era, of 100 government vacancies, ~96 went

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: The Non-Brahmin movement sought social justice — not anti-religion, but equity in representation. This is the conceptual foundation of Tamil Nadu's reservation politics today. Q 07 MEDIUM Factual Who was the first President of the Madras United League (Madras Aikkiya Kazhagam) ? Options: Raja of Panagal / Dr. Natesanar / Veeraswami Naidu / Thuraiswami Naidu ▼ ✅ Answer Option A — Raja of Panagal His original (full) name: P. Ramarayaningar (Bobbili Raja / Panagal Raja) 📌 Explanation When the Madras Non-Brahmin Association evolved into the Madras

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: He later became one of the most prominent Chief Ministers of Madras Presidency under the Justice Party — responsible for granting women voting rights and establishing the first PSC. Q 08 HARD Factual The books "Dravidian Virtues" (திராவிட தகுதிகள்) and "Non-Brahmin Letters" (பிராமணன் இல்லாத கடிதங்கள்) were published by the Madras Dravidian Association. Who published them? Options: Natesanar / V. Srinivasa Sastri / Lord Willingdon / Dr. Karunagara Meenan ▼ ✅ Answer Option D — Dr.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Karunagara Meenan 📌 Explanation Around 1910–1912, the Madras Dravidian Association published two important books to spread awareness about non-Brahmin rights and Dravidian identity. Karunagara Meenan was the publisher. These books laid the intellectual groundwork for the Justice Party movement. 💎 Live Gem — Dr. Nair's British Honour The teacher shared that Dr. Nair (Tharavath Madhavan Nair), one of the Justice Party's three pillars, received the "Kaiser-i-Hind" medal from the British — for his services as a ship's doctor on HMS Madras.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: He later returned it (as Gandhi did after Jallianwala Bagh). Periyar called him the "Dravidam Lenin" — drawing a comparison to Lenin (the Russian revolutionary who peacefully dismantled the Tsarist regime) for his role in dismantling Brahmin social dominance without bloodshed.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: 🏘️ Section 4 — Local Self-Government (73rd & 74th Constitutional Amendments) Q 09 EASY Factual The 73rd and 74th Constitutional Amendments — in which year were they introduced? Options: 1992 / 1995 / 1997 / 1990 ▼ ✅ Answer 1992 73rd Amendment → Panchayati Raj (Rural Local Self-Government) 74th Amendment → Municipalities / Nagarpalikas (Urban Local Self-Government) 📌 Key Facts These two amendments added Part IX (Articles 243–243O) for Panchayats and Part IX-A (Articles 243P–243ZG) for Municipalities to the Constitution.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: The 73rd Amendment also added the 11th Schedule listing 29 subjects for Panchayats, and the 74th added the 12th Schedule with 18 subjects for municipalities. 🏆 PYQ Alert This is a perennial question in all TNPSC exams. The year 1992 and the schedule numbers (11th for Panchayat, 12th for Municipality) are high-yield facts. Q 10 MEDIUM Factual Which of the following committees was appointed by the Planning Commission of India (Thittakuzu)? Options: Ashok Mehta Committee / G.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Rao Committee / L. Singhvi Committee / Balwantrai Mehta Committee ▼ ✅ Answer Option B — G. Rao Committee ( 1985 ) 📌 Explanation The Planning Commission of India (est. 1950, dissolved 2014 → replaced by NITI Aayog) appointed the G. Rao Committee in 1985 to study Panchayati Raj. The other committees were appointed by the Central Government directly. Key distinction: Planning Commission → G.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: 📊 Section 5 — Panchayati Raj Committees (Year-Matching) Q 11 HARD Factual Which committee's report is known as the "Magna Carta of Panchayati Raj" ? Whose report said: "Panchayati Raj is like grass without roots (வேர்கள் இல்லாத புல்)"? ▼ ✅ Answer Magna Carta of Panchayati Raj → Balwantrai Mehta Committee ( 1957 ) "Grass without roots" → G. Rao Committee ( 1985 ) 📌 All Four Committees — Year Chart Committee Year Key Contribution / Quote Balwantrai Mehta 1957 First committee on PR; recommended 3-tier system; "Magna Carta of Panchayati Raj" Asho

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Rao 1985 "Grass without roots" (PR in name only); recommended constitutional status L. Singhvi 1986 Recommended constitutional status for Panchayats — directly led to 73rd Amendment (1992) 💎 Live Gem — Shortcut Trick "Grass without roots" → starts with 'G' → G. Rao The teacher gave this shortcut: the phrase "Grass (வேர்கள்)" starts with G, just like G. Easy to remember! 🏆 PYQ Alert The Balwantrai Mehta Committee and the 11th Schedule connection is a high-frequency TNPSC question.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Singhvi (1986) → recommended constitutional status → led to 73rd Amendment (1992) is a popular sequential question. 🪤 TRAP NOTE Students often confuse Ashok Mehta (1977) with Balwantrai Mehta (1957) . Both have "Mehta" in the name! Remember: Balwantrai = older, first, Magna Carta. Ashok = 1977, 2-tier recommendation. Q 12 MEDIUM Factual Which committee recommended giving Constitutional Status to Panchayati Raj institutions? Rao / Balwantrai Mehta / L.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Singhvi / Ashok Mehta ▼ ✅ Answer Option C — L. Singhvi Committee ( 1986 ) 📌 Explanation The L. Singhvi Committee (1986) recommended that Panchayati Raj institutions be given constitutional recognition and protection through a constitutional amendment. This recommendation was implemented through the 73rd Constitutional Amendment of 1992 , which added Part IX to the Constitution. 🔴 Critical — Homework Question Homework Q (from session): The Balwantrai Mehta Committee report is connected to which Schedule of the Constitution?

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Options: 3 / 5 / 9 / 11 Answer: 11th Schedule (lists 29 subjects for Panchayats under 73rd Amendment)

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: 1909 Founded by P. Subramaniam + Purushothama Naidu Madras United League ~1912 President: Raja of Panagal Dravidian Assoc. ~1914 Published "Dravidam Virtues" books — Dr. Karunagara Meenan S. Liberal Federation ~1916 Final pre-party stage Justice Party (Formal) 1917 Electoral politics entry 🏘️ LOCAL SELF-GOVERNMENT — Key Facts 73rd Amendment 1992 Panchayati Raj → 11th Schedule (29 subjects) 74th Amendment 1992 Municipalities → 12th Schedule (18 subjects) Balwantrai Mehta 1957 "Magna Carta of PR"; recommended 3-tier system Ashok Mehta 1977 Recom

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Rao 1985 "Grass without roots"; appointed by Planning Commission L. Singhvi 1986 Recommended constitutional status → led to 73rd Amendment NITI Aayog 2014 Replaced Planning Commission under PM Modi 📝 Session Summary — 10 Key Points The Noon Meal Scheme was first introduced by P. Thyagarayar in 1921 at a school in Thousand Lights (Ayiram Vilakku) , Madras — NOT Adyar. Tamil Nadu's TNPSC is the first Public Service Commission in India (started as Staff Selection Board in 1924, became PSC in 1929).

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Justice Party was converted to Dravidar Kazhagam at the Salem Conference, 27 August 1944 . Periyar announced it; Anna moved the resolution . Women in Madras Presidency were given voting rights and the right to contest elections in 1921 — 26 years before Indian Independence. The three key leaders of Justice Party: Dr. Thyagarayar, Raja of Panagal . Madras Non-Brahmin Association was founded in 1909 by P. Subramaniam and Purushothama Naidu , inspired by Justice Sankar Nair's 1908 speech.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: The 73rd (Panchayati Raj) and 74th (Municipalities) Amendments were both introduced in 1992 . Balwantrai Mehta Committee (1957) = Magna Carta of Panchayati Raj. Rao (1985) = "Grass without roots". Singhvi (1986) = recommended constitutional status. The State Industries Assistance Act was passed in 1922 (NOT 1923). Nair was called "Dravidam Lenin" by Periyar and received the Kaiser-i-Hind medal from the British.
