chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: All Easy Medium Hard 💎 Gems Only 🏆 PYQ Only Expand All ▾ [CRITICAL] Must-know fact [TRAP] Common wrong answer [TIP] Memory shortcut [PYQ] Previous Year Q alert [GEM] Extra insight

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 55 Total Qs 18 Easy 25 Medium 12 Hard 14 Gems 💎 16 PYQ 🏆 Law & Sovereignty Types of Law Sources of Law Jallikattu Case Citizenship Marshall's Analysis Types of Rights Fundamental Rights Writs Political Obligation Senior Citizens Act RTI / Right to Privacy

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 2 — Definitions, Root Word, Purposes Q1 EASY Factual Gr. 4/VAO 🏆 PYQ The term 'Law' is derived from which root word, and what does it mean? சட்டம் என்ற சொல் எந்த மூல சொல்லிலிருந்து உருவானது? அதன் பொருள் என்ன? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer The term 'Law' is derived from an old Teutonic root word 'lag' , which means "something which lies fixed or evenly. " 🔴 CRITICAL The root is Teutonic (Germanic), not Latin or Greek. Don't confuse with 'lex' (Latin).

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 💚 TIP Mnemonic: Teutonic = T for "Tied down" / Fixed — law lies fixed like a foundation stone. Q2 MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 🏆 PYQ 💎 GEM According to John Austin, what is Law? According to Salmond? ஜான் ஆஸ்டின் மற்றும் சால்மண்ட் படி சட்டம் என்றால் என்ன? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Austin: "Law is the command of the sovereign. " Salmond: "Law is the collection of rules which the state recognizes and applies in the administration of Justice. " 🏆 PYQ ALERT Austin's definition appeared in TNPSC Group 4 (2019) and Group 2 (2016).

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Expect match-the-following or fill-in-blank format. 💎 GEM Austin's positivist view says law is law regardless of morality (command theory). Salmond's is more pragmatic — linking law to justice administration. This difference is a frequent comparison question. 🪤 TRAP Bodin said "Law is the command of the sovereign" — the textbook attributes this to Austin. Don't confuse Bodin (who said it in a different context) with Austin's formal definition. Q3 EASY Factual Gr.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 4/VAO What are the two kinds of laws based on what they regulate? ஒழுங்குபடுத்துவதன் அடிப்படையில் சட்டம் எத்தனை வகைப்படும்? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Physical law — regulates nature. Human law — regulates human life / human action. Q4 MEDIUM Conceptual Gr. 2 💎 GEM List any five purposes of Law as given in the textbook. பாடப்புத்தகத்தில் கொடுக்கப்பட்ட சட்டத்தின் நோக்கங்களை ஐந்து கூறுக. ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer 1. Protect basic human rights 2.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Promote fairness 3. Help resolve conflicts 4. Promote justice 5. Promote order and stability 6. Promote desirable social and economic behaviour 7. Represent the will of the majority 8. Protect the rights of minorities 💎 GEM TNPSC sometimes asks: "Which of the following is NOT a purpose of law? " — Be careful with invented options like "punish the innocent" or "promote inequality. " Q5 EASY Factual Gr. 4/VAO 🏆 PYQ Aristotle said: "If there is no law, even man will behave like a ___.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: " அரிஸ்டாட்டில் கூற்றில் சட்டம் இல்லாவிடில் மனிதன் எவ்வாறு நடந்துகொள்வான்? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer A beast . Aristotle pointed out that without law, even man will behave like a beast. 🏆 PYQ ALERT This quote has appeared as a fill-in-the-blank in TNPSC Group 4 Prelims (2018). Very commonly tested.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 3 — 8 types with key features Q6 MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 🏆 PYQ What are Private Laws? Who described the state's role in private laws? தனியார் சட்டங்கள் என்றால் என்ன? தனியார் சட்டங்களில் அரசின் பங்கை யார் விவரித்தார்? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Private laws determine the relationship between citizens and regulate relations among one another . In private laws, the parties concerned are private individuals, and the state acts as an impartial arbiter — described by Holland .

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 🔴 CRITICAL The scholar who described state's role in private law is Holland (not Salmond or Austin). Q7 MEDIUM Conceptual Gr. 2 🏆 PYQ 💎 GEM Distinguish between Private Law and Public Law. தனியார் சட்டம் மற்றும் பொது சட்டம் — வேறுபாடு கூறுக. ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Private Law: Regulates relationships between private individuals; state acts as impartial arbiter above both parties. Public Law: Determines the relation of citizens to the state; state is both an arbiter AND one of the parties interested.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 💎 GEM Key difference: In Public Law, the state is a party to the dispute; in Private Law, the state is only an arbiter . This is a common match-the-following or assertion-reason question. Q8 EASY Factual Gr. 4/VAO 🏆 PYQ What are Constitutional Laws? அரசியலமைப்பு சட்டங்கள் என்றால் என்ன? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Constitutional laws are the basic laws according to which the government in a state conducts itself . They define, interpret, and regulate the functions of the government.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Examples: Election of President, powers and functions of the Supreme Court, method of appointment of the Governor. Q9 EASY Factual Gr. 4/VAO What are Statute Laws? Who frames them? சட்டமன்றச் சட்டங்கள் என்றால் என்ன? யார் இயற்றுகின்றனர்? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Statute laws are laws framed by the Legislative Assembly or by the Parliament . In democratic governments, most laws are framed by Parliaments. Q10 MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 🏆 PYQ 💎 GEM What are Ordinances?

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Who issues them, and in what situation? அவசரச் சட்டங்கள் (Ordinances) என்றால் என்ன? யார், எந்தச் சூழ்நிலையில் பிறப்பிக்கிறார்? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Ordinances are issued by the executive branch of the government. They are temporary by nature and are issued by the President in the absence of Parliament , especially to face emergencies. 💎 GEM In India, Article 123 of the Constitution empowers the President to issue ordinances. A Governor can also issue ordinances under Article 213.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Ordinances must be approved by Parliament within 6 weeks of its reassembly. 🪤 TRAP Students confuse: Ordinances are temporary executive orders — not permanent laws. They lapse if not ratified by Parliament. Q11 MEDIUM Factual Gr. 4/VAO Common laws rest on _____ but are enforced by courts like statute law. In which country are common laws popular? வழக்காறு சட்டங்கள் (Common Laws) எதன் அடிப்படையில் அமைந்தவை? எந்த நாட்டில் பிரபலம்? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Common laws rest on customs but are enforced by courts like statute law.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Common laws are popular in England . Q12 MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 What are Administrative Laws? When do administrative courts intervene? நிர்வாக சட்டங்கள் என்றால் என்ன? நிர்வாக நீதிமன்றம் எப்போது தலையிடும்? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Administrative laws interpret and govern the office and responsibilities of government servants . When a dispute arises between a citizen and a government servant , the administrative court resolves the issue using administrative laws.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Q13 HARD Conceptual Gr. 1 💎 GEM What governs International Law in the absence of a formal legal framework? What examples of international law are given? சர்வதேச சட்டத்தை நிர்வகிப்பது எது? உதாரணங்கள் தருக. ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer International law is backed by public opinion and the rules of the UNO . It determines the conduct of civilized states in their relations with other states. Examples: Sea Law (governing international maritime borders) and Air Law (requiring aircraft to fly with permission of respective nations).

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 💎 GEM Unlike domestic law, international law has no central enforcement agency — it relies on consent, diplomatic pressure, and international bodies (UN, ICJ). This is why it is sometimes called "soft law.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 4 — Customs, Religion, Judiciary, Equity, Scientific Commentaries, Legislature Q14 MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 🏆 PYQ 💎 GEM Name the six sources of law. Which classical example is given for customs as a source of law in Tamil Nadu? சட்டத்தின் ஆறு ஆதாரங்களை கூறுக. தமிழ்நாட்டில் வழக்காற்றிலிருந்து சட்டம் உருவான உதாரணம் என்ன? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Six sources of law: Customs, Religion, Judicial decisions, Equity, Scientific commentaries, Legislature .

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Tamil Nadu example: The Bull-taming sport (Jallikattu) culture of Tamils resulted in the creation of the Jallikattu Law in 2017 — a classical example of custom becoming law. 🏆 PYQ ALERT Jallikattu Law 2017 — TNPSC asked about this in current affairs context in 2018. The connection to Article 29(1) (cultural rights) and Article 21 (right to life of animals) makes it a high-yield concept. 💎 GEM Jallikattu is conducted in Alanganallur, Madurai district , during the month of January with Pongal festival .

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: The 2014 Supreme Court judgment held animals as sentient beings with right to life under Article 21. Article 29(1) protects cultural rights. This contradiction led to the 2017 legislation. Q15 HARD Factual Gr. 1 What is the origin of Hindu law and Mohammedian law? What is the source of divine law for Christians? இந்து சட்டம் மற்றும் இஸ்லாமிய சட்டம் எங்கிருந்து உருவானது? கிறிஸ்தவர்களுக்கான தெய்வீக சட்டம் என்ன? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Hindu law: Traced in the Code of Manu .

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Mohammedian law: Traced in Shariat law . Christian: The Ten Commandments were the first law given by the Lord Almighty — considered the basis of law for Christians. 🔴 CRITICAL God is the ultimate source of Divine law. Divine law is revealed through man from God — this is frequently tested as a definition question. Q16 MEDIUM Conceptual Gr. 2 What is Equity as a source of law? What does it mean in English Law? சட்டத்தின் ஆதாரமாக நியாயசாஸ்திரம் (Equity) என்றால் என்ன?

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer When laws are ambiguous and do not fit , principles of equity are applied — cases are decided according to common sense and fairness . In English Law, equity means the body of rules originally enforced only by the Court of Chancery . Q17 HARD Factual Gr. 1 💎 GEM What are Scientific Commentaries as a source of law? Name the scholars cited by A. அறிவியல் விளக்கவுரைகள் (Scientific Commentaries) சட்டத்தின் ஆதாரமாக இருப்பது எப்படி?

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: அப்படுரை குறிப்பிடும் அறிஞர்கள் யாவர்? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Scientific commentaries on law are accepted as correct law over time — initially they are arguments, but their authority grows more authoritative than judicial decisions. Scholars cited by A. Appadurai: England: Coke and Blackstone America: Story and Kent India: Vijnaneswara and Apararka 💎 GEM These pairs are match-the-following bait: England→Coke & Blackstone | America→Story & Kent | India→Vijnaneswara & Apararka.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: The quote is from scholar A. Q18 MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 🏆 PYQ Name the three main sources from which the Indian Constitution was derived. இந்திய அரசியலமைப்பின் மூன்று முக்கிய ஆதாரங்கள் யாவை? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer i) Government of India Act, 1935 ii) UK Constitution iii) US Constitution 🏆 PYQ ALERT TNPSC Group 2 (2016, 2019) and Group 4 (2019) asked about sources of the Indian Constitution. Note: The textbook lists these three but India's Constitution drew from many more sources (Irish, Australian, etc.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: ) — the lesson specifically mentions only these three. 🪤 TRAP Students often write "French Constitution" or "Australian Constitution" — those are correct broadly but this textbook passage specifically names only GOI Act 1935, UK, and US. Answer as per lesson for TNPSC.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 6 — Distinctions and Relationships Q19 MEDIUM Conceptual Gr. 2 💎 GEM State three key distinctions between Law and Morality as given in the textbook. சட்டம் மற்றும் நெறிமுறை (Morality) வேறுபாட்டை மூன்று புள்ளிகளில் தருக. ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer 1. Laws are enforced by the state — disobedience leads to punishment. Morality can only lead to social boycott at most. Morality covers both internal and external affairs of man; Law covers only external affairs .

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Law punishes only those who violate by external actions ; morality deals with intentions too. 💎 GEM Plato's quote: "The best state is that which is nearest in virtue to the individual. " This links state, law, and morality — a bad state creates bad citizens. Used in Assertion-Reason questions. Q20 EASY Factual Gr. 4/VAO What is a Moral Law? Give examples of evils that moral laws aim to eliminate. தார்மீக சட்டம் என்றால் என்ன? உதாரணங்கள் தருக. ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer A Moral Law is a law framed with the purpose of eliminating evils such

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Laws based on morality remain permanent . Q21 MEDIUM Conceptual Gr. 2 What is Public Opinion? How is it related to law in a democracy? பொதுக் கருத்து என்றால் என்ன? ஜனநாயகத்தில் சட்டத்துடன் அதன் தொடர்பு என்ன? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Public opinion is the opinion held by people for the common welfare . In democracy, laws are framed based on public opinion support . People use peaceful demonstrations to express opinions. Common welfare and social progress are primary considerations of public opinion.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 2 — Introduction, City-state, Marshall's Analysis, India, Global Citizenship Q22 EASY Factual Gr. 4/VAO 🏆 PYQ How did Aristotle define Citizenship? அரிஸ்டாட்டில் குடியுரிமையை எவ்வாறு வரையறுத்தார்? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Aristotle defined citizenship as: "He who has the power to take part in the deliberative or judicial administration of any state. " He also defined citizenship with reference to birth place, family lineage, and culture .

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 🏆 PYQ ALERT Aristotle's definition of citizenship is a frequently tested quote in TNPSC exams. Fill-in-blank format: "He who has the power to take part in the _____ or _____ administration of any state. " Q23 MEDIUM Conceptual Gr. 2 💎 GEM What are the three divisions of Citizenship according to Marshall's Analysis? மார்ஷல்லின் பகுப்பாய்வின்படி குடியுரிமையின் மூன்று பிரிவுகள் யாவை? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer According to Marshall (liberal-social democrat), citizenship has three essential divisions: 1.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Civil — right to freedom 2. Political — right to participate in political decision-making 3. Social — standard of living and enjoying its fruits (social services) 💎 GEM Marshall linked citizenship to social class in the context of capitalism . He stressed social services because mere civil and political rights are not enough if economic inequalities are stark. This is a nuanced UPSC-level concept tested in Group 1. Q24 MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 🏆 PYQ In which Part of the Indian Constitution are provisions about Citizenship found?

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: How many ways can citizenship be acquired? இந்திய அரசியலமைப்பில் குடியுரிமை விதிகள் எந்த பகுதியில் உள்ளன? எத்தனை வழிகளில் குடியுரிமை பெறலாம்? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Citizenship provisions are found in Part II of the Indian Constitution. Citizenship can be acquired by: Birth, Descent, Registration, Naturalisation, or Inclusion of territory — five ways. 🏆 PYQ ALERT TNPSC Group 4 (2017, 2019) asked about ways to acquire Indian citizenship.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: The five methods are directly from the Citizenship Act, 1955. 🔴 CRITICAL Citizenship is in Part II (Articles 5–11) , NOT Part III (Fundamental Rights). Q25 MEDIUM Conceptual Gr. 2 Distinguish between National Citizenship and Global Citizenship. தேசிய குடியுரிமை மற்றும் உலகளாவிய குடியுரிமை வேறுபாடு கூறுக. ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer National Citizenship: Assumes the state can provide protection and rights needed to live; focused within national boundaries.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Global Citizenship: Deals with citizenship across national boundaries; requires cooperative action by people and governments of many states. It reminds us that states are interconnected and resolves socio-economic inequalities. Q26 HARD Factual Gr. 2 💎 GEM What does the Maintenance and Welfare of Parents and Senior Citizens Act, 2007 state about maintenance amount and penalties? பெற்றோர் மற்றும் மூத்த குடிமக்கள் பராமரிப்பு சட்டம் 2007 பராமரிப்புத் தொகை மற்றும் தண்டனை பற்றி என்ன கூறுகிறது?

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Key provisions of the Act: • Established the Maintenance Tribunal for speedy relief • Maximum maintenance: ₹10,000 per month • Maintenance includes: food, clothing, residence, medical attendance and treatment • Lawyers are not allowed to present cases before the Tribunal • Abandoning a senior citizen: Fine of ₹5,000 or imprisonment of 3 months or both • Condition: Person must be unable to maintain themselves from their own earnings 💎 GEM The Act was passed in 2007 .

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: State governments appoint a maintenance officer to represent elderly interests. The prohibition on lawyers in Tribunal proceedings ensures speedy, affordable justice. 🏆 PYQ ALERT The maximum maintenance amount (₹10,000), prohibition of lawyers in Tribunal, and fine for abandoning (₹5,000) are frequently tested figures.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 3 — Natural, Moral, Legal, Contractual, Human Rights Q27 EASY Factual Gr. Green defined Rights as ___________. What are the five features of Rights? கிரீன் உரிமையை எவ்வாறு வரையறுத்தார்? உரிமையின் ஐந்து பண்புகள் யாவை? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer T. Green: "Rights are powers necessary for the fulfilment of man's vocation as a moral being. " Features of Rights: 1. Important for societal development 2. Available to all irrespective of caste, creed, race, or gender 3.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Rights and duties are interrelated — "No duties, no rights" 4. Rights are justiciable 5. It is the duty of the state to protect rights Q28 MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 🏆 PYQ Name the five types of Rights. Which are the three sub-types of Legal Rights? உரிமையின் ஐந்து வகைகளையும் சட்ட உரிமையின் மூன்று உட்பிரிவுகளையும் கூறுக. ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Five types of Rights: Natural, Moral, Legal, Contractual, Human Rights Three sub-types of Legal Rights: i) Civil Rights — right to lead civilized social life; includes Right to life, liberty, equalit

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: This is a frequent trap — students wrongly say "Natural Rights. " Q29 MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 🏆 PYQ 💎 GEM What are Human Rights? When is International Human Rights Day celebrated? மனித உரிமைகள் என்றால் என்ன? சர்வதேச மனித உரிமை நாள் எப்போது கொண்டாடப்படுகிறது? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Human Rights are the rights of the highest order . They are protected and supported by international and national laws and treaties . International Human Rights Day is celebrated on December 10 every year.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 🏆 PYQ ALERT December 10 — International Human Rights Day. This date appeared in TNPSC Group 4 (2018) and SSC CGL (2022). Don't confuse with World Human Rights Day observed by different bodies on different dates. 💎 GEM December 10, 1948 is when the UN General Assembly adopted the Universal Declaration of Human Rights (UDHR). The date commemorates this landmark adoption. Q30 EASY Factual Gr. 4/VAO What are Contractual Rights? How do they originate?

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: ஒப்பந்த உரிமைகள் என்றால் என்ன? அவை எவ்வாறு உருவாகின்றன? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Contractual Rights originate from the practice of promise-keeping . They apply to particular individuals to whom contractual promises have been made .

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Part III of Constitution — Bill of Rights, Fundamental Rights, Writs, New Rights Q31 MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 🏆 PYQ 💎 GEM When was the US Bill of Rights adopted? Who proposed it? What influenced it? அமெரிக்க உரிமைகள் சாசனம் எப்போது ஏற்றுக்கொள்ளப்பட்டது? யார் முன்மொழிந்தார்? எதனால் பாதிக்கப்பட்டது? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Adopted: December 15, 1791 Proposed by: James Madison Influenced by: George Mason's 1776 Virginia Declaration of Rights and the 1689 English Bill of Rights It derives from: Magna Carta (1215) and the English Bill of Righ

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Knowing this chain impresses examiners in Group 1 mains. Q32 HARD Factual Gr. 2 🏆 PYQ How long did the Constituent Assembly take to frame the Indian Constitution? அரசியலமைப்பு சட்ட சபை இந்திய அரசியலமைப்பை வடிவமைக்க எவ்வளவு நேரம் எடுத்தது? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer The Constituent Assembly took 2 years, 11 months, and 20 days to frame the Constitution. 🏆 PYQ ALERT Exact duration is a TNPSC favourite: 2 years, 11 months, 20 days . This appeared in Group 4 (2016) and Group 2 (2018).

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 🪤 TRAP Students confuse "3 years" or "2 years 11 months" — the exact answer is 2 years, 11 months and 20 days. Don't round off! Q33 EASY Factual Gr. 4/VAO 🏆 PYQ Fundamental Rights are contained in which Part of the Indian Constitution? இந்திய அரசியலமைப்பின் எந்த பகுதியில் அடிப்படை உரிமைகள் உள்ளன? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Fundamental Rights are contained in Part III of the Indian Constitution. 🔴 CRITICAL Part I = Union and its territory | Part II = Citizenship | Part III = Fundamental Rights | Part IV = DPSP | Part IVA = Fundamental D

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Know your Parts! Q34 MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 🏆 PYQ What is Preventive Detention? Under what conditions can the state detain a person? தடுப்புக் காவல் என்றால் என்ன? எந்தச் சூழ்நிலையில் அரசு ஒருவரை தடுத்து வைக்கலாம்? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Preventive Detention is a check on the actions of miscreants. If the State feels that a person can be a threat to law and order, peace and security of the nation , it can arrest or detain that person without formal trial.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 🏆 PYQ ALERT Appeared in TNPSC Group 2 (2019): "Detention of a person to protect law and security of the nation is called? " Answer: Preventive Detention. (Not Habeas Corpus, not Mandamus! ) Q35 MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 What does the Right against Exploitation prohibit? What is the age limit for child labour? சுரண்டலுக்கு எதிரான உரிமை என்ன தடை செய்கிறது? குழந்தைத் தொழிலாளர் வயது வரம்பு என்ன? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer The Right against Exploitation prohibits: 1.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Trafficking in human beings 2. Beggary and forced labour 3. Employment of children below the age of 14 years in any factory or mine or any kind of hazardous labour. 🔴 CRITICAL Age limit = below 14 years . Students often write 15 or 16. The Constitution specifies 14. Q36 HARD Factual Gr. 2 🏆 PYQ 💎 GEM Which Article provides Constitutional Remedies at the Supreme Court? What five writs can be issued? உச்ச நீதிமன்றத்தில் அரசியலமைப்பு தீர்வுகளை எந்த சட்டப்பிரிவு வழங்குகிறது?

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: ஐந்து ரிட்கள் யாவை? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Article 32 — Supreme Court Article 226 — High Court Five writs: 1. Habeas Corpus — "Produce the body" (unlawful detention) 2. Mandamus — "We command" (compel public official to perform duty) 3. Certiorari — Transfer case from lower to higher court 4. Quo Warranto — Challenge to holding public office 5. Prohibition — Prevent lower court from exceeding jurisdiction 🏆 PYQ ALERT Article 32 appeared in TNPSC Group 2 Prelims (2016, 2019) and Group 4 (2018).

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Ambedkar called Article 32 "the heart and soul of the Constitution" — expect this quote in MCQs. 💎 GEM Mnemonic for 5 writs: HAMCQ → H abeas Corpus, A … wait — better: "Help Mankind — Certiorari, Quo, Prohibition" = Habeas corpus, Mandamus, Certiorari, Quo Warranto, Prohibition. Q37 MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 🏆 PYQ 💎 GEM What is the Right to Information Act, 2005? What is its purpose? தகவல் அறியும் உரிமைச் சட்டம் 2005 என்றால் என்ன? அதன் நோக்கம் என்ன?

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer The RTI Act of 2005 mandates that citizens can pose queries to the government about its working. It empowers citizens and initiates transparency and accountability . An informed citizen remains more vigilant on the instruments governing government functions. 💎 GEM RTI 2005 came into force on October 12, 2005. It replaced the Freedom of Information Act, 2002. The Central Information Commission (CIC) oversees RTI at the national level.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: This is a new right — not originally in the Constitution but added through legislation. Q38 HARD Conceptual Gr. 1 Under which Article is the Right to Privacy protected? How did the Supreme Court recognize it? தனியுரிமை உரிமை எந்த சட்டப்பிரிவின் கீழ் பாதுகாக்கப்படுகிறது? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Right to Privacy is protected as an intrinsic part of the Right to Life and Personal Liberty under Article 21 and as part of the freedoms guaranteed by Part III of the Constitution.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 🔴 CRITICAL In 2017, the Supreme Court in Justice K. Union of India declared Right to Privacy as a Fundamental Right under Article 21 — a landmark 9-judge bench ruling. [EXPANDED FROM KNOWLEDGE] Q39 MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 What is the status of Transgenders in India as per the Supreme Court? இந்தியாவில் திருநங்கைகளின் சட்டப்பூர்வ அந்தஸ்து என்ன? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Transgenders are addressed as the Third Gender . The Supreme Court has directed the Union and State Governments to grant legal recognition of their gender identity .

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Q40 EASY Factual Gr. 4/VAO What does Article 26 of the Indian Constitution allow? இந்திய அரசியலமைப்பின் 26-ஆம் பிரிவு என்ன அனுமதிக்கிறது? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Article 26 allows establishing and maintaining institutions related to religious affairs and charitable purposes , and owning and administering movable or immovable property in accordance with law. Q41 HARD Factual Gr. 2 What protection does Article 29(1) provide? How did it conflict with Jallikattu?

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: சட்டப்பிரிவு 29(1) என்ன பாதுகாப்பு அளிக்கிறது? ஜல்லிக்கட்டுடன் அதன் முரண்பாடு என்ன? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Article 29(1) is a Fundamental Right under Part III that protects educational and cultural rights . In Jallikattu: The 2014 Supreme Court judgment declared animals as sentient beings with a fundamental right to life under Article 21 . This clashed with Tamils' cultural right under Article 29(1) — leading to the Jallikattu Law 2017 compromise.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 🔴 CRITICAL Article 29(1) = Cultural & Educational Rights (Minorities). Article 21 = Right to Life. In Jallikattu, Article 21 was applied to animals — a landmark extension. Q42 EASY Factual Gr. 4/VAO What does the Right to Life and Personal Liberty (Article 21) guarantee regarding arrest? கைது செய்யப்பட்ட ஒருவருக்கு சட்டப்பிரிவு 21 என்ன உரிமைகளை வழங்குகிறது? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer • No person can be detained without informing the grounds of arrest • The arrested person has the liberty to consult and be defended by a lawyer of his choi

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 4 — Meaning, Authority, Features, Kinds Q43 MEDIUM Conceptual Gr. 2 What is Political Obligation? What are the three major aspects of Political Obligation? அரசியல் கடமை என்றால் என்ன? அதன் மூன்று முக்கிய அம்சங்கள் யாவை? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Political obligation binds a person to the performance of duties as mentioned in the Constitution . Three major aspects: 1. The identifiable authority to which political obligation is rendered 2. To what extent political obligation can be rendered 3.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: The basis of Political Obligation Q44 MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 💎 GEM What are the four features of Political Obligation? அரசியல் கடமையின் நான்கு சிறப்பியல்புகள் யாவை? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer 1. There prevails a source of political spirit and social service 2. Honesty and integrity are essential in the performance of public duty 3. There must prevail political legitimacy and effectiveness 4. Citizens have the responsibility of guarding their guardians 💎 GEM "Citizens have the responsibility of guarding their guardians" — this means citize

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: This is the essence of democratic accountability — a high-value concept in Group 1 mains answers. Q45 HARD Conceptual Gr. 1 Explain the difference between Political Obligation and Political Authority, with example. அரசியல் கடமை மற்றும் அரசியல் அதிகாரம் — வேறுபாடு உதாரணத்துடன் விளக்குக. ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer When the state has political authority , it has the right to compel non-compliers. Example: If the state has authority to levy taxes, it can compel citizens to pay taxes.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Political obligation goes beyond legal compulsion — even if the state does not enforce its authority, it remains the moral duty of citizens to comply with laws . 🔴 CRITICAL Political obligation = moral duty (even without enforcement). Political authority = legal right of state to compel. Moral duty survives even when enforcement is absent. Q46 MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 Name the four kinds of Political Obligations. Explain Moral Obligation. அரசியல் கடமையின் நான்கு வகைகளை பட்டியலிடுக.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: தார்மீக கடமையை விளக்குக. ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Four kinds of Political Obligations: i) Moral Obligation ii) Legal Obligation iii) Religious Obligation iv) Natural Obligation [EXPANDED FROM KNOWLEDGE — textbook mentions four kinds, only Moral is elaborated] Moral Obligation: Examples — being hospitable to guests, helping the poor, caring for parents in old age. These do NOT legally bind individuals. If you violate them, you cannot be legally punished.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Q47 HARD Application Gr. 2 🏆 PYQ 💎 GEM Match the following: Types of laws with their correct descriptions (Assertion-Reason style) சட்டத்தின் வகைகளை அவற்றின் விளக்கங்களுடன் பொருத்துக. ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Type of Law Key Feature Private Law Regulates relations between citizens; state = impartial arbiter Public Law Regulates citizen-state relation; state = party + arbiter Statute Law Framed by Legislature/Parliament Ordinance Temporary; issued by President when Parliament absent Common Law Based on customs; popular in England Admin

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Memorise especially the distinction between Private (Holland's arbiter) and Public (state as party) law. Q48 HARD Trap Gr. 2 🏆 PYQ A uniform civil code can be obtained through which provision? (Trap: Fundamental Rights or DPSP? ) சீரான குடிமை சட்டம் எந்த வழிமுறையில் பெறப்படலாம்? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer A uniform civil code can be obtained through Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSP) — Article 44 of the Constitution. 🏆 PYQ ALERT This was directly asked in TNPSC Group 2 (2019) MCQ: "A uniform civil code can be obtained through (

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: " Answer: (c) Directive Principles. 🪤 TRAP Students confuse DPSP with Fundamental Rights. DPSPs are non-justiciable (Article 36–51) and are guidelines to the state — Uniform Civil Code is Article 44, a DPSP. Q49 MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 What are Natural Citizens vs. Naturalised Citizens? இயற்கை குடிமக்கள் மற்றும் இயல்பாக்கப்பட்ட குடிமக்கள் — வேறுபாடு என்ன? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Natural Citizens: Citizens by virtue of their birth . Naturalised Citizens: Those who acquire citizenship (through registration, naturalisation, etc.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Q50 MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 Where did citizenship as a concept first gain prominence? What happened to it in feudal systems? குடியுரிமை கருத்து முதலில் எங்கு வலுப்பெற்றது? நிலப்பிரபுத்துவ காலத்தில் என்ன நடந்தது? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Citizenship was an important theme in ancient Greek and Roman Republics . It disappeared from the feudal systems but was later revived as a desirable aspect of civic humanism during the Renaissance . In ancient Athens, citizenship was considered only as participation of duties.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Q51 EASY Factual Gr. 4/VAO Who said "Law is a tyrant for criminal and a guardian for its citizen"? "சட்டம் குற்றவாளிக்கு அடக்குமுறையாளன், குடிமகனுக்கு பாதுகாவலன்" என்று கூறியவர் யார்? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer This statement is from the textbook introduction (not attributed to a specific scholar). It is used to introduce the dual nature of law — oppressive for criminals, protective for citizens. Q52 MEDIUM Application Gr. 2 💎 GEM Section 66A of the IT Act dealt with what subject?

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Was it upheld by the Supreme Court? IT சட்டத்தின் பிரிவு 66A எதனைப் பற்றியது? இது நீதிமன்றத்தால் நிலைநிறுத்தப்பட்டதா? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Section 66A of the Information Technology Act dealt with online speech and objectionable content online . It was challenged by Shreya Singhal (21-year-old from Delhi) in the Supreme Court. The SC struck it down in 2015 (Shreya Singhal vs. Union of India) as it violated the Right to Freedom of Expression (Article 19).

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 💎 GEM Shreya Singhal Case (2015): SC held Section 66A as unconstitutional for being vague and overbroad, violating Article 19(1)(a). This case is a landmark on digital free speech in India. [EXPANDED FROM KNOWLEDGE] Q53 HARD Conceptual Gr. 1 Explain the divine theory of political obligation. அரசியல் கடமையின் தெய்வீகக் கோட்பாட்டை விளக்குக. ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer The Divine Theory holds that political authority and obligation are derived from God .

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: According to this theory, rulers are God's representatives on earth and citizens are divinely obligated to obey them. The state's authority is sacred and divinely ordained. This theory was dominant in medieval Europe and is associated with the concept of "Divine Right of Kings. " 🔴 CRITICAL The Divine theory is contrasted with the Social Contract theory (Locke, Rousseau, Hobbes) in which authority is derived from the consent of the governed — not divine ordinance.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: [EXPANDED FROM KNOWLEDGE] Q54 MEDIUM Conceptual Gr. 2 💎 GEM What is Locke's Utilitarian justification of political obligation? லாக்கின் பயன்பாட்டு நியாயமாக்கல் என்ன? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Locke's utilitarian justification argues that political obligation exists because obeying the state provides the greatest good (utility) for citizens . Citizens obey laws not out of divine command but because the state protects their natural rights (life, liberty, property).

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: When the state fails to do so, citizens have the right to withdraw their obligations (right to revolution). [EXPANDED FROM KNOWLEDGE] 💎 GEM Locke's justification is the philosophical foundation of liberal democracy: Citizens obey the state because it benefits them. If the state becomes tyrannical, the social contract is broken and rebellion is justified. Q55 EASY Factual Gr. 4/VAO 🏆 PYQ Which right entitles an individual to voice their opinions publicly?

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: பொதுவில் கருத்துக்களை வெளிப்படுத்தும் உரிமை எது? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ Answer Right to Free Expression (Freedom of Speech and Expression — Article 19(1)(a)). 🏆 PYQ ALERT This appeared as MCQ Q. 1 in the textbook evaluation section: "Which right entitles an individual to voice their opinions publicly? " — (a) Right to liberty, (b) Right to education, (c) Right to free expression ✅, (d) Right to religion.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Law derives from the Teutonic root 'lag' meaning "fixed/evenly. " Austin: "Command of sovereign"; Salmond: "Rules recognized by the state for administration of justice. " Eight types of law: Private, Public, Constitutional, Statute, Ordinance, Common, Administrative, International — each serving a distinct function in governance. Six sources of law: Customs, Religion, Judicial decisions, Equity, Scientific commentaries, Legislature — Jallikattu Law 2017 is a textbook example of custom becoming law.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Indian Constitution's primary sources: Government of India Act 1935, UK Constitution, US Constitution — it is described as a classical example of borrowing best provisions. Citizenship in India (Part II) can be acquired in five ways: birth, descent, registration, naturalisation, inclusion of territory. Marshall identified three components: civil, political, social. Five types of Rights: Natural, Moral, Legal (Civil + Political + Economic), Contractual, Human.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Human Rights Day = December 10. Fundamental Rights are in Part III. Article 32 (SC) and Article 226 (HC) provide constitutional remedies. Five writs: Habeas Corpus, Mandamus, Certiorari, Quo Warranto, Prohibition. New rights — RTI Act 2005 (transparency/accountability), Right to Privacy (Article 21 — 2017 SC ruling), Rights of Transgenders (Third Gender — SC direction). Political Obligation = duty to comply with constitutional obligations. Even without state enforcement, it is the moral duty of citizens.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Moral obligations (caring for parents, hospitality) are not legally enforceable. Maintenance and Welfare of Parents Act 2007: Maximum maintenance ₹10,000/month; abandonment penalty ₹5,000 or 3 months imprisonment; lawyers barred from Tribunal.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Q: The detention of a person to protect the law and security of the nation is called __________. 🔥 90% Probability Appeared in TNPSC Group 2 (2019) Evaluation Q. 6; textbook-exact MCQ Q: Which Article of the Indian Constitution provides Constitutional Remedies to citizens at the Supreme Court? 🔥 88% Probability Article 32 — B. Ambedkar's "heart and soul" — repeated in Group 4 and Group 2 cycles Q: Name the five writs issued by the Supreme Court under Article 32.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 🔥 85% Probability Match-the-following or list format; all five writs with meanings tested in Group 2 mains Q: The Jallikattu Law was enacted in __________. The bull-taming sport is conducted at __________ in __________ district. ⚡ 78% Probability Current affairs + Constitutional law overlap. Articles 21 and 29(1) conflict makes it ideal for reasoning questions Q: What are the three essential divisions of Citizenship according to T. ⚡ 72% Probability Marshall's Civil-Political-Social framework is standard Group 2 / UPSC optional content; high G

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: # Question Answer 1 Root word of 'Law' Teutonic 'lag' (lies fixed/evenly) 2 Austin's definition of law Command of the sovereign 3 Salmond's definition of law Rules the state recognizes for administration of justice 4 Aristotle on law Without law, even man will behave like a beast 5 Physical vs Human law Physical = regulates nature; Human = regulates human life 6 Holland on Private Law State acts as impartial arbiter between private parties 7 Public Law Regulates citizen-state relation; state is both party and arbiter 8 Ordinances — who issues?

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Executive/President in absence of Parliament (temporary) 9 Common Law — country England; based on customs 10 Jallikattu Law year 2017; Alanganallur, Madurai; Articles 21 vs 29(1) 11 Hindu law origin Code of Manu 12 Mohammedian law origin Shariat law 13 Scientific commentaries — India scholars Vijnaneswara and Apararka (per A. Appadurai) 14 Sources of Indian Constitution GOI Act 1935, UK Constitution, US Constitution 15 Aristotle's definition of citizenship He who has power to take part in deliberative or judicial administration 16 Marshall's 3

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Green's definition of Rights Powers necessary for fulfilment of man's vocation as a moral being 24 Right to life, liberty and equality Civil Rights (Legal Rights) 25 Human Rights Day December 10 (every year) 26 Bill of Rights adopted December 15, 1791 (USA) 27 Bill of Rights proposed by James Madison 28 Constituent Assembly duration 2 years, 11 months, 20 days 29 Fundamental Rights — Part of Constitution Part III 30 Child labour age limit (Constitution) Below 14 years 31 Constitutional Remedies — Article Article 32 (SC), Article 226 (HC) 32 Fiv

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Third Gender (SC directive for legal recognition) 36 Article 26 allows Establish and maintain religious/charitable institutions 37 Article 29(1) protects Cultural and Educational Rights 38 Uniform Civil Code — Article Article 44 (DPSP) 39 Political Obligation definition Duty to perform constitutional obligations; moral duty even without enforcement 40 Moral Obligation Not legally binding; violation cannot be legally punished (e. , caring for parents)

chunks.text: 21 (animals) vs Art. 29(1) (culture) Excellent for conceptual/application questions — both Articles must be known Sources of Constitution GOI Act 1935 + UK + US (as per this textbook passage) Write only these three when asked "as per the textbook" Marshall's Citizenship Civil (freedom) + Political (participation) + Social (standard of living) Match-the-following: Civil = freedom, Political = voting, Social = services Citizenship — Part II 5 modes: Birth, Descent, Registration, Naturalisation, Inclusion of territory Mnemonic: BDRNI.

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.section: 🎯 Master Cheat Card — Concept | Key Detail | Exam Tip
chunks.text: Never write 6 modes — exactly 5! Human Rights Day December 10, annually Tricky: Don't confuse with World Human Rights Day on same date — same! Five Types of Rights Natural, Moral, Legal, Contractual, Human Legal Rights only are of 3 sub-types: Civil, Political, Economic Civil Rights (Legal) Right to life, liberty, equality TRAP: Students say "Natural Rights" — correct answer is CIVIL RIGHTS Bill of Rights USA Dec 15, 1791; James Madison; influenced by Virginia Declaration + English BoR Chain: Magna Carta(1215)→English BoR(1689)→Virginia(1776)→U

chunks.title: Q&A Crack Sheet
chunks.section: 🎯 Master Cheat Card — Concept | Key Detail | Exam Tip
chunks.text: Never round to 3 years. TNPSC is specific. Article 32 vs 226 Art. 226 = HC; 5 writs same for both Art. 32 = fundamental right itself (Ambedkar: "heart and soul") Preventive Detention State detains person if threat to law, order, peace, security Question trap: Not Habeas Corpus (that challenges detention) — it IS Preventive Detention Child Labour (Art. 24) Children below 14 years; factories, mines, hazardous labour Age = 14 (not 15 or 16). Constitutional provision, not ILO.

chunks.text: Senior Citizens Act 2007 ₹10,000/month max; ₹5,000 fine or 3 months for abandonment; no lawyers in Tribunal Three numbers to remember: 10000 / 5000 / 3 months RTI Act 2005; transparency and accountability; empowers citizens Year = 2005. Purpose = transparency & accountability (two words) Political Obligation Moral duty to follow Constitution; citizens must "guard their guardians" Moral obligation ≠ legal obligation. Visiting guests hospitably = moral, not legal.

TN Krishnambal Jagannathan Anti-Hindi Agitation 1937 Quit India Movement TN DPSP — Art. 36–51 Article 37, 38, 39, 39A Art. 40, 43A, 43B, 45 Maternity Benefit Act 1961 Muthulatxmi Reddy Scheme

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q1 Medium Factual TNPSC Gr. 4 Who served as the Mayor of Madras (Chennai) in 1939? 1939ஆம் ஆண்டு சென்னை மேயராக பணியாற்றியவர் யார்? Satyamurthy C Srinivasa Iyengar D G. Subramaniam ✅ Correct Answer B — S. Satyamurthy Served as Mayor of Madras in 1939 📌 Explanation S. Satyamurthy was a prominent Congress leader from Tamil Nadu who served as Mayor of Madras in 1939. He is most famously known as the political guru (mentor) of Karmaveerar Kamarajar .

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Satyamurthy was a brilliant orator and nationalist who deeply influenced Kamarajar's political career. 💎 Live Gem — Political Guru Chain The teacher expanded this into a memorable "Political Guru Chain" — a high-frequency exam pattern: Kamarajar's Political Guru → S. Satyamurthy Mahatma Gandhi's Political Guru → Gopal Krishna Gokhale Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose's Political Guru → C. Das (Chitta Ranjan Das) Netaji's Spiritual/Other Guru mentioned by students → Muthurama­lingam Thevar (noted by students) Note: The teacher confirmed Netaji's polit

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Das — NOT Gokhale. This is a frequent TRAP in exams. 🏆 PYQ Alert Political Guru relationships have been directly asked in TNPSC Group 2A (2018) and Group 4 (2019) exams. The Kamarajar–Satyamurthy link is a top-repeat question.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q2 Hard Factual TNPSC Gr. 2 Who donated her jewels in support of the Neel Statue (James Neill) removal agitation? நீல் சிலை சத்தியாகிரகத்தை ஆதரிப்பதாக தனது நகைகளை வழங்கியவர்? A Manju Vasini B Padmavathi Ammal (Padmanabha Iyer's wife) C Padmavathi Asher D Swarnam Ammal ✅ Correct Answer B — Padmavathi Ammal Donated her jewels to fund the Neel Statue Agitation (1927) 📌 Explanation — Full Story Who was James Neill? A British military officer associated with the 1857 Revolt.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: During the Kanpur (Cawnpore) massacre — where many British soldiers were killed — Neill retaliated by massacring thousands of innocent Indians, including children. He is thus known as the perpetrator of the "Kanpur Massacre. " The Neill Statue: In 1920s, a statue of James Neill was erected on Mount Road (now Anna Salai), Chennai — an act Indians considered insulting, as the man had slaughtered their people. The Agitation (1927): A person named Somayajulu from Tirunelveli led the Neel Statue Removal Agitation.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Padmavathi Ammal donated her jewellery to fund this protest movement. Outcome: After Rajaji became CM in 1937, the statue was removed and placed in the Madras Museum — where it can be visited today. 🏆 PYQ Alert This question was directly asked in TNPSC Group 2 exam. The "jewels donated for agitation" angle is a classic trick question. Padmavathi Ammal is the answer — not Manjuvasini. 💎 Live Gem — James Neill's History The teacher connected the Neill Statue agitation to the 1857 Great Revolt — linking it to the Kanpur Massacre perpetrated by J

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: This historical context helps remember WHY the statue was controversial. The statue remained until 1937 when Rajaji's Congress government removed it.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q3 Hard Factual TNPSC Gr. 2 Who was the first woman representative of the All India Congress Party from Tamil Nadu? அகில இந்திய காங்கிரஸ் கட்சியின் தமிழ் மாநிலத்தின் முதல் பெண் பிரதிநிதி யார்? A Krishnambal Jagannathan B S. Sundarambal C Nagammaiyar (Periyar's first wife) D Muthulakshmi Reddy ✅ Correct Answer A — Nagammaiyar First woman member of the Tamil Nadu Congress Committee (TNCC) 📌 Explanation Nagammaiyar was the first wife of Periyar (E.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: She was elected as the first woman representative of the All India Congress Party from Tamil Nadu. She participated actively in all of Periyar's freedom struggle movements. Notable connection: In 1921, when Gandhi announced the Temperance Movement (Kallu Kadai Mariyaal — Liquor Shop Picketing), a British journalist asked Gandhi when he would stop this movement. Gandhi famously replied: "The power to stop this movement is not in my hands; it is in the hands of two women from Erode.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: " — referring to Nagammaiyar and Periyar's sister. 💎 Live Gem — Gandhi's Famous Quote about Nagammaiyar Gandhi's statement about "two women from Erode" having the power to stop the Temperance Movement is a deeply significant fact. The two women were: (1) Nagammaiyar (Periyar's first wife) and (2) Periyar's younger sister . This shows the immense power these women wielded in the freedom struggle — a fact rarely discussed in standard textbooks but frequently asked in TNPSC.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: ⚠️ Trap Note Students commonly confuse this with Krishnambal Jagannathan or Muthulakshmi Reddy. The keyword is "First Woman Representative of TNCC (Tamil Nadu Congress Committee)" = Nagammaiyar.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q4 Medium Application TNPSC Gr. 1 Who is described in the following statements? (i) She launched the "Land for Tillers' Freedom" (LAFTI) programme in 1981. (ii) She presided over the Coral Jubilee (75th anniversary) of the Vedaranyam Salt March in 2006. இவர் 1981ல் நிலமற்றவர்களுக்கு நிலம் திட்டத்தை துவங்கினார். இவர் 2006ல் வேதாரணம் உப்பு சத்தியாகிரகத்தின் பவள விழாவிற்கு தலைமை தாங்கினார். ✅ Correct Answer Krishnambal Jagannathan 📌 Explanation Krishnambal Jagannathan was a prominent freedom fighter and social reformer from Tamil Nadu.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Vedaranyam Salt March (1930): Rajaji (C. Rajagopalachari) led approximately 100 people on a march from Thiruchirapalli to Vedaranyam (April 13 to April 28, 1930) to defy the British salt tax — parallel to Gandhi's Dandi March. Krishnambal Jagannathan's Role: She participated in this Salt Satyagraha and was also involved in the Individual Satyagraha. She notably participated in the Quit India Movement (1942). Pavazhavizha = Coral Jubilee: Pavazhavizha = 75 years anniversary (Coral Jubilee).

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: In 2006, the 75th year of the Vedaranyam Salt March was celebrated, and Krishnambal Jagannathan presided over it. 💎 Live Gem — Jubilee Terminology for Exams Important Jubilee names frequently tested: • Silver Jubilee = 25 years • Golden Jubilee = 50 years • Diamond Jubilee = 60 years • Platinum Jubilee = 70 years • Coral Jubilee (Pavazhavizha) = 75 years The teacher specifically highlighted "Pavazhavizha = 75 years" as an unusual fact that appears in TNPSC exams.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q5 Medium Factual + Assertion-Reason TNPSC Gr. Ramasamy (Periyar) organised an Anti-Hindi Conference in Salem. Reason: When the first Congress ministry came to power, Rajaji abolished the Sales Tax. கூற்று: பெரியார் சேலத்தில் இந்தி எதிர்ப்பு மாநாட்டை நடத்தினார். காரணம்: காங்கிரஸின் முதல் அமைச்சரவையின் போது ராஜாஜி விற்பனை வரியை ரத்து செய்தார். A Both Assertion and Reason are correct, and Reason explains Assertion B Both Assertion and Reason are correct, but Reason does NOT explain Assertion C Assertion is correct but Reason is WRONG D Assertion

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: When Rajaji became CM in 1937 and made Hindi compulsory in 125 schools across Madras, Periyar organised an Anti-Hindi conference in Salem and led protests. Periyar marched students from Salem to Madras and protested in front of Iraiyavar School. He was arrested and later imprisoned in Vellore. Reason (WRONG): Rajaji did NOT abolish the Sales Tax. What actually happened: When Rajaji became CM in 1937 (after Congress's first election win), he banned liquor (alcohol) .

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: To compensate for the revenue lost from the liquor ban, he INTRODUCED the Sales Tax (Virpanai Vari) — not abolished it. 🚨 Critical — Common Mistake Rajaji INTRODUCED Sales Tax (not abolished it) to compensate for revenue lost from the liquor ban. Many students get this reversed. Assertion ✅ + Reason ❌ = Answer C. 💎 Live Gem — Why Congress Avoided Elections from 1920 to 1937 The teacher posed a bonus question: "Why did the Congress Party not contest elections from 1920 to 1937 (17 years)?

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: " — This was part of Gandhi's Non-Cooperation Movement strategy where Congress boycotted British institutions including legislatures. Congress first contested elections in 1937 under the Government of India Act, 1935. This context helps answer related questions about Rajaji becoming the first Congress CM.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q6 Hard Factual TNPSC Gr. 1 During the Quit India Movement (1942), where did the clash between Congress volunteers and the British Army take place in Tamil Nadu? வெள்ளையனை வெளியேற்றும் போராட்டத்தின் போது காங்கிரஸ் தொண்டர்களுக்கும் ராணுவத்துக்கும் இடையே மோதல் நடைபெற்ற இடம் எது? A Erode B Madras (Chennai) C Salem D Madurai ✅ Correct Answer D — Madurai The clash between Congress volunteers and the British Army occurred in Madurai during the Quit India Movement (1942) 📌 Explanation The Quit India Movement was launched on August 8, 1942 in Bombay (

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Kamarajar represented Tamil Nadu at the Bombay session. On his way back, he received information at Arakkona­m Railway Station that Congress leaders were being arrested. He escaped from there, went underground, mobilised people, and later voluntarily surrendered for arrest. During this movement, the major clash between Congress volunteers and the British Army took place in Madurai . 💎 Live Gem — Kamarajar's Escape Story The Full Kamarajar–Arakkona­m Story: • Gandhi held the Quit India session in Bombay, Aug 8, 1942 • Each state sent only selec

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q7 Hard Trick/Trap — Incorrect Statement TNPSC Gr. 4 Which of the following is INCORRECT about the Quit India Movement? (i) The "Quit India" resolution was passed on August 8, 1943. (ii) Kamarajar went underground and organised the public, then came out for arrest. வெள்ளையனை வெளியேற்றும் தீர்மானம் ஆகஸ்ட் 8 1943ல் நிறைவேற்றப்பட்டது. காமராஜர் தலைமறைவாகி மக்களை திரட்டி பணியை மேற்கொண்டார். ✅ Incorrect Statement (Answer = A = Statement I) Statement I is WRONG — The resolution was passed on Aug 8, 1942 (NOT 1943) Statement II is CORRECT — Kamarajar d

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Changing the year by one is a classic TNPSC trick. Always verify: 1942 = Quit India .

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q8 Medium Factual TNPSC Gr. 4 Who was enrolled as a member of Pala Mandiri (Bala Mandir) in 1949 under the encouragement of Kamarajar? 1949ல் காமராஜரின் ஊக்கத்துடன் பாலமந்திரில் மஞ்சுவாசினி பதிவு செய்தார் — Kamarajar was the guru of whom? ✅ Correct Answer Manjuvasini The student (chela) of Satyamurthy (the guru covered in Q1) — enrolled in 1949 under Kamarajar's encouragement 📌 Explanation This question connects Q1 and Q8 beautifully: • Satyamurthy = Political Guru of Kamarajar (Q1) • Kamarajar = Encouraged and mentored Manjuvasini • Manjuvasi

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: 📜 DPSP — Directive Principles of State Policy Q9 – Q14 💡 DPSP Quick Intro DPSP = Directive Principles of State Policy ( அரசுக்கு வழிகாட்டும் நெறிமுறைகள் ) • Located in Part IV of the Indian Constitution • Articles 36 to 51 • Borrowed from the Irish Constitution • NON-JUSTICIABLE — Courts cannot enforce these • These are ADVISORY to the government (not binding like Fundamental Rights) • Fundamental Rights = Individual rights | DPSP = State/Government guidelines

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q9 Medium Conceptual TNPSC Gr. 4 SSC Choose the CORRECT statement regarding Article 37 of the Indian Constitution. (i) DPSP does NOT control/bind the government. (ii) Courts CAN question whether DPSP provisions are implemented. அரசு நெறிமுறை கோட்பாடுகள் அரசாங்கத்தை கட்டுப்படுத்தாது. நடைமுறைப்படுத்துவது குறித்து நீதிமன்றங்கள் கேள்வி எழுப்ப முடியும். ✅ Correct Answer Only Statement (i) is correct DPSP does NOT bind the government. Courts CANNOT question non-implementation of DPSP.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: 📌 Explanation — Article 37 Art. 37 Article 37 itself states the nature of DPSP: • DPSP are NOT enforceable in any court • Courts CANNOT compel the government to implement DPSP • DPSP is like advice to the government — if followed, good; if not, courts cannot intervene • Analogy used by teacher: "Like a parent advising a child — 'if you study, you'll get a government job' — but no one can force you to follow the advice. " Key Rule: Fundamental Rights = Justiciable (Courts enforce them) | DPSP = Non-Justiciable (Courts cannot enforce them).

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Article 37 explicitly states this distinction. 💎 Live Gem — Small vs Capital Letters in Constitution Critical insight about Constitutional Articles: • Original Constitution (Jan 26, 1950) used small letters : 1a, 1b, 1c • Capital letters (21A, 39A, etc. ) = Added LATER by Constitutional Amendment So: Article 21A (Right to Education for 6–14 yr olds) was NOT in the original Constitution — it was added by the 86th Constitutional Amendment, 2002 . Capital letter = amendment-added article.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q10 Easy Factual TNPSC Gr. 4 SSC Which Article states that the government should make efforts to improve the well-being of people and maintain social order? மக்களின் நல்வாழ்வை மேம்படுத்த அரசாங்கம் முயற்சிகளை ஏற்படுத்தி சமூக ஒழுங்கை பாதுகாக்க வேண்டும் என கூறுகிறது? A Article 39 B Article 39A C Article 38 D Article 37 ✅ Correct Answer C — Article 38 📌 Memory Shortcut from Teacher Teacher's Shortcut: "At what age does someone typically place an 'order'?

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: " → Article 38 = Social Order Art. 38 The State shall strive to promote the welfare of the people by securing and protecting a social order in which justice — social, economic, and political — shall inform all institutions of national life. 🏆 PYQ Alert Article 38 (Social Order/Welfare) has been asked in TNPSC Group 4 and VAO exams multiple times. The keyword "social order" = Art.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q11 Medium Factual TNPSC Gr. 4 SSC What does Article 39A of the Indian Constitution provide? ஆர்டிகிள் 39A என்ன கூறுகிறது? ✅ Correct Answer Free Legal Aid to the Poor Article 39A — The State shall make provisions for securing equal justice and free legal aid to the poor and weaker sections. 39 Equal pay for equal work + Right to adequate means of livelihood Art. 39A Free Legal Aid to the Poor and Weaker Sections (Capital A = Amendment-added)

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q12 Hard Trick/Trap — Incorrect Statement TNPSC Gr. 4 Choose the CORRECT statement about Article 40: (i) The government should establish Panchayati Raj institutions. (ii) The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992 implemented Panchayati Raj. ஆர்டிகிள் 40 — பஞ்சாயத்துராஜ் அமைப்புகளை ஏற்படுத்த வேண்டும். 73வது அரசியலமைப்பு திருத்த சட்டம் 1993ம் ஆண்டு நடைமுறைப்படுத்தப்பட்டது. ✅ Correct Answer — Statement I is correct, Statement II has an error Statement I ✅ | Statement II ❌ (Year is wrong) The 73rd Amendment was PASSED in 1992 but came into FORCE

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: 40 / 73rd Amendment 73rd Constitutional Amendment: • Passed by Parliament: 1992 • Came into Force: April 24, 1993 Exam options often say "1993" for the amendment — if the question asks when it was "enacted/passed," answer is 1992. If "when it came into force/implemented," answer is 1993. Read the question carefully! 💎 Live Gem — Art. 40 is a Perennial TNPSC Favourite The teacher explicitly said: "Article 40 comes in TNPSC exams every year — like Pongal, Diwali, Christmas, Eid — it comes without fail!

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: " This is the Panchayati Raj Article — extremely high frequency in TNPSC exams. Combined with: • Art. 40 = DPSP for Panchayats • 73rd Amendment 1992/1993 = Made Panchayati Raj a constitutional reality • National Panchayati Raj Day = April 24 (commemorating the date it came into force)

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q13 Medium Factual TNPSC Gr. 4 Which Article says the government must ensure workers' participation in the management of factories? எந்த விதி தொழிற்சாலை நிர்வாகத்தில் தொழிலாளர்களுக்கு பங்கு கொள்ள வழிவகை செய்ய வேண்டும் என கூறுகிறது? A Article 43 B Article 43A C Article 45 D Article 44 ✅ Correct Answer B — Article 43A Workers' Participation in Management of Industries Art. 43 Living Wage and decent working conditions for workers (Gandhi's dream) Art.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: 43A Workers' Participation in Management of Factories (Capital A = Amendment) Art. 43B Promotion of Co-operative Societies (Cooperative Societies = Gandhi's dream)

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q14 Easy Factual TNPSC Gr. 2 SSC Which Article directs the State to provide free and compulsory education to children between 6 and 14 years? எந்த விதி ஆறு முதல் 14 வயது வரை உள்ள குழந்தைகளுக்கு இலவச கட்டாய கல்வி வழங்க வேண்டும் என கூறுகிறது? A Article 44 B Article 45 C Article 46 D Article 47 ✅ Correct Answer B — Article 45 DPSP directive for free and compulsory education for children (6–14 years) ⚠️ Trap Note — Art. 21A Article 45 (DPSP) = Directive to provide free education to children 6–14 — Non-Justiciable Article 21A (Fundamental Right) = R

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: 21A "Which Article directs the State to provide free education (DPSP)? 45 🏆 PYQ Alert Both Art. 21A regarding education have been asked in TNPSC multiple times. 45 vs 21A distinction is a classic TNPSC trap!

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q15 Medium Factual + Application TNPSC Gr. 4 In which year was the Maternity Benefit Act enacted? பேர்கால உதவி சட்டம் எந்த ஆண்டு இயற்றப்பட்டது? A 1960 B 1961 C 1962 D 1963 ✅ Correct Answer B — 1961 Maternity Benefit Act, 1961 — provides maternity leave with pay 📌 Explanation + Schemes Maternity Benefit Act, 1961: • Applies to all women workers in government and private sector • Provides 12 months paid maternity leave (approximately 1 year) Muthulakshmi Reddy Maternity Scheme (Tamil Nadu Govt): Under the Muthulakshmi Reddy Scheme, pregnant wome

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: 41's principle of public assistance for those who cannot earn • TVK leader Vijay's promise of ₹4,000/month for unemployed youth — also connects to Art. 41's DPSP directive The teacher warned: Always study ALL government schemes regardless of which party introduced them — TNPSC asks about Jayalalithaa schemes even when DMK was ruling.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Satyamurthy = Mayor of Madras (1939) + Political Guru of Kamarajar — a top PYQ fact. Political Guru chain: Gokhale→Gandhi | C. Das→Netaji | Satyamurthy→Kamarajar — all three are exam-ready. Padmavathi Ammal donated jewels for the Neel Statue Agitation (1927). James Neill was responsible for the Kanpur Massacre (1857). Statue removed by Rajaji in 1937. Nagammaiyar (Periyar's first wife) = First Woman Representative of Tamil Nadu Congress Committee.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Gandhi called two Erode women pivotal to the Temperance Movement. Krishnambal Jagannathan: LAFTI (1981) + presided over Coral Jubilee (75th year) of Vedaranyam Salt March (2006). She participated in Quit India Movement (1942). Anti-Hindi Agitation (1937): Periyar + Bharathidasan + Maraimalai Adigal. He was a prominent Congress leader and orator from Tamil Nadu. 💎 LIVE GEM — Political Guru Chain The teacher drew a direct link between political gurus: Kamarajar's political guru = S.

Q 02 HARD Factual TNPSC Gr. 1 Who was registered / enrolled at Bala Mandir in 1949 under the encouragement of Kamarajar? Options: Vanchivadani / Padmabai Ammal / Padmathi / Shoornath Ammal 1949ல் பால மந்திரில் காமராஜரின் ஊக்கத்தின் கீழ் யா

chunks.text: ✅ CORRECT ANSWER Option D — Vanchivadani (வஞ்சுவாசினி) 📌 EXPLANATION Vanchivadani was S. Satyamurthi's disciple (shishya). While Q1 asked about the guru (Satyamurthi), Q2 asks about the disciple — this is a direct conceptual follow-on. In 1949, under Kamarajar's encouragement, Vanchivadani enrolled at Bala Mandir to do social work for the freedom movement. 🪤 TRAP — Guru vs Disciple Questions Q1 and Q2 are linked: Q1 = Guru (Satyamurthi), Q2 = Disciple (Vanchivadani).

chunks.text: Exam setters place these back-to-back to test if you confuse the roles. Always note the year (1949) and the institution (Bala Mandir). Q 03 HARD Factual TNPSC Gr. 1 Who donated her jewellery to support the Neel Statue (Neil Statue) Removal Satyagraha? Options: Vanchivadani / Padmabai Ammal / Padmathi Ashir / Sornath Ammal நீல் சிலை சத்யாரகத்தை ஆதரிப்பதாக தனது நகைகளை வழங்கியவர் யார்? ✅ CORRECT ANSWER Padmabai Ammal (பத்மாவாசினி / பத்மாபாய் அம்மாள்) — Option B 🏆 PYQ ALERT — TNPSC Group 2 Previous Paper This exact question was asked in a previous

chunks.title: High
chunks.section: Tamil Nadu Freedom Struggle — Key Personalities TN HISTORY
chunks.text: The answer is Padmabai Ammal who donated her jewellery to fund the Neil Statue Removal agitation (1927). 💎 LIVE GEM — James Neill Story (Background) Who was James Neill? A British officer stationed in Madras. During the 1857 Great Revolt (Kanpur Massacre), he massacred thousands of innocent Indians and children at Kanpur as revenge. Later (around 1920), a statue of James Neill was erected on Mount Road (now Anna Salai), Chennai. Why was the statue controversial?

chunks.title: High
chunks.section: Tamil Nadu Freedom Struggle — Key Personalities TN HISTORY
chunks.text: Indians were outraged that a man who killed Indian civilians had a statue on Indian soil. A protest was led by Somayajulu (from Tirunelveli) to remove the statue. Padmabai Ammal donated her jewellery to fund this agitation. When was it finally removed? In 1937 , after Rajaji became Chief Minister, the statue was removed and placed in the Madras Museum — where it can still be seen today. Q 04 HARD Factual TNPSC Gr. 1 Who was the FIRST WOMAN REPRESENTATIVE of the All India Congress Party from Tamil Nadu?

She was elected as the first woman representative of the Tamil Nadu Congress Committee (TNCC). chunks.text: She also launched the "Land for Tillers' Freedom" (LAFTI) scheme in 1981 for landless labourers. 💎 LIVE GEM — Vedaranyam March Details Rajaji's Salt March (Tamil Nadu): • Started: 13 April 1930 • Ended: 28 April 1930 • Route: Tiruchirappalli → Vedaranyam • Approximately 100 marchers participated • Krishnambal Jagannathan also participated in the Quit India Movement (1942) — she worked actively in the "Vellaiyanai Veliyeru" (Quit India) agitation.

The key identifier is the 1981 LAFTI scheme combined with the 2006 Vedaranyam jubilee.

chunks.text: Q 06 MEDIUM Conceptual / Assertion-Reason TNPSC Gr. 2 Statement (Assertion): Periyar held an Anti-Hindi Conference in Salem. Reason: During the first Congress ministry (Rajaji), the Sales Tax was abolished. Choose the correct answer. தந்தை பெரியார் சேலத்தில் இந்தி எதிர்ப்பு மாநாட்டை நடத்தினார். காங்கிரஸின் முதல் அமைச்சரவையின் போது ராஜாஜி விற்பனை வரியை ரத்து செய்தார். ✅ CORRECT ANSWER Assertion is CORRECT . Reason is WRONG . → Answer: Option C (A is true, R is false) 💎 LIVE GEM — 1937 Congress & Rajaji's Actions Why 1937 was the FIRST time Cong

chunks.title: High
chunks.section: Anti-Hindi Agitation, Quit India Movement & Key Facts TN HISTORY
chunks.text: In 1937 they first contested and won; Rajaji became CM. What Rajaji actually did as CM: 1. He BANNED liquor (alcohol prohibition) in Madras. To compensate for lost revenue, he INTRODUCED the Sales Tax. He did NOT abolish it — he INTRODUCED it. Hence the Reason in the question is FALSE. Periyar's Anti-Hindi Agitation (1937): Rajaji made Hindi compulsory in 125 Madras schools. Periyar, along with Bharathidasan and Maraimalai Adigal, led protests from Salem, marched students to Madras, protested at Irayananar School.

chunks.text: Periyar was arrested and sent to Vellore prison. 🚨 CRITICAL — Rajaji's Policy: Introduced vs Abolished Rajaji INTRODUCED Sales Tax (to replace liquor revenue) — he did NOT abolish it. This is a frequent exam trap. chunks.text: ✅ CORRECT ANSWER Madurai (மதுரை) Q 08 HARD Trick / Incorrect Statement TNPSC Gr. 1 Choose the INCORRECT statement: A. The Quit India Resolution was passed on 8 August 1943. Kamarajar went underground and rallied the people for the Quit India cause. தவறான கூற்றுகளை தேர்வு செய்க: ஆகஸ்ட் 8, 1943ஆம் ஆண்டு வெள்ளையனை வெளியேறு தீர்மானம் நிறைவேற்றப்பட்டது. ✅ CORRECT ANSWER (= the INCORRECT statement) Statement A is INCORRECT → Correct answer = Option A only 🚨 CRITICAL — Year Error: 1942 not 1943 The Quit India Resolution was passed on 8 August 1942 (N

chunks.title: High
chunks.section: Anti-Hindi Agitation, Quit India Movement & Key Facts TN HISTORY
chunks.text: Statement A says 1943 — that is the error. Statement B about Kamarajar going underground is CORRECT. 💎 LIVE GEM — Kamarajar's Escape at Arkonam After the Bombay session (August 8, 1942), the British arrested Congress leaders at every railway station. Kamarajar was on the train from Bombay back to Madras. At Arkonam (Arakkonam) railway station , he received a tip that police were waiting at Chennai Central. He escaped at Arkonam, went underground, mobilised people for the Quit India cause with Gandhi's instructions, and then voluntarily surrend

chunks.title: High
chunks.section: Directive Principles of State Policy — Introduction & Article 37 POLITY · DPSP
chunks.text: Q 09 EASY Conceptual TNPSC Gr. 2/2A Choose the correct statement about Article 37 of the Indian Constitution (DPSP): A. DPSP does NOT control the government; courts cannot question non-implementation. DPSP controls the government; courts can question it. அரசு நெறிமுறை கோட்பாடுகள் அரசாங்கத்தை கட்டுப்படுத்தாது — எந்த நீதிமன்றமும் கேள்வி எழுப்ப முடியாது. ✅ CORRECT ANSWER Option A — DPSP does NOT control the government; courts CANNOT question non-implementation.

chunks.title: High
chunks.section: Directive Principles of State Policy — Introduction & Article 37 POLITY · DPSP
chunks.text: 💎 LIVE GEM — DPSP vs Fundamental Rights (Key Distinction) Fundamental Rights = Given to every INDIVIDUAL citizen. If violated, any individual can go to court. DPSP = Advisory instructions given to the GOVERNMENT by the Constitution. They are like a policy wish-list — "If the government follows these, the nation will be better. " Teacher's analogy: "It's like advice — 'Study properly and you can become a government officer. ' Your dad says it. If you don't follow it, he can't drag you to court!

chunks.title: High
chunks.section: Directive Principles of State Policy — Introduction & Article 37 POLITY · DPSP
chunks.text: " DPSP Location: Part IV, Articles 36–51 of the Indian Constitution. Nature: Non-justiciable (not enforceable by courts).

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q 10 MEDIUM Factual TNPSC Gr. 2/2A Which Article says the government should make efforts to improve the well-being of the people and maintain social order? Options: 39 / 39A / 38 / 37 ✅ CORRECT ANSWER Article 38 💡 TIP — Memory Trick from Teacher "Order Poduvar — 38 vayasula! " Think of someone placing an ORDER at age 38. Article 38 = Social ORDER / Well-being of people. Teacher's mnemonic: "At 38 years old, he placed his order" → Article 38 = Social Order maintenance.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: 💎 LIVE GEM — Article 39A Article 39A = The State shall provide free legal aid to poor/weaker sections to ensure they can access justice. This is a Directive Principle that resulted in the Legal Services Authorities Act. Q 11 MEDIUM Factual / Year-Based Trap TNPSC Gr. 4/VAO Choose the correct statement about Article 40 (Panchayati Raj): A. Government should establish Panchayati Raj systems. 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act of 1993 operationalised Panchayati Raj.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: ஆர்டிகள் 40 — பஞ்சாயத்துராஜ் அமைப்புகளை ஏற்படுத்த முயற்சிகள் மேற்கொள்ள வேண்டும். 73வது சட்டத்திருத்தம் 1993ல் நடைமுறைப்படுத்தப்பட்டது. ✅ CORRECT ANSWER Statement A = CORRECT . Statement B = INCORRECT (year is wrong) . 🚨 CRITICAL — 73rd Amendment: 1992 vs 1993 The 73rd Constitutional Amendment ACT was passed in 1992 . It came into force/effect in 1993 . Questions often trap students by asking about the year. "Passed" = 1992. "Operationalised/Implemented" = 1993.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: The statement in this question says "1993 was enacted" — this is partially misleading; the Act was 1992 but implemented 1993. Know BOTH years. 🏆 PYQ ALERT Article 40 + Panchayati Raj + 73rd Amendment is asked repeatedly in TNPSC exams every year. The teacher described it as "coming like Pongal every year. 40 → 73rd Amendment → 1992/1993. Q 12 MEDIUM Factual TNPSC Gr. 2/2A Which Article states that workers should be given the right to participate in the management of factories/industries?

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Options: 43 / 45 (etc. ) ✅ CORRECT ANSWER Article 43A 💡 TIP — Article 43A vs 43B Article 43A = Workers' participation in management of factories. Article 43B = Promotion of Co-operative Societies (Cooperative Societies) — Gandhi's dream. This was added later by amendment. Do NOT confuse these two sub-articles. Q 13 MEDIUM Factual TNPSC Gr. 2/2A Which Article says the government should provide free and compulsory education to children between ages 6 to 14?

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Options: 44 / 45 / 46 / 47 ஆறு முதல் 14 வயது வரை உள்ள குழந்தைகளுக்கு இலவச கட்டாய கல்வி வழங்க வேண்டும் என எந்த விதி கூறுகிறது? ✅ CORRECT ANSWER Article 45 💎 LIVE GEM — Article 21A vs Article 45 Connection Original DPSP (Art. 45): Free and compulsory education for children 6–14 years — this was a Directive (advisory). 86th Constitutional Amendment, 2002 added Article 21A which made this a Fundamental Right (Right to Education Act, 2009). So the progression is: Art.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: 45 (DPSP) → Art. 21A (Fundamental Right after amendment). Also related: Fundamental Duties (Art. 51A) — added by the 42nd Amendment (teacher set this as the comment-section question for students to answer). 🚨 CRITICAL — Article 45 vs Article 21A Art. 45 in DPSP = policy directive for 6–14 education. 21A = Fundamental Right to Education (added by 86th Amendment, 2002). Both relate to the same subject but are in DIFFERENT parts of the Constitution.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Q 14 MEDIUM Factual / Scheme-Based TNPSC Gr. 4/VAO In which year was the Maternity Benefit Act enacted? Options: 1960 / 1961 / 1962 / 1963 பேர்கால உதவி சட்டம் எப்போது இயற்றப்பட்டது? ✅ CORRECT ANSWER 1961 (Option B) 💎 LIVE GEM — TN Maternity Scheme: Muthu Lakshmi Ammal Scheme Tamil Nadu's Muthulakshmi Reddy Maternity Benefit Scheme (மகப்பேறு உதவி திட்டம்) provides ₹18,000 to pregnant women in THREE instalments of ₹6,000 each. Purpose: Nutritional support for mother and child (pre-natal and post-natal).

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Beneficiaries: Below-poverty-line pregnant women. Current Maternity Leave: Paid maternity leave in India = 26 weeks (≈ 6 months) for government and private sector employees (Maternity Benefit Amendment Act, 2017 extended it from 12 to 26 weeks).

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Satyamurthi was Mayor of Madras in 1939 AND the political guru of Kamarajar. Vanchivadani (Satyamurthi's disciple) enrolled at Bala Mandir in 1949 with Kamarajar's encouragement. Padmabai Ammal donated her jewellery to the Neil Statue Removal agitation (1927); this was a PYQ in TNPSC Gr. Nagammaiyar (Periyar's first wife) = First woman representative of Tamil Nadu Congress Committee (TNCC). Krishnambal Jagannathan = Identified by LAFTI (1981) + Vedaranyam Coral Jubilee (2006).

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Also participated in Quit India Movement 1942. Rajaji (1937 CM) INTRODUCED Sales Tax (not abolished it); he banned alcohol and introduced sales tax to compensate revenue. Quit India Resolution: 8 August 1942 (NOT 1943) at Bombay. Kamarajar escaped at Arakkonam station . 36–51) = Non-justiciable; courts cannot enforce non-implementation. 37 = DPSP is not enforceable by courts. Article 40 = Panchayati Raj (mandatory to establish); 73rd Amendment = 1992 passed / 1993 implemented.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: Article 45 = Free compulsory education 6–14 yrs (DPSP); later elevated to Art. 21A Fundamental Right (86th Amend.

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: HIGH PROBABILITY — TNPSC Gr. 4 Krishnambal Jagannathan identification question: LAFTI 1981 + Vedaranyam 2006 Coral Jubilee. Previously asked; likely to reappear in new format. HIGH PROBABILITY — TNPSC Gr. 2A Article 43A (Workers' participation in factory management) vs Article 43B (Co-operative societies) distinction — increasing frequency in recent papers. MODERATE — TNPSC Gr. 2 Assertion-Reason: "Periyar organised Anti-Hindi conference in 1937" + "Rajaji abolished/introduced Sales Tax" — trap in the Reason; correct answer is Assertion-True, R

chunks.title: High
chunks.text: HIGH PROBABILITY — All TNPSC Levels Article 45 (DPSP) → 86th Amendment → Article 21A (Right to Education) chain question. Year of 73rd Amendment (1992 Act / 1993 implementation) is also a recurring trap question. MODERATE — TNPSC Gr. 2A / VAO Neil Statue Removal agitation: Padmabai Ammal / Somayajulu / Year 1927 / Statue moved to Madras Museum in 1937 by Rajaji — multi-option MCQ likely.
