chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: §1 · Languages & 8th Schedule (Q25–Q30) Q25 Which Schedule of the Indian Constitution deals with languages? EASY Factual Gr. 4 ✅ Answer 8th Schedule (Eighth Schedule) — Part XVII of the Constitution (Articles 344–351) Part XVII of the Constitution begins at Article 363 (as noted by teacher). The 8th Schedule lists officially recognised languages. Part XVII covers Articles 343–351 dealing with Official Language. 🏆 PYQ ALERT Frequently asked in TNPSC Gr.

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: 4, and Group 1 Prelims. "Which Schedule lists official languages? " is a standard question. Q26 Where is the dance form called "Theyyam" celebrated? EASY Factual Gr. 4 ✅ Answer Kerala 💎 Live Gem Other Kerala classical dances: Mohiniyattam and Kathakali — all three belong to Kerala. A trick: If you see Theyyam / Mohiniyattam / Kathakali in an option, the answer is always Kerala. Q27 In which year was Malayalam declared a Classical Language (Shastriya Bhasha)?

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 ✅ Answer 2013 💎 Live Gem — Full Classical Languages Timeline Master this table — every year it comes in TNPSC: Year Language 2004 Tamil 2005 Sanskrit 2008 Telugu 2008 Kannada 2013 Malayalam 2014 Odia 2024 Marathi, Bengali, Pali, Assamese, Prakrit (5 new) School books cover 6 (up to 2014). Total now = 11 Classical Languages 🏆 PYQ ALERT Year of Tamil being declared classical language (2004) appeared in TNPSC 2019. Malayalam year is a common distractor.

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: Q28 Which place receives the least amount of rainfall in India? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 4 ✅ Answer Jaisalmer, Rajasthan Jaisalmer is in the Thar Desert region of Rajasthan near Pokhran . It is the driest place in India due to its location in the arid western zone. 🪤 TRAP Leh (Ladakh) is also very dry, but Jaisalmer is the standard answer for "least rainfall in India" in TNPSC. Don't confuse with "highest rainfall" = Mawsynram / Cherrapunji (Meghalaya).

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: Q29 Which city is called the "Pink City" of India? EASY Factual Gr. 4 / VAO ✅ Answer Jaipur, Rajasthan 💎 Live Gem — Rajasthan City Nicknames Jaipur = Pink City | Jodhpur = Blue City | Udaipur = City of Lakes | Jaisalmer = Golden City. All in Rajasthan — easy confusion zone in TNPSC. Q30 How many languages are currently recognised by the Indian Constitution? EASY Factual All Exams ✅ Answer 22 Languages (8th Schedule / Eighth Schedule) ⚠️ Critical Note The 8th Schedule has 22 languages.

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: Part XVII covers Official Language. Don't confuse "8th Schedule" with "17th Part" — both relate to languages but they are different things.

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: §2 · Famous Books & Personalities (Q31–Q39) Q31 Who wrote "The Discovery of India"? EASY Factual All Exams ✅ Answer Jawaharlal Nehru Q32 Bhangra is the folk dance of which Indian state? EASY Factual Gr. 4 ✅ Answer Punjab Q33 How many Classical Languages have been declared so far in India? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 ✅ Answer 11 Classical Languages (current total) 🪤 TRAP School textbooks (based on pre-2024 data) mention only 6 classical languages . If the exam is based on school books, answer is 6.

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: If asking "current", answer is 11. Read the question carefully. Q34 According to the 2011 Census, which district in Tamil Nadu has the lowest sex ratio? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 / VAO ✅ Answer Dharmapuri District Q35 Who wrote the books "Mission 2020" and "Mission India"? Also, who is called the "Missile Man of India" and "Missile Woman of India"? MEDIUM Multi-Fact Gr. 4 ✅ Answer Mission 2020 & Mission India → Dr. Abdul Kalam 💎 Live Gem — Missile Nicknames Missile Man of India = Dr.

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: Abdul Kalam Rocket Man of India = K. Sivan (ISRO Chief) Missile Woman of India = Tessy Thomas (she headed Agni missile project) Also: Agne (Agni) Wings (Tamil: அக்னி சிறகுகள்) is another book by Kalam. Q36 Who is called "Babasaheb"? EASY Factual All Exams ✅ Answer Dr. Ambedkar Other titles for Ambedkar: Father / Architect of the Indian Constitution | Modern Manu of India | Babasaheb | Chief Draftsman of Indian Constitution . Q37 Who is called the "Missile Man / Rocket Man of India"?

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: EASY Factual Gr. Abdul Kalam (Missile Man) | K. Sivan (Rocket Man) Abdul Kalam served as 11th President of India from 2002–2007 . He was nominated by BJP; Congress counter-nominated Lakshmi Sahgal (INA Commander under Subhash Chandra Bose). Q38 In which year did Viswanathan Anand receive the Padma Vibhushan award? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 4 ✅ Answer 2007 💎 Live Gem In 1988, Anand became India's first Chess Grandmaster. Also in 1988: MGR received the Bharat Ratna.

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: Two important 1988 facts — don't confuse. Q39 Whose book is "Ezhuchi Deepangal" (எழுச்சி தீபங்கள் / Torches of Inspiration)? MEDIUM Factual Gr. Abdul Kalam 💎 Live Gem — Kalam's favourite Thirukkural & book Kalam's favourite Thirukkural: "அறிவற்றம் காக்கும் கருவி" (Knowledge is the tool that protects virtue). His favourite book: "Lights from Many Lamps" by Lillian Watson .

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: §3 · Indian Constitution — Core Facts (Q40–Q55) Q40 Who became the permanent Chairman of the Constituent Assembly of India? MEDIUM Factual Gr. Rajendra Prasad (Permanent Chairman) | Sachchidananda Sinha (Temporary/Provisional Chairman) Rajendra Prasad later became India's first President . Sachchidananda Sinha was the oldest member, chosen as temporary chairman following the French system of giving preference to the eldest. Q41 Which Article of the Indian Constitution prohibits discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex, or place o

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: EASY Factual All Exams ✅ Answer Article 15 💡 TIP Article 14 = Equality before law (all are equal). Article 15 = Prohibition of discrimination. Article 17 = Abolition of Untouchability. Q42 Which Article of the Constitution treats untouchability as a crime? What is Untouchability Eradication Day? EASY Multi-Fact All Exams ✅ Answer Article 17 abolishes untouchability. Untouchability Eradication Day = January 30 💎 Live Gem — Shortcut for January 30 January 30, 1948 = Mahatma Gandhi was assassinated by Godse → This date is also Untouchability Era

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: Q43 According to the 2011 Census, which district in Tamil Nadu has the highest literacy rate? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 / VAO ✅ Answer Kanyakumari District (92. 14% literacy rate) 💎 Live Gem — TN Census Quick Reference Category District Highest Literacy Kanyakumari (92. 14%) Lowest Literacy Dharmapuri Highest Sex Ratio Nilgiris 2nd Highest Sex Ratio Thanjavur Lowest Sex Ratio Dharmapuri Dharmapuri appears in BOTH lowest literacy and lowest sex ratio — remember this fact!

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: Q44 Who is called the "People's President" of India? EASY Factual Gr. Abdul Kalam Also note: Pratibha Patil was India's first woman President. Q45 Who was the first person to receive the Rajiv Gandhi Khel Ratna Award? What is it called now? MEDIUM Multi-Fact Gr. 4 ✅ Answer First recipient: Viswanathan Anand | Current name: Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna Award 💎 Live Gem The BJP government renamed "Rajiv Gandhi Khel Ratna" to "Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna Award.

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: " This is a classic TNPSC trap — the old name appears in old question papers, but current name is Major Dhyan Chand. Q46 According to the 2011 Census, which district in Tamil Nadu has the highest sex ratio? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 / VAO ✅ Answer Nilgiris (highest sex ratio) | Thanjavur (2nd highest) Q47 In which year did Viswanathan Anand become a Chess Grandmaster? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 4 ✅ Answer 1988 Q48 Mariyappan Thangavelu is associated with which sport?

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: Which category? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 4 ✅ Answer High Jump (Para-athletics) — T63 category. He won Gold at 2016 Rio Paralympics. Mariyappan is from Salem district , Tamil Nadu. He won Gold at Rio 2016 and Silver at Tokyo 2020 Paralympics. Q49 On which date did Ambedkar take charge as Chairman of the Drafting Committee of the Indian Constitution? HARD Factual Gr. 1 ✅ Answer August 29, 1947 💎 Live Gem — Memory Shortcut India got Independence on August 15, 1947 .

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: Add two weeks (14 days) → August 29, 1947 = Ambedkar took charge as Drafting Committee Chairman. This shortcut ensures you never forget this date! Q50 Who was the Chairman of the Drafting Committee of the Indian Constitution? What did Naziruddin Ahmad call it? MEDIUM Conceptual Gr. Ambedkar was Chairman. Naziruddin Ahmad called it a "Drifting Committee" (not Drafting) — implying it just copied from existing sources. 💎 Live Gem Naziruddin Ahmad later went to Pakistan after partition.

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: He criticised the committee saying the Constitution was mostly copied from the Government of India Act 1935 — calling it a "drifting committee" rather than a "drafting committee. " This is a very popular TNPSC Group 1 question. Also appears in Lakshmikant's Indian Polity book. Q51 In which year was the Bharat Ratna awarded to Dr. MEDIUM Factual All Exams ✅ Answer 1990 (posthumous) 💎 Live Gem Kalam received Bharat Ratna in 1997 . Ambedkar in 1990 .

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: Nelson Mandela was released from prison in 1990 — same year. MGR's Bharat Ratna — 1988 . All three years are frequently confused. Q52 According to the 2011 Census, which district in Tamil Nadu has the lowest literacy rate? EASY Factual Gr. 2 / VAO ✅ Answer Dharmapuri (also has lowest sex ratio) Q53 Which Article of the Constitution says "All are equal before the law"? EASY Factual All Exams ✅ Answer Article 14 Q54 How many years did Dr. Ambedkar spend in prison?

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: HARD Trick/Trap Gr. Ambedkar was NEVER imprisoned. 💎 Live Gem — Exam Trap! This is a deliberate trap question . Ambedkar supported the British administration and worked for the rights of Depressed Classes (Dalits). He never went to prison unlike Gandhi or Nehru. If a student assumes "freedom fighter = jail time," they'll answer wrongly. Q55 In which year did Mariyappan Thangavelu win a Gold medal at the Olympics/Paralympics? EASY Factual Gr. 4 ✅ Answer 2016 (Rio Paralympics — Gold in High Jump T63)

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: §4 · National Symbols, Days & Anthem (Q56–Q75) Q56 The Untouchability Eradication Act (Protection of Civil Rights Act) was passed in which year? EASY Factual Gr. 2 / VAO ✅ Answer 1955 💎 Live Gem — 10 Important Acts of 1955 1955 saw many landmark laws: Citizenship Act, Untouchability Eradication Act, Hindu Marriage Act, etc. Teacher noted that there's a "box of 10 laws" from 1955 — worth memorising as a Shorts/flashcard set. Q57 True or False: The National Flag is hoisted by the President on Independence Day at the Red Fort, Delhi.

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: MEDIUM Trick/Trap All Exams ✅ Answer FALSE — The Prime Minister hoists the flag on Independence Day (Aug 15). The President hoists the flag on Republic Day (Jan 26). ⚠️ Critical Distinction Independence Day (Aug 15) = Prime Minister at Red Fort | Republic Day (Jan 26) = President at Rajpath/Kartavya Path Q58 What is the prescribed duration for singing the National Anthem? EASY Factual All Exams ✅ Answer 52 seconds Q59 Who designed the National Flag of India?

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: MEDIUM Factual Gr. 4 ✅ Answer Pingali Venkaiah The National Flag was adopted on January 24, 1950 . The flag was designed by Pingali Venkaiah from Andhra Pradesh. Q60 Who composed India's National Anthem "Jana Gana Mana"? When was it first sung? MEDIUM Multi-Fact All Exams ✅ Answer Rabindranath Tagore — first sung at the Calcutta Session of INC in 1911 💎 Live Gem — National Anthem vs National Song National Anthem : "Jana Gana Mana" — written & sung by Rabindranath Tagore .

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: First sung at Calcutta Congress Session, 1911 . National Song : "Vande Mataram" — written by Bankim Chandra Chatterjee (from the novel Anandamath). First sung at Calcutta Congress Session, 1896 . TRAP: Both were first sung at Calcutta sessions . Both were performed by Rabindranath Tagore on stage. But the author of Vande Mataram is Bankim Chandra — NOT Tagore. 🏆 PYQ ALERT "Who wrote Vande Mataram? " has appeared multiple times in TNPSC — answer is Bankim Chandra Chatterjee, NOT Tagore.

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: Q61 In which year was Lotus declared the National Flower of India? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 4 ✅ Answer 1950 (along with Republic Day and constitutional recognition of national symbols) Q62 Where was the first National Flag of independent India woven? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 ✅ Answer Gudiyatham, Vellore District, Tamil Nadu Q63 What is India's National Micro-organism? In which year was it declared? HARD Factual Gr. 2 ✅ Answer Lactobacillus Delbrueckii — declared in 2012 This beneficial bacterium is used in curd/yoghurt production and was designated as In

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: Q64 What is the ratio of length to breadth of the Indian National Flag? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 4 / VAO ✅ Answer 3:2 (Length : Breadth) 💎 Live Gem — Simple Visualisation A rectangle — the longer side is always the length. If you see this on screen (16:9 widescreen), length is the horizontal. For flags and most rectangles: length > breadth . So flag ratio is 3:2 (length:breadth). Never reverse it! Q65 At which Congress session was the National Anthem first sung?

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: EASY Factual All Exams ✅ Answer Calcutta (Kolkata) Congress Session, 1911 Q66 On which day is International Non-Violence Day observed? When was it declared by the UN? MEDIUM Multi-Fact All Exams ✅ Answer October 2 (Gandhi's birthday). Declared by UN in 2007. 💎 Live Gem — September vs October Dates September 15 = International Democracy Day (UN, 2007) + Anna (C. Annadurai) Birthday + Vishweswaraiah Birthday (Engineers' Day in India) September 17 = Periyar's Birthday + Social Justice Day October 2 = Gandhi's Birthday + International Non-Violence

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: Q67 Which king introduced the silver coin called "Rupiya" in the 16th century? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 ✅ Answer Sher Shah Suri (Sur Empire) Sher Shah Suri introduced the "Rupiya" (silver coin) — from which the modern Indian Rupee derives its name. He also built the Grand Trunk Road. Q68 How many days after Republic Day celebrations does the "Beating Retreat" ceremony take place? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 ✅ Answer 3 days later (January 29) Q69 Examine these two statements: (1) "Aaduvome Pallandu Paduvome" was sung by D.

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: Pattammal on Independence Day. (2) The Tamil National Pledge was written by Bharatiyar. Which is correct? HARD Trick/Trap Gr. 2 ✅ Answer Both statements are FALSE. (1) The song belongs to Bharatiyar (not Bharatidasan as some confuse). (2) The Tamil National Pledge was written by Pithimari Venkata Subbarao — NOT Bharatiyar. 💎 Live Gem Tamil Nadu National Pledge (தமிழ் தேசிய உறுதி மொழி) was written by Pithimari Venkata Subbarao . This is a very common TNPSC trap — students assume Bharatiyar wrote everything Tamil.

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: Always verify the pledge author separately. Q70 Who designed the Rupee symbol (₹) in 2010? MEDIUM Factual All Exams ✅ Answer D. Udaya Kumar — from Villupuram district, Tamil Nadu. Adopted on July 15/19, 2010. Q71 When was the Gangetic River Dolphin declared India's National Aquatic Animal? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 ✅ Answer 2010 💎 Live Gem — 2010 Dual Fact Two important 2010 declarations: Gangetic Dolphin = National Aquatic Animal (2010) and Rupee Symbol designed (2010) .

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: Both in the same year — link them together for memory. Q72 Which animal does NOT appear on the Sarnath Lion Capital (the national emblem base)? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 ✅ Answer Tiger — Animals on the Sarnath pillar: Lion, Elephant, Horse, and Bull. Tiger does NOT appear. Q73 Who wrote the National Pledge of India? EASY Factual Gr. 4 ✅ Answer Pithimari Venkata Subbarao (Pydimarri Venkata Subba Rao) Q74 Who wrote the National Song "Vande Mataram"? EASY Factual All Exams ✅ Answer Bankim Chandra Chatterjee (from the novel Anandamath) Q75 Where is the

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 / VAO ✅ Answer Viralimalai, Pudukkottai District, Tamil Nadu 💎 Live Gem — TN Wildlife Shortcut Peacock → Viralimalai (Pudukkottai) | Squirrel → Srivilliputhur (wildlife sanctuary) §5 · Geography Quick Facts (Q76) Q76 Give the lengths of Brahmaputra and Ganges rivers. Also: height of Mt. Everest, and depth of Mariana Trench. HARD Multi-Fact Gr. 2 ✅ Answer Brahmaputra = 3,848 km | Ganges = 2,525 km | Mt. Everest = 8,848 m | Mariana Trench = 10,994 m (~11 km deep) 💎 Live Gem — Memory Table Feature Value Location Brahmaputra

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: Everest (highest) 8,848 m Nepal Mariana Trench (deepest) 10,994 m Pacific Ocean

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: §6 · Constitution Structure & Assembly (Q77–Q95) Q77 In which year was the POCSO Act (Protection of Children from Sexual Offences) enacted? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 ✅ Answer 2012. POCSO defines a child as anyone under 18 years. 💡 TIP Child definition varies by law: POCSO = under 18 | Child Labour Act = under 14 (or 18 for hazardous work). Don't generalise. Q78 In which year and where was the "Poorna Swaraj" (Complete Independence) resolution passed?

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: MEDIUM Factual All Exams ✅ Answer 1929, Lahore Congress Session. First Independence Day celebrated: January 26, 1930. Q79 What are the current number of Parts, Schedules, and Articles in the Indian Constitution? MEDIUM Factual All Exams ✅ Answer Currently: 25 Parts | 12 Schedules | 465+ Articles | 106 Amendments 💎 Live Gem — Original vs Current Original (1950) Current Articles 395 465+ Parts 22 25 Schedules 8 12 Amendments 0 106 Q80 How many Articles, Parts, and Schedules were there in the Constitution when it was originally framed?

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: MEDIUM Factual All Exams ✅ Answer Original: 395 Articles | 22 Parts | 8 Schedules Q81 From approximately how many countries was the Indian Constitution drawn? HARD Factual Gr. 1 ✅ Answer Approximately 60 countries 💎 Live Gem The Constitution borrowed from about 60 countries . The cost of drafting was ₹64 lakh (not rounded — kept as-is since there was no formal budget). This is a trick: 60 is rounded, 64 lakh is exact. Q82 What was the cost of drafting the Indian Constitution?

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: MEDIUM Factual Gr. 1 ✅ Answer ₹64 lakh Q83 Who among the following was NOT a member of the Drafting Committee of the Indian Constitution? HARD Trick/Trap Gr. Giri was NOT a member. Krishnamachari were members. Q84 How many members did the Constituent Assembly have when it was constituted? After partition? MEDIUM Multi-Fact Gr. 2 ✅ Answer Originally 389 members. After partition (members left for Pakistan): 299 members remained. Q85 What is "Constitution Day" (Indian Constitution Day / Samvidhan Divas)?

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: Don't confuse it with Republic Day! MEDIUM Trick/Trap All Exams ✅ Answer November 26 (1949) = Constitution Day (Samvidhan Divas) — the day the Constitution was adopted by the Assembly. January 26 (1950) = Republic Day — the day it came into force. 💎 Live Gem — Critical Distinction This is one of the most common TNPSC mistakes. Candidates write January 26 for both. Remember: Adopted (Nov 26, 1949) ≠ Enforced/Implemented (Jan 26, 1950) . Constitution Day was officially declared in 2015 by the Modi government.

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: 🏆 PYQ ALERT Constitution Day vs Republic Day distinction has appeared in multiple years of TNPSC Group 2 and Group 4. Q86 Which woman was NOT a member of the Constituent Assembly? HARD Trick/Trap Gr. Muthulakshmi Reddy was NOT in the Constituent Assembly (she was associated with the Justice Party / non-Congress group). Sarojini Naidu, Vijayalakshmi Pandit, and Annie Mascarene WERE members. Q87 When did the first session of the Constituent Assembly take place?

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: MEDIUM Factual All Exams ✅ Answer December 9, 1946 💎 Live Gem — Temporary Chairmanship from France At the first session, Sachchidananda Sinha was made temporary chairman — not because of his merit but because he was the oldest member . This tradition of giving preference to the eldest was borrowed from the French system . Q88 How many Fundamental Rights were originally given to citizens when the Constitution was first framed? MEDIUM Factual All Exams ✅ Answer Originally 7 Fundamental Rights.

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: Currently 6 (Right to Property was removed by the 44th Amendment, 1978). 💡 TIP Right to Property (Article 31) was removed by the 44th Amendment, 1978 and is now a legal right (Article 300A) — no longer a Fundamental Right. Q89 How long did it take to draft the Indian Constitution? MEDIUM Factual All Exams ✅ Answer 2 years, 11 months, and 18 days 💎 Live Gem This was used as a criticism — "it took too long. " Critics said it was the world's longest written constitution and took nearly 3 years (just 12 days short of 3 years).

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: Q90 When did Dr. Ambedkar take charge as Chairman of the Drafting Committee? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 ✅ Answer August 29, 1947 Q91 True/False: (1) The person who served as the Legal Advisor during the drafting of the Indian Constitution was Ambedkar. (2) Sachchidananda Sinha was the Permanent Chairman of the Constituent Assembly. HARD Trick/Trap Gr. 1 ✅ Answer Both are FALSE. (1) The Legal Advisor was B. Rau (Sir Benegal Narsing Rau). Ambedkar was Drafting Committee Chairman.

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: (2) Sachchidananda Sinha was TEMPORARY Chairman; Dr. Rajendra Prasad was PERMANENT Chairman. Rau = Constitutional Advisor. Ambedkar = Chairman of Drafting Committee. These are different roles and a very common exam trap. Q92 Who is called the "Father / Architect" of the Indian Constitution? EASY Factual All Exams ✅ Answer Dr. Ambedkar He was also the first person from India to go to America specifically to pursue higher studies (Columbia University).

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: Q93 How many women members were there in the Constituent Assembly? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 ✅ Answer 15 women members 💡 TIP Any question about women / religion / round numbers in the Constituent Assembly — the answer is usually 15. This is a rough pattern but helps eliminate wrong options quickly. Q94 On which day was "Poorna Swaraj" (Complete Independence) day first celebrated? MEDIUM Factual All Exams ✅ Answer January 26, 1930 — one month after the Lahore Congress Session (1929) passed the Poorna Swaraj resolution.

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: This is why January 26 was chosen as Republic Day in 1950 — to commemorate the first Poorna Swaraj Day of 1930. Q95 True or False: The Drafting Committee of the Indian Constitution had 8 members. MEDIUM Trick/Trap Gr. 2 ✅ Answer FALSE — The Drafting Committee had 7 members (including Ambedkar). 🪤 TRAP Many students include an 8th person by mistake. Ambedkar + 6 others = 7 total. This is a famous "off by one" TNPSC trap. §7 · Early History & Pre-History (Q96–Q98) Q96–Q98 In which type of governance system do USA and Canada operate?

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: What are examples of Presidential democracies? MEDIUM Conceptual Gr. 2 ✅ Answer USA and Canada operate under Presidential Democracy. India and UK operate under Parliamentary Democracy (Prime Minister system). 💎 Live Gem — Roman Empire The Roman Empire was based in the Italian peninsula — modern-day Italy. Rome is still the capital of Italy. This came up when discussing Rome's role in developing democratic thought (city-state model).

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: §8 · Democracy — World & India (Q99–Q108) Q99 What is the birthplace of democracy? Name the notable democracies with unique distinctions (Iceland, New Zealand, Switzerland). MEDIUM Multi-Fact All Exams ✅ Answer Birthplace of Democracy = Greece (Athens). Iceland = Oldest working parliament. New Zealand = First country to give women voting rights (1893). Switzerland = Direct Democracy. 💎 Live Gem — Memory Shortcuts ICE land → has been there a long time (ICE is ancient) → oldest parliament NEW Zealand → always new systems → first country for wome

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: Q100 Who said "Government of the people, by the people, for the people"? EASY Factual All Exams ✅ Answer Abraham Lincoln. Birthplace of democracy = Greece. Q101 Match: Which of these is wrongly paired? (a) Basis of political philosophy = Greece; (b) Magna Carta 1215 = UK; (c) Man Island = between England and Ireland; (d) Roman Empire = Italian Peninsula HARD Trick/Trap Gr. 1 ✅ Answer Option (b) is the wrong pair — Magna Carta was signed in 1215, NOT 1615.

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: All others are correct. 💎 Live Gem The Magna Carta (1215) = world's first constitutional document; signed by King John of England. The teacher noted this is a deliberate year-swap trap: 1215 vs 1615. Q102 In which year did the UN General Assembly declare September 15 as International Democracy Day? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 ✅ Answer 2007 Q103 Which country was the first in the world to give women the right to vote? EASY Factual All Exams ✅ Answer New Zealand (1893) Q104 In Direct Democracy, who are the voters?

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: EASY Conceptual Gr. 4 ✅ Answer Voters / Citizens themselves (they directly make laws and decisions — no representatives) Q105 Which country has the oldest and longest functioning parliament? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 ✅ Answer Iceland (Althing parliament, founded in 930 AD) 💎 Live Gem — Republic of San Marino If the question asks "oldest continuously functioning republic" (not just parliament), the answer is San Marino (founded 301 AD). Different from Iceland's "oldest parliament.

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: " Q106–Q108 Who is called the "Father of Political Science"? Who is the Father of Zoology/Biology? EASY Factual All Exams ✅ Answer Father of Political Science = Aristotle | Father of Zoology = Aristotle | Teacher of Alexander the Great = Aristotle 💎 Live Gem — Indian Democracy Stats According to surveys, approximately 79% of Indians have faith in their democracy (support the democratic system). This percentage has appeared in TNPSC descriptive questions.

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: §9 · Tamil Nadu Urban Local Bodies (Q109–Q125) Q109 Which of the following is NOT a Municipal Corporation (Maanagar Aatchi) in Tamil Nadu? (Salem, Tiruppur, Thanjavur, Karur) MEDIUM Trick/Trap Gr. 4 / VAO ✅ Answer Karur is NOT a Municipal Corporation. Salem, Tiruppur, and Thanjavur are all corporations. Q110 In which year was the Chennai Corporation (India's oldest local body) established? Give the full Chennai history timeline. HARD Multi-Fact Gr.

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: 2 ✅ Answer Chennai Corporation established: 1688 💎 Live Gem — Full Chennai Timeline (6 Key Facts) Year Event 1639 Francis Day purchased land from Nayak Damarla Chennappa Nayakudu 1688 Chennai Municipal Corporation established (India's oldest) 1801 Chennai became a Presidency under Wellesley 1950 Chennai (Madras) became a State 1956 (Nov 1) Madras reorganised as a State under States Reorganisation Act 1969 (Jan 14) Madras State renamed Tamil Nadu 1996 Madras city renamed Chennai Q111 What is the minimum population required to designate a town a

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: MEDIUM Factual Gr. 4 / VAO ✅ Answer 1 lakh (100,000) — Municipality requires minimum 1 lakh population. Municipal Corporation requires 10 lakh. Town Panchayat (Peeraatchi) requires 50,000. 💎 Live Gem — Population Hierarchy Shortcut Bus fare analogy: Bus (Peeraatchi/Town Panchayat) = minimum fare = 50,000 people Municipality (Nagaratchi) = 1 lakh Municipal Corporation (Maanagar) = 10 lakh Minimum bus fare for District Panchayat Ward = 50,000 Q112 Which was the first Municipality established in Tamil Nadu?

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: HARD Factual Gr. 2 ✅ Answer Vellajapet (Walajapet / Valajaapettai), Ranipet District Q113 Statement 1: The district with the most municipalities in TN is Perambalur. Statement 2: The Town Panchayat (Peeraatchi) system was first introduced in India by Tamil Nadu. Which is correct? HARD Multi-Fact Gr. 2 ✅ Answer Statement 1 is FALSE (Kanchipuram has the most municipalities in TN). Statement 2 is TRUE (Tamil Nadu was the first state in India to introduce Town Panchayat / Peeraatchi).

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: 💎 Live Gem — Kanchipuram Trivia Kanchipuram district has the most: municipalities, lakes/tanks, and temples. If you see "most _____ in Tamil Nadu" and Kanchipuram is an option, it's likely correct! Q114 Which of the following is correct? (a) Municipal Corporation has an IAS officer as Commissioner. (b) Municipality has government officers as commissioners. (c) Town Panchayat has an Executive Officer (EO). Choose the correct option. MEDIUM Application Gr.

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: 2 ✅ Answer All three (a), (b), and (c) are CORRECT. Answer = "All of the above" 💎 Live Gem — Administrative Heads Municipal Corporation → IAS Officer as Commissioner (e. , BBMP, Chennai Corporation Commissioner) Municipality → Government Officer as Municipal Commissioner Town Panchayat → Executive Officer (EO) Career note: If you score well in Group 2, you could become a Municipal Commissioner — possible posting! Q115 What acts as a bridge between the people and the government?

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: EASY Conceptual All Exams ✅ Answer Gram Panchayat (Village Panchayat / Kiraama Oraatchi) As Gandhi often said, the Gram Panchayat is the foundation of Indian democracy — connecting the grassroots people with the government structure. Q116 How many Panchayat Unions (Oraatchi Ondriyam) do Nilgiris and Perambalur districts each have? MEDIUM Factual VAO ✅ Answer Each has 4 Panchayat Unions (least in TN). Villupuram has the most Panchayat Unions. Q117 On what basis is a district divided into District Panchayat (Maavatta Oraatchi) wards?

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: MEDIUM Factual Gr. 4 / VAO ✅ Answer Population — each District Panchayat ward must have a minimum of 50,000 population. Q118 Which of the following is NOT a source of income for Village Panchayat (Kiraama Oraatchi)? (a) Water connection charges (b) Share from lottery proceeds (c) Tax on shops (d) Entertainment tax MEDIUM Application Gr. 4 / VAO ✅ Answer Entertainment tax (d) is NOT a source of income for Village Panchayat — it belongs to Municipal Corporation revenue.

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: Q119 Which of the following are sources of income for a Municipal Corporation? (House tax, Water tax, Professional tax, Entertainment tax, Shop tax) MEDIUM Application Gr. 4 ✅ Answer ALL of the above — All five are revenue sources for Municipal Corporations. Q120 When was the National Panchayati Raj Day declared? What is the relevant Article? MEDIUM Multi-Fact All Exams ✅ Answer April 24 = National Panchayati Raj Day = National Rural Local Body Day = National Gram Swaraj Day.

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: The 73rd Amendment Act (1992) came into force. Article 243K governs State Election Commission for local body elections. 💎 Live Gem — April 24 Multiple Days April 24 celebrates: (1) National Panchayati Raj Day, (2) National Rural Self-Governance Day, (3) National Local Body Day, and (4) Birthday of G. Pope (Tamil scholar). All on April 24! Article 243K = State Election Commission (K for Koyambedu — local body HQ in Chennai per teacher's mnemonic) Q121 In TN, municipalities are designated when a town has what minimum population?

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: What about Town Panchayat (Maavatta Oraatchi)? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 4 / VAO ✅ Answer Municipality = 1 lakh+ population. Town Panchayat = more than 1 lakh (i. , above Town Panchayat threshold). Q122 In the 2011 Local Body Elections in Tamil Nadu, what percentage of seats were won by women? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 ✅ Answer Approximately 36–38% (teacher noted many students wrongly answered 33%; actual result was higher) 🪤 TRAP 33% is the reservation quota for women, but the actual percentage who won was higher — approximately 36–38%.

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: Don't confuse reservation percentage with actual win percentage. Q123 In which year did the Tamil Nadu government amend the law to give 50% reservation in local bodies for women? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 ✅ Answer 2016 (by J. Jayalalithaa's AIADMK government) Q124 True or False: The Central Election Commission conducts local body elections in TN. HARD Trick/Trap All Exams ✅ Answer FALSE — The State Election Commission conducts local body elections. The Central Election Commission only handles Parliamentary and State Legislative Assembly elections.

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: 💎 Live Gem — Article 243K Trick Article 243K mandates a State Election Commission in every state for local body elections. In Tamil Nadu, it is located at Koyambedu, Chennai . Memory trick: K = Koyambedu . This is a Group 1/2 level question that trips up many candidates. Q125 What is the head of a Municipal Corporation called? EASY Factual All Exams ✅ Answer Mayor (மேயர்)

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: §10 · Road Safety & Traffic Rules (Q126–Q137) Q126 What are "Belisha Beacons"? EASY Factual Gr. 4 ✅ Answer Belisha Beacons = the flashing orange lights on poles marking pedestrian crossings (zebra crossings) — introduced by British Minister Leslie Hore-Belisha. Q127 Children above what age should be seated in the rear seat of a vehicle? EASY Factual Gr. 4 ✅ Answer Children above 12 years should be in the rear seat with a separate seatbelt. Q128 In which year was the Pedestrian Crossing (Zebra Crossing) first established?

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: MEDIUM Factual Gr. 4 ✅ Answer 1934, in Britain (UK) Q129 Which state recorded the most road accidents in India in 2019? EASY Factual Gr. 4 ✅ Answer Tamil Nadu (highest road accidents in India in 2019) Q130 What type of clothing should pedestrians wear at night for safety? EASY Application Gr. 4 ✅ Answer Bright / reflective / white-coloured clothing — so drivers can see them clearly. Q131 Where was the pedestrian crossing (zebra crossing) first established, and in which year?

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: MEDIUM Factual Gr. 4 ✅ Answer Britain (UK), 1934 Q132 True or False: When a yellow (amber) traffic light is showing, you should stop your vehicle. MEDIUM Trick/Trap Gr. 4 ✅ Answer FALSE — Yellow means "get ready to move" (prepare to proceed). Red means stop. Green means go. 💎 Live Gem — Traffic Light Meaning Red = Stop | Yellow/Amber = Get ready to proceed | Green = Go. Many students say "yellow = stop" — that's wrong. Only RED = stop. Q133 What are road signs/traffic signs used for?

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: EASY Conceptual Gr. 4 ✅ Answer Road signs regulate traffic, warn of hazards, and guide road users — called "Road Signs / Traffic Signs. " Q134 What shape are Warning Signs on Indian roads? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 4 ✅ Answer Triangular (equilateral triangle with point upward) 💎 Live Gem — Road Sign Shape Table Sign Type Shape Warning Signs Triangle (▲) Informatory / General Signs Rectangle (▬) Mandatory/Regulatory Signs Circle (○) Directional Signs Square (□) Q135 Children below what age must cross roads only with the help of an adult?

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: EASY Factual Gr. 4 ✅ Answer Below 8 years of age (up to Class 3) — must be accompanied by an adult to cross roads. 💡 TIP Memory link: 8 years = up to 3rd standard. From 4th standard (9 years+), children can cross alone. Think: "3rd std baby needs hand-holding. " Q136 What shape are Informatory / Instructional Signs on Indian roads? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 4 ✅ Answer Rectangle (rectangular shape) 💎 Live Gem Mandatory signs (Do's and Don'ts) are circular .

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: Informatory/General signs are rectangular . Warning signs are triangular . Speed limit signs (circles with red border) are mandatory circular signs. Q137 What shape are Mandatory/Regulatory road signs (Do's and Don'ts)? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 4 ✅ Answer Circular / Round (what you must do = square shaped directional sign; what you must NOT do = circle with red border)

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: §11 · APJ Abdul Kalam — Complete Profile (Q138–Q144) Q138 What is the full name of Dr. MEDIUM Factual All Exams ✅ Answer Avul Pakir Jainulabdeen Abdul Kalam 💎 Live Gem — Decoding A. A = Avul Pakir | P refers to Pakir | J = Jainulabdeen (his father's name). The acronym APJ comes from the initials. Don't confuse order: Avul Pakir Jainulabdeen → APJ . Q139 In which year was Dr. Abdul Kalam awarded the Bharat Ratna? EASY Factual All Exams ✅ Answer 1997 Q140 For how many years was Nelson Mandela imprisoned?

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: MEDIUM Factual All Exams ✅ Answer 27 years (1964–1990) 💎 Live Gem — Mandela Effect Tie-in The "Mandela Effect" (false collective memory) is named after Mandela because many people falsely remembered he died in prison. He was actually released in 1990 and became President of South Africa. He is also called the "Gandhi of South Africa. " Q141 Nelson Mandela is associated with ending what system in South Africa? EASY Conceptual All Exams ✅ Answer Apartheid (racial segregation system) Q142 Where did Dr.

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: Abdul Kalam complete his school education? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 4 ✅ Answer Ramanathapuram (his hometown). He later studied at St. Joseph's College, Tiruchirappalli, and then joined ISRO in Chennai. Q143 Who was India's 11th President? EASY Factual All Exams ✅ Answer Dr. Abdul Kalam (2002–2007) Q144 What does "Pre-judice" (பாரபட்சம்) mean? MEDIUM Conceptual Gr. 4 ✅ Answer Prejudice = making a negative decision about a person without knowing them; judging unfairly in advance ("pre" = before, "judge" = form an opinion)

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: §12 · Final Mix — Fundamental Rights & Census (Q145–Q150) Q145 Which Article of the Constitution prohibits discrimination based on religion, race, caste, sex, and place of birth? EASY Factual All Exams ✅ Answer Article 15 Q146 According to 2011 Census, what is the district with the 2nd highest sex ratio in Tamil Nadu? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 / VAO ✅ Answer Thanjavur (2nd highest sex ratio). Nilgiris = 1st Q147 In which year did Viswanathan Anand become India's first Chess Grandmaster?

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: EASY Factual All Exams ✅ Answer 1988 Q148 What does "Consensus" (ஒத்த கருத்து) mean? EASY Conceptual Gr. 4 ✅ Answer Consensus = general agreement; a shared or commonly held view among a group of people. Q149 According to the 2011 Census, what is the literacy rate of Kanyakumari district (highest in TN)? MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 / VAO ✅ Answer 92. 14% (Kanyakumari district, highest literacy in Tamil Nadu) Q150 What is the full name of Dr. MEDIUM Factual All Exams ✅ Answer Bhimrao Ramji Ambedkar (Bhimrao = first name, Ramji = father's name) 💎 Live G

chunks.title: Live Revision
chunks.text: Ambedkar was the first person from India to travel to America (Columbia University) specifically for higher studies. He held multiple doctorates — from Columbia University (USA) and London School of Economics (UK).

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: 🏙️ Group 1 — Municipal Corporations (மாநகராட்சிகள்) Q1 EASY Factual TNPSC Gr. 4 How many Municipal Corporations (மாநகராட்சிகள்) are there in Tamil Nadu? (As per the textbook) ▼ ✅ ANSWER 15 Municipal Corporations as per the textbook. The 15th Corporation added was Avadi . 💡 TIP The current ground reality: 21 corporations are functioning; the government has announced 25. For exam purposes, go with what the textbook says (15) unless the question specifies "current" or "latest.

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: " 🏆 PYQ ALERT This question and its variants about the 15th corporation (Avadi) have appeared in TNPSC Group 4 / VAO papers. The teacher explicitly highlighted this as a textbook-box question. Q2 MEDIUM Factual 💎 GEM Which is the world's first Municipal Corporation? Which is India's first? Which is the world's second? ▼ ✅ ANSWER • World's 1st Municipal Corporation: London • World's 2nd Municipal Corporation: Madras (Chennai) • India's 1st Municipal Corporation: Madras (Chennai) — established in 1688 💎 LIVE GEM The teacher taught a triple-fac

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: Remember: Chennai Corporation (1688) = "Two-in-One champion. " The full story of how Chennai became a city will be covered in Unit 6. 🏆 PYQ ALERT Both "World's first corporation" (London) and "India's first corporation" (Madras/Chennai, 1688) are classic PYQs in TNPSC Group 2 & Group 4. Q3 EASY Factual Which district in Tamil Nadu has the highest number of municipalities (நகராட்சிகள்)? ▼ ✅ ANSWER Kanchipuram District has the highest number of municipalities in Tamil Nadu.

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: 💎 LIVE GEM The teacher gave a powerful memory cluster: Kanchipuram is the champion of "most" in Tamil Nadu — Most Municipalities, Most Tanks/Lakes (ஏரிகள்), Most Temples (கோவில்கள்). Connect all three together for Kanchipuram! Q4 MEDIUM Factual Which district in Tamil Nadu has the most Panchayat Unions (ஊராட்சி ஒன்றியங்கள்) — specifically 22? ▼ ✅ ANSWER Villupuram District — has the maximum number of Panchayat Unions (22) in Tamil Nadu. 💡 TIP — DISTRICT MEMORY MAP • Kanchipuram → Most Municipalities, Tanks, Temples • Villupuram → Most Panchay

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: Q5 MEDIUM Trap/Trick Which of the following is NOT a Municipal Corporation in Tamil Nadu? (Options typically include: Salem, Tiruppur, Thanjavur, Karur) ▼ ✅ ANSWER Karur is NOT a Municipal Corporation in Tamil Nadu. Salem, Tiruppur, and Thanjavur ARE corporations. 🪤 TRAP NOTE The teacher mentioned: "Karur has been in the news (Karur incident), but it is still NOT a Municipal Corporation — simple to remember. " Don't confuse being in the news with administrative upgrade.

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: Q6 HARD Factual 💎 GEM The Chennai Municipal Corporation — India's oldest local self-government institution — was established in which year? Connect: Who founded Chennai city? When did it become a Province? When did Madras become a State? When was its name changed to Tamil Nadu? When was the district renamed Chennai? ▼ ✅ ANSWER — COMPLETE CHENNAI TIMELINE 1. 1639 — Francis Day (of East India Company) acquired land from Chennappa Nayak and founded Madras city.

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: 1688 — Madras Municipal Corporation established (~40 years later; St. George's Fort area). 1801 — Madras became a Province (associated with Lord Wellesley). 1956, November 1 — Madras became a separate State (States Reorganisation). 1969, January 14 (Pongal Day) — CM Arignar Anna renamed the state from Madras to Tamil Nadu . 1996 — CM Kalaignar (M. Karunanidhi) renamed Madras district to Chennai (in memory of Chennappa Nayak, at the request of his descendants).

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: 💎 LIVE GEM — COMPLETE DATE CHAIN The teacher drilled this as a 6-fact revision chain . Any one of these 6 dates could appear as a standalone question. Memorise all 6 in sequence: 1639 → 1688 → 1801 → 1956 → 1969 → 1996 . 🏆 PYQ ALERT All 6 dates have appeared across TNPSC exams (1688 = very frequent). "Who renamed Madras to Tamil Nadu? " → Arignar Anna, 1969 — appeared in Group 4 paper. "Who renamed Madras district to Chennai? " → Kalaignar, 1996.

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: 📊 Group 2 — Population Thresholds for Urban Bodies Q7 MEDIUM Factual 💎 GEM What is the minimum population required to classify an area as a Municipality (நகராட்சி) in Tamil Nadu? ▼ ✅ ANSWER A Municipality (நகராட்சி) requires a population of more than 1,00,000 (1 Lakh) . 💎 LIVE GEM — COMPLETE POPULATION SHORTCUT TABLE The teacher gave a brilliant shortcut system — memorise all four together: 🏙️ Municipal Corporation (மாநகராட்சி) → > 10 Lakh (1 Million) 🏘️ Municipality (நகராட்சி) → > 1 Lakh 🚌 Town Panchayat / Peruratchi (பேரூராட்சி) → > 10,

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: NAGAR → 10 Lakh 🪤 COMMON TRAP Students confuse Municipality (1 Lakh) with Town Panchayat (10,000). Many write 50,000 for Municipality — that's WRONG. 50,000 is for District Panchayat. Q8 EASY Factual What is the population threshold for a Municipal Corporation (மாநகராட்சி)? ▼ ✅ ANSWER More than 10 Lakh (1 Million) population → Municipal Corporation. 💡 TIP The head of a Municipal Corporation is called the Mayor (மேயர்) . If a district question is political: Mayor rules.

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: If administrative: Collector (District Collector) rules. Both are true — different domains. Q9 EASY Factual What is the population threshold for a Town Panchayat / Peruratchi (பேரூராட்சி)? ▼ ✅ ANSWER More than 10,000 population → Town Panchayat (Peruratchi / பேரூராட்சி). 💎 LIVE GEM — BUS TICKET TRICK "பேருந்து" (Bus) → "பேரூராட்சி" (Peruratchi). Basic bus ticket minimum in Tamil Nadu = ₹10. So Peruratchi = 10,000 people! The teacher used this brilliantly — pēruṇdu ticket ₹10 → pēruratchi 10,000.

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: Q10 EASY Factual What is the population threshold for a District Panchayat (மாவட்ட ஊராட்சி)? ▼ ✅ ANSWER 50,000 population → District Panchayat (Mavatta Uratchi / மாவட்ட ஊராட்சி). Q11 HARD Application 💎 GEM Match the Urban Local Body with its administrative officer: (1) Municipal Corporation (2) Municipality (3) Town Panchayat/Peruratchi (4) Panchayat Union (ஊராட்சி ஒன்றியம்) ▼ ✅ ANSWER 1. Municipal Corporation → IAS Officer / Commissioner 2. Municipality → Commissioner (ஆணையர்) 3.

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: Town Panchayat (Peruratchi) → Executive Officer (EO / செயல் அலுவலர்) 4. Panchayat Union → Block Development Officer (BDO / பிடிஓ / வட்டார வளர்ச்சி அலுவலர்) 💎 LIVE GEM — OFFICER SHORTCUT Teacher's logic: "Peruratchi only needs a regular bus-fare — so only an Executive Officer (EO) is needed, not an IAS officer! " Big body → big officer. Small body → smaller officer. The answer to this common matching question: 1-IAS, 2-Commissioner, 3-EO, 4-BDO .

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: 🏆 PYQ ALERT This matching-type question (Local body ↔ Administrative Officer) is a recurring question in TNPSC Group 4 papers. Answer: Option A (All four correct in sequence).

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: 🏛️ Group 3 — First Institutions & Districts Q12 MEDIUM Factual What is the first Municipality (நகராட்சி) established in Tamil Nadu (and India)? ▼ ✅ ANSWER Valajaapet (வாலாஜாப்பேட்டை) — the first municipality established in Tamil Nadu. Q13 EASY Factual How many total districts are there in Tamil Nadu? ▼ ✅ ANSWER 38 Districts in Tamil Nadu. Q14 MEDIUM Factual Which district was the 38th district added to Tamil Nadu, and on what date? ▼ ✅ ANSWER Mayiladuthurai (மயிலாடுதுறை) — became the 38th district on March 24, 2020 .

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: 🪤 TRAP NOTE Students commonly guess April 20 or wrong months. The correct date is March 24, 2020 . No one in the live session guessed it correctly — the teacher pointed this out! Q15 EASY Factual Which state in India was the first to establish a Town Panchayat / Peruratchi (பேரூராட்சி) system? ▼ ✅ ANSWER Tamil Nadu was the first state in India to introduce the Peruratchi (பேரூராட்சி / Town Panchayat) administrative system. Q16 EASY Factual Which district does Avadi (the 15th Municipal Corporation) belong to?

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: ▼ ✅ ANSWER Avadi belongs to Thiruvallur District (திருவள்ளூர்) . 🪤 TRAP NOTE Do not confuse Avadi with being its own district. The teacher warned: "Don't think Avadi itself is a district. " It remains in Thiruvallur district.

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: 🌾 Group 4 — Panchayati Raj & Village Administration Q17 MEDIUM Factual 💎 GEM How many times does the Gram Sabha (கிராம சபை) meet in a year in Tamil Nadu currently? ▼ ✅ ANSWER As per the 2021 amendment (new government) , the Gram Sabha meets SIX (6) times per year. Old rule (pre-2021): 4 times per year. 💎 LIVE GEM — MEETING DATES The six scheduled Gram Sabha meeting dates are: 📅 January 26 (Republic Day) | May 1 (Labour Day) | August 15 (Independence Day) | October 2 (Gandhi Jayanti) | November 1 | March 22 Old 4 dates: Jan 26, Aug 15, Oct 2

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: The teacher clarified: "If a question says 'meets 3 times' → WRONG. Old = 4, New = 6. " 🪤 TRAP NOTE Sept 15 (Arignar Anna's birthday) is NOT one of the meeting dates. Answer option D in the live session was wrong — Sept 15 was listed as a trap option. Q18 EASY Conceptual Which institution acts as the link bridge (இணைப்பு பாலம்) between the people and the government? ▼ ✅ ANSWER Panchayat (ஊராட்சி) — acts as the direct link between citizens (who live in villages) and the government.

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: 💡 TIP Just as the Governor links the Central Government and State Government, the Panchayat links the people directly to the government. This is the concept-based answer. Q19 MEDIUM Factual How many Panchayat Unions (ஊராட்சி ஒன்றியங்கள்) are there each in Nilgiris and Perambalur districts? ▼ ✅ ANSWER Both Nilgiris and Perambalur districts have 4 Panchayat Unions each (the lowest in Tamil Nadu). 💡 TIP These two districts form separate constituencies.

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: The teacher mentioned Perambalur's separate constituency status but noted slight uncertainty — Villupuram definitely has the MOST (22) while Nilgiris & Perambalur have the FEWEST (4 each). Q20 EASY Factual Who is the administrative officer of a Panchayat Union (ஊராட்சி ஒன்றியம்)? ▼ ✅ ANSWER Block Development Officer (BDO) — பிடிஓ / வட்டார வளர்ச்சி அலுவலர் . Q21 MEDIUM Trap/Trick Find the INCORRECT statement: (A) Every Panchayat has a Gram Sabha (B) Gram Sabha is a permanent body (C) Gram Sabha is the foundation of democracy (D) Gram Sabha meets

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: Gram Sabha does NOT meet 3 times — it meets 6 times (current rule) or was 4 times previously. 💡 TIP A, B, and C are all correct. Gram Sabha = permanent body ✅. It is the "aaṇivēr" (ஆணிவேர் = foundation/taproot) of Indian democracy ✅.

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: 📅 Group 5 — Key Dates, Acts & Appointments Q22 HARD Factual 💎 GEM When was the National Panchayati Raj Day (தேசிய பஞ்சாயத்துராஜ் தினம்) / National Local Body Governance Day declared, and on which date does it fall? ▼ ✅ ANSWER National Panchayati Raj Day = April 24 National Panchayati Raj Act came into force on April 24. Also called: National Gram Panchayat Day / National Local Body Day — ALL refer to April 24 . 💎 LIVE GEM — APRIL DATES MASTER LIST The teacher gave a complete April month exam calendar : 📅 April 14 → Dr.

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: Ambedkar's Birthday (Ambedkar Jayanti) 📅 April 24 → National Panchayati Raj Day | J. Prince Edward Island born — the father of Panchayati Raj) 📅 April 29 → Tamil Language Development Day (Bharathidasan's Birthday) — asked in the LAST Group 4 exam! Don't confuse: April 14 = Ambedkar | April 24 = Panchayati Raj | April 29 = Tamil Day 🏆 PYQ ALERT April 29 (Tamil Development Day / Bharathidasan's birthday) was asked in the LAST Group 4 paper. April 24 (Panchayati Raj Day) is a recurring question across Group 2, 4, and VAO.

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: Q23 HARD Factual 💎 GEM Where was Balwant Rai Mehta (also called J. — the father of Panchayati Raj in India) born? ▼ ✅ ANSWER Balwant Rai Mehta was born in Prince Edward Island, Canada (NOT France, NOT the UK). 🚨 CRITICAL — COMMON EXAM CONFUSION Students read "Francis" in the name and write "France" — WRONG! "Edward" sounds like "England/UK" — also WRONG! He was born in Canada's Prince Edward Island . The teacher said "This question always trips students.

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: " 💎 LIVE GEM — SHORTCUT April 24 = Panchayati Raj Day. On that date, J. Prince Edward (Island) was born. So "Panchayati Raj Day" → "Prince Edward Island, Canada. " Connect the date to the birthplace! Q24 MEDIUM Factual When did Tamil Nadu's Panchayat Act (ஊராட்சி சட்டம்) come into effect? ▼ ✅ ANSWER Tamil Nadu Panchayat Act came into force on April 22, 1994 . 🪤 TRAP NOTE Many students incorrectly write 1992 (the year of the 73rd Constitutional Amendment).

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: The TN Panchayat Act itself came in 1994 — not 1992. The Act number is different from the constitutional amendment year. Q25 MEDIUM Factual 💎 GEM Who coined/named the term "Panchayati Raj"? ▼ ✅ ANSWER The name "Panchayati Raj" was coined by Mahatma Gandhi . Answer = C 💎 LIVE GEM — GANDHI vs NEHRU vs AMBEDKAR • Gandhi → Named Panchayati Raj | Believed "Village is the backbone of the nation" • Nehru → Inaugurated the FIRST Panchayat (in Rajasthan, on April 24, 1959) • Ambedkar → OPPOSED Panchayati Raj — believed villages would reinforce caste h

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: 🗳️ Group 6 — Elections, Age Requirements & Women's Reservation Q26 HARD Factual 💎 GEM What is the minimum age to contest in local body (உள்ளாட்சி) elections in Tamil Nadu? ▼ ✅ ANSWER Minimum age to contest in Local Body elections (Panchayat/Municipality) = 21 years . 💎 LIVE GEM — COMPLETE AGE CHART The teacher gave the full age eligibility list — memorise this table: Position Min. Age Local Body Elections (Panchayat/Municipality) 21 years MLA (Member of Legislative Assembly) / Chief Minister 25 years Lok Sabha MP / Prime Minister 25 years Ra

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: PM = Lok Sabha MP's selection → 25. Rajya Sabha = Upper House → 30. Governor/President = Top constitutional posts → 35. " 🏆 PYQ ALERT Age eligibility questions appear in almost every TNPSC exam. The teacher has a dedicated video on the YouTube channel covering all ages, retirement ages, and tenure for every post. Q27 MEDIUM Factual In the 2011 local body elections, what percentage of seats did women candidates win? ▼ ✅ ANSWER Women won 38% of seats in the 2011 Tamil Nadu local body elections (despite having only 33% reservation at the time).

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: 🪤 TRAP NOTE Everyone in the live session answered wrong (33%, 35%, or 50%). The correct answer is 38% . The reservation was 33% but women won MORE than their reserved share — 38%. Q28 MEDIUM Factual Which year did Tamil Nadu pass a law giving 50% reservation for women in local body elections, and who was the Chief Minister at the time? ▼ ✅ ANSWER 2016 — Tamil Nadu amended the law to provide 50% reservation for women in local body elections. CM at the time: J.

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: Q29 EASY Factual What is the tenure (பதவி காலம்) of local body representatives (பிரதிநிதிகள்)? ▼ ✅ ANSWER 5 years (ஐந்து ஆண்டுகள்) Q30 MEDIUM Factual 💎 GEM Who conducts local body elections in Tamil Nadu? Where is Tamil Nadu's State Election Commission located? ▼ ✅ ANSWER • Local body elections are conducted by the State Election Commission (மாநில தேர்தல் ஆணையம்) — NOT the Central Election Commission. • Tamil Nadu's State Election Commission is located at Koyambedu, Chennai .

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: • The relevant constitutional article: Article 243 . 💎 LIVE GEM — 243K SHORTCUT The teacher's brilliant shortcut: Tamil Nadu has 234 constituencies . Reverse it → 243 = the Article for State Election Commission. "K" for Koyambedu, "O" for start of Koyambedu spelling (கோ). 234 constituencies → REVERSE → Article 243 → K for Koyambedu 🪤 TRAP NOTE "The Central Election Commission conducts local body elections" → this is the WRONG statement that appears as a trick in Q17 (find the incorrect statement).

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: Always: Central = Lok Sabha/Vidhan Sabha. State = Local Bodies. ⚙️ Group 7 — Obligatory & Optional Functions of Local Bodies Q31 MEDIUM Conceptual Which of the following is NOT an Optional Function (விருப்பப் பணி) of a local body? (A) Laying out parks/gardens (B) Establishing a library (C) Street lighting (D) Establishing a playground ▼ ✅ ANSWER C — Street lighting is NOT an optional function. It is an Obligatory Function (அவசியப் பணி) . 💡 EXPLANATION Obligatory Functions (must do): Drinking water supply, Road maintenance, Street lighting, San

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: No — it's a necessity. Can it exist without a library? Yes, for a while — so that's optional. " — Teacher's explanation. Q32 MEDIUM Conceptual Which of the following is NOT an Obligatory Function of a local body? (A) Drinking water supply (B) Street cleaning (C) Library establishment (D) Road construction ▼ ✅ ANSWER C — Library establishment is NOT obligatory. It is an Optional Function. Q33 MEDIUM Factual Who are the officers who attend Gram Sabha (கிராம சபை) meetings?

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: ▼ ✅ ANSWER All of the following attend Gram Sabha meetings: • Block Development Officer (BDO) • Teachers (ஆசிரியர்) — who present reports/proposals • District Collector (may attend) Answer = All of the above

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: 🌍 Group 8 — Democracy Concepts & Global Comparisons Q34 EASY Factual Who said: "Government of the people, by the people, for the people is democracy (மக்களாட்சி)"? ▼ ✅ ANSWER Abraham Lincoln — 16th President of the United States. 🪤 TRAP NOTE Do NOT write "Nehru" or any Indian leader. This quote belongs to Abraham Lincoln, a US President. Q35 EASY Factual What is the birthplace of democracy (மக்களாட்சியின் பிறப்பிடம்)? ▼ ✅ ANSWER Greece (கிரேக்கம்) — Birthplace of democracy, birthplace of political science, birthplace of the constitution.

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: 💎 LIVE GEM — GREEK QUARTET + SHORTCUT The four founders of Greek political thought (in ORDER): சாக் → பிரி → அரி → அள்ளு சாக்ரடீஸ் (Socrates) → பிளாட்டோ (Plato) → அரிஸ்டாட்டில் (Aristotle) → அலெக்சாண்டர் (Alexander) Tamil shortcut: " Chakka pirichchu arisi allu " (சாக்க பிரிச்சு அரிசி அள்ளு) = Soak rice, separate, take rice, scoop — kitchen memory! • Aristotle = Father of Political Science AND Father of Zoology • Alexander = Student of Aristotle, world conqueror Q36 MEDIUM Conceptual 💎 GEM Which country successfully practices Direct Democracy

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: ▼ ✅ ANSWER Switzerland (சுவிட்சர்லாந்து) — successfully practices Direct Democracy. 💎 LIVE GEM — THREE LAND SHORTCUTS Master the three "-land" countries with ONE mnemonic: 1️⃣ New Zealand (நியூசிலாந்து) → "New" thing = First country to give voting rights to women (1893) 2️⃣ Switzerland (சுவிட்சர்லாந்து) → "Switch" = Switch is Direct — click switch = law enacted directly by people = Direct Democracy 3️⃣ Iceland (ஐஸ்லாந்து) → "Ice" preserves things longest = World's oldest parliament (Althing, since 930 AD) NEW → 1893 Women Vote | SWITCH → Direc

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: (Common options: India, UK, USA, Switzerland) ▼ ✅ ANSWER Switzerland — because Switzerland practices Direct Democracy, not Representative/Indirect Democracy. Q38 MEDIUM Factual In which type of democracy does the President hold real power? ▼ ✅ ANSWER Presidential Democracy (அதிபர் மக்களாட்சி) — President holds real executive power. Examples: USA, France . In India: President is a nominal/constitutional head (puppet role — Prime Minister holds real power).

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: 💡 TIP Donald Trump (USA) = Presidential democracy. In UK: Prime Minister is real power = Parliamentary Democracy. India = Parliamentary Democracy (PM is real, President is nominal). Q39 MEDIUM Factual In which year did the UN General Assembly declare September 15 as International Day of Democracy? ▼ ✅ ANSWER The UN General Assembly declared September 15 as International Day of Democracy in 2007 . 💡 TIP — SEPTEMBER 15 TRIPLE SIGNIFICANCE September 15 is: (1) International Day of Democracy | (2) Birthday of Arignar Anna (Tamil Nadu CM) | (3) In

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: New Zealand — 1893 (World's first) 2. USA — 1920 💎 LIVE GEM — ANSWER PATTERN The teacher asked for correct ORDER (1-3-2 is WRONG; 1-2-3 is right). The answer option in the live session was: New Zealand (1) → UK (2) → USA (3) = Option B. All students who got it right chose Option B! Q41 MEDIUM Factual Which country has the world's oldest parliament (நீண்ட காலமாக செயல்படும் நாடாளுமன்றம்)? ▼ ✅ ANSWER Iceland (ஐஸ்லாந்து) — The Althing (established ~930 AD) is the world's oldest continuous parliament.

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: 💎 LIVE GEM — ICE TRICK "Ice preserves things for a very long time — fossils, DNA, food. Iceland's parliament is preserved in 'ice' — it's the oldest! " — Teacher's memory trick. Q42 HARD Factual Which country has the world's oldest written constitution currently in use (தற்போது நடைமுறையில் உள்ள பழமையான அரசியலமைப்பு)? ▼ ✅ ANSWER San Marino (சான் மரினோ) — a small country within Italy — has the world's oldest written constitution still in use. 🚨 CRITICAL NOTE DO NOT confuse with Iceland (oldest parliament) or Greece (birthplace of democracy).

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: San Marino → Oldest constitution currently in use. No shortcut provided by teacher — must memorise: San Marino, located in Italy . Q43 EASY Factual Who is considered the chief architect of India's Constitution (நவீன மனு / இந்திய அரசியலமைப்பின் சிற்பி)? Ambedkar — also called "Baba Saheb" and "Father/Architect of the Indian Constitution. Ambedkar was born on April 14 (Ambedkar Jayanti). He OPPOSED Panchayati Raj (unlike Gandhi who supported it). Both facts frequently tested.

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: Q44 HARD Factual According to statistics, what percentage of Indian citizens believe in their country's democracy? ▼ ✅ ANSWER 79% of Indian citizens believe in their country's democracy (as per the statistic cited in the textbook). Q45 HARD Conceptual Find the INCORRECT statement about democracy: (A) People have significant power in democratic systems (B) In Direct Democracy, people have law-making power (C) In democratic systems, decision-making power is concentrated with the leader (D) In Direct Democracy, all law amendments are approved by t

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: 💡 EXPLANATION B, C, D are all correct statements about Direct Democracy (Switzerland model). A is technically the incorrect one — in India's democracy, a regular citizen cannot pass laws or force a vote. Power is with elected officials. The teacher confirmed D is correct for Direct Democracy (Switzerland). Q46 EASY Conceptual In Direct Democracy (நேரடி மக்களாட்சி), who has the power to make laws? ▼ ✅ ANSWER The voters / citizens themselves (வாக்காளர்கள்) — not their representatives.

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: This is why Switzerland is the best example: citizens directly vote on laws. 🪤 TRAP NOTE "People's representatives" → WRONG answer for Direct Democracy. In India (Representative/Indirect Democracy), MLAs/MPs make laws. In Direct Democracy, citizens themselves do. Don't mix these up. Q47 HARD Factual Features of democratic systems: Which is INCORRECT? (A) Roman Empire — Civilizational growth and global spread (B) Manu Island — Self-governance under monarchy (C) 1615 — Written Magna Carta by United States (D) Aristotle — Foundation of political

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: The Magna Carta was NOT by the United States — it was by King John of England (UK) in 1215 (not 1615). The USA didn't even exist in 1215/1615. 💎 LIVE GEM America was "discovered" by Columbus ~1492 and became independent in 1776. The Magna Carta (1215) was signed by King John of England. These two events are centuries apart — so connecting Magna Carta with "United States" is always wrong. Q48 MEDIUM Factual Identify the correct population threshold for a District Panchayat (மாவட்ட ஊராட்சி வார்டு) division.

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: ▼ ✅ ANSWER District Panchayat areas are divided into wards based on 50,000 population units. Q49 EASY Factual Which state first introduced the Panchayat (in the context of the first Panchayat inauguration by Nehru)? ▼ ✅ ANSWER PM Nehru inaugurated the first Panchayat in Rajasthan on April 24, 1959. Q50 MEDIUM Factual Arrange New Zealand, UK, and USA in the correct chronological order of granting women the right to vote. ▼ ✅ ANSWER Correct order: New Zealand (1893) → UK (1918) → USA (1920) Answer: Option B (1-3-2 if listed as NZ, USA, UK → selec

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: New Zealand (1893) is always first. The UK–USA order (1918 vs 1920) is the tricky part.

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: 📋 Session Summary — 10 Key Takeaways SESSION OVERVIEW Tamil Nadu has 15 corporations (textbook), 21 current, 25 announced . The 15th = Avadi (Thiruvallur District). World's 1st Corporation = London | World's 2nd = Madras | India's 1st = Madras (1688) Population scale: Mavatta Uratchi (50k) → Peruratchi (10k) → Municipality (1L) → Corporation (10L) Kanchipuram = Most Municipalities + Most Tanks + Most Temples. Villupuram = Most Panchayat Unions (22).

chunks.title: QA
chunks.text: Gram Sabha meets 6 times/year (after 2021 amendment). Old rule was 4 times. April calendar: 14 (Ambedkar) → 24 (Panchayati Raj Day + JBP born) → 29 (Tamil Day / Bharathidasan) Democracy countries: Switzerland = Direct | Iceland = Oldest Parliament | New Zealand = First Women's Vote Greek order: Socrates → Plato → Aristotle → Alexander.
