chunks.text: Sovereignty · Equality · Liberty TNPSC Group 1 TNPSC Group 2 TNPSC Group 4 / VAO SSC CGL / CHSL 🎯 50 Questions 💎 18 Gems 🏆 22 PYQ Alerts All Easy Medium Hard 💎 Gems Only 🏆 PYQ Only ▾ Expand All

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: #1 Sovereignty — Origin & Meaning #2 Characteristics of Sovereignty #3 Aspects & Types of Sovereignty #4 De-facto & De-jure #5 Pluralism #6 Equality — Meaning & Importance #7 Types of Equality #8 Liberty & Equality #9 Indian Constitution — Equality #10 Liberty — Meaning & Types #11 Safeguards of Liberty #12 Textbook Evaluation Q&A 📝 Topic Summary 🔮 2026 Predictions ⚡ Flashcard Table 🗂 Master Cheat Card 🔴 CRITICAL — Must Know 🟡 TRAP — Common Error 🟢 TIP — Mnemonic/Shortcut 🔵 PYQ — Past Year Q Alert 💜 GEM — Extra Insight 50 Total Qs 18 Ea

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: #1 Sovereignty — Origin & Meaning Q1 EASY Factual Gr. 4/VAO 🏆 PYQ From which Latin word is 'Sovereignty' derived, and what does it mean? இறையாண்மை என்ற சொல் எந்த லத்தீன் வார்த்தையிலிருந்து வந்தது? அதன் பொருள் என்ன? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER Sovereignty is derived from the Latin word 'superanus' , which means 'supreme' or 'paramount' . 🏆 PYQ ALERT This exact etymology has appeared in TNPSC Group 4 (2018) as a direct one-liner. "Superanus → Supreme" must be memorised verbatim.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 💎 GEM Roman jurists used two alternate terms: summa potestas (highest power) and plenitude potestas (fullness of power). Both refer to the same concept of supreme state authority. Q2 EASY Factual Gr. 4/VAO 🏆 PYQ When was the term 'Sovereignty' first used in Political Science, and who introduced it? அரசியல் அறிவியலில் 'இறையாண்மை' என்ற சொல் எப்போது முதலில் பயன்படுத்தப்பட்டது? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER The political use of 'Sovereignty' dates back to 1576 , with the publication of Jean Bodin's 'The Republic' ( Les Six livres de la Républi

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 🔴 CRITICAL Jean Bodin is called the Father of Sovereignty . This is a direct MCQ trap — Bodin (not Austin, Plato, or Aristotle). Textbook Q1 in Evaluation directly asks this. 🏆 PYQ ALERT TNPSC Group 2 (2016, 2019) asked "Who is the father of sovereignty? " Answer: Jean Bodin. 🪤 TRAP NOTE John Austin is the father of the Legal Theory of sovereignty — NOT the father of sovereignty as a concept. Common confusion! Q3 MEDIUM Conceptual Gr. 2 💎 GEM What does the word 'Sovereign' in the Preamble of the Indian Constitution signify?

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: இந்திய அரசியலமைப்பின் முன்னுரையில் உள்ள 'இறையாண்மை' என்ற வார்த்தை என்ன குறிக்கிறது? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER It means the Indian state has the power to legislate on any subject in conformity with constitutional limitations. Sovereignty here signifies the legal supremacy of the state . 💎 GEM In Indira Gandhi v. Raj Narain (1975) , the Supreme Court added "India as a Sovereign Democratic Republic" as a fundamental element of the Basic Structure of the Constitution.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Sovereignty cannot be amended away. 🏆 PYQ ALERT Indira Gandhi vs Raj Narain case is a TNPSC Group 1 favourite — it established the Basic Structure doctrine AND sovereignty as its component.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: #2 Characteristics of Sovereignty Q4 MEDIUM Factual Gr. 4/VAO 🏆 PYQ 💎 GEM List the 8 characteristics of Sovereignty as described in the textbook. இறையாண்மையின் 8 பண்புகளை பட்டியலிடுக. ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER 1. Permanence — lasts as long as the state lasts 2. Exclusiveness — only one sovereign in one state 3. All-comprehensiveness — applies to every individual & association 4. Inalienability — cannot be alienated (transferred) without destroying the state 5.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Unity and Everlasting — sovereign power is unified and perpetual 6. Indivisibility — the lifeline of sovereignty 7. Absoluteness — unconditional and unlimited 8. Originality — power by its own right, not by anyone's mercy 🟢 TIP — Mnemonic PEAIU-IAO → P ermanence, E xclusiveness, A ll-comprehensive, I nalienability, U nity, I ndivisibility, A bsoluteness, O riginality 🏆 PYQ ALERT TNPSC Group 4 (2017) asked: "Which is the chief characteristic of sovereignty?

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: " Answer: Permanence . 💎 GEM The phrase "King is dead, Long live the King" illustrates Permanence — the king dies but sovereignty passes instantly to the successor. This is a classic exam quote. Q5 EASY Factual Gr. 4/VAO What does the characteristic 'Exclusiveness' of Sovereignty mean? இறையாண்மையின் 'பிரத்யேகத்தன்மை' என்ற பண்பு என்ன குறிக்கிறது? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER There cannot be two sovereigns in one independent state . If two sovereigns exist, the unity of the state will be destroyed.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 🪤 TRAP NOTE Federal systems like India have Union and State governments but sovereignty is NOT divided — the Constitution is the supreme sovereign. Don't confuse division of powers with division of sovereignty. Q6 EASY Factual Gr. 4/VAO What is the significance of 'Inalienability' as a characteristic of Sovereignty? இறையாண்மையின் 'பரிமாற்ற இயலாமை' பண்பின் முக்கியத்துவம் என்ன? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER Sovereignty is the life and soul of the state .

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: It cannot be alienated (given away) without destroying the state itself. 💎 GEM Lieber's quote: "Sovereignty can no more be alienated than a tree can alienate its right to sprout or a man can transfer his life to another without self-destruction. " — This quote-based MCQ appears frequently.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: #3 Aspects & Types of Sovereignty Q7 MEDIUM Conceptual Gr. 2 🏆 PYQ 💎 GEM Distinguish between Internal and External Sovereignty. உள் இறையாண்மை மற்றும் வெளி இறையாண்மை — வேறுபாடு என்ன? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER Internal Sovereignty: Absolute authority over all individuals and associations within the state. External Sovereignty: Equivalent to National Freedom — every state determines its own foreign policy and is independent of other states.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 🏆 PYQ ALERT TNPSC Group 2 (2022) asked to differentiate internal and external sovereignty. External sovereignty = national independence is the key point. 💎 GEM External sovereignty is the basis of International Law — all nations are theoretically equal under it (Sovereign equality of states in the UN Charter, Article 2). Q8 HARD Conceptual Gr. 1 🏆 PYQ 💎 GEM Describe the four types of Sovereignty: Nominal & Real, Legal, Political, and Popular.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: நான்கு வகையான இறையாண்மைகளை விளக்குக. ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER 1. Nominal & Real Sovereignty: In ancient monarchies, kings had Real Sovereignty. After the French Revolution, Council of Ministers became Real Sovereign while kings retained only Nominal power. Legal Sovereignty: The authority that has legal power to issue and enforce final commands. It is always definite, determinate, absolute, and supreme. Political Sovereignty: In representative democracies, it is the entire mass of people (electorate/public opinion).

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: It rests with the class of people who influence the masses. Popular Sovereignty: People are supreme. Historically used as a weapon to challenge absolutism of monarchs. Garner: "Sovereignty of the people means the power of the majority of the electorate. " 🏆 PYQ ALERT "Sovereignty resides with General Will" — said by Rousseau (Textbook Evaluation Q5). General Will = Popular Sovereignty. 🪤 TRAP NOTE TNPSC often asks: "In democracy, sovereignty resides with ___?

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: " Answer: People (not Government, not State, not Media). Textbook Evaluation Q3 confirms this. 💎 GEM John Austin's Legal Theory: Sovereign = determinate human superior whose commands are habitually obeyed. Austin's book: Lectures on Jurisprudence (1832). He is greatest exponent of Monistic Theory.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: #4 De-facto & De-jure Sovereignty Q9 MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 🏆 PYQ 💎 GEM Distinguish between De-facto and De-jure Sovereignty with examples. உண்மையிலான இறையாண்மை மற்றும் சட்டப்பூர்வ இறையாண்மை வேறுபாடு என்ன? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER De-facto Sovereign: Has NO legal claim to sovereignty but possesses it in fact and exercises necessary force to make and enforce laws. De-jure Sovereign: Has a legal claim to sovereignty but does NOT possess it in fact.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Examples from text: Napoleon, Franco, Mussolini, and Hitler were de facto sovereigns — they controlled the legal sovereign from outside/within while Parliament remained the de jure sovereign. 🏆 PYQ ALERT De-facto vs De-jure is a TNPSC classic. Also appears in SSC CGL. Mussolini's "black shirt march on Rome" — October 28, 1922 — is a fact-based MCQ. 💎 GEM Glossary defines: De-jure = "In accordance with law" | De-facto = "Factually, one who rules really and concretely.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: " These Latin phrase meanings themselves are asked in exams. 🟢 TIP Memory trick: De-JURE = JUDICIAL/LEGAL claim (J = Jure = Judicial). De-FACTO = FACT, actually in power . Q10 MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 🏆 PYQ What is the definition of Sovereignty according to Jean Bodin? ஜீன் போடின் கூறிய இறையாண்மையின் வரையறை என்ன? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER "Sovereignty is the absolute and perpetual power of the state , that is, the greatest power to command. " — Jean Bodin 🔴 CRITICAL Key words in Bodin's definition: absolute + perpetual + greatest power to

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: These three elements define sovereignty. Fill-in-the-blank MCQs based on this quote appear frequently.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: #5 Pluralism Q11 MEDIUM Conceptual Gr. 2 🏆 PYQ 💎 GEM What is Pluralism, and who are its chief exponents? பன்மைவாதம் என்றால் என்ன? அதன் முக்கிய ஆதரவாளர்கள் யார்? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER Pluralism is a powerful protest against the Monistic Theory of Sovereignty , which endows the state with supreme and unlimited power. Pluralists believe power should be distributed among various groups/associations. Exponents: Harold J. Figgis · Ernest Barker · G.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Cole · Maciver 🏆 PYQ ALERT TNPSC Group 1 (2015) asked about Pluralism's exponents. Laski is the most important name. Also asked: "Monistic Theory propounded by? " → Austin/Hegel (Evaluation Q2). 💎 GEM The Dawn of Pluralism : Rise of welfare state → rapid increase in state functions → state interfered in every sphere → revolt/reaction against sovereign state → birth of Pluralism. This causal chain is an essay-type answer. 🪤 TRAP NOTE John Austin (1780–1859) is the greatest exponent of Monistic Theory — opposite of Pluralism.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: His book: Lectures on Jurisprudence (1832). Q12 MEDIUM Conceptual Gr. 2 What are the criticisms of Pluralism as discussed in the textbook? பன்மைவாதத்திற்கான விமர்சனங்கள் என்ன? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER 1. The Sovereign State brings about unity and regulates all associations in society. Laws are framed by the state (unlike pluralist belief). State is needed to protect people from the excesses of associations. Division of sovereignty leads to destruction of sovereignty, resulting in anarchy .

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 🔴 CRITICAL "Division of sovereignty leads to destruction of sovereignty and in the absence of sovereignty, anarchy will prevail in society. " — Direct quote-based MCQ.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: #6 Equality — Meaning & Importance Q13 EASY Factual Gr. 4/VAO 🏆 PYQ 💎 GEM What is the etymological meaning of the word 'Equality'? 'சமத்துவம்' என்ற வார்த்தையின் வேர்ச்சொல் அர்த்தம் என்ன? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER Equality is derived from the Latin words aequs / aequalis , meaning 'fair' . It signifies "having the same rights, privileges, treatments, status, and opportunities. " 💎 GEM Equality is treated as a distributive principle — rights, treatments, and opportunities are distributed fairly.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: It relates to the principle of justice — not all circumstances require identical treatment; unequal treatment for those who are unequal is also equality. Q14 MEDIUM Conceptual Gr. 2 🏆 PYQ 💎 GEM What was the famous slogan of the French Revolution related to Equality? What was its context? பிரெஞ்சு புரட்சியின் பிரபல முழக்கம் என்ன? அதன் பின்னணி என்ன? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER The slogan was "Liberty, Equality and Fraternity" . It was raised by French Revolutionaries in the 18th century to revolt against the landed feudal aristocracy and m

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 🏆 PYQ ALERT "Liberty, Equality, Fraternity" appears in TNPSC Group 4 (multiple years) as a fill-in-the-blank regarding French Revolution slogans. Also relevant to Indian Constitution — borrowed in spirit. 💎 GEM The French Declaration of Rights of Man and Citizens declared: "Men are born and always continue free and equal in their rights. " — UN Charter of Human Rights (10 December 1948) also laid stress on social equality. Both dates can be asked.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Q15 HARD Conceptual Gr. 1 💎 GEM According to Laski and Barker, what does Equality mean? Compare their views. லாஸ்கி மற்றும் பார்க்கர் கூறும் சமத்துவ கருத்துகளை ஒப்பிடுக. ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER Laski's view of Equality: • Absence of privileges (equal will for all) • Adequate opportunities for all to realise their personality • Access to social benefits without restriction • Absence of economic and social exploitation Barker's view of Equality: • Fundamental equality of all • Equality of opportunity • Equality of conditions (making lif

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: #7 Types of Equality Q16 MEDIUM Factual Gr. 4/VAO 🏆 PYQ What are the 5 types of Equality mentioned in the textbook? பாடபுத்தகத்தில் கூறப்பட்டுள்ள 5 வகையான சமத்துவங்கள் யாவை? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER 1. Social Equality — no discrimination based on birth, caste, religion, race, colour, gender 2. Civil Equality — equal civil rights and liberties for all citizens 3. Political Equality — equal right to participate in affairs of state 4. Economic Equality — equal opportunities to develop economically 5.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Equality of Opportunity and Education — equal educational and developmental chances 🟢 TIP Mnemonic: SCPEE → Social, Civil, Political, Economic, Education-Opportunity 🏆 PYQ ALERT TNPSC Group 4 repeatedly asks "What type of equality ensures equal right to vote? " → Answer: Political Equality . Q17 EASY Factual Gr. 4/VAO 🏆 PYQ From which Latin words is 'Civil' derived? What does Civil Equality mean? 'சிவில்' எந்த லத்தீன் வார்த்தையிலிருந்து வந்தது?

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: சிவில் சமத்துவம் என்றால் என்ன? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER The word 'civil' is derived from the Latin civilis or civis , meaning 'citizen' . Civil Equality means each citizen is provided with equal civil rights and liberties . Civil laws should treat all individuals equally. 🏆 PYQ ALERT Civil equality is related to Rule of Law — a common MCQ pairing asked in TNPSC Group 4 (2019). Q18 MEDIUM Application Gr. 2 🏆 PYQ 💎 GEM What was the Jasmine Revolution?

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: How is it related to economic inequality? ஜாஸ்மின் புரட்சி என்றால் என்ன? அது பொருளாதார சமத்துவமின்மையோடு எவ்வாறு தொடர்புடையது? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER The Jasmine Revolution was an uprising in Tunisia that ended Ben Ali's 23-year rule in a 29-day struggle . The trigger was the self-immolation of Mohammad Bouazizi , an unemployed man harassed by police in Sidi Bouzid on December 17, 2010 . Jasmine is Tunisia's national flower. This sparked the wider Arab Spring.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 🏆 PYQ ALERT Jasmine Revolution details — country (Tunisia), trigger person (Bouazizi), date (Dec 17, 2010), duration (29 days), ruler deposed (Ben Ali, 23 years) — all are MCQ-worthy. 💎 GEM The textbook explicitly links this to Economic Inequality causing political instability. The Arab Spring = "revolt against a manifestly unjust economic order" — not just democracy vs dictatorship. Q19 MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 🏆 PYQ 💎 GEM Who was Martin Luther King Jr.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: , and what movement did he lead? What is the Indian parallel? மார்டின் லூதர் கிங் ஜூனியர் யார்? அவர் எந்த இயக்கம் வழிநடத்தினார்? இந்திய நிகரழி யார்? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER Martin Luther King Jr. was the leader of the nonviolent Civil Rights Movement in the USA during the 1950s and 1960s — a struggle for Blacks to gain equal rights under US law. Indian parallel: Dr. Ambedkar's liberation movement for millions of dalits was the corresponding historic movement for social equality in India.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 🏆 PYQ ALERT MLK Jr. + Civil Rights + 1950s–60s + nonviolent = standard TNPSC match-the-following. Ambedkar-Dalit movement is asked in all TNPSC exams as a social equality context question. Q20 MEDIUM Conceptual Gr. 2 What is the difference between Natural Inequality and Social Inequality? இயற்கை சமத்துவமின்மை மற்றும் சமூக சமத்துவமின்மை வேறுபாடு என்ன? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER Natural Inequality: Emerges from different capabilities and talents with which people are born.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: These are inherent differences in skills, intelligence, and abilities. Social Inequality: Created by society — unequal treatment based on race, colour, gender, and caste. These are unjust because they arise from exploitation and unequal opportunity, not natural differences. 🟢 TIP Natural inequality = born with it (acceptable in a limited sense). Social inequality = created by society (unacceptable, must be eliminated).

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: #8 Liberty & Equality — Relationship Q21 HARD Conceptual Gr. 1 🏆 PYQ 💎 GEM Explain the ideological perspectives on Equality as described by Andrew Heywood. ஆண்ட்ரூ ஹேவுட் கூறும் சமத்துவம் பற்றிய சித்தாந்த கண்ணோட்டங்களை விளக்குக. ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER Liberals: People are 'born' equal in moral worth → formal equality (legal and political), but social equality sacrifices freedom. Conservatives: Society is naturally hierarchical → dismiss equality as abstract and unachievable.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Socialists: Equality is a fundamental value → endorse social equality (relative in social democracy, absolute in communism). Anarchists: Stress political equality = absolute right to personal autonomy. Anarcho-communists: absolute social equality through collective ownership. Fascists: Racial inequality between nations, but all members of same nation/race are equal in core identity. Feminists: Sexual equality = equal rights and opportunities (liberal feminism) or equal social/economic power (social feminism).

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Ecologists: Biocentric equality — all life forms have equal right to "live and blossom. " 🏆 PYQ ALERT TNPSC Group 1 and Group 2 Mains ask ideology-based questions. "Which ideology believes in biocentric equality? " → Ecologists (Deep ecology). 💎 GEM Laissez-faire (Adam Smith) — free market without government intervention — led to capitalists exploiting workers → gap between rich and poor widened → rise of Socialism. This causal chain explains how economic inequality led to a new ideology.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Q22 MEDIUM Conceptual Gr. 2 🏆 PYQ What is Laissez-faire? Who was its ardent supporter, and what were its consequences? தாரா கொள்கை என்றால் என்ன? அதன் ஆதரவாளர் யார்? விளைவுகள் என்ன? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER Laissez-faire is an economic system where transactions between private parties are free from government intervention (no regulation, tariffs, subsidies). Adam Smith was its ardent supporter. Consequence: capitalists exploited labor → dangerous results in Europe → rise of Socialism to condemn individualism.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 🏆 PYQ ALERT "Das Capital" book written by → Marx (Textbook Evaluation Q9). Connected to Socialism rising against Laissez-faire capitalism. Q23 MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 How can equality be promoted? Name the three methods described in the textbook. சமத்துவத்தை எவ்வாறு மேம்படுத்தலாம்? பாடபுத்தகத்தில் கூறப்பட்ட மூன்று முறைகள் யாவை? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER 1. Establishing formal equality 2. Equality through Differential Treatment 3. Affirmative Action (India: reservation system; other countries: affirmative action programs) 🟢 TIP India's res

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: This is the Constitutional mechanism connecting Article 14 (equality) with Articles 15-16 (anti-discrimination + reservations).

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: #9 Equality in Indian Constitution Q24 HARD Factual Gr. 2 🏆 PYQ 💎 GEM List the Right to Equality provisions (Articles 14–18) in the Indian Constitution. இந்திய அரசியலமைப்பில் சமத்துவ உரிமை விதிகள் (பிரிவுகள் 14–18) என்னென்ன? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER Article 14 — Equality before law & equal protection of laws Article 15 — Prohibition of discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex, place of birth Article 16 — Equality of opportunity in matters of public employment Article 17 — Abolition of Untouchability Article 18 — Aboliti

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Article 14 = most important. Article 21 (Right to Life) also mentioned in context: "No person shall be deprived of life or personal liberty except according to procedure established by law. " 🏆 PYQ ALERT TNPSC Group 4 asks: "Abolition of Untouchability — which article? " → Article 17 . "Abolition of Titles — which article? " → Article 18 . 💎 GEM Fundamental Rights source: Drawn from USA Constitution (Textbook Evaluation Q8). Article 21 also reinforces equality — "reasonable fair and just procedure" must be followed.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 🪤 TRAP NOTE Article 14 gives equality "before law" (British concept) AND "equal protection of laws" (American concept). These are two DIFFERENT concepts in one article. Q25 MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 🏆 PYQ What is Distributive Justice? Give an example. பகிர்வு நீதி என்றால் என்ன? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER Distributive Justice requires a principle of equality where unequal distribution is effected to ensure the principle of equality . Example: The state should not tax a poor person and a rich person equally — progressive taxation is distributi

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 🏆 PYQ ALERT Taxation example for distributive justice is a classic application-type TNPSC question.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: #10 Liberty — Meaning & Types Q26 EASY Factual Gr. 4/VAO 🏆 PYQ 💎 GEM From which Latin word is 'Liberty' derived? What is its meaning? 'சுதந்திரம்' எந்த லத்தீன் வார்த்தையிலிருந்து வந்தது? அதன் பொருள் என்ன? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER Liberty is derived from the Latin word 'Liber' , which means "free from all shackles" or the absence of all restraints. 💎 GEM Inscription on the building of Central Secretariat, New Delhi: "Liberty does not descend upon a person.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: People must raise themselves to liberty. Liberty is a blessing that must be earned in order to be enjoyed. " — This inscription-based MCQ appears in TNPSC. Q27 MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 🏆 PYQ Distinguish between Positive Liberty and Negative Liberty. நேர்மறை சுதந்திரம் மற்றும் எதிர்மறை சுதந்திரம் வேறுபாடு என்ன? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER Positive Liberty: Freedom to do something — the individual should have rights and opportunities to develop his personality.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Negative Liberty: Associated with J. Mill — freedom from restraint. No restriction should be imposed on man and his actions. 🏆 PYQ ALERT "On Liberty" book written by → J. Mill (Textbook Evaluation Q6). Negative liberty concept = J. TNPSC Group 2 (2021) asked about this. 🪤 TRAP NOTE J. Mill = Negative Liberty (freedom FROM). Positive liberty = freedom TO develop personality. Don't mix them up. Q28 HARD Factual Gr. 1 💎 GEM List and explain all 9 types of Liberty described in the textbook.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: பாடபுத்தகத்தில் கூறப்பட்ட 9 வகையான சுதந்திரங்களை விளக்குக. ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER i. Natural Liberty — absence of all restraint (theoretical state of nature) ii. Civil Liberty — enjoyment of rights within law; reflects "Rule of Law" iii. Political Liberty — right to vote, contest elections, hold public views, criticise government, petition iv. Personal Liberty — freedom from arbitrary restraint; right to choose dress, food, education, marriage v.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Economic Liberty — freedom to earn one's daily bread beyond caste/colour/gender vi. Fiscal Liberty — "no taxation without representation" (slogan by middle classes) vii. Domestic Liberty — equal rights for women and children; protection from maltreatment viii. National Liberty — liberty of a sovereign independent nation ix. International Liberty — peace, international cooperation, world federation of states 🟢 TIP — Mnemonic N-C-P-P-E-F-D-N-I → "Nice Cops Protect People Every Friday During Night Investigations" 💎 GEM Fiscal Liberty = "No taxat

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: The Statute of Liberty in New York = universal symbol of freedom and 'hope' for American citizens. Q29 MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 🏆 PYQ 💎 GEM What were the key events in the struggle for Liberty in England and France? (Historical Context) இங்கிலாந்து மற்றும் பிரான்சில் சுதந்திரத்திற்கான போராட்டத்தின் முக்கிய நிகழ்வுகள் என்ன? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER • People revolted against absolute monarchy → Emperor John bowed down (Magna Carta context) • Tudor and Stuart monarchs continued absolute rule → Civil War • King Charles was beheaded; Cromwell

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 💎 GEM "Double citizenship exists in USA" (Textbook Evaluation Q4) — US citizens hold both federal and state citizenship simultaneously. India has single citizenship. Q30 EASY Factual Gr. 4/VAO What are the views of Gettel, G. Cole, Mahatma Gandhi, and Harold Laski on Liberty? சுதந்திரம் பற்றிய கெட்டல், கோல், காந்தி, லாஸ்கி கண்ணோட்டங்கள் என்ன? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER Gettel: "Liberty is the positive power of doing and enjoying those things which are worthy of enjoyment and work.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Cole: "Liberty is the freedom of the individual to express without external hindrances to personality. " Mahatma Gandhi: "Liberty does not mean the absence of restraint but it lies in development of liberty. " Harold Laski: "Liberty is the eager maintenance of that atmosphere in which men have the opportunity to be their best selves. " 🟢 TIP Match-the-quote MCQ pattern: Gandhi's unique view is that liberty = development (not absence of restraint).

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: #11 Safeguards of Liberty Q31 MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 🏆 PYQ 💎 GEM List all 8 safeguards of Liberty as described in the textbook. பாடபுத்தகத்தில் கூறப்பட்ட சுதந்திரத்திற்கான 8 காப்பு நடவடிக்கைகள் யாவை? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER i. Democracy — safest form of government for liberty ii. Constitution — authority of state dwells in the constitution iii. Fundamental Rights — confines the authority of the state (Articles 14–18, 19–22, 23–24, 25–28, 29–30, 32–35) iv.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Decentralization of Powers — divide into central, provincial, local v. Independent Judiciary — free from executive control vi. Economic Security — prerequisite for liberty vii. Rule of Law — all equal before law (England, USA, India) viii. Political Education and Eternal Vigilance — educated citizens are acutely aware of rights 🟢 TIP — Mnemonic D-C-F-D-I-E-R-P → "Democracy Creates Freedom, Decentralization Inspires Every Rights-conscious Person" 🏆 PYQ ALERT "Main objective of Rule of Law is to ensure?

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet

Dicey (not mentioned in text but TNPSC asks this). Rule of Law = equality before law, supremacy of law, no arbitrary power. Q32 MEDIUM Factual Gr. 2 🏆 PYQ 💎 GEM How does the Indian Constitution ensure an Independent Judiciary? இந்திய அரசியலமைப்பு சுதந்திரமான நீதித்துறையை எவ்வாறு உறுதி செய்கிறது?

Supreme Court judges stay in office till 65 years ; High Court judges till 62 years 🔴 CRITICAL Retirement ages: SC judges = 65 , HC judges = 62 . These are direct one-liner MCQs asked in TNPSC Group 4 repeatedly. 🏆 PYQ ALERT TNPSC Group 4 (2018, 2020): "At what age do Supreme Court judges retire? "High Court judges? Q33 MEDIUM Conceptual Gr.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 2 What is the legal maxim 'Ejusdem Generis' and how is it used in context of Article 19? 'Ejusdem Generis' என்ற சட்ட தத்துவம் என்ன? பிரிவு 19-ல் அதன் பயன்பாடு என்ன? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER Ejusdem Generis — general words in a law take their meaning from the specific words used alongside them. Under Article 19, the general word 'freedom' derives its "colour and texture" from the specific freedoms listed with it. Under Article 21, there are no such particular words used.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 💎 GEM [EXPANDED FROM KNOWLEDGE] This distinction matters in courts: Article 19 rights (freedoms of speech, assembly, etc. ) can be limited based on the Ejusdem Generis principle, but Article 21 (right to life) is interpreted more expansively since no limiting words accompany it. Q34 EASY Factual Gr. 4/VAO 🏆 PYQ How does Law protect Liberty? (3 ways from textbook) சட்டம் சுதந்திரத்தை எவ்வாறு பாதுகாக்கிறது? (3 வழிகள்) ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER i.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Law punishes criminals and defends individual rights. Law guarantees enjoyment of rights and duties; state punishes those who harm others. Constitution is the custodian of liberty — it confines state authority and protects fundamental rights. 🏆 PYQ ALERT John Locke: "Where there is no law there is no freedom. " — Quote-to-author MCQ is common in TNPSC Group 4. Q35 EASY Factual Gr. 4/VAO List all 6 categories of Fundamental Rights in the Indian Constitution with their article numbers.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: இந்திய அரசியலமைப்பில் உள்ள 6 அடிப்படை உரிமைகளை பட்டியலிடுக. ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER i. Right to Equality — Articles 14–18 ii. Right to Freedom — Articles 19–22 iii. Right against Exploitation — Articles 23–24 iv. Right to Freedom of Religion — Articles 25–28 v. Cultural and Educational Rights — Articles 29–30 vi. Right to Constitutional Remedies — Articles 32–35 🔴 CRITICAL These 6 categories + article ranges are HIGH FREQUENCY MCQs across all TNPSC groups.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Memorise article ranges. Originally there were 7 (Right to Property - Article 31 removed by 44th Amendment 1978). 💎 GEM Fundamental Rights borrowed from USA Constitution. Ambedkar called Article 32 the "heart and soul" of the Constitution — the Right to Constitutional Remedies.

Father of Sovereignty → a) Bodin Q2. Monistic theory propounded by → a) Austin Q3. In democracy sovereignty resides with → c) People Q4. Double citizenship exists in → a) USA Q5. "Sovereignty resides with General Will" said by → a) Rousseau Q6.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: 1 💎 GEM Examine the relationship between Liberty, Sovereignty, and Law. சுதந்திரம், இறையாண்மை மற்றும் சட்டம் ஆகியவற்றின் உறவை ஆய்வு செய்க. ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER Liberty exists only in a state of order. The sovereign state makes law , and operates through these laws. There is a close three-way relationship: • Without law → no freedom (John Locke) • Without rights → no liberty (Laski) • Sovereignty creates and enforces law; law protects liberty; liberty validates sovereignty 💎 GEM James Madison: "Liberty may be endangered by the abus

(Glossary term) சட்டவிதி (Statute) என்றால் என்ன? ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER A Statute is a written law passed by the legislative body of a country, added to parliamentary laws after appropriate discussion. TNPSC match-the-following from this chapter is a high-probability question every year. Q50 HARD Conceptual Gr. 1 🏆 PYQ 💎 GEM Assertion-Reason: (A) Sovereignty is indivisible. (R) Division of sovereignty leads to anarchy. கூற்று-காரணம்: இறையாண்மை பிரிக்க இயலாதது. அதை பிரிப்பதால் அராஜகம் ஏற்படும்.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: ▾ Show Answer & Details ✅ ANSWER Both (A) and (R) are TRUE , and (R) is the correct explanation of (A). Indivisibility is the lifeline of sovereignty because any attempt to divide it destroys it and results in anarchy — the absence of authority leads to lawlessness. 🏆 PYQ ALERT Assertion-Reason on sovereignty is a TNPSC Group 2 favourite format. The textbook explicitly states: "Division of sovereignty leads to destruction of sovereignty and in the absence of sovereignty, anarchy will prevail.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: Sovereignty from Latin 'superanus' (supreme) — Jean Bodin coined it in 1576; John Austin gave the Legal Theory (1832). Bodin = Father of Sovereignty. 8 Characteristics: Permanence, Exclusiveness, All-comprehensive, Inalienability, Unity, Indivisibility, Absoluteness, Originality. Chief = Permanence. 4 Types of Sovereignty: Nominal/Real, Legal, Political, Popular. In democracy = Popular Sovereignty (Rousseau: General Will). De-facto = rules in fact (Napoleon, Hitler, Mussolini).

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet
chunks.text: De-jure = legal claim but no real power. Pluralism = protest against monistic sovereign state. Exponents: Laski, Figgis, Barker, Cole, Maciver. Opposite: Austin's Monistic Theory. Equality from Latin 'aequus' (fair). 5 types: Social, Civil, Political, Economic, Educational-Opportunity. Slogan: "Liberty, Equality, Fraternity" (French Revolution). Jasmine Revolution (Tunisia, Dec 17, 2010) = economic inequality → political instability. Bouazizi's self-immolation sparked Arab Spring.

chunks.title: QA Crack Sheet

Positive = freedom TO; Negative (J. Mill) = freedom FROM restraint. 9 types: Natural to International. 8 Safeguards of Liberty: Democracy, Constitution, Fundamental Rights, Decentralization, Independent Judiciary (SC=65yrs, HC=62yrs), Economic Security, Rule of Law, Political Education.

Mill 8 Safeguards of Liberty D-C-F-D-I-E-R-P SC=65yrs, HC=62yrs (most tested fact) Jasmine Revolution Tunisia; Bouazizi; Dec 17, 2010; 29 days; Ben Ali 23 yrs Every figure is MCQ-worthy Glorious Revo
