chunks.text: General Tamil (Pothu Tamil) · Bharathiyar Life, Works & Tributes — 100 Q&A Deep Dive 📚 100 Questions 🎯 TNPSC Gr. 4 / VAO ✅ Tamil Literature 💎 27 Live Gems 🔴 15 Trap Questions TOC → Overview Biography Works & Journals Tributes by Others Titles & Names Contemporaries Matching Qs Summary 2026 Predictions Flashcards Cheat Card All Questions 🟢 Easy 🟡 Medium 🔴 Hard 💎 Gems Only 🏆 PYQ Only Easy — Direct recall Medium — Needs context Hard — Tricky / Rare data 🔴 CRITICAL — Must-know 🪤 TRAP — Confusion likely 💡 TIP — Memory trick 🏆 PYQ — Appe

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: 100 Total Questions 42 Easy 38 Medium 20 Hard / Rare 27 Live Gems 18 PYQ Alerts Key Sub-Topics: Bharathiyar's biography & dates · Works (Kuyil Paattu, Kannaan Paattu, Paanchaali Sabatham, Gnanaratham) · Journals (India, Vijaya, Sooddesa Mitran) · Tributes by Rajaji, Namakkal Kavignar, Kavimani, Bharathidasan · Titles & special names · Contemporaries Bharathidasan, Namakkal Kavignar, Kavimani · Matching questions on works & authors · Rare data (Pondicherry stay, death cause, nationalization year)

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Section 1 · Biography & Personal Facts Q1 EASY Factual 🏆 PYQ Who said "நாடும் மொழியும் நமதிரு கண்கள்" — "Both the Nation and the Language are our two eyes"? Tamil: நாடும் மொழியும் நமதிரு கண்கள் என்று கூறியவர் யார்? ✅ Mahakavi Subramaniya Bharathiyar (மகாகவி சுப்பிரமணிய பாரதியார்) This famous quote is one of Bharathiyar's most celebrated patriotic lines, equating the nation and its language as equal and essential — like two eyes of the body. 💎 LIVE GEM The teacher connected Q1 and Q7, which both involve this same quote — Q1 asks for the matchi

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Recognizing this pattern saves time in exams. 🏆 PYQ ALERT This exact quote has appeared in TNPSC General Tamil sections multiple times. Know it in both formats: "Who said this? " and "Choose the matching question form. " Q2 EASY Factual Who sang "நெஞ்சை அல்லும் சிலப்பதிகாரம்" — "The heart-piercing Silappathikaram"? Tamil: நெஞ்சை அல்லும் சிலப்பதிகாரம் என்று பாடியவர் யார்? ✅ Bharathiyar (பாரதியார்) Bharathiyar praised the classical epic Silappathikaram with this phrase, highlighting how emotionally moving it is.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: This is a previous year question (TNPSC). 💡 TIP "நெஞ்சை அல்லும்" (heart-piercing / heart-wrenching) → Bharathiyar. Also: "நெஞ்சு வேண்டும்" → Vairamuthu. Know the difference! Q3 EASY Factual 🏆 PYQ Bharathiyar was born on which date and year? Tamil: பாரதியார் பிறந்த தேதி மற்றும் ஆண்டு என்ன? ✅ December 11, 1882 (டிசம்பர் 11, 1882) 💡 SHORTCUT TIP — LIVE GEM 💎 Remember the number 11 : Born on December 11 → Also received the title "Bharathi" at age 11 → Also died on September 11 .

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Three 11s to remember him! Q4 EASY Factual When did Bharathiyar pass away, and what was the cause of his death? Tamil: பாரதியார் எப்போது இறந்தார்? ✅ September 11, 1921 — due to dysentery (வயிற்று போக்கு / diarrhea) A temple elephant struck him with its trunk in the Parthasarathy temple, Triplicane. After this injury, weakened health led to dysentery, and he passed away on September 11, 1921. 🪤 COMMON TRAP Most students say "He died because an elephant trampled him" — THIS IS WRONG.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: The elephant only pushed/struck him with its trunk. He actually died of dysentery (diarrhea) sometime later. Only 14 people attended his funeral. 💎 LIVE GEM Teacher's analogy: "Just like Newton's apple — the apple didn't kill Newton, it inspired him. Similarly, the elephant didn't kill Bharathiyar; dysentery did. School books simplify for children. " Q5 EASY Factual Where was Bharathiyar born? In which district was it located during his time? Tamil: பாரதியார் பிறந்த ஊர் எது?

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: அது அப்போது எந்த மாவட்டத்தில் இருந்தது? ✅ Ettayapuram (எட்டையாபுரம்) — then part of Tirunelveli District (now Thoothukudi District) 🪤 TRAP NOTE Ettayapuram is NOW in Thoothukudi district, but in Bharathiyar's time it was under Tirunelveli district. Exams may ask for the historical district. Answer: Tirunelveli. Q6 EASY Factual What was Bharathiyar's childhood name (given by his father)? Tamil: பாரதியாரின் குழந்தை பருவப் பெயர் என்ன? ✅ Subbaiah (சுப்பையா) His full name given at birth was Subbaiah / Subramaniam Iyer (சுப்பையா → சுப்பிரமணி ஐயர்).

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Q7 EASY Factual What are the names of Bharathiyar's parents? Tamil: பாரதியாரின் பெற்றோர் பெயர்கள் என்ன? ✅ Father: Chinnaswami Iyer (சின்னசுவாமி ஐயர்) · Mother: Lakshmi Ammal (லட்சுமி அம்மாள்) His mother Lakshmiammal passed away when Bharathiyar was only 5 years old. He wrote a poem about this grief when he was 7. 💎 LIVE GEM — Beautiful Poem The poem Bharathiyar wrote at age 7 about his mother who died when he was 5: "என்னை ஈன்று எனக்கு ஐந்து பிராயத்தில் ஏங்கவிட்டு விண்ணெய்திய தாய்" — "O Mother who bore me and went to heaven when I was just fiv

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: " This may appear as a fill-in-the-blank. Q8 EASY Factual At what age did Bharathiyar receive the title "Bharathi"? When exactly was it conferred? Tamil: பாரதியாருக்கு "பாரதி" பட்டம் எத்தனை வயதில் வழங்கப்பட்டது? ✅ Age 11, in the year 1893, by the King of Ettayapuram (எட்டையாபுர மன்னன்) The title was given to both Subramaniya Bharathi and Somasundara Bharathi at the same event. The Ettayapuram king recognized his poetic genius at this young age. 💎 LIVE GEM The teacher noted this was a Group 2 question that appeared previously.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Two key facts in one: Age = 11, Year = 1893. Both Subramaniya Bharathi and Somasundara Bharathi received the title at the same event. Q9 EASY Factual Who are Bharathiyar's children (daughters)? Tamil: பாரதியாரின் குழந்தைகள் யார்? ✅ Thangammal (தங்கம்மாள்) and Shakuntala (சகுந்தலா) His wife Chellamma (செல்லம்மா) — also called Valli (வள்ளி), his pet name for her — appears in his songs as "Kannamma" (கண்ணம்மா). Q10 MEDIUM Factual At what age did Bharathiyar begin writing Tamil poetry?

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: What poem did he write at age 7? Tamil: பாரதியார் எத்தனை வயதில் கவிதை எழுத ஆரம்பித்தார்? ✅ Age 7 — wrote a poem mourning his mother who died when he was 5 💎 LIVE GEM Key word is "பிராயம்" (பிராயத்தில் = at the age of). The poem says "ஐந்து பிராயத்தில்" = at age five. So: Mother died when he was 5; he wrote the poem at age 7. Two different ages — don't confuse them. Q11 MEDIUM Factual In which year did Bharathiyar go to Pondicherry, and why did he go?

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Tamil: பாரதியார் பாண்டிச்சேரிக்கு எப்போது சென்றார்? ✅ 1908 — to escape arrest by the British due to his revolutionary journalism in "India" newspaper In 1908, during the Swadeshi movement, V. Chidambaram Pillai (Vaauci) and Subramanya Siva were arrested. The British also issued an arrest warrant for Bharathiyar due to his articles in "India" journal. He escaped to Pondicherry (then under French rule). 💎 LIVE GEM His friend in Pondicherry who welcomed him was Kuvalai Kannan (குவளைக் கண்ணன்) — the French government initially refused to give him

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Kuvalai Kannan arranged a house near a cow shed for Bharathiyar to stay. This is out-of-textbook data! Q12 MEDIUM Factual 🏆 PYQ When did Bharathiyar meet Mahatma Gandhi, and where did the meeting take place? Tamil: பாரதியார் மகாத்மா காந்தியை எப்போது சந்தித்தார்? ✅ 1919 — at Rajaji's house in Chennai This is a 7th standard textbook fact. Bharathiyar met Gandhi at the residence of C. Rajagopalachari (Rajaji) in Chennai in 1919. Q13 HARD Factual Who attended Bharathiyar's final rites?

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: How many people were present? Tamil: பாரதியாரின் இறுதி சடங்கில் கலந்துகொண்டவர்கள் யாவர்? ✅ Only 14 people — including Paralasu Nellaiappar (பரலேசு நெல்லையப்பர்) and Kamal Kannan (கமலக் கண்ணன்) Kamal Kannan was the one who helped transport the ailing Bharathiyar in a bullock cart to the hospital. Kuvalai Kannan was the Pondicherry helper; Kamal Kannan was the Chennai helper. 💎 LIVE GEM — Name Distinction Two different helpers: • Kuvalai Kannan (குவளைக் கண்ணன்) → helped in Pondicherry • Kamal Kannan (கமலக் கண்ணன்) → helped in Chennai; transporte

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Who was the Chief Minister then, and from whom were the works purchased? Tamil: பாரதியாரின் நூல்கள் எந்த ஆண்டு தமிழ்நாடு அரசால் நாட்டுடைமையாக்கப்பட்டன? ✅ 1949 — by Chief Minister Omandur Ramaswami Reddiar (ஓமந்தூர் ராமசாமி ரெட்டியார்), purchased from A. Meiyappa Chettiar (ஏவிஎம் மெய்யப்ப செட்டியார்) 💎 LIVE GEM AVM = A. Meiyappa Chettiar (the famous film studio owner). He had purchased Bharathiyar's manuscripts/rights. When nationalized in 1949, anyone could freely use Bharathiyar's works — that's why they are copyright-free today.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Section 2 · Works, Journals & Publications Q15 EASY Factual 🏆 PYQ What are the three great works (மூப்பெரும் இலக்கியங்கள்) Bharathiyar composed while in Pondicherry? Tamil: பாரதியார் புதுவையில் இயற்றிய மூப்பெரும் இலக்கியங்கள் யாவை? Kuyil Paattu (குயில் பாட்டு) · 2. Kannan Paattu (கண்ணன் பாட்டு) · 3. Paanchaali Sabatham (பாஞ்சாலி சபதம்) 🏆 PYQ ALERT This has appeared in TNPSC multiple times. All three were composed during his Pondicherry exile (1908–1919).

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Q16 MEDIUM 🪤 TRAP The lines "நாட்டினிலும் காட்டினிலும் நாளெல்லாம் நன்றொளிக்கும் பாட்டினிலும் நெஞ்சை பறிகொடுத்தேன் பாவியேன்" appear in which work? ✅ Kuyil Paattu (குயில் பாட்டு) 🪤 MAJOR TRAP — LIVE DISCUSSION Almost ALL students answered "Paappa Paattu" — WRONG! The correct answer is Kuyil Paattu. Teacher stressed this repeatedly. • Paappa Paattu = About children/women • Kuyil Paattu = About beauty of music/nature (the kuyil/cuckoo is the symbol) 💎 LIVE GEM Kuyil Paattu is about the beauty of music.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Kannan Paattu is a Pillai Tamil (Pillaittamizh — a genre where a divine being is treated as a child) — Bharathiyar treated Lord Krishna as his child in this work. Q17 EASY Factual What is Tamil's first prose work (தமிழின் முதல் உரைநடை இலக்கியம்) — and who wrote it? ✅ Gnanaratham (ஞானரதம்) — by Bharathiyar Gnanaratham is classified as Tamil's first prose work (உரைநடை இலக்கியம்). It falls under the category of prose literature (உரைநடை — prose / non-verse writing).

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Q18 EASY Factual What was the first journal in which Bharathiyar published a poem? (First poem journal) Year? Tamil: பாரதியாரின் முதல் பாடல் வெளிவந்த இதழ் எது? ✅ Viveka Banu (விவேக பானு) — 1904, published in Madurai V. Chidambaram Pillai (Vaauci) was associated with this journal. After working at Viveka Banu, Bharathiyar got a major offer from Sooddesa Mittiran and moved there. Q19 MEDIUM Factual 🏆 PYQ In which year did Bharathiyar join Sooddesa Mittiran (சுதேசமித்திரன்) as sub-editor?

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Tamil: பாரதியார் சுதேசமித்திரன் இதழில் துணையாசிரியராக சேர்ந்த ஆண்டு? ✅ 1904 Sooddesa Mittiran was founded in 1891 and became a daily newspaper in 1899. Iyer (ஜிஎஸ் ஐயர்) was the chief editor. Bharathiyar joined as sub-editor (துணையாசிரியர்) in 1904. 💎 LIVE GEM — Key Story G. Iyer restricted Bharathiyar from writing against the British (fearing newspaper ban). Because Bharathiyar had strong views, he left and co-founded "India" journal — where he had total freedom.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: This explains why "India" was eventually banned and he fled to Pondicherry in 1908. Q20 MEDIUM 🪤 TRAP In which journal did Bharathiyar translate Bankim Chandra Chatterjee's "Vande Mataram" into Tamil? Tamil: வந்தே மாதரம் பாடலை தமிழில் மொழிபெயர்த்து பாரதியார் எந்த பத்திரிகையில் வெளியிட்டார்? ✅ Vijaya (விஜயா) journal 💎 LIVE GEM — Smart Shortcut Vande Mataram praises India as a mother (female form). Vijaya = the journal for women readers. So: Vande Mataram (female/mother theme) → Vijaya (women's journal).

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: India = the general national journal. This shortcut prevents confusion between the two journals. 🪤 TRAP Many will answer "India" journal — WRONG. It was "Vijaya. " Know: India journal = general patriotic writing; Vijaya = women-focused journal where Vande Mataram Tamil translation appeared. Q21 EASY Factual What was Bharathiyar's first poetry collection (முதல் கவிதை தொகுப்பு)? When was it published? ✅ Sooddhesa Keethangal (சுதேச கீதங்கள்) — 1908 This was published in 1908 before he left for Pondicherry.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: After reaching Pondicherry, he wrote the three major works (Kuyil Paattu, Kannan Paattu, Paanchaali Sabatham). Q22 MEDIUM Factual Who published Bharathiyar's poems for the first time? Tamil: பாரதியார் பாடல்களை முதலில் வெளியிட்டவர் யார்? ✅ Krishnaswami Iyer (கிருஷ்ணசுவாமி ஐயர்) Krishnaswami Iyer was also the person who frequently addressed Bharathiyar as "Mahakavi" (மகாகவி), thus popularizing that title in common usage. Q23 MEDIUM Factual Who published the book "Bharathiyar's Letters" (பாரதியாரின் கடிதங்கள்)?

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Padmanabhan (ரா. பத்மநாபன்) This is mentioned in the 12th standard Tamil textbook, First Unit — in the box section about the letter Bharathiyar wrote to his brother Nellaiyappar. 💎 LIVE GEM — Distinguish! • Published "Bharathiyar's Letters" = Ra. Padmanabhan • Paralasu Nellaiappar = called Bharathiyar "my younger brother (தம்பி)". So Q: "Who called Bharathiyar his younger brother? " → Paralasu Nellaiappar (NOT the publisher of letters). Q24 EASY Factual Where did Bharathiyar start the journal "Sooriyodayam" (சூரியோதயம்)?

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: ✅ Pondicherry (பாண்டிச்சேரி) All three major journals associated with Bharathiyar's Pondicherry period — India, Vijaya, Sooriyodayam — were run from Pondicherry. Note: India journal was started earlier in Chennai. Q25 MEDIUM Factual What were Bharathiyar's English-language journals/magazines? Tamil: பாரதியாரின் ஆங்கில இதழ்கள் என்னென்ன? Balabaratham (பாலபாரதம்) — monthly magazine · 2. Karmayogi (கர்மயோகி) — also an English journal 💎 LIVE GEM Balabaratham = monthly (மாத இதழ்).

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Karmayogi = also English. These are the two English periodicals by Bharathiyar. Tamil journals: India, Vijaya, Sooddesa Mittiran (as sub-editor), Sooriyodayam. Q26 EASY Factual What is Bharathiyar's dream magazine that he could never launch due to lack of funds? ✅ Sithiravali (சித்திராவலி) — a pictorial / illustrated magazine with political cartoons Bharathiyar wanted to launch India's first Tamil illustrated magazine with political cartoons. He even planned the first Tamil newspaper to publish political caricatures in red ink.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Due to financial difficulties, Sithiravali could never be launched. The "India" journal did publish some caricatures in red color. Q27 EASY Factual What is the first line (முதல் வரி) of Bharathiyar's poetry? Tamil: பாரதியாரின் கவிதையின் முதல் வரி என்ன? ✅ "மனதில் உறுதி வேண்டும்" — "We need firmness of mind" 🔴 CRITICAL This is also the most famous line for TNPSC exams — "What was the first line of Bharathiyar's poem? " → மனதில் உறுதி வேண்டும். Essential for any government exam aspirant.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Q28 MEDIUM Factual What was the last song Bharathiyar sang in a public gathering? Tamil: பாரதியார் பொதுமேடையில் பாடிய கடைசி பாட்டு எது? ✅ "பாரத சமுதாயம் வாழ்கவே" — "Long live the Indian community/society"

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Section 3 · Tributes, Quotes & Descriptions by Others Q29 HARD Factual 🏆 PYQ "Tilak sowed the seed — it sprouted as Bharathiyar; Gandhi sowed the seed — it sprouted as Rajaji. " Who said this? Tamil: திலகர் விதைத்த விதை பாரதியாக முழைத்தது; காந்தி விதைத்த விதை ராஜாஜியாக முழைத்தது — என்று கூறியவர் யார்? ✅ Salem Vijayaraghavachariar / Salem Vijayaraghavachariyar (சேலம் விஜயராகவாச்சாரியார்) 💎 LIVE GEM — Important Distinction Many students said "Rajaji" — WRONG!

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: It was Salem Vijayaraghavachariyar (Rajaji's political guru). He made this comparative statement about both. Rajaji is being compared in the statement, so Rajaji could NOT have said it about himself. 🏆 PYQ ALERT This appeared in TNPSC Group 2 papers. The TNPSC school textbook version says: "Tilak sowed seed → Bharathi; Gandhi sowed seed → Namakkal Kavignar" — attributed differently. The out-of-textbook version (Rajaji connection) = Salem Vijayaraghavachariyar.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Q30 HARD Factual Who described Bharathiyar as "An Avatar Purusha — Tamils should treat his works as their Veda"? Tamil: "பாரதியார் ஒரு அவதார புருஷர், இவர் நூலை தமிழர் வேதமாக கொள்வாராக" என்று கூறியவர் யார்? ✅ Paralasu Nellaiappar (பரலேசு நெல்லையப்பர்) — who called Bharathiyar his younger brother Q31 MEDIUM Factual Who said: "When I think of Bharathiyar, the spirit of freedom surges through me — I feel the pride of being an Indian"? Tamil: பாரதியை நினைத்துவிட்டாலும் சுதந்திரத்தின் ஆவேசம் சுருக்கென்று ஏறும் — என்று கூறியவர் யார்?

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: ✅ Namakkal Kavignar (நாமக்கல் கவிஞர் — V. Ramalingam Pillai) 💎 LIVE GEM — Keyword The keyword in this quote is "நல் இருமாப்பு" (noble pride / self-respect). Whenever you see "இருமாப்பு" (pride/self-esteem) in a quote about Bharathiyar, the answer is Namakkal Kavignar. He was also the first State Poet (அரசவை கவிஞர்) — appointed by Rajaji. Don't confuse with Rajaji being the one who appointed him. Q32 MEDIUM Factual Kavimani said: "In his Paappa Paattu, I gave away my heart.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: " Who is being described here, and who said this? Tamil: "பாட்டுக்கொரு புலவன் பாரதி, இவரின் பாப்பா பாட்டில் நெஞ்சை பறிகொடுத்தேன்" என்று கூறியவர் யார்? ✅ Kavimani (Kavimani Desiga Vinayagam Pillai) said this about Bharathiyar (பாரதியார்) 💎 LIVE GEM — Comparison Table • Namakkal Kavignar about Bharathiyar = "இருமாப்பு" (noble pride) • Kavimani about Bharathiyar = "நெஞ்சை பறிகொடுத்தேன்" (gave my heart) • Paralasu Nellaiappar about Bharathiyar = "அவதார புருஷர்" (divine incarnation) Learn all three separately — exam may give the quote and ask who s

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Q33 HARD Factual Who said "Gnanaratham போல் ஒரு நூல் எழுதுவதற்கு நான்நிலத்தில் ஆளில்லை" (There is no one on this earth who can write a work like Gnanaratham)? ✅ Bharathidasan (பாரதிதாசன்) 💎 LIVE GEM Despite being a close disciple/follower of Bharathiyar, Bharathidasan praised Bharathiyar's prose work Gnanaratham with this strong statement. Most students incorrectly answered "Bharathiyar" (self-praise) or "Rajaji. " Q34 MEDIUM Factual Who is called "Bharathi's younger brother (தம்பி)"?

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: What is notable about this person? ✅ Paralasu Nellaiappar (பரலேசு நெல்லையப்பர்) called Bharathiyar his younger brother. His actual brother's name was Sanmugasundaram Pillai (சண்முகசுந்தரம் பிள்ளை) — also known as "Vande Mataram Pillai" 💎 LIVE GEM — Rare Data Paralasu Nellaiappar's elder brother = Sanmugasundaram Pillai. He is the ONLY person in all of India to have the title "Vande Mataram Pillai" — because Bankim Chandra Chatterjee's phrase "Vande Mataram" was used as his honorific.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: No one else in India has this title. Q35 MEDIUM Factual Who said Bharathiyar was "the credit of directing poetry toward ordinary/common people" (எளிய மக்களை நோக்கி கவிதை கருவி திருப்பி அமைத்த பெருமை)? ✅ Bharathiyar himself — this describes his contribution; the credit goes to Bharathiyar Q36 MEDIUM Factual Who called Bharathiyar "the pinnacle of knowledge" (அறிவின் சிகரம்)? ✅ Vaauci (வாவு சிதம்பரனார் — V. Chidambaram Pillai, also called Vaauci Sithambaranar) Vaauci Sithambaranar, Bharathiyar's close friend, called him "அறிவின் சிகரம்" (pinnacle

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Section 4 · Titles, Special Names & Honorary Labels Q37 MEDIUM Factual 🏆 PYQ Who officially gave Bharathiyar the title "Mahakavi" (மகாகவி)? Who frequently USED the title in everyday address? ✅ Officially conferred: Va. Ramaswami Iyengar (வா. ராமசாமி ஐயங்கார்) · Frequently used/popularized: Krishnaswami Iyer (கிருஷ்ணசுவாமி ஐயர்) 💎 LIVE GEM — Critical Distinction! Two DIFFERENT answers for two DIFFERENT questions: • "Who GAVE the Mahakavi title? Ramaswami Iyengar • "Who FREQUENTLY CALLED him Mahakavi / popularized the title?

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: " → Krishnaswami Iyer Teacher's analogy: Just like how Periyar was first called "Periyar" by Sithambaranar, and others later used it — someone can popularize a title without officially bestowing it. Ramaswami Iyengar also served as assistant editor (துணை ஆசிரியர்) to Bharathiyar. Both facts — title-giver AND assistant editor — are about the same person. Q38 HARD 💎 GEM List all the special names/titles of Bharathiyar (சுப்பிரமணிய பாரதியார்'s honorary names).

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: ✅ Complete list of Bharathiyar's special names/titles: Special Name Meaning / Reason மகாகவி (Mahakavi) Great Poet — official title தேசிய கவி (Deshiya Kavi) National Poet — patriotic poems செல்லிதாசன் (Sellithaasan) Self-given name meaning "cherished devotee" முண்டாசு கவி (Mundasu Kavi) "Turban Poet" — always wore a turban பைந்தமிழ் தேர்பாகன் (Paindamizh Therpagan) "Charioteer of fresh Tamil" — master of Tamil குப்பம்மாள் செல்வன் (Kuppaammaal Selvan) Cherished son of his aunt Kuppaammaal who raised him அமரக்கவி (Amarakavi) Immortal Poet முன்னறி

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Key unusual ones: முண்டாசு கவி (turban), பைந்தமிழ் தேர்பாகன் , குப்பம்மாள் செல்வன் (aunt who raised him), சாவித்திரி (pen name for women's topics). Q39 MEDIUM Factual Who formed a literary association (சங்கம்) in Bharathiyar's name? Tamil: பாரதிக்காக சங்கத்தை அமைத்தவர் யார்? ✅ Kalki (கல்கி — R. Krishnamurthy) Q40 MEDIUM Factual Who drew/painted the most famous portrait of Bharathiyar? What did this person do in 1932? Tamil: பாரதியாரின் படத்தை வரைந்தவர் யார்?

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: ✅ Aariyam alias Pashyam (ஆரியம் அலியாஸ் பாஷயம்) — on January 26, 1932, this person hoisted the flag at Fort St. George, Chennai 💎 LIVE GEM January 26, 1932 = Before independence! This was a symbolic act of patriotism — hoisting the Indian flag at the British government's Fort St. George (now Tamil Nadu Secretariat). The person who painted the iconic Bharathiyar portrait was also a freedom fighter.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Section 5 · Contemporaries — Bharathidasan, Namakkal Kavignar, Kavimani Q41 MEDIUM Factual Who is described as "Tamil Nadu's Rasul Gamzatov" (தமிழ்நாட்டின் ரசூல் கம்சதேவ்)? Tamil: தமிழ்நாட்டின் ரசூல் கம்சதேவ் என அழைக்கப்படுவர் யார்? ✅ Bharathidasan (பாரதிதாசன்) — because he was deeply influenced by Russian/Soviet ideology 💎 LIVE GEM — Distinction • Bharathiyar → influenced by the West (USA/UK) — specifically by American poet Walt Whitman in prose poetry • Bharathidasan → influenced by Russia/Soviet ideology This directional distinction (West v

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Q42 EASY Factual In whose style did Bharathiyar begin writing prose poetry (வசன கவிதை)? Who is this American poet? ✅ Walt Whitman (வால்ட் விட்மன்) — American poet whose free verse style inspired Bharathiyar Q43 MEDIUM 🪤 TRAP Who said "தொண்டு செய்வாய் தமிழுக்கு துறைதோறும் துடித்தெழுந்தே" (Serve Tamil — arise with passion in every field)? This is a GROUP 2 previous year question. ✅ Bharathidasan (பாரதிதாசன்) — NOT Bharathiyar 🪤 MAJOR TRAP — LIVE DISCUSSION ALL students answered Bharathiyar — WRONG!

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: The teacher predicted this exact trap. Bharathiyar lived before independence, so he couldn't have spoken about developing Tamil across all fields (post-independence development context). "துறைதோறும்" (in every field/sector) — Bharathidasan, post-independence, spoke about Tamil development across all sectors. 💎 LIVE GEM Don't default to Bharathiyar for every Tamil-related quote. When the quote is about Tamil development/progress across sectors, think Bharathidasan.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Q44 MEDIUM 🪤 TRAP "தொண்டு செய்து பழுத்த பழம் தூய தாடி மார்பில் விழும்" — about whom was this written, and who wrote it? ✅ Bharathidasan wrote this about Periyar (E. Ramasamy Naicker) 🪤 TRAP Many students will say "about Bharathiyar" or "Bharathiyar wrote it about Periyar. " Wrong on both counts. Bharathidasan wrote it; it's about Periyar. Bharathiyar and Periyar had ideological conflicts — they were NOT allies. So Bharathiyar could NOT have praised Periyar.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Q45 MEDIUM Factual Who is the "Kavimani" (கவிமணி) — what is their full name and special title? ✅ Kavimani Desiga Vinayagam Pillai (கவிமணி தேசிக விநாயகம் பிள்ளை) — also called "Nanchil Nadu Kavignar" (நாஞ்சில் நாட்டு கவிஞர்) and "Gandhi Kavignar" (காந்திய கவிஞர்) Q46 MEDIUM Factual Match the poets with their special names: Deshiya Kavi (தேசிய கவி), Puratchi Kavignar (புரட்சி கவிஞர்), Gandhi Kavignar (காந்திய கவிஞர்), Kavimani (கவிமணி). ✅ Full matching: Special Name Poet Note தேசிய கவி (National Poet) Bharathiyar Patriotic songs புரட்சி கவிஞர் (R

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: 1st State Poet = Namakkal Kavignar (appointed by Rajaji); 2nd State Poet = Kannadasan (appointed by MGR). Rajaji and Namakkal Kavignar had fights; Kannadasan and MGR had fights — ironic that the appointers and appointees clashed!

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Section 6 · Matching Questions (Works ↔ Authors / Journals ↔ Poets) Q47 MEDIUM Factual Match the works with their authors: (1) Sooriyakaanthi (சூரியகாந்தி) (2) Gnanaratham (ஞானரதம்) (3) Eluthu (எழுத்து) (4) Kuyil Paattu (குயில் பாட்டு) ✅ Matching answers: Work Author சூரியகாந்தி (Sooriyakaanthi) Na. காமராசன்) ஞானரதம் (Gnanaratham) Bharathiyar (பாரதியார்) எழுத்து (Eluthu) Si. Chellappa (சிசு செல்லப்பா) குயில் பாட்டு (Kuyil Paattu) Bharathiyar (பாரதியார்) 🪤 TRAP "Kuyil Paattu — Bharathidasan" is a common wrong answer.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Kuyil Paattu = Bharathiyar. Do not confuse! Q48 MEDIUM Factual Match the journals with the editors/poets: Thaesabhaktan (தேசபக்தன்), India (இந்தியா), Vijaya (விஜயா), and Kuyil (குயில்) / Ponni (பொன்னி) ✅ Matching answers: Journal Person தேசபக்தன் (Thaesabhaktan) Thiruveeka (திருவிக்கா — Thiruvi. Kalyanasundaram) இந்தியா (India) Bharathiyar (பாரதியார்) விஜயா (Vijaya) Bharathiyar (பாரதியார்) குயில் / பொன்னி Bharathidasan (பாரதிதாசன்) தீபம் (Deepam) Parthsarathi (பார்த்தசாரதி) Q49 HARD Factual Match the works with authors: Suthaanandha Bharathi (ச

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: The book is about the Buddha. So Kavimani wrote about Buddha's life. "Nava Aasiya Jothi" (நவீன ஆசிய ஜோதி) = Modern Light of Asia → refers to Nehru (Jawaharlal Nehru was called the Modern Light of Asia). Q51 HARD Factual Match the works/journals: Kambar (கம்பர்), Seethalaaseer (சீட்டுக் கவி), Sekkilaar Pillai Tamil (சேக்கிழார் பிள்ளை தமிழ்), Zoolamani (சூலாமணி) ✅ Matching: Item Poet/Connection கம்பர் (Kambar) Bharathiyar praised him: "Kamban isaitta kaviyellaam naan" (கம்பன் இசைத்த கவியெல்லாம் நான்) சீட்டுக் கவி (Seethalaaseer / Note Poet) Bhara

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Pichamurthy–India and Vijaya; Meera–Annam Vidu Thoodu (அண்ணம்விடு தூது); Pererunchitranar–Thenmozhi, Thamizh Chittu; Hanuman–Nava Indiya ✅ Wrong pairs: Pairs 1 and 4 are incorrect (options 1 and 4) Pair Correct Pairing Annam Vidu Thoodu (அண்ணம்விடு தூது) Meera (மீரா) — Correct India & Vijaya (இந்தியா & விஜயா) Bharathiyar — NOT Pichamurthy Nava India & Hanuman Pichamurthy (பிச்சமூர்த்தி) — NOT Hanuman 💎 LIVE GEM For wrong-pair questions, don't just look at the answer options as A/B/C/D.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Identify the correct matching yourself first, then see which option represents the "wrong pair. " This self-identification method is faster in exams. Section 7 · Bharathiyar's Views, Actions & Political Stands Q54 MEDIUM Factual In the 1906 Calcutta Congress session, what did Bharathiyar oppose? (This is a TNPSC-relevant statement + reason question) ✅ He opposed the attempt by moderates to appoint a British person (Steed) as the chairman of the Calcutta Congress session — calling it "foolishness" (மூடத்தனம்) Both statements in the Q55 assertion

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: (2) He condemned as "moodathanam" (foolishness) the effort to appoint an Englishman as the Congress session's president. Q55 MEDIUM Factual Statement: Bharathiyar had faith in political assassination and armed revolution. Tamil: பாரதியாருக்கு அரசியல் கொலையிலும் ஆயுத புரட்சியிலும் நம்பிக்கை இருந்தது. ✅ FALSE — Bharathiyar did NOT believe in political assassination or armed revolution This is a trap statement. He believed in constitutional/democratic methods and social reform, not violence.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: The Ash murder case (Paanjinaatham Ash kolai) shocked him — he described it as an "unexpected tragedy," not a cause for celebration. Q56 MEDIUM Factual Which of these is WRONG about Bharathiyar? (a) He opposed the caste system. (b) He removed his sacred thread (பூணூல்). (c) He participated in all temple festivals with his family. (d) He opposed widow remarriage. ✅ Option (d) is WRONG — Bharathiyar SUPPORTED widow remarriage, he did not oppose it (a) Correct — he opposed caste.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: (b) Correct — he gave his sacred thread to Dalit men at his rooftop. (c) Correct — participated in temple festivals. (d) WRONG — he was a progressive reformer who SUPPORTED widow remarriage, women's education, and women's freedom. Q57 MEDIUM Factual "Laws and rules are made for people — people are not made for laws and rules. " Whose statement is this? Tamil: சட்டமும் விதிமுறைகளும் மக்களுக்காகவே ஏற்படுத்தப்பட்டவை. சட்டத்துக்காகவும் விதிமுறைகளுக்காகவும் மக்கள் இல்லை — யாரது?

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: ✅ Kamarajar (காமராஜர்) 🪤 TRAP Many students said "Bharathiyar" — WRONG! This is a Kamarajar quote. Not every inspiring Tamil quote is by Bharathiyar. The teacher specifically warned: "Don't default to Bharathiyar for every quote. " Q58 HARD Factual "Tamil Nadu cannot exhaust its merit — for the glory of getting the first resolution in the first Congress session goes to Tamil Nadu's own G. " Who said this? ✅ Bharathiyar (பாரதியார்) — written in the "India" journal about G.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Iyer's historic role in the Indian National Congress G. Iyer (Suddhesamittiran's editor) moved the first resolution at the first Congress session. Bharathiyar praised this in the "India" journal, calling it Tamil Nadu's pride. Q59 HARD Factual Bharathiyar translated Bankim Chandra Chatterjee's "Vande Mataram" into Tamil. Who is Bankim Chandra Chatterjee and what is his connection to Paralasu Nellaiappar's brother? ✅ Bankim Chandra Chatterjee introduced the slogan "Vande Mataram.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: " Paralasu Nellaiappar's elder brother Sanmugasundaram Pillai is the ONLY person in India called "Vande Mataram Pillai" — a unique honorific derived from Bankim's phrase. 💎 LIVE GEM — Chain Connection Bankim Chandra Chatterjee → wrote "Vande Mataram" → Bharathiyar translated it → Paralasu Nellaiappar's brother = Vande Mataram Pillai. Three-step chain that can generate 3 different questions from one concept. Q60 HARD Factual Which statement about Bharathiyar's "India" journal is WRONG?

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: (a) Bharathiyar praised Vanga's resistance to partition. Iyer's Swadeshi activity was praised in "India" journal. Chidambaram's ships were described as "two devoted sons of Mother India. " (d) Bharathiyar was the extreme nationalist representative of Tamil Nadu. ✅ Option (a) is WRONG — Bharathiyar did NOT praise Bengal (Vanga) for partition resistance in the India journal. He wrote critically about Bengal partition in Sooddesa Mittiran, not in "India.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: " Key: The Bengal partition (1905) criticism was written in Sooddesa Mittiran. The India journal was founded after he left Sooddesa Mittiran precisely because G. Iyer restricted his anti-British writing there. Q61 MEDIUM Factual In which year did Bharathiyar organize a massive public meeting in Chennai to celebrate Self-Rule Day (சுயராஜ்ய தினம்)? ✅ 1908 Q62 HARD Factual Who did Bharathiyar invite to speak in Chennai in 1907? (A specific Congress leader) ✅ Bipin Chandra Pal (பிபின் சந்திரபால்) — in 1907, Bharathiyar invited him to deliver a spee

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: ✅ Tilak (திலகர் — Bal Gangadhar Tilak) — "Tenets of the New Party" was Tilak's speech which Bharathiyar translated and published in Tamil 💎 LIVE GEM Tilak and Gandhi had no direct literary connection with Bharathiyar as translator. It was Tilak's speech that Bharathiyar translated. Also: Bharathiyar himself wrote "Engal Congress Yaathirai" (எங்கள் காங்கிரஸ் யாத்திரை) — don't confuse these two documents. Q64 EASY Factual Bharathiyar said "நமக்கு தொழில் கவிதை, நாட்டிற்கு உழைத்தல்" — "Our work is poetry; our service is to the nation.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: " To whom is this attributed? ✅ Bharathiyar (சுப்பிரமணிய பாரதியார்) Also associated: "இமை பொழுதும் சேராது இருத்தல்" — meaning not a moment's rest from work for the nation. Both quotes are from Bharathiyar. Q65 MEDIUM Factual "Kambhan iasaiththa kaviyellaam naan, maadan siththar maadan" — who said this, and what does it mean? Tamil: கம்பன் இசைத்த கவி எல்லாம் நான் ஒரு மாடன் சித்தர் என்று கூறியவர் யார்? ✅ Bharathiyar — "I am a modern sage (maadan sitthar) who encompasses all the poetry that Kambar composed.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: " He claimed to be the culmination of the Tamil poetic tradition. 💎 LIVE GEM Bharathiyar frequently praised Avvaiyar and Kambar. Among living Tamil poets, he considered himself in the same league. "Maadan" = modern-day sage/wanderer. This type of self-description quote can appear in fill-in-the-blank questions. Q66 EASY Factual "யாமறிந்த புலவர்களிலே கம்பனை போல், இளங்கோவை போல் எங்கும் காணோம்" — who praised Kamban and Ilanko in this way? ✅ Bharathiyar (பாரதியார்) Q67 EASY Factual "சந்திரமண்டலத்தில் கண்டு தெளிவோம்" and "ஜாதி இல்லையடி பாப்பா, ஓடி

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: ✅ Both by Bharathiyar (பாரதியார்) — the first from his school textbook song; the second is his famous anti-caste line in Paappa Paattu Q68 MEDIUM Factual "நாமருக்கும் குடிய எல்லாம் நம்மனை அஞ்சோம்" — the word "நம்மன்" in this line refers to whom? Who originally sang a similar song that inspired this? Tamil: நம்மனை என்பது யாரை குறிக்கும்? ✅ "நம்மன்" = Yama / the God of Death (எமன்). Original song: Thiruvavukkarasar (Naavukkarasar / திருநாவுக்கரசர்) sang "நாமா இருக்கும் குடியெல்லாம் நம்மனை எஞ்சோம்" first — which Bharathiyar's "Acham illai" is insp

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: 💎 LIVE GEM "அச்சமில்லை அச்சமில்லை அச்சம் என்பதில்லையே" — this famous song by Bharathiyar was inspired by Thirunavukarasar's Devaram. The earlier Devaram said "We are from the clan of those who fear not the god of death. " Bharathiyar echoed this in his freedom poem. Q69 EASY Factual "பெற்ற தாயும் பிறந்த பொன்னாடும் நற்றவாயினும் நனி சிறந்தனவே" — whose poem is this? ✅ National Poet Bharathiyar (தேசிய கவி பாரதியார்) This is a patriotic verse meaning "Mother and motherland are greater even than heaven.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: " Bharathiyar was the poet who expressed this — parallel to Thirukural's "யாயும் ஞாயும்" concept. Q70 MEDIUM Factual Fill in the blank: "சீன மிஸ்ரம் ஜனவகரம் இன்னும் தேசம் பலவும் புகழ்வீசி கலைஞானம் படைத்தொழில் வாணிபம் மிக நன்று வளர்த்த ___" — what does Bharathiyar name here? ✅ தமிழ்நாடு (Tamil Nadu) Bharathiyar sings about Tamil Nadu as the land that surpasses China, Egypt, and other great civilizations in art, knowledge, trade, and culture. The answer is Tamil Nadu — he praises his homeland as the greatest.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: 💎 LIVE GEM — Useful Shortcut When the last word of such fill-in-the-blank is about a region and involves Bharathiyar: • If "தமிழ்நாடு" is the blank → Bharathiyar (he was a Tamil Nadu patriot) • If "வையகமே" (the whole world) is the blank → Bharathidasan (he was a Pondicherry native, broader Tamil-world view) Q71 MEDIUM Factual Who wrote "Putthiya Aatticchoodi" (புதிய ஆத்திச்சூடி — New Aathichoodi)? Tamil: புதிய ஆத்திச்சூடி யார் எழுதியது? ✅ Bharathiyar (பாரதியார்) There are 6 types of Aathichoodi in Tamil.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Three in school textbooks: 1. Original Aathichoodi by Avvaiyar · 2. Puthiya (New) Aathichoodi by Bharathiyar · 3. Arivial (Science) Aathichoodi by Nellaichudhantha Muthu. 💎 LIVE GEM — Aathichoodi Classification "Evanam kaathal sei" (எவனம் காத்தல் செய்) — this specific phrase appears in the Puthiya Aathichoodi by Bharathiyar. The word "kaathal" (love/protect) helps identify it as Bharathiyar's version (not Avvaiyar's). Q72 MEDIUM Factual Which Tamil poet translated Bankim Chandra Chatterjee's "Vande Mataram" into Tamil?

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: And what is the connection to the word "Bankim Chandra"? ✅ Bharathiyar (பாரதியார்) translated Vande Mataram. Bankim Chandra = Bankim (crescent moon-shaped) Chandra (moon). He introduced the slogan "Vande Mataram" in his novel "Anandamath. " Q73 MEDIUM Factual Who among the following was called the first State Poet (முதல் அரசவை கவிஞர்)? Who appointed them? ✅ First State Poet = Namakkal Kavignar (நாமக்கல் கவிஞர்) — appointed by Rajaji (C. Rajagopalachari) as Chief Minister.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Second State Poet = Kannadasan (கண்ணதாசன்) — appointed by MGR. 💎 LIVE GEM — Irony Note Rajaji appointed Namakkal Kavignar as State Poet — but they had political fights. MGR appointed Kannadasan — but they too had conflicts. The teacher said: "Even in appointments, relationships were complicated! " Q74 MEDIUM Factual Who said: "செந்தமிழ் தேனி சிந்துக்கு தந்தை குவிக்கும் கவிதை குயில் இந்நாட்டினை கவிழ்க்கும் பகையை கவிழ்க்கும் கவியரசு" — and who is it about?

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: ✅ Said by Bharathidasan (பாரதிதாசன்) about Bharathiyar (பாரதியார்) Bharathidasan praised Bharathiyar as the emperor of Tamil poetry who defeats enemies of the nation. The answer option is D (both Bharathiyar and Bharathidasan are connected in this question). Q75 EASY Factual Kamarajar's midday meal scheme — when and where was it started? Tamil: காமராஜர் மதிய உணவு திட்டம் எந்த ஆண்டு எங்கு தொடங்கப்பட்டது? ✅ 1955 — in Ettayapuram (எட்டையாபுரம்), Bharathiyar's birthplace 🪤 TRAP — Critical Year Many say 1956 — WRONG.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Previous year TNPSC papers show 1955. The scheme was started in Ettayapuram — Bharathiyar's birthplace. This is why the question appears in this Bharathiyar session. Section 8 · Rare Data & Out-of-Textbook Questions Q76 HARD 💎 GEM What is the connection between Bharathiyar and V. Chidambaram Pillai's (Vaauci) shipping company shares? ✅ Paralasu Nellaiappar's brother sold 400 shares (25 rupees each) of V. 's shipping company to fund Bharathiyar's cause — traveling to Kallanai area in Trichy/Thanjavur to sell the share bonds and support Bharathi

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: 💎 LIVE GEM — Complete Story Vaauci's company had 400 shares × ₹25 each. Paralasu Nellaiappar's elder brother sold all these shares/bonds to generate funds to support Bharathiyar. The share papers were bundled and the brother traveled to villages around Kallanai (Grand Anicut) to sell them. This is completely out-of-textbook data that reveals how Bharathiyar's literary work was financially supported by dedicated friends. Q77 HARD Factual In the assertion-reason format: (1) Bharathiyar was the sub-editor of India journal → got an arrest warrant.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: (2) Due to this, in 1906 he decided to go to Pondicherry. Are both statements correct? If not, correct the error. ✅ Statement 1 is Correct. Statement 2 has a DATE ERROR — he went to Pondicherry in 1908, NOT 1906. Both statements' core content is correct, but the year in Statement 2 is wrong. 🔴 CRITICAL DATE Pondicherry move = 1908 (NOT 1906). 1906 = Calcutta Congress session year. Don't mix these up. Q78 MEDIUM Factual What is Bharathiyar's correct description?

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: He was a (a) poet (b) writer (c) journalist (d) freedom fighter (e) social reformer. Which ONE option correctly combines all? ✅ Option B — Social Reformer (சமூக சீர்திருத்தவாதி) is the additional descriptor beyond just "freedom fighter. " Full description: Poet + Writer + Journalist + Freedom Fighter + Social Reformer. Q79 MEDIUM Factual Bharathiyar said "ஓரொருக்கொரு நாட்டுக்குரிய தான் ஓட்டைச் சான் நினைப்புடையவர் அல்லர்" — who is he referring to?

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: ✅ This is by Bharathiyar himself — he is speaking about great poets/thinkers who don't limit their vision to one nation alone. The statement means: "Great people don't think only of their own nation. " 🪤 TRAP NOTE This is a Bharathiyar quote — but the teacher warned: don't assume every quote about Tamil literature is Bharathiyar. Context clues like "ஓரொருக்கொரு நாட்டு" (universal scope) + the style = Bharathiyar confirmed. Q80 MEDIUM Factual "வள்ளுவன் தன்னை உலகினுக்கே தந்து வான்புகழ் கொண்ட தமிழ்நாடு" — who wrote this line?

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: What's the distinguishing test? ✅ Bharathiyar — the line praises "Tamil Nadu" specifically (not "the whole world" / vaiagame). When the scope is Tamil Nadu → Bharathiyar. When the scope is the whole Tamil world → Bharathidasan. Q81 MEDIUM Factual Who described Bharathiyar as "செல்லிதாசன்" (Sellithaasan) — and who used that name? ✅ Bharathiyar himself — he self-referred as "செல்லிதாசன்" (cherished devotee/darling), one of his own pen names. Q82 MEDIUM Factual "பாட்டினை போல் ஆச்சரியம் பாரின் மிசை இல்லையடா" — who said this?

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: What does "மிசை" mean? ✅ Bharathiyar — "மிசை" means "above/more than" or "on this earth. " The line means: "There is nothing on this earth more wonderful than poetry. " Q83 MEDIUM Factual Which divinity has NO Pillaittamizh (பிள்ளைத் தமிழ்) written for them? Tamil: பிள்ளைத் தமிழே இயற்றப்படாத கடவுள் யார்? ✅ Lord Shiva (சிவன்) — because Pillaittamizh is a genre that treats a god as a CHILD and praises their childhood. Shiva has no stories of childhood in Tamil tradition.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: 💎 LIVE GEM — Pillaittamizh Logic Pillaittamizh = 10-section poem treating the subject as a child. Works for Murugan (childhood stories), Pillayar (childhood tales), Krishna (childhood Krishna stories). NOT for Shiva — no childhood episodes = no Pillaittamizh. This is from a Deviram textbook note. Q84 HARD Factual Which is the first Tamil newspaper to publish political caricatures (கேலி சித்திரம்), and who founded it? ✅ India (இந்தியா) journal by Bharathiyar — the first Tamil newspaper to publish political caricatures in red ink Q85 HARD 💎 GEM

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: What is "இமை பொழுது"? ✅ Bharathiyar — "இமை பொழுது" = the blink of an eye / the shortest moment. He says even a blink-moment of rest from serving the nation is not acceptable. 💎 LIVE GEM — Vocabulary "இமை" = eyelid / blink. "பொழுது" = time/moment. So "இமை பொழுது" = the time of one blink = an instant. "சேராது இருத்தல்" = not allowing oneself to rest. This vocabulary can appear in sentence meaning questions. Q86 MEDIUM Factual Match the works with authors: Kambar (கம்பர்) — Aerezhupathu (ஏரெழுபது), Bharathiyar — Paanchaali Sabatham, Bharathidasan

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: ✅ Matching: Author Work கம்பர் (Kambar) ஏரெழுபது (Aerezhupathu) + சிலை எழுபது + சரஸ்வதி அந்தாதி பாரதியார் (Bharathiyar) பாஞ்சாலி சபதம் (Paanchaali Sabatham) பாரதிதாசன் (Bharathidasan) குடும்ப விளக்கு (Kudumba Vilakku) ஒட்டக்கூத்தர் (Ottakoothar) முவர்ால (3 kings' histories) Q87 EASY Factual Bharathiyar said "Seethala kavi" (சீட்டுக் கவி) about himself — what does this mean? ✅ Seethala kavi = "Note Poet" / "Chit Poet" — Bharathiyar wrote poems on small slips of paper (chits).

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: He was known for spontaneous, impromptu poetry written on any piece of paper at hand. Q88 MEDIUM Factual Who said "Which Tamil poet aroused national and patriotic feelings" — and what is the answer? Tamil: தேசிய மற்றும் தேசிய உரிமைப் பாட்டில் உணர்வுகளை தூண்டினவர் எந்த தமிழ் கவிஞர்? ✅ Bharathiyar (பாரதியார்) — he is the Tamil poet who most powerfully aroused national and patriotic feelings 🪤 TRAP Don't answer Bharathidasan — he was a social revolutionary, not primarily a nationalist poet.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Bharathiyar = national poet; Bharathidasan = social reformer/revolutionary. Q89 HARD Factual The Minto-Morley Reforms of 1909 — how did Bharathiyar react to them? ✅ Bharathiyar mocked (கேலி செய்தார்) the Minto-Morley Reforms — he criticized them as insufficient and satirized the British for offering cosmetic changes rather than real self-rule Q90 HARD 💎 GEM "The Ash murder case (Paanjinaatham Ash kolai) — June 17, 1911" — how did Bharathiyar react?

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: What is significant about this date? ✅ Bharathiyar was shocked and described it as "an unforeseen tragic accident" — he did NOT celebrate political violence. Date: June 17, 1911 — this is a TNPSC PYQ. 💎 LIVE GEM The Ash murder (Collector Robert Ashe was assassinated at Maniyachi Railway Station) was done by Vanchi Maniyachar. Bharathiyar, despite being anti-British, opposed political assassination. This shows his nuanced position — freedom through constitutional means, not violence.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Bharathiyar: Born Dec 11, 1882 in Ettayapuram (then Tirunelveli dist. ); died Sep 11, 1921 — NOT from elephant attack, but from dysentery. Three landmark works from Pondicherry: Kuyil Paattu + Kannan Paattu + Paanchaali Sabatham. All composed 1908–1919. Key distinction: "India" journal = Bharathiyar (general patriotic); "Vijaya" journal = women-focused; Vande Mataram Tamil translation → appeared in Vijaya. Mahakavi title: officially given by Va. Ramaswami Iyengar; frequently popularized by Krishnaswami Iyer.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Two different answers for two different questions. Paralasu Nellaiappar = called Bharathiyar "younger brother"; his actual brother Sanmugasundaram Pillai = only person in India called "Vande Mataram Pillai. " "Tilak seed → Bharathi; Gandhi seed → Rajaji" quote = Salem Vijayaraghavachariyar (NOT Rajaji himself). Bharathiyar's works nationalized in 1949 by CM Omandur Ramaswami Reddiar — bought from AVM Meiyappa Chettiar. TRAP: "துறைதோறும் தமிழுக்கு" = Bharathidasan (NOT Bharathiyar).

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: "Kamarajar midday meal" year = 1955 (NOT 1956). Bharathidasan praised Gnanaratham: "No one on this four-directional earth can write such a work. " Bharathidasan = Russian-influenced; Bharathiyar = Walt Whitman (USA) influenced in prose poetry. Complete special names list including unusual ones: குப்பம்மாள் செல்வன் (aunt's son), சாவித்திரி (pen name for women's topics), முண்டாசு கவி (turban poet), பைந்தமிழ் தேர்பாகன் — all examinable.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: PRED 1 Bharathiyar's death — cause and date. Expect: "What caused Bharathiyar's death? " → Dysentery (NOT elephant). This trap is frequently tested and frequently answered wrong. HIGH probability. PRED 2 Quote attribution — "துறைதோறும் தமிழுக்கு" and "நல் இருமாப்பு. " These quotes about Bharathiyar by Namakkal Kavignar and Bharathidasan are exactly the type of out-of-textbook data TNPSC Group 2/4 favors. MEDIUM-HIGH probability. PRED 3 "Who gave Mahakavi title?

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: " vs "Who frequently used Mahakavi? " The distinction between Va. Ramaswami Iyengar (gave) and Krishnaswami Iyer (popularized) has strong likelihood of appearing as a trap option question. PRED 4 Vande Mataram Tamil translation journal — Vijaya (NOT India). This is a classic trap. TNPSC may give "India" as a distractor. Know the shortcut: female theme → Vijaya. PRED 5 Bharathiyar's works nationalization year (1949) + CM name (Omandur Ramaswami Reddiar).

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: This combination as an assertion-reason or direct question is likely, especially in Group 4/VAO papers.

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Quick Flashcard Revision Table # Question Answer 1 Bharathiyar's birthdate December 11, 1882 2 Birth place & original district Ettayapuram, Tirunelveli (now Thoothukudi) 3 Childhood name Subbaiah (சுப்பையா) 4 Parents Chinnaswami Iyer & Lakshmi Ammal 5 Mother died — his age Age 5 6 Wrote poem about mother — his age Age 7 7 Received "Bharathi" title — age & year Age 11, 1893, by Ettayapuram king 8 Gave title "Mahakavi" Va. Ramaswami Iyengar (was also sub-editor) 9 Popularized "Mahakavi" frequently Krishnaswami Iyer (also first published his poems

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Iyer) 11 First poem journal Viveka Banu, 1904, Madurai 12 Went to Pondicherry 1908 (arrest warrant for "India" journal) 13 Three Pondicherry works Kuyil Paattu, Kannan Paattu, Paanchaali Sabatham 14 Met Gandhi 1919, at Rajaji's house, Chennai 15 Death date & cause Sep 11, 1921 — dysentery (NOT elephant) 16 Funeral attendees Only 14 people 17 Works nationalized — year & CM 1949, Omandur Ramaswami Reddiar (from AVM Meiyappa Chettiar) 18 First poetry collection Sooddesa Keethangal (1908) 19 First line of his poetry மனதில் உறுதி வேண்டும் 20 Last pu

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Padmanabhan 27 Formed Bharathi Sangam Kalki (R. Krishnamurthy) 28 Painted Bharathiyar's portrait Aariyam alias Pashyam 29 Pondicherry helper Kuvalai Kannan (arranged house) 30 Chennai helper (final illness) Kamal Kannan (bullock cart to hospital) 31 "Tilak seed → Bharathi" quote Salem Vijayaraghavachariyar 32 "Avatar Purusha" quote Paralasu Nellaiappar 33 "இருமாப்பு" quote about Bharathi Namakkal Kavignar 34 "நெஞ்சை பறிகொடுத்தேன்" about Bharathi Kavimani Desiga Vinayagam Pillai 35 "Gnanaratham போல் நூல் இல்லை" quote Bharathidasan 36 "அறிவின் சி

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: ) Sanmugasundaram Pillai (Paralasu Nellaiappar's brother) 38 1st State Poet (appointed by? ) Namakkal Kavignar (appointed by Rajaji) 39 2nd State Poet (appointed by? ) Kannadasan (appointed by MGR) 40 Walt Whitman connection Bharathiyar was inspired by Whitman's free verse for prose poetry (vasana kavithe) 41 "துறைதோறும் தமிழுக்கு" — who? Bharathidasan (NOT Bharathiyar) — TRAP! 42 Kamarajar midday meal — year & place 1955, Ettayapuram (NOT 1956) 43 Pillaittamizh NOT written for which deity?

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Lord Shiva (no childhood stories) 44 New Aathichoodi author Bharathiyar (Putthiya Aathichoodi) 45 Tamil Nadu's Rasul Gamzatov Bharathidasan

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Pattern / Rule Application Number "11" pattern Born Dec 11 · Got title at 11 · Died Sep 11 → all Bharathiyar Yearwise Timeline 1882 Birth → 1893 Title → 1904 Sooddesa Mittiran → 1908 India journal + Pondicherry → 1919 Met Gandhi → 1921 Death → 1949 Nationalization Journals — Quick Match India + Vijaya = Bharathiyar · Kuyil + Ponni = Bharathidasan · Thaesabhaktan = Thiruveeka · Deepam = Parthsarathi Quote → Author pattern இருமாப்பு → Namakkal Kavignar | நெஞ்சை பறிகொடுத்தேன் → Kavimani | Tilak seed quote → Salem Vijayaraghavachariyar தமிழ்நாடு vs

chunks.title: Bharathiyar
chunks.text: Ramaswami Iyengar | Frequently used = Krishnaswami Iyer (also published poems) Works nationalization 1949, Omandur CM, from AVM Meiyappa Chettiar → now copyright free Western influence Bharathiyar → West (Walt Whitman/USA) for prose poetry | Bharathidasan → East (Russia/Soviet ideology) Kamarajar year TRAP Midday meal 1955 (NOT 1956), started at Ettayapuram (Bharathiyar's birthplace) ↑

chunks.title: Indian Constitution

chunks.title: Indian Constitution

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: WE, THE PEOPLE OF INDIA, having solemnly resolved to constitute India into a SOVEREIGN SOCIALIST SECULAR DEMOCRATIC REPUBLIC and to secure to all its citizens: JUSTICE, social, economic and political; LIBERTY of thought, expression, belief, faith and worship; EQUALITY of status and of opportunity; and to promote among them all FRATERNITY assuring the dignity of the individual and the unity and integrity of the Nation; IN OUR CONSTITUENT ASSEMBLY this twenty-sixth day of November, 1949, do HEREBY ADOPT, ENACT AND GIVE TO OURSELVES THIS CONSTITUT

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: KEYWORD MEANING / SIGNIFICANCE ADDED BY Sovereign India is independent; not under any foreign power. Final authority rests with the people. Original (1949) Socialist 🔥 Commitment to social and economic equality; no extreme concentration of wealth. 42nd Amendment (1976) Secular 🔥 State has no official religion; equal respect and protection for all religions. 42nd Amendment (1976) Democratic Government elected by the people; adult franchise. Original (1949) Republic Elected Head of State (President), not hereditary monarchy.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: Original (1949) Justice Social, economic & political; borrowed from Russian Revolution ideals. Original (1949) Liberty 5 aspects: thought, expression, belief, faith, worship. Original (1949) Equality Equal status and opportunity for all citizens. Original (1949) Fraternity Brotherhood among citizens; ensures dignity and national unity. Original (1949) Integrity 🔥 Unity and integrity of the nation added to emphasize national cohesion. 42nd Amendment (1976) 🔴 CRITICAL The Preamble was amended ONLY ONCE — by the 42nd Amendment Act, 1976 (during

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: Three words were inserted: Socialist, Secular, Integrity . The Preamble is NOT enforceable in court but is a key interpretive tool. 🟡 TRAP The Preamble is NOT a source of power nor a limitation on legislative power. In Kesavananda Bharati (1973) , the Supreme Court held that the Preamble is part of the Constitution and can be amended but its basic structure cannot be destroyed. S · S · D · R — "SOME STATES DO REFORM" S Sovereign — independent authority S Socialist — equal wealth distribution (42nd Amend.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: ) S Secular — no state religion (42nd Amend. ) D Democratic — elected government R Republic — elected Head of State J · L · E · F — "JUST LIKE EQUAL FRIENDS" J Justice — social, economic, political L Liberty — thought, expression, belief, faith, worship E Equality — status and opportunity F Fraternity — dignity + unity + integrity

chunks.text: 🟢 TIP Most-tested parts in TNpSC Group 1: Part III (Fundamental Rights), Part IV (DPSP), Part IVA (Fundamental Duties), Part V (Union), Part VI (States), Part XIV (Services), Part XVIII (Emergency), Part XX (Amendment) . chunks.text: Mnemonic: "STAY SAFE ON ROADS — ALWAYS CHECK LEFT SIDE EVERY NIGHT" 1 States & UTs 2 Salaries — President, Governors, Judges, etc. 3 Oaths & Affirmations forms 4 Rajya Sabha — Allocation of seats 5 Scheduled Areas administration 6 Tribal Areas (NE States) administration 7 Three Lists — Union, State, Concurrent 8 22 Official Languages 9 Acts protected from Judicial Review 10 Anti-Defection Law 11 Panchayats — 29 subjects 12 Municipalities — 18 subjects SCH.

chunks.text: ) — added by 1st Amendment 1951 Art. 31B High 10th 🔥 Anti-Defection Law — added by 52nd Amendment 1985 Art. 102(2), 191(2) 🔥 High 11th 🔥 29 subjects under Panchayats — added by 73rd Amendment 1992 Art. 243G 🔥 High 12th 🔥 18 subjects under Municipalities — added by 74th Amendment 1992 Art. 243W High 🔴 CRITICAL 7th Schedule: Union List (List I) — 99 subjects; only Parliament can legislate. State List (List II) — 61 subjects; only State Legislature.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: Concurrent List (List III) — 52 subjects; both can legislate, but Union prevails in conflict (Art. Original counts were 97, 66, 47 — reduced over amendments. 🟡 TRAP The 9th Schedule was originally thought to be totally immune from judicial review. State of Tamil Nadu (2007) , the SC held that Acts inserted in the 9th Schedule after 24th April 1973 (Kesavananda Bharati date) can be challenged if they violate the Basic Structure doctrine.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: ARTICLE PROVISION KEY NOTES 1 🔥 Name and Territory of the Union India, that is Bharat, shall be a Union of States. Three categories of territory: States, UTs, and territories acquired. Note: "Union of States" not "Federation of States" — deliberate choice by Ambedkar. 2 Admission or Establishment of New States Parliament may by law admit new States or establish new States on such terms as it thinks fit. Example: Sikkim admitted as 22nd state via 36th Amendment (1975).

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: [2A] Sikkim to be associated with the Union — Omitted Inserted by 35th Amendment (1974), omitted by 36th Amendment (1975) when Sikkim became full state. 3 🔥 Formation of New States and Alteration of Areas, Boundaries or Names Parliament can: (a) form new state by separation/union (b) increase/diminish area of state (c) alter boundaries (d) alter name. Bill requires President's recommendation + State Legislature's opinion (not consent). President can override state opinion.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: Example: Telangana (2014) carved from Andhra Pradesh. 4 Laws under Art. 2 and 3 to Amend First and Fourth Schedules Laws made under Art. 3 shall contain provisions for amending the First Schedule (States & UTs list) and Fourth Schedule (Rajya Sabha seat allocation). Such laws are NOT constitutional amendments under Art. 368 — only simple majority needed. 🔴 CRITICAL Under Art. 3 , Parliament can create, merge, rename, or alter boundaries of states by a simple majority — it is NOT a constitutional amendment.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: The concerned State Legislature's opinion must be sought but need not be accepted . This shows India's Union is indestructible — states can be created or destroyed but the Union cannot be. Contrast with USA where states cannot be divided without their consent. 3 bills require President's prior recommendation before introduction in Parliament — NOT Speaker's permission. The state legislature's view is only consultative. Parliament is not bound by it.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: Also, newly created states get representation automatically through amendment of 1st and 4th Schedules under Art. 4 — no separate constitutional amendment needed.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: ARTICLE PROVISION KEY NOTES 5 🔥 Citizenship at the Commencement of the Constitution A person is a citizen at commencement (26 Jan 1950) if: (a) born in India, OR (b) either parent born in India, OR (c) ordinarily resident in India for 5 years preceding commencement. 6 Rights of Citizenship of Persons who Migrated from Pakistan Persons who migrated from Pakistan to India before 19 July 1948 — citizen if they or their parents/grandparents were born in undivided India.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: After 19 July 1948 — must have registered with prescribed authority after 6 months residence. 7 Rights of Citizenship of Certain Migrants to Pakistan Persons who migrated to Pakistan after 1 March 1947 shall NOT be citizens. Exception: those who returned under a permit for resettlement or permanent return can apply for citizenship. 8 Rights of Citizenship of Persons of Indian Origin Residing Outside India Person (or either parent/grandparent born in undivided India) residing outside India can be registered as citizen by diplomatic/consular repr

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: Basis for OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) concept. 9 Persons Voluntarily Acquiring Citizenship of a Foreign State Not to be Citizens If a person voluntarily acquires citizenship of any foreign country, they automatically cease to be an Indian citizen. India does NOT allow dual citizenship (unlike USA, UK). OCI card is NOT dual citizenship. 10 Continuance of the Rights of Citizenship Every person who is or deemed to be a citizen under Art. 5–8 shall continue to be a citizen subject to any law made by Parliament.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: Parliament can provide for termination of citizenship. 11 🔥 Parliament to Regulate the Right of Citizenship by Law Parliament has the exclusive power to make provisions with respect to the acquisition and termination of citizenship. Led to the Citizenship Act, 1955 — the substantive law governing citizenship in India today. Also basis for the Citizenship (Amendment) Act, 2019 (CAA). 🔴 CRITICAL India follows Single Citizenship (borrowed from UK) — unlike USA where citizens have both national and state citizenship.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 5–11 deal only with citizenship at the commencement of the Constitution (26 Jan 1950). For all subsequent citizenship matters, Citizenship Act, 1955 (enacted under Art. The Act provides for citizenship by: Birth, Descent, Registration, Naturalisation, and Incorporation of Territory. 🟡 TRAP OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) is NOT dual citizenship — it is a long-term visa with certain rights but OCI holders cannot vote, hold constitutional office, or work in government.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: PIO (Person of Indian Origin) card scheme was merged with OCI in 2015. 9 , voluntary acquisition of foreign citizenship automatically terminates Indian citizenship — even without any formal declaration. The CAA 2019 (Citizenship Amendment Act) grants expedited citizenship to Hindu, Sikh, Buddhist, Jain, Parsi, and Christian minorities from Pakistan, Bangladesh, and Afghanistan who entered India before 31 Dec 2014 — but NOT to Muslims from those countries.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 5 to 11 — "BORN CAME LEFT OVERSEAS — CONTINUE PARLIAMENT" 5 Born / Domicile in India at commencement 6 Came from Pakistan (migrated to India) 7 Left to Pakistan (migrated out — not citizen) 8 Overseas Indians of Indian origin 9 No dual citizenship — foreign citizenship = loss 10 Continue as citizen subject to Parliament's law 11 Parliament regulates all citizenship matters

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 🟢 TIP Originally 7 Fundamental Rights. Right to Property (Art. 31) was removed as a Fundamental Right by the 44th Amendment (1978) and made a legal right under Art. Now only 6 Fundamental Rights remain.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: ARTICLE RIGHT KEY NOTES 14 🔥 Equality before law AND equal protection of laws Two concepts from English & American law. "Reasonable classification" is permitted. 15 Prohibition of discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex, place of birth Art. 15(3) allows special provisions for women & children. 15(4) & 15(5) for socially/educationally backward classes. 16 Equality of opportunity in public employment Art. 16(4) allows reservations for backward classes.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 16(4A) — SC/ST promotion reservation. 17 🔥 Abolition of Untouchability Untouchability abolished and its practice is a punishable offence. 17 is enforceable against private individuals too. 18 Abolition of Titles No title except military/academic. State shall not confer titles. Bharat Ratna etc. are NOT "titles" — clarified by SC.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: ARTICLE RIGHT KEY NOTES 19 🔥 6 Freedoms: (a) Speech & Expression (b) Assembly (c) Association (d) Movement (e) Residence (f) Profession Originally 7; Art. 19(1)(f) — Property was deleted by 44th Amendment. All subject to reasonable restrictions. 20 Protection in respect of conviction for offences No ex-post facto law, no double jeopardy, no self-incrimination. Cannot be suspended even during Emergency. 21 🔥 Protection of life and personal liberty "Heart of the Constitution.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: " Extended by Maneka Gandhi case (1978) — includes right to live with dignity, education, health, privacy etc. 21A Right to Education (6–14 years) Added by 86th Amendment (2002) . Free and compulsory education. 22 Protection against arrest and detention Right to be informed of grounds, consult lawyer, produced before magistrate within 24 hours. MISA, COFEPOSA, NSA are preventive detention laws.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 23 Prohibition of traffic in human beings and forced labour (begar). Enforceable against private individuals too. 24 Prohibition of employment of children below 14 years in factories, mines or hazardous employment.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 25 Freedom of conscience and free profession, practice and propagation of religion. Subject to public order, morality, health. 26 Freedom to manage religious affairs. Right to establish & maintain institutions for religious & charitable purposes. 27 No person shall be compelled to pay taxes for promotion of any particular religion. 28 No religious instruction in State-funded educational institutions. Students in aided institutions cannot be compelled to attend religious instruction.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 29 Right of any section to conserve its language, script, culture. No denial of admission on grounds of religion, race, caste, language. 30 Right of minorities (religious or linguistic) to establish and administer educational institutions. State shall not discriminate in granting aid.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 32 🔥 Right to move Supreme Court for enforcement of Fundamental Rights. Ambedkar called it "the heart and soul of the Constitution. " Cannot be suspended except during Emergency under Art. 33 Parliament may modify FRs for armed forces, police, intelligence. 35 Parliament (not state) has exclusive power to legislate on FRs under Arts. 16(3), 17, 23, 24.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: WRIT MEANING PURPOSE AGAINST WHOM Habeas Corpus 🔥 "You shall have the body" To release a person from illegal detention Any authority or private person Mandamus 🔥 "We command" To direct a public authority to perform its legal duty Public authority, NOT against President/Governor Prohibition "To forbid" To prevent inferior court from exceeding jurisdiction Judicial/quasi-judicial bodies only Certiorari "To be certified" To quash order of inferior court/tribunal Judicial/quasi-judicial bodies Quo Warranto "By what authority" To challenge unlawfu

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 13 declares any pre-constitutional law and post-constitutional law that is inconsistent with or takes away/abridges Fundamental Rights to be void. It establishes the doctrine of judicial review . "State" includes Government, Parliament, Legislature of States, and all local or other authorities within India. 💎 LIVE GEM — THE EVOLUTION OF ART. State of Madras (1950) , SC held Art. 21 had a narrow meaning — procedure of any law, even harsh. But in Maneka Gandhi v.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: Union of India (1978) , SC overruled Gopalan and held that procedure must be just, fair and reasonable (importing due process). Since then, Art. 21 has been expanded to include: right to privacy, right to education, right to livelihood, right to a clean environment, right to speedy trial, right to die with dignity, and over 30 other rights. This is why Art. 21 is called the "expanding universe" of the Constitution.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 🟢 TIP DPSPs are borrowed from the Irish Constitution . They are non-justiciable (cannot be enforced by courts) but fundamental in governance . 37 says they are "fundamental in the governance of the country and it shall be the duty of the State to apply these principles in making laws.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: ARTICLE DIRECTIVE 38 State to promote welfare by securing a social order with justice (social, economic, political) 39 🔥 Adequate means of livelihood, equal pay for equal work (39d), no concentration of wealth (39c), right to work, education, public assistance (added by 42nd Amend) 39A Equal justice and free legal aid — added by 42nd Amendment 41 Right to work, education and public assistance in old age, sickness, disablement 42 Just and humane conditions of work, maternity relief 43 Living wage, decent standard of life, worker participation i

chunks.text: ARTICLE DIRECTIVE 40 Organisation of village panchayats 43 Cottage industries in rural areas 46 Promotion of educational and economic interests of SCs, STs and other weaker sections 47 Prohibition of consumption of intoxicating drinks and drugs 48 Organisation of agriculture and animal husbandry; prohibition of cow slaughter

chunks.text: ARTICLE DIRECTIVE 44 🔥 Uniform Civil Code (UCC) — State shall endeavour to secure a uniform civil code for all citizens throughout the territory of India 45 Early childhood care and education for children below 6 years (amended by 86th Amendment; originally was 6–14 years free education) 48 Organisation of agriculture, animal husbandry on modern scientific lines 48A Protection and improvement of environment; safeguarding forests & wildlife — added by 42nd Amendment 49 Protection of monuments, places and objects of national importance 50 🔥 Sep

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: But after Kesavananda Bharati (1973) and the 42nd Amendment , Parliament added Art. 31C to protect laws implementing Arts. Later in Minerva Mills (1980) , SC held that harmony between FRs and DPSPs is part of the Basic Structure . Neither can completely abrogate the other.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 🔴 CRITICAL Fundamental Duties added by 42nd Amendment (1976) on recommendation of Swaran Singh Committee . Originally 10 duties . The 11th duty (provide opportunities for education to children aged 6–14) was added by the 86th Amendment (2002) . Borrowed from the Soviet Constitution . Non-justiciable but Parliament can enforce them. # DUTY KEY NOTES 1 Abide by the Constitution and respect its ideals, institutions, the National Flag and the National Anthem Civics basics 2 Cherish and follow the noble ideals which inspired our national struggle f

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: ARTICLE PROVISION 52 There shall be a President of India 54 🔥 Election by Electoral College — elected members of both Houses + elected members of State Legislative Assemblies (+ UT assemblies after 70th Amendment) 55 Manner of election — proportional representation, single transferable vote 56 Term of office: 5 years. Can resign to Vice-President. 61 🔥 Impeachment: for violation of the Constitution — by both Houses with 2/3 majority of total membership .

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: No judicial immunity during impeachment. 72 Pardoning powers: Pardon, Reprieve, Respite, Remission, Commutation. In cases of death sentence, court martial, union executive offences. 74 Council of Ministers headed by PM to aid and advise the President. President shall act on such advice. (44th Amend — "shall" replaced "may") 78 Duties of Prime Minister — to communicate to the President all decisions of Council of Ministers, inform President of matters relating to administration.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 🔴 CRITICAL — PRESIDENT'S VETO POWER The President has THREE types of veto: Absolute Veto — Withhold assent to a bill (used on advice of Cabinet or for private member bills) Suspensive Veto — Return bill for reconsideration (cannot do for Money Bills); must assent if re-passed Pocket Veto — Takes no action, keeps the bill pending indefinitely. India has this power unlike USA where 10-day limit applies.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 63 🔥 There shall be a Vice-President of India. Ex-officio Chairman of Rajya Sabha. 66 Election: by members of both Houses of Parliament in joint sitting. Elected by proportional representation with single transferable vote. 67 Term: 5 years. Can resign to President. Removal by Rajya Sabha by majority and agreed by Lok Sabha.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 75 PM appointed by President. Other Ministers appointed on advice of PM. Council of Ministers collectively responsible to Lok Sabha (Art.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: ARTICLE PROVISION 79 Constitution of Parliament: President + Rajya Sabha + Lok Sabha 80 🔥 Rajya Sabha: max 250 members (238 elected + 12 nominated by President for arts, literature, science, social service). 81 🔥 Lok Sabha: max 552 members (530 States + 20 UTs + 2 Anglo-Indian nominated — 2 Anglo-Indians abolished by 104th Amendment 2020) 83 Duration: Lok Sabha — 5 years (can be extended during Emergency). Rajya Sabha — permanent (1/3rd retire every 2 years, 6-year term) 85 🔥 Sessions: President summons/prorogues.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: Gap between sessions cannot exceed 6 months. Lok Sabha dissolved by President on advice of PM. 100 Quorum: 1/10th of total membership 108 🔥 Joint sitting of both Houses — called by President to resolve deadlock. Presided by Lok Sabha Speaker. Not applicable for Money Bills or Constitutional Amendment Bills. 109 🔥 Special procedure for Money Bills — Rajya Sabha has only 14 days to recommend; Lok Sabha may accept or reject recommendations. Lok Sabha has ultimate say.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 110 Definition of Money Bill: deals only with taxation, borrowing, appropriation, etc. Certified by Lok Sabha Speaker. 112 🔥 Annual Financial Statement (Union Budget) — presented to both Houses by President 117 Special provisions for Financial Bills 🟡 TRAP — RAJYA SABHA SPECIAL POWERS Rajya Sabha is a permanent body. It has two exclusive powers: (1) Art. 249 — can by 2/3 majority empower Parliament to legislate on a State List subject for national interest.

chunks.text: ASPECT GOVERNOR PRESIDENT Appointment By President (Art. 155) Elected (Art. 54) Term 5 years "during pleasure of President" (Art. 156) 5 years fixed (Art. 56) Removal By President, no set procedure Impeachment by Parliament Eligibility Indian citizen, 35+ years (Art. 157) Indian citizen, 35+ years (Art. 58) Pardoning Power Art. 161 — cannot pardon death sentences (only President can) Art. 72 — including death sentences Discretion Has discretionary powers (Art.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 163); can reserve bill for President Largely acts on Cabinet advice 🔴 CRITICAL — ART. 356 (PRESIDENT'S RULE) Under Art. 356, President's Rule can be imposed if the constitutional machinery of a state has failed. After SR Bommai v. Union of India (1994) , the SC held: (1) Imposition subject to judicial review; (2) Floor test must be conducted before dismissal of government; (3) Proclamation must be ratified by Parliament within 2 months (prior to SR Bommai it was not justiciable).

chunks.text: 368: AMENDMENT PROCEDURE Three types of amendment procedures: Simple Majority — Ordinary laws (not under Art. , creating new states (Art. 3), admission of new states Special Majority — By 2/3 majority of members present & voting AND majority of total membership of each House Special Majority + State Ratification — For provisions relating to federal structure (e. 54, 55, 73, 162, 241, Chapter IV of Part V, Chapter V of Part VI, any list of 7th Schedule, representation in Parliament, Art.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 368 itself) No joint sitting for constitutional amendment bills. SC cannot be enlarged by amendment.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: KESAVANANDA BHARATI v. STATE OF KERALA | 1973 | 13-judge bench Established the Basic Structure Doctrine — Parliament can amend any provision of the Constitution but cannot destroy its basic structure. Basic structure includes: supremacy of Constitution, secular character, democratic republic, federal character, separation of powers, rule of law, judicial review, free and fair elections, individual freedoms. Golak Nath case. Most important constitutional case ever decided.
