chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 🔵 PYQ CRITICAL Q1. Which amendment inserted the words "Socialist", "Secular" and "Integrity" in the Preamble of the Constitution? Show Answer ▼ ✅ ANSWER: 42nd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1976 The 42nd Amendment (1976), passed during the Emergency period under PM Indira Gandhi, added three words: Socialist, Secular, Integrity . It is sometimes called the "Mini Constitution" as it made the most sweeping changes to the Constitution. The Preamble has been amended only once in India's constitutional history.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 💎 GEM Remember: Only 42nd Amendment changed the Preamble. The three new words make up the phrase " S-S-I " — Socialist Secular Integrity. These reflect India's socialist welfare state goals, religious neutrality, and national unity priorities of that era. 🔵 PYQ TRAP Q2. How many Fundamental Duties are currently there in the Indian Constitution? Show Answer ▼ ✅ ANSWER: 11 Fundamental Duties TRAP ALERT: Many candidates answer 10. Originally 10 duties were added by the 42nd Amendment (1976).

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: The 11th duty was added by the 86th Amendment (2002) : "It shall be the duty of every citizen who is a parent or guardian to provide opportunities for education to his child or ward between the age of six and fourteen years. " Currently 11 Fundamental Duties. 🔵 PYQ CRITICAL Q3. Which article of the Indian Constitution is called the "Heart and Soul of the Constitution" by Dr. Show Answer ▼ ✅ ANSWER: Article 32 — Right to Constitutional Remedies Dr.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: Ambedkar called Article 32 (Right to move the Supreme Court for enforcement of FRs) the "Heart and Soul of the Constitution. " Without this article, all other FRs would be meaningless as there would be no mechanism to enforce them. Article 32 itself is a Fundamental Right and cannot be suspended during Emergency under Art. 352 (though Art. 358-359 suspends other FRs). 🔵 PYQ TRAP Q4. A Money Bill can be introduced in which house of Parliament and what is Rajya Sabha's role?

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: Show Answer ▼ ✅ ANSWER: Only Lok Sabha; Rajya Sabha has 14 days to return it TRAP: Rajya Sabha cannot introduce or reject Money Bills. 109, a Money Bill can only be introduced in Lok Sabha on the recommendation of the President. After passing in Lok Sabha, it is transmitted to Rajya Sabha which has only 14 days to return it with recommendations (which Lok Sabha may or may not accept). If Rajya Sabha holds it beyond 14 days, the bill is deemed passed by both Houses.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: What is the "Basic Structure Doctrine" and which case established it? Show Answer ▼ ✅ ANSWER: Kesavananda Bharati v. State of Kerala (1973) The Basic Structure Doctrine holds that while Parliament has wide power to amend the Constitution under Art. 368, it cannot alter or destroy the "basic structure" of the Constitution. Elements of Basic Structure include: Supremacy of Constitution, Republican & Democratic form of government, Secular character, Federal structure, Separation of powers, Judicial Review, Rule of Law, Parliamentary system, Free &

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 💎 GEM This was decided by a 13-judge bench by a slim 7-6 majority. It is arguably the most significant constitutional decision in Indian legal history. Khanna's concurring opinion later became the majority position. The doctrine was used to strike down parts of the 42nd and 39th Amendments and continues to be the ultimate check on Parliament's amending power. 🔵 PYQ CRITICAL Q6. How many times has National Emergency been proclaimed in India, and under what circumstances?

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: Show Answer ▼ ✅ ANSWER: Three times — 1962, 1971, 1975 1st Emergency (1962): October 26 – January 10, 1968 — during Chinese aggression (still in force during 1965 Indo-Pak war) 2nd Emergency (1971): December 3–March 21, 1977 — during Bangladesh Liberation War (Pakistani aggression) 3rd Emergency (1975): June 25–March 21, 1977 — "Internal disturbance"; most controversial; led to suspension of FRs, press censorship, political arrests. Led to 44th Amendment to prevent abuse.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: Which article provides for the right to education, and what amendment inserted it? Show Answer ▼ ✅ ANSWER: Article 21A, inserted by 86th Amendment (2002) The 86th Amendment Act, 2002 inserted Art. 21A making free and compulsory education a Fundamental Right for children aged 6 to 14 years . This led to the Right to Education (RTE) Act, 2009. The same amendment also: (1) amended Art. 45 (DPSP) for early childhood care for children below 6 years; (2) added the 11th Fundamental Duty requiring parents to provide education to children aged 6–14.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 🔵 PYQ TRAP Q8. The Right to Property was removed as a Fundamental Right by which amendment? Where does it stand now? Show Answer ▼ ✅ ANSWER: 44th Amendment (1978); now a Legal/Constitutional Right under Art. 300A The 44th Amendment Act, 1978 under Janata government deleted the Right to Property from Part III (Fundamental Rights). Originally Art. 19(1)(f) and Art. 31 gave property rights. 300A provides: "No person shall be deprived of his property save by authority of law" — a legal right, not a FR.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: This means property disputes go to civil courts, not directly to Supreme Court under Art. 🔵 PYQ CRITICAL Q9. What is the meaning and significance of Article 17 of the Indian Constitution? Show Answer ▼ ✅ ANSWER: Abolition of Untouchability — enforceable against private individuals Art. 17 abolishes "Untouchability" and forbids its practice in any form. Enforcement of any disability arising from untouchability is an offence punishable by law. The Protection of Civil Rights Act, 1955 and SC/ST (Prevention of Atrocities) Act, 1989 give legal teet

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: Unique significance: most FRs are against the State, but Art. 17 is directly enforceable against private individuals too, making it a horizontal right. 🔵 PYQ GEM Q10. What are the exclusive special powers of Rajya Sabha that Lok Sabha does not have? Show Answer ▼ ✅ ANSWER: Art. 312 Rajya Sabha has two exclusive powers: Art. 249: By a resolution supported by not less than 2/3 of its members present and voting, Rajya Sabha can authorise Parliament to make laws on a State List subject if it is in the national interest.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: Such resolution is valid for 1 year and can be renewed. 312: Rajya Sabha can create new All India Services (like IAS, IPS) common to both Centre and States by a 2/3 majority resolution. 🔵 PYQ CRITICAL Q11. Under Article 352, what is the required majority for Parliamentary approval of National Emergency? Show Answer ▼ ✅ ANSWER: Special Majority — 2/3 of members present & voting + absolute majority of total membership After the 44th Amendment (1978), National Emergency proclamation must be approved by both Houses within one month by special majo

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: The Lok Sabha can also revoke it by a simple majority passed through a special session called when at least 1/10th of Lok Sabha members requisition it. 🔵 PYQ TIP Q12. What are the Socialistic, Gandhian, and Liberal-Intellectual DPSPs? Give one example each. Show Answer ▼ ✅ ANSWER: Three categories of DPSPs Socialistic DPSPs (welfare state): Art. 39 — equal pay for equal work, Art. 41 — right to work, Art. 42 — maternity relief, Art. 47 — raising standard of living.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: Gandhian DPSPs (Gram Swaraj): Art. 40 — panchayats, Art. 43 — cottage industries, Art. 46 — upliftment of SCs/STs, Art. 48 — cow protection. Liberal-Intellectual DPSPs : Art. 44 — Uniform Civil Code, Art. 45 — early childhood care, Art. 48A — environmental protection, Art. 51 — international peace. 🔵 PYQ TRAP Q13. The election of the Vice-President of India is conducted by whom? Show Answer ▼ ✅ ANSWER: Electoral College of both Houses of Parliament (elected + nominated members) — NOT just elected members TRAP: Many confuse Vice-President's ele

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: President's election (Art. 54): Elected members of Lok Sabha + Rajya Sabha + State Assemblies + UT Assemblies (elected members only) Vice-President's election (Art. 66): Members of BOTH Houses of Parliament — both elected AND nominated members — in a JOINT SITTING. State Assemblies do NOT participate. Single transferable vote system. 🔵 PYQ CRITICAL Q14. The Uniform Civil Code is mentioned in which article? What is its current status? Show Answer ▼ ✅ ANSWER: Article 44 — DPSP; not implemented nationwide; Goa has a partial UCC Art.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 44 directs the State to secure a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) for all citizens. Being a DPSP, it is non-justiciable. Supreme Court in Shah Bano case (1985), Sarla Mudgal case (1995), and John Vallamattom case (2003) urged Parliament to enact UCC. Uttarakhand became the first state to enact a UCC (2024). Goa is cited as the only state with a partial UCC through the Portuguese Civil Code still in operation. 🔵 PYQ GEM Q15. What is the SR Bommai case (1994) and why is it significant for Indian federalism?

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: Show Answer ▼ ✅ ANSWER: SR Bommai v. Union of India — landmark for federalism and Art. 356 SR Bommai (CM of Karnataka) challenged dismissal of his government under Art. The 9-judge bench held: 1. Federalism is part of Basic Structure — States are not mere agents of Centre 2. 356 is subject to judicial review 3. Before dismissal, floor test must be conducted 4. The President acts on Cabinet's written advice 5. Proclamation must be ratified by Parliament This case practically stopped rampant misuse of Art.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 💎 LIVE GEM 1 — WHY THE BASIC STRUCTURE DOCTRINE MATTERS Before 1973, Parliament could amend any part of the Constitution, including Fundamental Rights. The Indira Gandhi government used this to remove property rights, curtail freedoms, and concentrate power. The Basic Structure Doctrine of Kesavananda Bharati (1973) created an unwritten limit on Parliament's amending power . It has since been used to: • Strike down the 39th Amendment (1975) which immunized PM's election from challenge • Strike down parts of the 42nd Amendment (1976) — Minerva

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 💎 LIVE GEM 2 — HOW SR BOMMAI TRANSFORMED INDIAN FEDERALISM Between 1950–1994, Article 356 (President's Rule) was imposed over 100 times , often for blatant political reasons — to dismiss opposition-run governments. The Sarkaria Commission (1988) recommended reforms. When SR Bommai's government was dismissed in 1989, the case took 5 years to reach its landmark verdict. Post-1994, impositions of Article 356 dropped dramatically. The doctrine of floor test before dismissal, judicial review of proclamations, and Parliament's mandatory ratification

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: India's federal democracy became substantially more stable — not through constitutional amendment but through judicial interpretation. 💎 LIVE GEM 3 — ART. 21 — THE EXPANDING UNIVERSE OF RIGHTS Article 21 simply reads: "No person shall be deprived of his life or personal liberty except according to procedure established by law. " Yet through judicial interpretation — especially post-Maneka Gandhi (1978) — it has become the most expansive article in the Constitution.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: The Supreme Court has read into Art. 21: • Right to privacy (Puttaswamy, 2017) • Right to live with dignity • Right to a clean environment • Right to speedy trial • Right to free legal aid • Right to livelihood • Right to education (later given express FR status by Art. 21A) • Right to health and medical care • Right to die with dignity This expansive reading is unique — the US has struggled with implied rights, but India's SC has been willing to find them through purposive interpretation, making Art.

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 21 truly the "heart of the Constitution. " 💎 LIVE GEM 4 — THE CURIOUS CASE OF INDIA'S FEDERALISM India is not a classical federation like the USA (where states came together) nor a unitary state like the UK. It has been called a "quasi-federal" state, a "federation with a unitary bias," or "cooperative federalism. " Key features that show India's unique federal character: • Single citizenship (unlike USA's dual citizenship) • Residuary powers with Centre (unlike USA where they're with states) • Single Constitution for Centre and States • Union

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 1 was deliberate — Ambedkar noted that states had no role in creating the Union, unlike American states. 💎 LIVE GEM 5 — THE DPSP-FRs TENSION AND ITS RESOLUTION The Constitutional drafters envisioned FRs and DPSPs as complementary — individual liberty and state welfare. But clashes were inevitable: • State of Madras v. Champakam (1951) — FRs prevail → 1st Amendment added Art. 15(4) for backward class reservations • Golaknath (1967) — FRs cannot be abridged even for DPSPs • Kesavananda (1973) + 24th Amendment — Parliament can amend FRs • 42nd Am

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: 31C gave total primacy to DPSPs • Minerva Mills (1980) — SC held that the balance between FRs and DPSPs is itself part of Basic Structure. Neither can be sacrificed for the other. Today's understanding: the Constitution envisions a welfare state that also respects individual liberty — the tension itself keeps the constitutional dialogue alive. 💎 LIVE GEM 6 — PANCHAYATI RAJ: THE THIRD TIER OF DEMOCRACY Gandhi's vision of village swaraj finally found constitutional status through the 73rd Amendment (1992).

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: Key constitutional innovations: • Mandatory 3-tier structure: Gram Panchayat, Panchayat Samiti, Zila Parishad • Mandatory 1/3 reservation for women (some states 50%) • State Finance Commission + State Election Commission mandatory • 11th Schedule: 29 functions devolved (agriculture, primary education, drinking water, sanitation, etc. ) • Direct elections at all 3 tiers India now has the world's largest democratic institution at the grassroots — over 30 lakh elected representatives, with 10+ lakh women, making it the biggest experiment in partic

chunks.title: Indian Constitution
chunks.text: Yet "devolution without resources" remains a challenge — most Panchayats lack financial autonomy.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: TNPSC often asks about BOTH. Tamil Nadu initially did NOT join PM-JAY (has its own CMCHIS) but later integrated. Check current status. 💡 TIP PM-JAY is the world's largest government-funded health insurance programme. Launched Sep 23, 2018 from Ranchi, Jharkhand. Golden Card is issued to beneficiaries for cashless treatment. 💡 MNEMONIC: "5-10-50" = ₹5 lakh cover, 10. 74 crore families, 50 crore beneficiaries Key numbers for Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY 📘 PYQ TNPSC 2019: Ayushman Bharat was launched in which city?

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-05 PM Garib Kalyan Anna Yojana (PMGKAY) Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food & Public Distribution MAR 2020 BJP/NDA ▼ Why Launched COVID-19 lockdown severely disrupted livelihoods and food security of the poor. Emergency free food grain distribution was needed to prevent hunger during the pandemic. Objective Provide free additional food grains (over NFSA entitlement) to NFSA beneficiaries Ensure no poor goes hungry during economic distress Merged with NFSA from January 2023 for permanent free grain distribution Key Features 5 kg free grain per

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: 35 crore NFSA beneficiaries; One Nation One Ration Card enabled Government bears full cost — ₹2 lakh crore+ spent on free grain Target Beneficiaries 81. 35 crore beneficiaries under National Food Security Act 2013 ⚠ TRAP PMGKAY was originally a COVID-19 emergency measure but became permanent from Jan 2023. Under merged PMGKAY+NFSA, Priority Households get 5 kg FREE, Antyodaya Anna Yojana families get 35 kg FREE per month. 💡 TIP The merger of PMGKAY with NFSA was announced for 5 years (2023–2028), making India's free food grain programme the wo

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: 💡 MNEMONIC: "GARIB = Grain At Really Incredible Bulk" Reminds you it's a food grain scheme for the poor, free of cost

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-06 Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM) Ministry of Jal Shakti AUG 2019 BJP/NDA ▼ Why Launched Only 17% of rural households had tap water connections in 2019. Women walked kilometres daily to fetch water, affecting health, education and productivity. Objective Provide Functional Household Tap Connection (FHTC) to every rural household by 2024 Ensure adequate quantity (55 lpcd) of prescribed quality water on regular basis Promote community participation through Village Water & Sanitation Committees Key Features Target: 19.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: 27 crore rural households; 14+ crore connected by 2024 Funding: 90:10 (Himalayan/NE states), 50:50 (others); UTs fully funded by Centre Convergence with SBM, MGNREGS, PMKSY for grey water management Jal Jeevan Mission (Urban) also launched for cities — separate scheme Budget / Outlay ₹3. 60 lakh crore (2019–2024); largest water supply scheme in the world ⚠ TRAP JJM replaced the National Rural Drinking Water Programme (NRDWP). Also note: JJM Urban is a SEPARATE scheme under MoHUA launched in 2021.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Do not confuse them. 💡 TIP Target norm under JJM: 55 litres per capita per day (lpcd). Har Ghar Jal is the brand name. Launched on Independence Day 2019 (Aug 15) by PM Modi from Red Fort. 💡 MNEMONIC: "JJM = Jal for Joy Mission — 55 lpcd, Har Ghar Jal" 55 lpcd water norm + tagline Har Ghar Jal = brand of Jal Jeevan Mission

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-07 Mahatma Gandhi NREGS (MGNREGS) Ministry of Rural Development FEB 2006 UPA/INC ▼ Why Launched Rural unemployment and seasonal migration of agricultural labourers was a persistent problem. A rights-based guaranteed employment framework was needed to address rural poverty. Objective Guarantee 100 days of wage employment per year to every rural household Create durable assets (roads, ponds, check dams) in rural areas Strengthen the livelihood resource base of the rural poor Key Features Legally guaranteed 100 days employment; extended to 150 d

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: It was renamed MGNREGS in 2009 after Gandhi's name. The ACT (2005) and LAUNCH DATE (2006) are different — common exam trap! 💡 TIP MGNREGS is the world's largest social protection/public works programme. It started in 200 districts, expanded to all districts by April 2008. Gram Sabha approves works; Gram Panchayat executes at least 50% of works. 💡 MNEMONIC: "100-15-1/3" = 100 days guaranteed, 15 days to provide work, 1/3 women mandatory Three key numbers for MGNREGS that appear frequently in exams 📘 PYQ TNPSC 2018: MGNREGS was originally laun

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-08 PM Street Vendor's AtmaNirbhar Nidhi (PM SVANidhi) Ministry of Housing & Urban Affairs JUN 2020 BJP/NDA ▼ Why Launched COVID-19 lockdown devastated street vendors who lost working capital overnight and couldn't access formal credit due to lack of collateral. Objective Provide affordable collateral-free working capital loan to street vendors Enable their reintegration into the formal financial system Reward digital transactions with cashback incentives Key Features Loan tiers: ₹10,000 (1st), ₹20,000 (2nd), ₹50,000 (3rd) — on timely repaymen

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: ; 1-year tenure Monthly cashback up to ₹100 for digital transactions via UPI Vendors identified via ULB survey; certificate of vending issued Target Beneficiaries ~50 lakh street vendors in urban areas as of 2020 ⚠ TRAP PM SVANidhi is for STREET VENDORS specifically — do not confuse with PM Mudra Yojana (for micro-enterprises broadly) or PMEGP (for small industries). SVANidhi is urban-focused; Mudra covers rural & urban. 💡 TIP PM SVANidhi was first launched exclusively for COVID rehabilitation of street vendors.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: The scheme was later extended. It has a unique digital incentive component — ₹1,200/year cashback on digital payments. 💡 MNEMONIC: "SVANidhi = Street Vendor Atmanirbhar NIDHI (Fund)" NIDHI = fund/treasury; scheme name itself is the mnemonic for its purpose

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-09 PM Mudra Yojana (PMMY) Ministry of Finance / MUDRA Ltd. (under SIDBI) APR 2015 BJP/NDA ▼ Why Launched Micro and small enterprises — the backbone of employment — were excluded from formal bank credit due to lack of collateral and documentation, forcing them to depend on usurious moneylenders. Objective Provide collateral-free loans to non-corporate, non-farm micro enterprises Fund the unfunded and under-funded micro entrepreneurs Create formal credit ecosystem for 5.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: 77 crore micro enterprises Key Features 3 loan products: Shishu (<₹50,000), Kishore (₹50K–5L), Tarun (₹5L–10L); 4th: Tarun+ (₹10L–20L) added 2024 No collateral required; Mudra card (RuPay-linked debit card) issued Available through banks, NBFCs, MFIs, RRBs, small finance banks Credit Guarantee through MUDRA Credit Guarantee Fund Target Beneficiaries Non-corporate small business owners, artisans, small traders, vegetable vendors — anyone in non-farm informal sector ⚠ TRAP MUDRA = Micro Units Development & Refinance Agency.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: It's a refinancing institution, not a direct lending bank. Also: Shishu-Kishore-Tarun names are frequently mixed up in MCQs. Shishu = smallest (up to ₹50K), Tarun = largest (up to ₹10L). 💡 TIP Since 2015 launch, MUDRA has sanctioned loans worth ₹27+ lakh crore to 45+ crore accounts. Women borrowers account for ~68% — a key statistic for exams. 💡 MNEMONIC: "SKT = Shishu Kid, Tarun Teen" (child to teen growth = loan size growth) Shishu (child) = smallest loan, Kishore (adolescent) = medium, Tarun (teen/youth) = largest

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-10 Skill India Mission / PMKVY Ministry of Skill Development & Entrepreneurship (MSDE) JUL 2015 BJP/NDA ▼ Why Launched India's demographic dividend was at risk — the working-age population was growing but without marketable skills, threatening unemployment and productivity. 7% of the workforce had formal vocational training (vs. 52% in USA, 75% in Germany). Objective Skill 40 crore people by 2022 through various programmes Create a demand-driven, outcome-based skilling ecosystem Align skilling with industry requirements through National Skill

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: 0 (2022–26): Focus on Industry 4. 0, AI, coding, robotics, drones Target Beneficiaries Youth aged 15–45 years; PMKVY 4. 0 targets 1 crore youth (2022–2026) ⚠ TRAP Skill India Mission ≠ PMKVY. Skill India is the umbrella mission; PMKVY is one scheme under it. MSDE was created as a NEW ministry in 2014 specifically for skilling. 💡 TIP National Skill Development Corporation (NSDC) implements PMKVY. Sector Skill Councils (SSCs) set standards for 30+ sectors.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: NSQF levels 1–10 align qualifications nationally. 💡 MNEMONIC: "SKILL = Sector-based Kaushal Integrated Livelihood Learning" Skill India focuses on sector-based, demand-driven skilling for livelihood

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-11 Digital India Programme Ministry of Electronics & IT (MeitY) JUL 2015 BJP/NDA ▼ Why Launched India lagged in digital infrastructure, internet penetration and e-governance. Bridging the digital divide and creating a knowledge economy required a coordinated national programme. Objective Transform India into a digitally empowered society and knowledge economy Ensure government services are digitally available to citizens Promote digital literacy and connectivity in rural areas Key Features 9 Pillars: BharatNet, e-Kranti, IT for Jobs, Digital

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: 5 lakh Gram Panchayats Common Service Centres (CSCs) as delivery points India Stack: Aadhaar + UPI + DigiLocker + eSign ecosystem Target Beneficiaries All citizens of India; special focus on rural and underserved populations ⚠ TRAP Digital India is a programme with 9 pillars — not a single scheme. BharatNet is a sub-component. UPI, Aadhaar, DigiLocker are part of India Stack but not exclusively under Digital India. 💡 TIP India has ~1. 3 billion Aadhaar holders (world's largest biometric ID), 600+ million UPI users, and 5 lakh+ Common Service C

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: 💡 MNEMONIC: "Digital India's 9 pillars = 'BEI-JIDEAS'" BharatNet, E-Kranti, IT-Jobs, Jobs(literacy), Infrastructure, Data(security), Electronics, Access(universal), Services(e-gov)

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-12 Make in India Department for Promotion of Industry & Internal Trade (DPIIT) SEP 2014 BJP/NDA ▼ Why Launched India's share in global manufacturing was just 2% in 2014 against a target of 25%. Weak investment climate, poor infrastructure and complex regulations deterred manufacturing FDI. Objective Increase manufacturing sector's contribution to GDP from 15% to 25% by 2025 Create 100 million additional manufacturing jobs by 2022 Make India a global manufacturing hub and improve Ease of Doing Business Key Features Covers 27 sectors including

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Symbol is a lion made of cogs/gears. Do not confuse with Startup India (Jan 2016) or Atma Nirbhar Bharat (May 2020). 💡 TIP Make in India 2. 0 focuses on 27 sectors — reduced from original 25 to add new emerging sectors. DPIIT (earlier DIPP) is the nodal ministry. Lion logo symbolises strength, courage and manufacturing. 💡 MNEMONIC: "Make in India = Sep 2014, Lion Logo, 25% GDP target" Month (Sep), Year (2014), Symbol (Lion), Goal (25% manufacturing GDP)

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-13 Startup India Initiative DPIIT, Ministry of Commerce & Industry JAN 2016 BJP/NDA ▼ Why Launched India had massive entrepreneurial potential but startups faced regulatory hurdles, limited access to funding, and lack of mentorship ecosystems. Objective Build a robust startup ecosystem for nurturing innovation and driving sustainable economic growth Provide tax benefits, funding support, and simplified regulations for startups Create 1 lakh startups and develop India as the 3rd largest startup ecosystem Key Features 3-year income tax exemptio

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Not ALL new businesses are "startups" under this scheme. 💡 TIP India has 100,000+ DPIIT-recognised startups (2023), making it the 3rd largest startup ecosystem globally after USA and China. Unicorns (valuation >$1 billion) = 100+ as of 2023. 💡 MNEMONIC: "Startup India = 16 Jan 2016 — New Year, New Ventures" Jan 16, 2016 launch date; Action Plan had 19 points announced that day

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-14 PM POSHAN Shakti Nirman (Mid-Day Meal → PM POSHAN) Ministry of Education SEP 2021 BJP/NDA ▼ Why Launched The Mid-Day Meal Scheme (1995) was renamed and revamped as PM POSHAN in 2021 to improve nutritional outcomes and boost school enrolment and attendance. Objective Provide nutritious cooked meals to children in government schools (Classes 1–8) Improve nutritional status, reduce hunger and boost school attendance Extend to pre-primary (Bal Vatika) classes in government schools Key Features Covers 11.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: 80 crore children in 11. 20 lakh schools Minimum 100–150 calories and 12–20g protein per meal depending on age Tithi Bhojan (community participation) encouraged Fortified rice introduced to address micronutrient deficiency ⚠ TRAP Mid-Day Meal Scheme (1995) was renamed to PM POSHAN in 2021. The scheme was judicially directed — Supreme Court ordered in 2001 (PUCL vs Union of India) to implement MDM in ALL states within 6 months. 💡 TIP PM POSHAN is the world's largest school feeding programme.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Tamil Nadu was the FIRST state to implement MDM under CM K. Kamaraj in 1925 — a key TNPSC fact! 💡 MNEMONIC: "POSHAN = Providing Optimal School Health And Nutrition" Links the scheme name to its nutrition and school attendance goals

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-15 Beti Bachao Beti Padhao (BBBP) WCD + Health + Education Ministries (tri-ministerial) JAN 2015 BJP/NDA ▼ Why Launched India's declining Child Sex Ratio (CSR) from 945 (2001) to 918 (2011) per 1000 boys, combined with low female education rates, required urgent intervention. Objective Prevent gender-biased sex selective elimination through enforcement of PC-PNDT Act Ensure survival and protection of the girl child Promote education and participation of girls Key Features Initially launched in 100 districts with low CSR; expanded to all distr

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana was launched the SAME DAY (Jan 22, 2015) as a companion financial scheme for girl child savings. 💡 TIP CSR improved from 918 (2014–15) to 934 (2019–20) — a positive outcome of BBBP. PM Modi launched BBBP himself at Panipat citing Haryana's alarmingly low sex ratio of 879. 💡 MNEMONIC: "BBBP = Bachao (Save) + Padhao (Educate) = B+P" Two action words: Bachao (protection) and Padhao (education) = dual objective of the scheme

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-16 Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY) Ministry of Finance (Post Office / Banks) JAN 2015 BJP/NDA ▼ Why Launched Girl children had no dedicated savings and investment vehicle. Families needed incentives to invest in daughters' education and marriage rather than treating daughters as financial burdens. Objective Provide a high-interest savings account for girl child's future education and marriage Encourage financial planning for girl child from birth to adulthood Offer tax benefits to incentivise savings for girl children Key Features Account open

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: (2024); best among small savings schemes Minimum deposit ₹250/year; maximum ₹1. 5 lakh/year; Tenure: 21 years from opening Tax exempt on deposit, interest, and maturity (EEE status under 80C) ⚠ TRAP SSY matures 21 years from account opening OR when girl turns 18 & married — whichever is earlier. Partial withdrawal (50%) allowed after girl turns 18 for education. Do NOT confuse maturity rules. 💡 TIP SSY has EEE (Exempt-Exempt-Exempt) tax status — the only small savings scheme with this status.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Part of BBBP scheme ecosystem. Can be opened at post offices and designated banks. 💡 MNEMONIC: "SSY = Save, Study, Yojana — Triple E tax, 21-year plan" Save (deposit), Study (education withdrawal at 18), Yojana — EEE tax benefit, 21-year maturity

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-17 PM Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY) Ministry of Petroleum & Natural Gas MAY 2016 BJP/NDA ▼ Why Launched Over 24 crore households were using chulhas (wood/dung fuel), causing severe indoor air pollution responsible for 5 lakh deaths annually, primarily affecting women and children. Objective Provide free LPG connections to BPL women to safeguard their health Reduce indoor air pollution (HAP — Household Air Pollution) Empower women through clean cooking fuel (Swachh Indhan, Behtar Jeevan) Key Features Phase 1 (2016): 5 crore LPG connections to BPL wome

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: 0, 2021): 1 crore more + migrants ₹1,600 financial support per connection (security deposit + fittings) EMI facility for stove and first refill; Aadhaar-linked DBT for subsidy Ujjwala 2. 0: Self-declaration for migrant workers without address proof ⚠ TRAP PMUY Phase 1 target was 5 crore connections; total target became 8 crore (with Phase 2). Launched at Ballia, UP on May 1, 2016. Connection is in WOMAN's name — key feature. 💡 TIP India became the world's 2nd largest LPG consumer after PMUY.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Before 2016, India had ~13 crore LPG connections; after PMUY it crossed 30 crore. LPG coverage went from ~55% to ~100% of households. 💡 MNEMONIC: "UJJWALA = Ujjwal (bright) future for women — Clean fuel, Clean lungs" Ujjwal = bright/luminous — LPG gives clean light and clean cooking to women

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-18 Swachh Bharat Mission (SBM) MoJal (SBM-G) / MoHUA (SBM-U) OCT 2014 BJP/NDA ▼ Why Launched India had one of the worst sanitation conditions globally — 600 million people defecating in the open, affecting women's dignity, child health and public hygiene. Objective Achieve Open Defecation Free (ODF) status across India by Oct 2, 2019 Construct individual household toilets, community toilets, and solid waste management infrastructure Change sanitation behaviour through IEC campaigns Key Features SBM Phase 1 (2014–19): 11+ crore toilets built;

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: SBM-U is under Ministry of Housing & Urban Affairs. TWO different ministries for two components! 💡 TIP World's largest behaviour change programme. Swachh Survekshan is the annual urban cleanliness survey under SBM-U. Indore has been ranked #1 in Swachh Survekshan consecutively for 7 years (2017–2023). 💡 MNEMONIC: "SBM-G = Jal Shakti Ministry; SBM-U = Urban Affairs Ministry" G=Gram=Rural=Jal Shakti; U=Urban=Housing & Urban Affairs — two different ministries

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-19 AMRUT — Atal Mission for Rejuvenation & Urban Transformation Ministry of Housing & Urban Affairs JUN 2015 BJP/NDA ▼ Why Launched Indian cities lacked basic infrastructure — water supply, sewerage, drainage and parks — particularly in small and medium towns. JNNURM had expired and a new urban infrastructure programme was needed. Objective Ensure basic services (water, sewerage, parks) in 500 cities with population 1 lakh+ Improve quality of life in urban areas through planned urban infrastructure AMRUT 2.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: 0 (2021–26): 100% water security in all ULBs — sewerage, water recycling Key Features AMRUT 1. 0: 500 cities; ₹50,000 crore outlay (2015–20); focus on water supply, sewerage, drainage, parks AMRUT 2. 0: 4,700 ULBs; ₹2. 77 lakh crore total investment envisaged State Annual Action Plans (SAAP) submitted by states; project-based funding Rejuvenation of 500 water bodies and 13,000+ parks built under AMRUT 1. 0 ⚠ TRAP AMRUT ≠ Smart Cities Mission. AMRUT covers 500 cities for basic infrastructure; Smart Cities is for 100 selected cities for smart sol

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Both launched same year (2015) under same ministry — frequently mixed up. 💡 TIP AMRUT = Atal Mission for Rejuvenation and Urban Transformation. Named after "amrut" (nectar/immortal). 0 focuses on circular economy of water — treating and reusing wastewater. 💡 MNEMONIC: "AMRUT = 500 cities, Basic infra; Smart Cities = 100 cities, Tech solutions" AMRUT is broader (500) but basic; Smart Cities is selective (100) but advanced

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-20 Smart Cities Mission Ministry of Housing & Urban Affairs JUN 2015 BJP/NDA ▼ Why Launched Indian cities needed technological transformation to improve urban governance, mobility, safety and citizen services. A model cities approach was needed to demonstrate smart urban solutions. Objective Develop 100 smart cities as models for other cities through Area-Based Development (ABD) Use technology (ICT) to improve infrastructure, governance, and quality of life Create Integrated Command and Control Centres (ICCC) in smart cities Key Features 100

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Tamil Nadu cities selected: Chennai, Madurai, Coimbatore, Thanjavur, Vellore, Salem, Tiruppur, Tiruchirappalli, Tirunelveli, Erode — 10 cities from TN! 💡 TIP Mission period was 2015–2023, extended to June 2024. Smart Cities Mission completed — a key current affairs update. ICCCs became COVID war rooms during 2020–21 pandemic. 💡 MNEMONIC: "100 Smart = 500 AMRUT = 4700 AMRUT 2. 0" — scaling of urban missions City count progression: 100 (Smart) → 500 (AMRUT) → 4700 (AMRUT 2.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-21 PM Gati Shakti — National Master Plan Ministry of Commerce & Industry / PM's Office OCT 2021 BJP/NDA ▼ Why Launched Siloed infrastructure planning led to delays, cost overruns and poor coordination between ministries. A unified digital platform was needed for multimodal connectivity planning. Key Features GIS-based digital master plan integrating 16 ministries' infrastructure data on one platform Aims to cut logistics costs from 13–14% of GDP to below 8% (global standard) Covers 6 pillars: Roads, Railways, Airports, Ports, Mass Transport,

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: It integrates existing schemes (Bharatmala, Sagarmala, UDAN etc. ) under one digital planning framework. 💡 TIP Launched on Oct 13, 2021 — PM Modi's birthday. GIS layers include socio-economic data, environment clearances, land ownership, population maps — enabling holistic infrastructure planning. 💡 MNEMONIC: "Gati Shakti = Speed + Strength — One portal for 16 ministries" Gati=Speed, Shakti=Strength — unified digital planning for infrastructure speed

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-22 Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme DPIIT / Respective sector ministries MAR 2020 BJP/NDA ▼ Why Launched India needed to boost domestic manufacturing competitiveness and attract global supply chains, especially after COVID-19 exposed risks of China-dependent global supply chains. Key Features Incentive of 4–6% on incremental sales over base year for 5 years Covers 14 sectors including Mobile Phones, Pharma, Medical Devices, Auto, Advanced Chemistry Cells, Textiles, Food Processing Total outlay: ₹1.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: 97 lakh crore across all 14 sectors Attracted Apple, Samsung, Foxconn to India for mobile manufacturing ⚠ TRAP PLI is NOT a subsidy scheme — it's an INCENTIVE on incremental production over a base year. Companies must first invest and produce more, then receive incentive. This output-linked model is key. 💡 TIP PLI for Mobile phones was first (2020); India became 2nd largest mobile phone manufacturer globally. PLI for Semiconductors announced 2022 — crucial for self-reliance in chips.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Tamil Nadu benefits from PLI in electronics, auto and textiles. 💡 MNEMONIC: "PLI = Pay Later Incentive — only if you produce more first" Output-linked: incentive paid AFTER increased production is demonstrated

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-23 National Education Policy 2020 (NEP 2020) Ministry of Education JUL 2020 BJP/NDA ▼ Why Launched India's education system (governed by NPE 1986) was outdated, rote-learning-focused and misaligned with 21st century skill requirements. NEP 2020 is the first education policy reform in 34 years. Key Features New school structure: 5+3+3+4 (replacing 10+2) — Foundational, Preparatory, Middle, Secondary Mother tongue instruction till Class 5 (at least); 3-language formula retained GER target in higher education: 50% by 2035 (from ~27% in 2020) Aca

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: The previous policy was NPE 1986 (modified 1992). NEP does NOT abolish Board exams — it reforms them to test core competencies, not rote learning. 💡 TIP Tamil Nadu has expressed reservations about the 3-language formula under NEP 2020, especially mandatory Hindi. TN follows a 2-language policy (Tamil + English). This is a frequently asked TNPSC topic. 💡 MNEMONIC: "NEP 5+3+3+4 = Foundational Prep Middle Secondary" Remember the new school structure replacing old 10+2 system

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-24 PM e-VIDYA Ministry of Education MAY 2020 BJP/NDA ▼ Why Launched COVID-19 school closures created a learning emergency. A multi-modal digital education platform was needed to ensure no child misses education during and after the pandemic. Key Features DIKSHA (Digital Infrastructure for Knowledge Sharing) — e-content for all grades in all states SWAYAM (MOOCs platform), SWAYAM Prabha (32 DTH channels) for higher education One Class One Channel — Doordarshan dedicates one TV channel per class (1–12) Shiksha Vani — podcasts; Radio and communi

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: DIKSHA is not a standalone scheme — it's under PM e-VIDYA. Do not confuse SWAYAM (MOOCs for higher ed) with SWAYAM Prabha (DTH channels). 💡 TIP DIKSHA has 3 crore+ e-content pieces in 36 languages. QR codes on textbooks (ENERGISED textbooks) link to DIKSHA content — a unique feature combining print and digital learning. 💡 MNEMONIC: "PM e-VIDYA = DIKSHA + SWAYAM + Prabha + Vani" Four components: DIKSHA(school), SWAYAM(higher ed MOOCs), Prabha(DTH), Vani(radio)

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-25 One Nation One Ration Card (ONORC) Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food & Public Distribution AUG 2019 BJP/NDA ▼ Why Launched Migrant workers (8–9 crore) lost access to subsidised food grains when they moved to other states as ration cards were state-specific and non-portable. Key Features Allows any NFSA beneficiary to claim food ration from any Fair Price Shop in India Aadhaar-based biometric authentication at PDS outlets (PoS machines) Implemented in all states/UTs as of 2022; Mera Ration App for beneficiaries Annavitaran portal tracks re

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Common misconception that a new card is issued. It's an interoperability system, not a new card. 💡 TIP ONORC became significant during COVID-19 when reverse migration occurred and workers needed food at origin states. Supreme Court directed states to implement ONORC quickly during 2020–21. 💡 MNEMONIC: "ONORC = One Nation, One Card, One Aadhaar — Ration Anywhere" Aadhaar authentication enables portability of PDS entitlement across all states

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-26 Atma Nirbhar Bharat Abhiyan Finance Ministry / PM's Office (Umbrella Mission) MAY 2020 BJP/NDA ▼ Why Launched COVID-19 economic crisis exposed India's over-dependence on imports and global supply chains. A self-reliant India was needed across sectors — from defence to electronics to agriculture. Key Features ₹20 lakh crore economic package (approx. 10% of GDP) announced in 5 tranches 5 pillars: Economy, Infrastructure, Technology-driven System, Vibrant Demography, Demand Sub-missions: ANB for Defence, ANB for MSMEs, PLI schemes, Emergency

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: The ₹20 lakh crore "package" included existing + new fiscal measures, not all fresh spending. Do not treat it as one scheme. 💡 TIP Announced by PM on May 12, 2020. FM Nirmala Sitharaman announced 5 tranches of measures over 5 consecutive days (May 13–17, 2020). ANB for Defence restricted imports of 300+ items — boosting domestic defence manufacturing. 💡 MNEMONIC: "ANB = 5 Pillars: E-I-T-V-D (Economy, Infrastructure, Tech, Vibrant Demography, Demand)" Five pillars of Atma Nirbhar Bharat for self-reliant India

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-27 PM Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY) Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers' Welfare FEB 2016 BJP/NDA ▼ Why Launched Previous crop insurance schemes (NAIS, MNAIS) had high farmer premium burden, limited coverage and delayed claims. Farmers needed comprehensive, affordable crop insurance. Key Features Premium: Kharif 2%, Rabi 1. 5%, Commercial/Horticultural 5% (government pays rest) Covers all stages: pre-sowing, post-harvest, mid-season adversity Voluntary from 2020; earlier compulsory for loanee farmers Technology use: satellite, drone imagery for qui

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Premium cap is on farmer's share only — actual premium can be higher, with state/centre bearing the rest. Also: PMFBY made VOLUNTARY (not compulsory) from Kharif 2020. 💡 TIP PMFBY uses "Area Approach" — crop loss is assessed at defined unit area (village/gram panchayat level), not individual farm level. This simplifies claims but may not reflect individual farmer's loss perfectly. 💡 MNEMONIC: "PMFBY: 2% Kharif, 1. 5% Rabi — farmer's share is always less than 5%" Premium formula: K=2, R=1.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-28 PM Krishi Sinchayee Yojana (PMKSY) Ministry of Jal Shakti & Agriculture JUL 2015 BJP/NDA ▼ Why Launched Only 47% of India's net sown area was irrigated; 53% depended on rainfall. Expanding irrigation and improving water use efficiency was critical for agricultural security. Key Features Tagline: "Har Khet ko Pani, More Crop Per Drop" 4 components: AIBP (Accelerated Irrigation Benefits), Har Khet Ko Pani, Per Drop More Crop, Watershed Development Micro-irrigation Fund (₹5,000 crore with NABARD) for drip and sprinkler irrigation Convergence

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Often asked as: "Which component is under which ministry? " 💡 TIP Jal Shakti Abhiyan is different from PMKSY — it's a campaign for water conservation. PMKSY's micro-irrigation target: cover 1 crore hectares under drip irrigation and 1. 5 crore hectares under sprinkler irrigation. 💡 MNEMONIC: "Har Khet Ko Pani + More Crop Per Drop = PMKSY's two-sided goal" First slogan = expand coverage; Second slogan = improve efficiency

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-29 Soil Health Card (SHC) Scheme Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers' Welfare FEB 2015 BJP/NDA ▼ Why Launched Indiscriminate use of chemical fertilisers degraded soil health, reduced crop yields and increased input costs for farmers who lacked scientific soil nutrient data. Key Features SHC tests soil for 12 parameters: Macro (N,P,K), Secondary (S, Ca, Mg), Micro (Zn, Fe, Mn, Cu, Bo, Mo) Card issued every 2 years with crop-wise fertiliser recommendations 2.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: 5 crore soil samples tested; 22 crore+ SHCs distributed Soil Health Card Portal and mobile app for farmers ⚠ TRAP SHC tests 12 parameters (not 9 or 10 — a frequent MCQ trap). The card is issued every 2 years (or 3 years in some interpretations for rainfed areas). Launched on World Soil Day — Feb 19, 2015 at Suratgarh, Rajasthan. 💡 TIP SHC Scheme was ranked by UNDP as a best practice in soil management. Farmers can get recommendations for balanced fertiliser use, reducing excess urea application which causes eutrophication.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: 💡 MNEMONIC: "SHC tests 12 soil parameters — 3 Macro + 3 Secondary + 6 Micro" N,P,K (macro) + S,Ca,Mg (secondary) + Zn,Fe,Mn,Cu,Bo,Mo (micro) = 12 total

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-30 eNAM — National Agriculture Market Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers' Welfare (SFAC) APR 2016 BJP/NDA ▼ Why Launched Farmers received only 30–40% of consumer price due to multiple intermediaries and fragmented APMC markets. A pan-India electronic market was needed to eliminate middlemen and improve price discovery. Key Features Online trading portal linking existing APMC mandis into one national market Implemented by Small Farmers Agribusiness Consortium (SFAC) 1,361+ mandis across 23 states/UTs integrated (as of 2024) Farmers get SMS aler

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Physical trade still happens at mandis; eNAM enables online price discovery and payment. Also: SFAC (Small Farmers Agribusiness Consortium) is implementing agency, not NAFED. 💡 TIP eNAM was piloted in 8 states with 21 mandis on Apr 14, 2016 — Ambedkar Jayanti. The platform covers 203 commodities. Farmers, traders and buyers can participate from anywhere. 💡 MNEMONIC: "eNAM = e-market connecting APMCs, not replacing them" eNAM digitises and links existing mandis; doesn't dismantle the APMC system

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-31 PM Kisan Maan Dhan Yojana (PM-KMY) Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers' Welfare AUG 2019 BJP/NDA ▼ Key Features Pension of ₹3,000/month on turning 60 for small/marginal farmers who enrolled at 18–40 years Farmer contributes ₹55–200/month (age-dependent); Central government matches contribution Implemented through LIC as Fund Manager; pension fund created PM-KISAN beneficiaries can auto-enrol — contribution deducted from PM-KISAN transfers ⚠ TRAP PM-KMY is for farmers; PM-SYM (Shram Yogi Maan Dhan) is for unorganised workers; NPS Traders is f

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Don't mix them up. 💡 TIP All three Maan Dhan schemes (PM-KMY, PM-SYM, NPS Traders) provide ₹3,000/month pension. Same pension amount, different beneficiary categories. Launched in 2019; LIC is fund manager for all three. 💡 MNEMONIC: "3 Maan Dhan = 3 beneficiaries: Farmer(KMY), Worker(SYM), Trader(NPS)" All three give ₹3,000/month pension but for different occupational groups

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-32 Atal Pension Yojana (APY) Ministry of Finance / PFRDA MAY 2015 BJP/NDA ▼ Key Features Guaranteed pension of ₹1,000–5,000/month from age 60 for unorganised sector workers Age 18–40 at entry; contribution varies by age and chosen pension amount Government co-contributes 50% (or ₹1,000/year max) for first 5 years for eligible subscribers On subscriber's death, spouse gets equal pension; nominee gets accumulated corpus on spouse's death ⚠ TRAP APY replaced Swavalamban Scheme in 2015.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Income tax payers are NOT eligible for government co-contribution (from 2023, IT payers excluded from the scheme entirely). Managed by PFRDA (not LIC or EPFO). 💡 TIP APY has 6 crore+ subscribers (2024). PFRDA (Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority) manages APY. Guaranteed pension with mortality risk borne by government — unique feature. 💡 MNEMONIC: "APY = Atal Pension: 18-40 entry, ₹1K-5K/month, PFRDA manages" Age range (18-40), pension range (1K-5K), regulator (PFRDA) are key APY facts

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-33 PM Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY) Ministry of Finance / Insurance Company MAY 2015 BJP/NDA ▼ Key Features ₹2 lakh life insurance cover for death from any cause; annual premium ₹436 (revised 2022) Renewable one-year life cover; age group 18–50; linked to bank account Auto-debit of premium from savings bank account on consent Administered by LIC and other life insurance companies ⚠ TRAP PMJJBY = LIFE insurance (₹2 lakh for ANY death). PMSBY = ACCIDENT insurance only (₹2 lakh for accidental death + ₹1 lakh for partial disability).

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Both cover ₹2 lakh but PMJJBY is much more expensive (₹436 vs ₹20 for PMSBY). 💡 TIP Both PMJJBY and PMSBY were launched on May 9, 2015 by PM at Kolkata. Together they form a micro-insurance ecosystem for the unbanked/under-insured poor along with APY (pension). 💡 MNEMONIC: "JJ = Jeevan Jyoti = Life light = LIFE insurance; SB = Suraksha Bima = Safety = ACCIDENT insurance" Jeevan (life) = any cause death; Suraksha (safety/security) = accidental death/disability

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-34 PM Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY) Ministry of Finance / Non-life Insurance Companies MAY 2015 BJP/NDA ▼ Key Features ₹2 lakh accidental death/total disability cover; ₹1 lakh for partial disability Annual premium just ₹20 (revised to ₹20 as of 2022 — earlier ₹12) Age 18–70; auto-debit from bank account; renewable annually Administered by non-life insurance companies (public sector preferred) ⚠ TRAP PMSBY covers only ACCIDENTAL death and disability — NOT natural death.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: PMJJBY covers all causes of death. Maximum age for PMSBY is 70 years (vs 50 for PMJJBY). The age limits differ — a common MCQ trap. 💡 TIP At ₹20/year (~₹1. 67/month), PMSBY is among the most affordable insurance globally. Over 34 crore PMSBY policies active (2023). Claims settled for road accidents are most common. 💡 MNEMONIC: "PMSBY = ₹20/year + Accident only + Age upto 70" Three key facts: cheapest premium (₹20), accidental only, older age limit (70 vs 50)

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-35 Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Grameen Kaushalya Yojana (DDU-GKY) Ministry of Rural Development SEP 2014 BJP/NDA ▼ Key Features Rural poor youth (15–35 years) from poor families skilled and placed in formal employment Part of National Rural Livelihoods Mission (NRLM); mandatory job placement post training Mandatory 3-year post-placement tracking; retention incentives for employers Previously known as Aajeevika Skills before 2014 rebranding ⚠ TRAP DDU-GKY is for RURAL youth under Ministry of Rural Development; PMKVY is for all youth (rural+urban) und

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Both are skilling schemes but under different ministries and target groups. 💡 TIP DDU-GKY uniquely mandates JOB PLACEMENT (not just training) — 75% of trained candidates must be placed in formal jobs. This outcome-focus differentiates it from other skilling schemes. 💡 MNEMONIC: "DDU-GKY = Rural poor youth + mandatory placement + Rural Dev Ministry" Rural focus + employment guarantee (not just training) = DDU-GKY's unique features

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-36 PM Employment Generation Programme (PMEGP) Ministry of MSME / KVIC AUG 2008 UPA/INC ▼ Key Features Credit-linked subsidy for setting up micro-enterprises in non-farm sector Subsidy: 15–35% of project cost (higher for SC/ST/women/NE regions) Implemented through KVIC, KVIBs, District Industries Centres Project cost limit: ₹50 lakh (manufacturing), ₹20 lakh (service sector) ⚠ TRAP PMEGP merged two older schemes: REGP (Rural Employment Generation Programme) and PMRY (Prime Minister's Rojgar Yojana).

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: KVIC (Khadi and Village Industries Commission) is the nodal implementing agency — not SIDBI or MSDE. 💡 TIP PMEGP targets 8 lakh persons annually for sustainable employment. Subsidy is deposited directly in the loan account; 3-year lock-in before subsidy is released to borrower. 💡 MNEMONIC: "PMEGP = KVIC implements, 15–35% subsidy, merged REGP+PMRY" Remember: KVIC agency, subsidy range, and that it's a merger of two old schemes

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-37 National Health Mission (NHM) Ministry of Health & Family Welfare APR 2013 UPA/INC ▼ Key Features Umbrella mission: NRHM (Rural) + NUHM (Urban) merged into NHM in 2013 Sub-missions: Reproductive Maternal Newborn Child Adolescent Health (RMNCH+A) ASHA (Accredited Social Health Activist) workers — 10. 40 lakh ASHAs nationwide Janani Suraksha Yojana (JSY) for institutional delivery; Rashtriya Bal Swasthya Karyakram ⚠ TRAP NHM = NRHM + NUHM. NRHM was launched in 2005; NHM subsumed it in 2013.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: ASHAs work under NHM — they are community health workers, NOT nurses. ASHA is paid performance-based incentives, not salary. 💡 TIP India's IMR dropped from 58 (2005) to 28 (2022) and MMR from 254 to 97 largely due to NHM interventions. ASHA workers are a critical success story — they link communities to health services. 💡 MNEMONIC: "NHM = NRHM (2005) + NUHM = Rural + Urban Health umbrella" NHM merged rural and urban health missions under one national framework in 2013

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-38 Saubhagya Scheme (PMGSY-P) Ministry of Power SEP 2017 BJP/NDA ▼ Key Features Pradhan Mantri Sahaj Bijli Har Ghar Yojana — free electricity connections to BPL households 2. 5 crore un-electrified households + 2. 5 crore poor electrified households targeted Last-mile connectivity including solar power kits for remote areas India achieved 100% household electrification by April 28, 2018 ⚠ TRAP Saubhagya ≠ DDUGJY (Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Gram Jyoti Yojana).

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: DDUGJY electrified VILLAGES (rural infrastructure); Saubhagya gave connections to HOUSEHOLDS within those villages. Village electrification ≠ household electrification. 💡 TIP India's electrification achievement: 18,452 un-electrified villages got power under DDUGJY (Apr 2018), THEN Saubhagya gave household connections. Saubhagya completed Sep 2019 (not Apr 2018 as often confused). 💡 MNEMONIC: "Saubhagya = household connection; DDUGJY = village electrification" Two-step process: village electrification (DDUGJY) → household connection (Saubhagy

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-39 UDAN — Ude Desh ka Aam Naagrik Ministry of Civil Aviation / AAI OCT 2016 BJP/NDA ▼ Key Features Regional Air Connectivity Scheme — fare capped at ₹2,500 for 1-hour flight (500 km) VGF (Viability Gap Funding) from Centre, State and AAI to airlines for operating unviable routes Connects tier 2 and tier 3 cities, unserved and underserved airports 540+ routes and 70+ airports operational under UDAN by 2023 ⚠ TRAP UDAN = Regional Connectivity Scheme under National Civil Aviation Policy 2016 (NCAP 2016).

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: NCAP 2016 is the policy; UDAN is a scheme under it. Don't confuse the two. 0 expanded to helicopters and seaplanes. 💡 TIP India has 148 operational airports (2024) — up from 74 in 2014. UDAN contributed significantly. Tagline: "Ude Desh ka Aam Naagrik" (Let the common man of the nation fly). Fare cap: ₹2,500 for 500 km. 💡 MNEMONIC: "UDAN = ₹2,500 cap, VGF funded, tier 2/3 cities — Common Man Flies" Fare cap + viability funding + regional connectivity = UDAN's three pillars

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-40 Sagarmala Programme Ministry of Ports, Shipping & Waterways MAR 2015 BJP/NDA ▼ Key Features Port-led development — modernise ports, enhance connectivity, promote coastal shipping 4 pillars: Port Modernisation, Port Connectivity Enhancement, Port-linked Industrialisation, Coastal Community Development 576 projects worth ₹6 lakh crore identified Focus on reducing logistics cost and boosting coastal and inland waterways ⚠ TRAP Sagarmala = port-led coastal/maritime development.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Bharatmala = road network. Setu Bharatam = bridges. All are connectivity schemes but for different modes. Tamil Nadu's Chennai and Ennore ports are key beneficiaries. 💡 TIP India has 12 Major Ports (under Central government) and 200+ minor ports (under State governments). Sagarmala focuses on both but central funding goes to major port modernisation. 💡 MNEMONIC: "Sagarmala = Sea (Sagar) + Garland (Mala) — stringing ports along the coast" Sagar=sea, Mala=garland — connecting India's 7,516 km coastline with port infrastructure

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-41 Bharatmala Pariyojana Ministry of Road Transport & Highways (NHAI) OCT 2017 BJP/NDA ▼ Key Features Phase 1: 34,800 km of national highways at ₹5. 35 lakh crore Focuses on Economic Corridors, Ring Roads, Expressways, National Corridor Efficiency Improvement Incorporates 44 economic corridors connecting major economic nodes Integrated with PM Gati Shakti for multimodal last-mile connectivity ⚠ TRAP Bharatmala Phase 1 originally targeted 2022 completion — significantly delayed to 2027+.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Don't quote original timelines as current facts. Also: PMGSY (rural roads) is SEPARATE from Bharatmala (national highways). 💡 TIP India's NH network expanded from 91,000 km (2014) to 1. 46 lakh km (2024) — a 60% increase. Chennai–Bengaluru Expressway and Delhi–Mumbai Expressway are key Bharatmala corridors. 💡 MNEMONIC: "Bharatmala = Roads, Sagarmala = Ports, UDAN = Air — transport trilogy" Three PM-era infrastructure schemes for three transport modes

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-42 Stand Up India Ministry of Finance / SIDBI / DFS APR 2016 BJP/NDA ▼ Key Features Loans of ₹10 lakh – ₹1 crore to SC/ST entrepreneurs and women for greenfield enterprises Each bank branch must lend to at least 1 SC/ST and 1 woman entrepreneur Stand-Up India portal and handholding support through SIDBI, NABARD Collateral-free under CGTMSE (Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for MSEs) ⚠ TRAP Stand Up India ≠ Startup India. Stand Up India = loans for SC/ST + women entrepreneurs (equity/inclusion focus).

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Startup India = ecosystem for innovation (technology/scale focus). Launched same year (2016) but completely different! 💡 TIP Stand Up India targets "greenfield" enterprises — first-time enterprises, not existing businesses. Women must be majority (51%) shareholders. Loan range: ₹10 lakh to ₹1 crore for composite loan (working capital + term loan). 💡 MNEMONIC: "Stand Up = SC/ST + Women standing up with ₹10L-1Cr loans" Social equity focus: SC/ST and women entrepreneurs, ₹10 lakh to ₹1 crore greenfield loans

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-43 PM Vishwakarma Yojana Ministry of MSME SEP 2023 BJP/NDA ▼ Key Features Supports traditional artisans and craftspeople (18 trade categories: blacksmiths, goldsmiths, potters, weavers, etc. ) Collateral-free credit: ₹1 lakh (1st tranche), ₹2 lakh (2nd tranche) at 5% interest Skill training, digital payments incentive, market linkage support ₹13,000 crore outlay over 5 years (2023–28) ⚠ TRAP PM Vishwakarma is for TRADITIONAL ARTISANS and craftspeople (not modern entrepreneurs).

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: The 18 trades are specific — includes carpenters, boat-makers, armorers, hammer-smiths, locksmiths, sculptors. Not all craft occupations are covered. 💡 TIP Launched on Vishwakarma Jayanti (Sep 17, 2023). PM Vishwakarma is the first scheme specifically for traditional artisans (OBC and general category craftspersons). Certificate and ID card issued — Vishwakarma recognition. 💡 MNEMONIC: "Vishwakarma = 18 trades, ₹1+2 lakh credit, 5% interest, Sep 2023" Lord Vishwakarma = craftsman deity; scheme for 18 traditional craft trades

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-44 National Apprenticeship Promotion Scheme (NAPS) Ministry of Skill Development & Entrepreneurship AUG 2016 BJP/NDA ▼ Key Features Government shares 25% of stipend (max ₹1,500/month) to employer for engaging apprentices Basic training reimbursement for ITI-pass apprentices (max ₹7,500) Target: 50 lakh apprentices by 2020; expanded to 10 lakh/year Governed under Apprentices Act 1961 (amended 2014 for flexibility) ⚠ TRAP NAPS ≠ National Apprenticeship Training Scheme (NATS).

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: NATS is for engineering diploma/degree holders (under Ministry of Education via BOAT); NAPS is for ITI and school-pass youth under MSDE. Two different schemes under two different ministries! 💡 TIP Apprenticeship Act 1961 was a major barrier — amended in 2014 to allow optional trades, industry-designed curricula, and private sector participation. NAPS incentivised apprenticeship post the 2014 amendment. 💡 MNEMONIC: "NAPS = Government pays 25% of stipend to employer — sharing cost of upskilling" Government subsidy (25% stipend) incentivises emp

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-45 Mission Shakti (Women Safety & Empowerment) Ministry of Women & Child Development 2021-22 BJP/NDA ▼ Key Features Umbrella scheme merging multiple women's schemes from 2021–22 Two sub-schemes: Sambal (safety/security) and Samarthya (empowerment) Sambal: One Stop Centres, Women Helpline (181), Beti Bachao Beti Padhao, Nari Adalat Samarthya: Ujjwala (anti-trafficking), Swadhar Greh, Working Women Hostels, STEP, PM Matru Vandana ⚠ TRAP PM Matru Vandana Yojana (PMMVY) is UNDER Mission Shakti / Samarthya — not a standalone scheme now.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Also: there is a DIFFERENT Mission Shakti in Odisha for SHG women's empowerment — do not confuse with this Central scheme. 💡 TIP One Stop Centres (Sakhi Centres) under Sambal provide integrated services (medical, legal, psychological, shelter) to women affected by violence. 700+ OSCs operational across India. 💡 MNEMONIC: "Mission Shakti = Sambal (Safety) + Samarthya (Empowerment)" Sambal=protect/support, Samarthya=empower/enable — two pillars of women's welfare

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-46 POSHAN Abhiyaan (National Nutrition Mission) Ministry of Women & Child Development MAR 2018 BJP/NDA ▼ Key Features Target: Reduce stunting, wasting, anaemia and low birth weight by 2–3% annually by 2022 Convergence of ICDS, NHM, SBM, PMGKAY, Jal Jeevan Mission for holistic nutrition Technology: POSHAN Tracker app; Anganwadi workers track growth in real-time Jan Andolan (People's Movement) for behavioural change on nutrition ⚠ TRAP POSHAN Abhiyaan ≠ PM POSHAN (Mid-Day Meal).

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: POSHAN Abhiyaan = National Nutrition Mission targeting under-5 children, adolescent girls, pregnant and lactating mothers. PM POSHAN = school meals for Class 1–8 students. 💡 TIP India's stunting rate fell from 38. 4% (NFHS-4, 2015–16) to 35. 5% (NFHS-5, 2019–21). September is observed as POSHAN Maah (Nutrition Month) nationally. NFHS-5 data showed mixed results on nutrition targets. 💡 MNEMONIC: "POSHAN Abhiyaan = Stunting, Wasting, Anaemia, LBW — SWAL targets" Four nutrition indicators: Stunting, Wasting, Anaemia, Low Birth Weight — reduce by

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-47 PM Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY) Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying SEP 2020 BJP/NDA ▼ Key Features ₹20,050 crore investment for fisheries sector (2020–25) — largest ever for fisheries Double fish production from 13. 75 MMT to 22 MMT by 2024–25 Double fishers' incomes; reduce post-harvest losses from 25% to 10% KCC extension to fishers and animal husbandry workers ⚠ TRAP PMMSY is separate from Fisheries and Aquaculture Infrastructure Development Fund (FIDF).

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: PMMSY = comprehensive fisheries development scheme; FIDF = infrastructure fund for fisheries sector. Tamil Nadu is a major beneficiary — leading maritime fishing state. 💡 TIP India is the 3rd largest fish producing country (after China, Indonesia) and 2nd largest aquaculture producer. Tamil Nadu coast: 1,076 km, with major fishing harbours at Chennai, Cuddalore, Nagapattinam, Rameswaram. 💡 MNEMONIC: "PMMSY = ₹20,050 crore, 22 MMT target, Blue Revolution flagship" Largest fisheries investment, targeting 22 million metric tonne production — "Bl

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-48 PM-KUSUM (Solar for Farmers) Ministry of New & Renewable Energy (MNRE) FEB 2019 BJP/NDA ▼ Key Features 3 components: A (2 MW solar plants on barren land), B (standalone solar pumps), C (solar grid-connected pump sets) 10 GW solar from component A; 17. 5 lakh solar pumps (B+C) Farmers earn from selling surplus solar power to DISCOM Reduces electricity subsidy burden on state governments ⚠ TRAP PM-KUSUM full form: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Urja Suraksha evam Utthaan Mahabhiyan.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Three components (A, B, C) are distinct — Component A = ground-mounted solar plants (barren/fallow land); not just solar pumps. All three together = PM-KUSUM. 💡 TIP Tamil Nadu targets PM-KUSUM Component B heavily — solar pumps replacing diesel/electric pumps in delta districts. A farmer with a 2 MW plant on 5 acres can earn ₹15–20 lakh annually from power sale. 💡 MNEMONIC: "KUSUM A+B+C = Land + Standalone pump + Grid-connected pump" Three components for three types of solar use by farmers

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-49 FAME India (Faster Adoption of EVs) Ministry of Heavy Industries APR 2015 BJP/NDA ▼ Key Features FAME I (2015–19): ₹895 crore for demand creation and EV infrastructure FAME II (2019–24): ₹10,000 crore for e-buses, e-3W, e-4W, charging infrastructure Demand incentive: up to ₹1. 5 lakh for e-cars, ₹15,000 for e-2W EMPS 2024 (Electric Mobility Promotion Scheme) replaced FAME II with focus on 2W, 3W ⚠ TRAP FAME II ended March 2024; replaced by EMPS 2024 (₹500 crore, Apr–Jul 2024), then PM e-Drive scheme (₹10,900 crore, 2024–26) announced.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Exam questions may still refer to FAME II — specify the scheme's active period when answering. 💡 TIP Tamil Nadu EV Policy 2023 aligns with FAME. TN targets 100% EV adoption in state transport by 2030. TNSRTC, MTC buses being replaced with e-buses funded partly through FAME II. 💡 MNEMONIC: "FAME I=2015(₹895Cr), FAME II=2019(₹10,000Cr) — ten-fold increase in EV support" FAME II is 10x FAME I in budget, reflecting India's accelerated EV push

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: C-50 National Green Hydrogen Mission Ministry of New & Renewable Energy (MNRE) JAN 2023 BJP/NDA ▼ Why Launched India's commitment to net-zero by 2070 and reducing fossil fuel dependence required development of Green Hydrogen as a clean energy carrier for hard-to-abate sectors. Key Features Target: 5 MMT green hydrogen production per year by 2030 Cost target: <₹1/SCM (standard cubic metre) by 2030 SIGHT (Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition): ₹17,490 crore incentives India to become global hub for green hydrogen export (Japan, S

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Grey Hydrogen = from natural gas (with CO2 emission). Blue Hydrogen = grey + carbon capture. Only GREEN is truly zero-carbon. Frequently asked in science + schemes combined questions. 💡 TIP India's 5 MMT green H2 target could replace import of ~1 MMT of urea (fertiliser) and power fuel-cell vehicles. ₹8 lakh crore investment expected; 6 lakh jobs projected by 2030. Tamil Nadu — Tuticorin port as potential green H2 export hub. 💡 MNEMONIC: "Green H2: 5 MMT by 2030, SIGHT scheme, <₹1/SCM cost target" Production target (5MMT) + scheme name (SIGHT

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-51 Kalaignar Magalir Urimai Thittam Social Welfare & Women Empowerment Dept. , TN SEP 2022 DMK ▼ Why Launched Women's unpaid domestic and care work was economically invisible. TN government sought to recognise women as household heads and provide direct income support as an entitlement, not charity. Objective Provide ₹1,000/month cash transfer to women heads of families directly in bank accounts Recognise women's economic contribution and enhance their purchasing power Financially empower ~1.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: 06 crore women beneficiaries in Tamil Nadu Key Features ₹1,000/month DBT to women aged 21+ who are heads of families No income criterion — universal for eligible women heads of household Fully funded by Tamil Nadu state government; ₹7,000 crore annual outlay Online application through AMMA app or ration shop Target Beneficiaries ~1. 06 crore women heads of households in Tamil Nadu aged 21 years and above Comparison Table Feature Magalir Urimai Karnataka Gruha Lakshmi Telangana Rythu Bandhu Amount ₹1,000/month ₹2,000/month ₹5,000/acre (farmers)

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Urimai Thittam is specifically cash transfer for women heads of household. The word "Urimai" means "right/entitlement" — positioning it as a rights-based scheme, not welfare. 💡 TIP Launched by CM MK Stalin on Sep 15, 2022. The scheme transfers ₹1,000 directly every month — making it one of the most significant state-level women's income transfer schemes in India. Beneficiaries receive via Direct Benefit Transfer. 💡 MNEMONIC: "Urimai = ₹1,000 right for 1 crore+ women — the 1-1-1 scheme" ₹1,000 per month × 1 crore+ beneficiaries × launched in 2

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-52 Chief Minister's Breakfast Scheme (Kalaignar Breakfast Scheme) School Education Dept. , TN SEP 2022 DMK ▼ Why Launched Studies showed that hunger in the morning severely affected children's attention span and learning outcomes. Many government school children came without eating breakfast due to family poverty. Key Features Free breakfast (idli, upma, pongal variants) provided before school hours to Classes 1–5 in government schools ~1. 14 lakh students in 1,545 government elementary schools in Phase 1 Expanded to all government elementary

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: CM Breakfast is a TN state scheme for breakfast (morning); PM POSHAN is a central scheme for lunch (afternoon). Both operate in TN government schools simultaneously. 💡 TIP Launched on Sep 15, 2022 at Kovilpatti, Thoothukudi by CM MK Stalin. TN has a legacy of school meal schemes — K. Kamaraj's 1925 noon-meal scheme was the world's first. CM Breakfast builds on this tradition by adding morning nutrition. 💡 MNEMONIC: "TN meals: 1925 Kamaraj Noon + 2022 Stalin Breakfast = 100 years of school feeding legacy" TN's school feeding history: K.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-53 Naan Mudhalvan Scheme School Education & Higher Education Dept. , TN MAY 2022 DMK ▼ Why Launched Government school and college students in TN lacked access to competitive exam coaching, job-readiness skills, and personality development — a gap that worsened inequalities in employment outcomes. Key Features Free coaching for NEET, JEE, TNPSC, banking, government job exams for government school/college students Soft skills, personality development, digital literacy included in curriculum Online + offline coaching; Naan Mudhalvan portal (naan

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: in) Industry partnerships for internships and placement support Target Beneficiaries Students of government schools (Classes 6–12) and government-aided colleges in Tamil Nadu ⚠ TRAP Naan Mudhalvan = skill/coaching scheme for existing students. Pudhumai Penn = scholarship for girl students to continue higher education. Both are education-focused DMK schemes but with different purposes — don't mix them. 💡 TIP "Naan Mudhalvan" means "I am first" in Tamil — empowerment tagline for students.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: The scheme has enrolled 15+ lakh students across 5,500+ government institutions. TNPSC aspirants studying in government colleges benefit directly! 💡 MNEMONIC: "Naan Mudhalvan = I am First — free coaching for gov't school students" Tamil meaning (I am first) reflects the aspirational positioning of the coaching scheme

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-54 Pudhumai Penn Scheme School Education Dept. , TN SEP 2022 DMK ▼ Why Launched Many girl students from government schools dropped out after Class 12 due to financial constraints and social pressure. A scholarship was needed to encourage girl students to pursue higher education. Key Features ₹1,000/month scholarship to girl students who studied in government schools (Class 6–12) and continue to higher education in any course Scholarship continues throughout the duration of the course (3-5 years) Covers students in government and aided college

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: The key eligibility: must have studied in government schools — not private schools. Girls in private schools are excluded. 💡 TIP "Pudhumai Penn" means "Modern/New Woman" in Tamil. Launched Sep 15, 2022. Unique feature: scholarship continues for the FULL DURATION of the higher education course — not just first year. Linked with bank accounts. 💡 MNEMONIC: "Pudhumai Penn = New Woman + ₹1,000/month throughout college + Government school girls only" Modern Woman (meaning) + continuous scholarship + government school eligibility criterion

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-55 Tamil Nadu Urban Livelihood Mission (TNULM) Municipal Administration & Water Supply, TN 2014 AIADMK ▼ Key Features State-level implementation of DAY-NULM (Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana – National Urban Livelihoods Mission) Targets urban poor through SHG formation, skill training, and self-employment support Urban Shelter for Homeless (USH) and training centres (MSTCs) under TNULM Integration with CMCHIS and Makkalai Thedi Maruthuvam for urban poor health ⚠ TRAP TNULM is the STATE IMPLEMENTATION of DAY-NULM (Central scheme).

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: The State scheme adds TN-specific components. Funding ratio for DAY-NULM: 75:25 (Centre:State) for most states; 90:10 for NE states. 💡 TIP Tamil Nadu's urban poverty rate (~8%) is lower than national average (~20%) but urban informal sector workers — street vendors, domestic workers, construction labour — need targeted support through TNULM. 💡 MNEMONIC: "TNULM = TN's version of DAY-NULM — SHGs + Skills + Shelter for urban poor" State-level implementation of central NULM with three foci: SHGs, skills, urban shelter

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-56 Innuyir Kaaval Thittam (Life Protection Scheme) Home Dept. / TN Police 2021 DMK ▼ Key Features 24×7 police protection mechanism for women and children at risk of violence Dedicated helpline and rapid response for distress calls from women Works alongside POCSO cells and Special Crime Investigation Cells Beat police officers trained in gender-sensitive handling of cases ⚠ TRAP Innuyir Kaaval = "Protection of Life" — specifically police protection for women/children at risk.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Do not confuse with Vidiyal Payanam (transport safety for students) or Nilavembu (disease control). Different safety schemes have different administrative departments. 💡 TIP "Innuyir" means "precious life/sweet life" in Tamil. The scheme enhances TN Police's responsiveness to gender-based violence — complementing the central Nirbhaya Fund for women's safety infrastructure. 💡 MNEMONIC: "Innuyir Kaaval = Precious Life Guard — TN Police women's safety net" Innuyir=precious life, Kaaval=guard/protection — police-led women safety programme

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-57 Vidiyal Payanam (Free Bus Travel for Students) Transport Dept. , TN / TNSTC SEP 2021 DMK ▼ Key Features Free bus travel on all government buses for students studying in government and government-aided schools and colleges Extended later to all women in Tamil Nadu government buses — free travel for all women TNSTC, MTC, SETC buses covered under this scheme Revenue compensation to transport corporations from state budget ⚠ TRAP Vidiyal Payanam started for students but was EXPANDED to free bus travel for ALL WOMEN in TN.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: The expansion to all women is the more significant and frequently tested fact. "Vidiyal" means dawn — a metaphor for a new beginning in accessibility. 💡 TIP Over 4 crore women benefit from free bus travel daily in Tamil Nadu after expansion. This is one of the world's largest free public transport schemes for women. Annual subsidy burden: ~₹2,000+ crore on state exchequer. 💡 MNEMONIC: "Vidiyal = Dawn + Payanam = Journey — free journey for women at dawn and always" Vidiyal(dawn/beginning) + Payanam(journey) = free bus travel marking a new begi

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-58 Tamil Nadu Startup & Innovation Policy Industries Dept. / StartupTN 2023 DMK ▼ Key Features TN aims to be Top 3 startup ecosystem nationally; Chennai has 5,000+ DPIIT startups StartupTN — nodal agency; TANSEED (TN Startup Seed Fund) provides early-stage funding Tidel Parks across TN cities as incubation hubs; IITM Research Park (Chennai) Sector focus: FinTech, MedTech, AgriTech, EduTech, CleanTech ⚠ TRAP StartupTN ≠ Startup India. StartupTN is the TN state body; Startup India is the Central scheme.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Both offer incentives but StartupTN provides TN-specific funding, incubation and policy relaxations that supplement central Startup India benefits. 💡 TIP Chennai is India's 4th largest startup hub. Tamil Nadu has Anna Hazare (TIDCO), SIPCOT, TIDEL parks as part of startup ecosystem. TN has produced unicorns including Freshworks, Zoho — global SaaS leaders from Tamil Nadu. 💡 MNEMONIC: "StartupTN = TANSEED + Tidel Parks + IITM RP — Chennai startup ecosystem" Three pillars of TN startup ecosystem: seed fund, physical hubs, and research instituti

chunks.title: Study Material
