chunks.text: T-59 Makkalai Thedi Maruthuvam (Healthcare Doorstep) Health & Family Welfare Dept. , TN AUG 2021 DMK ▼ Why Launched COVID-19 revealed that the most vulnerable — elderly, disabled, bed-ridden patients — could not access healthcare facilities. Door-step medical services became essential for inclusive healthcare. Key Features Medical teams visit homes to provide primary healthcare: BP/sugar screening, diagnostics, medicine 6. 4 crore people covered; covers rural and urban areas in all 38 districts Telemedicine integration: patients can consult spe

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: MTM is about doorstep primary healthcare delivery, not insurance or clinic construction. 💡 TIP Launched Aug 2021; PM Modi praised this as a model for other states. Over 6 crore health checkups conducted by 2023. 6,000+ medical teams deployed. TN invested heavily in telemedicine post-COVID — MTM is flagship example. 💡 MNEMONIC: "MTM = Medicine To Me (door-step health) — 6 crore covered, 38 districts" Healthcare comes TO the people (not people going to hospital) — home-based primary care

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Muthulakshmi Reddy Maternity Benefit Scheme Health & Family Welfare Dept. , TN 1987 (Revised) All Parties ▼ Key Features Financial assistance to pregnant women for nutrition, antenatal care and postnatal care Amount: ₹18,000 for 1st child, ₹12,000 for 2nd child (in instalments) Covers BPL women and those without ESI/ESIC coverage Named after Dr. Muthulakshmi Reddy — India's first woman legislator and social reformer from TN ⚠ TRAP PMMVY (Pradhan Mantri Matru Vandana Yojana) is the Central scheme (₹5,000 for 1st child only).

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Muthulakshmi scheme gives MORE money (₹18,000) and covers BOTH 1st and 2nd child. TN state scheme is MORE GENEROUS than central scheme here. Muthulakshmi Reddy (1886–1968) was a pioneering social reformer who fought against devadasi system, founded Adyar Cancer Institute, and was the first woman doctor to join government service in India — a key TNPSC personality question. 💡 MNEMONIC: "Muthulakshmi scheme: ₹18K (1st child) + ₹12K (2nd child) — TN beats Centre" TN's benefit (₹18K/₹12K) exceeds Centre's PMMVY (₹5K) — TN supplements central schem

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-61 Chief Minister's Comprehensive Health Insurance Scheme (CMCHIS) Health & Family Welfare Dept. , TN / SHAM 2009 AIADMK ▼ Key Features ₹5 lakh health insurance per family per year for secondary & tertiary hospitalisation Covers all families with annual income below ₹72,000 (approx. 5 crore families) 1,300+ surgeries and procedures covered in 600+ private hospitals + all government hospitals Implemented by State Health Assurance Mission (SHAM); cashless treatment ⚠ TRAP Tamil Nadu initially DID NOT JOIN Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY as TN had its ow

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Later, convergence was announced. CMCHIS is the TN analogue of PM-JAY — same ₹5 lakh cover but TN-specific. Both now coexist in TN. 💡 TIP CMCHIS was India's first state-level comprehensive health insurance scheme when launched in 2009 by AIADMK (renamed Kalaignar Insurance Scheme under DMK then back to CMCHIS). 13 crore cardholders in TN as of 2023. 💡 MNEMONIC: "CMCHIS = TN's PM-JAY: ₹5 lakh, SHAM implements, income <₹72,000/year" Same ₹5 lakh as PM-JAY but state-specific, implemented by SHAM, income criterion differs

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-62 Illam Thedi Kalvi (Education at Doorstep) School Education Dept. , TN 2021 DMK ▼ Key Features Remedial education programme — volunteers teach children at their homes in the evenings Targets Classes 3–8 in government schools; focuses on foundational literacy and numeracy 2 lakh+ volunteers trained; 36 lakh students enrolled Post-COVID learning recovery initiative — addresses learning losses from school closures ⚠ TRAP Illam Thedi Kalvi (education coming home) is a REMEDIAL programme, not a school replacement.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Regular school continues; ITK provides EVENING supplementary tutoring. Volunteers are local community members, not school teachers. 💡 TIP "Illam Thedi Kalvi" means "Education seeking homes" — inverse of school-going; education comes to the child. This community-based model was praised by UNESCO as an innovative COVID-learning recovery approach. 💡 MNEMONIC: "Illam=Home + Thedi=Seeking + Kalvi=Education — evening home tutoring" Scheme name translates directly: education comes to the child's home, not vice versa

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-63 Engal Mann Engal Makkal (Our Land Our People) Revenue & Disaster Management Dept. , TN 2021 DMK ▼ Key Features Scheme for distribution of land patta (title deeds) to landless poor and SC/ST communities Survey and settlement of disputes over government poramboke lands given to eligible poor Online patta distribution to beneficiaries; digital land records Links with Kalaignar Kanavu Illam for housing on distributed land ⚠ TRAP Engal Mann Engal Makkal is about LAND RIGHTS (patta distribution) — not housing construction.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Housing is done under Kalaignar Kanavu Illam. Land + Housing are two separate but linked TN programmes. 💡 TIP As of 2023, over 2 lakh pattas distributed under EMEM. TN's land reform history: 1974 Land Ceiling Act, 1980 urban land ceiling — EMEM continues this progressive tradition of land redistribution for the poor. 💡 MNEMONIC: "EMEM = Land patta for poor — Our Land (Engal Mann) for Our People (Engal Makkal)" Name translates to "Our Land, Our People" — land rights redistribution scheme for the poor

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-64 Amma Two Wheeler Scheme Social Welfare Dept. , TN 2016 AIADMK ▼ Key Features Subsidised two-wheelers for working women in government and private sector 50% subsidy (up to ₹25,000) on purchase of two-wheelers Women in BPL families and women in unorganised sector prioritised Bank loan + government subsidy model; delivered at district level ⚠ TRAP Amma Two Wheeler = AIADMK scheme (2016) for working women's mobility. Different from Vidiyal Payanam (free bus travel for women under DMK, 2021).

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Two different governments, two different mobility support approaches for women. 💡 TIP TN has the longest tradition of women's welfare schemes with "Amma" branding — Amma Unavagam, Amma Two Wheeler, Amma Pharmacy, Amma Mini Clinic — all AIADMK under J. Jayalalithaa era (2011–2016). 💡 MNEMONIC: "Amma 2W = AIADMK + 50% subsidy + working women's mobility" Part of Jayalalithaa's "Amma" welfare brand; two-wheeler subsidised for working women

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-65 Green Tamil Nadu Mission Environment & Climate Change Dept. , TN 2021 DMK ▼ Key Features Target: Plant 50 crore trees in 5 years; increase forest/tree cover from 23. 7% to 33% Roadside, coastal, urban, and watershed plantations; involves school children and communities Links with TN Climate Change Mission and net-zero goals Puliyamaram, Arasu, Neem trees — native species prioritised over exotic varieties ⚠ TRAP India's national forest cover target is 33% of land area (as per NPE 1988).

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: TN's current cover is ~23. 7% — below national target. Green TN Mission aims to bridge this gap. Note: "forest cover" and "tree cover" are measured differently by FSI. 💡 TIP TN State Action Plan on Climate Change (TN-SAPCC) is the policy framework; Green TN Mission is a flagship programme under it. Vaan Vannam (natural farming) and TN Wetland Conservation are linked programmes for ecological restoration. 💡 MNEMONIC: "Green TN = 50 crore trees, 5 years, 33% forest cover target" Key numbers: 50 crore trees, 5 years, 33% target (national standar

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-66 Tamil Nadu Solar Energy Policy 2023 MNRE / TEDA (TN Energy Dev Agency) 2023 DMK ▼ Key Features Target: 20 GW solar power by 2030 under TN Solar Policy Rooftop solar, ground-mounted, agri-solar (agrivoltaics) promoted Tamil Nadu has highest solar energy potential among non-desert states TEDA (Tamil Nadu Energy Development Agency) is nodal body; TANGEDCO distribution ⚠ TRAP TN Solar Policy target for 2030 is 20 GW (policy 2023). India's national solar target is 500 GW renewable by 2030.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Tamil Nadu was the FIRST state in India to commission offshore wind energy — Rameshwaram pilot (2011). 💡 TIP Tamil Nadu has the most solar parks (Tirunelveli, Ramanathapuram districts) and is 5th largest solar-producing state. Kamuthi Solar Park (648 MW, built in 8 months in 2016) was world's largest single-location solar plant when built. 💡 MNEMONIC: "TN Solar = TEDA + Kamuthi fame + 20 GW by 2030" TEDA agency + Kamuthi world record + 2030 target = key TN solar facts

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-67 CM Solar Powered Green House Scheme TANGEDCO / TEDA 2015 AIADMK ▼ Key Features Free solar panels (250W) to 1 lakh BPL households — eliminating electricity bills Provides clean energy and reduces power subsidy burden on TANGEDCO Targets coastal, tribal, and remote areas where grid electricity is unreliable Solar home lighting system with battery backup — lights and fan operation ⚠ TRAP CM Solar Green House Scheme = Free rooftop solar for BPL homes (state scheme).

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Different from PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana (2024 central scheme) which gives subsidised rooftop solar to all households. TN state scheme predates the central scheme. 💡 TIP TN's solar-for-poor approach was ahead of its time in 2015. The scheme specifically targeted islands (Rameshwaram, Pamban) and coastal fishing communities where grid power was intermittent. 💡 MNEMONIC: "CM Solar Green House = BPL families + free 250W solar + coastal/tribal focus" Free solar for poorest households; priority to off-grid coastal and tribal areas

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-68 Tamil Nadu Crop Insurance Scheme Agriculture Dept. , TN Ongoing (revised 2022) All Parties ▼ Key Features State-specific crop insurance supplementing Central PMFBY; covers crops not in PMFBY Comprehensive coverage for paddy, groundnut, sugarcane, banana — major TN crops Government pays full premium for small and marginal farmers in TN Cauvery Delta Zone farmers get special protection due to irrigation dependency ⚠ TRAP TN has its OWN crop insurance in addition to PMFBY.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: States can opt out of PMFBY and run their own scheme — Andhra Pradesh and Telangana did this. TN participates in PMFBY but supplements with state-specific coverage for crops PMFBY doesn't cover. 💡 TIP Tamil Nadu has unique crops like banana (Tiruvarur), turmeric (Erode), and chilli (Ramanathapuram) that require state-specific insurance products. Erode is the "Turmeric City" — India's largest turmeric market. 💡 MNEMONIC: "TN Crop Insurance = PMFBY supplement + free premium for small farmers + TN-specific crops" State scheme ADDS TO central cov

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-69 Tamil Nadu Farmers Relief Fund Agriculture Dept. , TN Ongoing All Parties ▼ Key Features Emergency relief fund for crop damage due to floods, drought, cyclone, pest attack TN government provides ex-gratia support over and above central SDRF/NDRF funds Cyclone Gaja (2018), Cyclone Nivar (2020), Cyclone Tauktae (2021) — TN activated this fund Instant relief to farmers — faster than insurance claim settlement process ⚠ TRAP Farmers Relief Fund ≠ crop insurance.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Relief fund = emergency ex-gratia payment after disaster (immediate); crop insurance = pre-agreed compensation for yield loss (takes time to process). Both operate in parallel in TN. 💡 TIP TN is one of the most cyclone-prone states — Bay of Bengal cyclone season (Oct–Dec) frequently damages rabi crops. Cauvery delta (Thanjavur, Tiruvarur, Nagapattinam) and coastal districts are most vulnerable. 💡 MNEMONIC: "Farmers Relief Fund = TN's emergency safety net BEYOND PMFBY" Quick ex-gratia after disasters — faster than insurance but not as comprehe

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-70 Kalaignar Kanavu Illam (Dream Home Scheme) Housing & Urban Dev Dept. , TN 2022 DMK ▼ Key Features Free houses for homeless BPL families; 3 lakh houses targeted by 2026 Houses with 300–400 sq ft built-up area; basic amenities (toilet, water, electricity) Convergence with PMAY-G for rural areas; state funds for urban homeless Beneficiary list verified through Ration Card and SECC data ⚠ TRAP Kalaignar Kanavu Illam = housing scheme (DMK 2022). Kalaignar Muthamizh Arignar Centenary Housing = another TN housing scheme announced 2023 as centenar

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Two DIFFERENT housing schemes both with "Kalaignar" in the name — read carefully! 💡 TIP "Kanavu Illam" means "Dream Home" — aspirational branding. TN housing schemes under DMK government aim to address the urban slum issue — Chennai alone has 2,400+ slums housing 30 lakh+ residents. 💡 MNEMONIC: "Kalaignar Kanavu = Kalaignar's Dream → free homes for BPL homeless families" Kanavu=dream, Illam=home — scheme fulfils the dream of homeownership for the homeless poor

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-71 Muthamizh Arignar Dr. Kalaignar Centenary Housing Housing & Urban Dev Dept. , TN 2023 DMK ▼ Key Features 1 lakh free houses for the poorest — launched to mark M. Karunanidhi's centenary year (born June 3, 1924) Targets families in coastal areas, hills and other vulnerable geographies Houses include title in woman's name; linked with government land patta distribution ₹3,500 crore allocated for centenary housing package ⚠ TRAP This is DIFFERENT from Kalaignar Kanavu Illam (different target, different announcement).

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: The centenary housing scheme has a specific historical trigger — Karunanidhi's 100th birth year. Both are DMK housing schemes running in parallel. Karunanidhi (1924–2018): 5 times CM of Tamil Nadu, Dravidian movement leader, Tamil scholar (Muthamizh Arignar = scholar of Three Tamils). Centenary celebrations 2023–24 saw multiple welfare scheme launches as tribute. 💡 MNEMONIC: "Centenary Housing = Kalaignar's 100th birthday gift to the poor — 1 lakh homes" Centenary=100 years, 1 lakh homes = centenary number linkage for memory

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-72 TNSHED — TN Self Help Groups (SHG) Programme Rural Dev & Panchayati Raj Dept. , TN / TNCDW 1989 (TNCDW) All Parties ▼ Key Features Tamil Nadu has 14+ lakh SHGs with 2 crore+ women members — one of highest in India Mahalir Thittam (TNCDW) promotes SHG credit-linkage with banks and NABARD Kudumbashree model (Kerala) inspired; SHGs link with NRLM/SRLM for NABARD refinance SHG members access Mudra loans, KCC, subsidised credit under NRLM ⚠ TRAP TNCDW = Tamil Nadu Corporation for Development of Women.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Mahalir Thittam is TN's SHG programme under TNCDW. This is different from national NRLM (Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana). TN's SHG programme predates NRLM by decades. 💡 TIP Tamil Nadu pioneered SHG banking linkage in India. 1983) is one of India's oldest women's development corporations. "Mahalir Thittam" SHG model was replicated across India — TN's SHG legacy is celebrated as a model. 💡 MNEMONIC: "TN SHGs: 14 lakh groups, 2 crore women — India's SHG model state" TN's scale (14 lakh SHGs) and pioneer status in SHG banking linkage

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-73 Tamil Nadu Skill Development Mission (TNSDM) Labour Welfare & Skill Development Dept. , TN 2014 AIADMK ▼ Key Features State-level skilling implementing PMKVY and DDU-GKY in TN; additional state-funded skilling TNSDC (TN Skill Development Corporation) — nodal agency; 1,500+ training partners Sector focus: auto, electronics, Naan Mudhalvan aligned; TNSDC disburses funds for industry-relevant training ⚠ TRAP TNSDM ≠ Naan Mudhalvan. TNSDM is the institutional framework (2014, AIADMK) implementing PMKVY + DDU-GKY + state funds.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Naan Mudhalvan is a DMK 2022 scheme for youth employability with broader scope including internships and career guidance. 💡 TIP Tamil Nadu ranks among top 3 states in PMKVY implementation. TNSDC (Tamil Nadu Skill Development Corporation) was established 2013, predating MSDE. Auto, electronics, garment, BFSI sectors are priority sectors. 💡 MNEMONIC: "TNSDM = TNSDC implements PMKVY + DDU-GKY + State skilling" State body (TNSDC) + Central schemes (PMKVY/DDU-GKY) + State funding = TNSDM framework

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-74 Chief Minister's Public Relief Fund (CMPRF) Revenue & Disaster Management Dept. , TN 1952 All Parties ▼ Why Launched Natural disasters, medical emergencies and individual hardships required a dedicated fund for quick relief disbursement directly from the CM's office without bureaucratic delays. Key Features Donations from public, corporates, NRIs accepted; 100% tax-exempt under Section 80G Provides relief for flood, cyclone, drought victims; medical expenses for serious illness Individual grants for accident victims, fire casualties, bridg

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Online application and transparent grievance redressal via TN ePay portal Target Beneficiaries Disaster victims, accident/fire survivors, medically distressed families across Tamil Nadu ⚠ TRAP CMPRF ≠ SDRF (State Disaster Response Fund). SDRF is a formal government fund under NDMA guidelines for declared disasters. CMPRF is voluntary donation-based; SDRF is government-budgeted. Both coexist in TN — don't confuse. 💡 TIP During Cyclone Vardah (2016) and Chennai floods (2015), CMPRF received hundreds of crores in public donations.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Aadhaar-linked direct bank transfers made CMPRF disbursement faster and fraud-resistant. 💡 MNEMONIC: "CMPRF = People's fund, 80G exempt, quick relief — CM's emergency cheque" Voluntary fund + tax benefit + quick disbursal = key CMPRF characteristics

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-75 Namma Ooru Namma Palli (Our Village Our School) School Education Dept. , TN 2022 DMK ▼ Why Launched Government schools suffered from infrastructure deficits, low enrolment and community disconnect. A community-driven school improvement model was needed to revive public education quality. Key Features Community mobilisation to improve school infrastructure through CSR, NRI donations and panchayat funds School Management Committees (SMCs) empowered with actual decision-making powers Convergence with Illam Thedi Kalvi and School of Excellence

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Don't confuse with School of Excellence (government schools upgraded as model schools with state budget directly). 💡 TIP TN has 37,000+ government schools (primary to higher secondary). Namma Ooru Namma Palli raised ₹1,000 crore+ through community contributions in its first year — demonstrating strong public trust in the scheme. 💡 MNEMONIC: "Namma Ooru = Community funds + empowered SMCs = Our school, our responsibility" Community ownership model for 37,000 government schools across Tamil Nadu

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-76 Anaithu Grama Anna Marumalarchi Thittam Rural Dev & Panchayati Raj Dept. , TN 2022 DMK ▼ Why Launched Rural villages lacked integrated infrastructure — roads, drains, water supply, street lights, burial grounds — causing quality-of-life deficits in TN's 12,524 village panchayats. Key Features Comprehensive village renewal: roads, culverts, drains, street lighting, water, sanitation Each gram panchayat gets minimum ₹10 lakh/year from TN state for village-level works Convergence with MGNREGS, SBM-G, JJM for multi-source funding at panchayat

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: "Anaithu" = all, "Grama" = village. Do not confuse with Anaithu Grama Oorani scheme (a specific scheme for ponds). 💡 TIP Tamil Nadu has 12,524 village panchayats, 385 panchayat unions, 38 district panchayats (restructured). This scheme reaches all 12,524 village panchayats — largest rural development programme in TN after MGNREGS. 💡 MNEMONIC: "Anaithu Grama = ALL villages + Anna (Annadurai) tribute + comprehensive infra" Named after CN Annadurai; covers all 12,524 gram panchayats; infrastructure focus

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-77 Tamil Nadu e-Governance Initiatives (TNeGA) Information Technology Dept. , TN / TNeGA 2006 (TNeGA) All Parties ▼ Why Launched Citizen services were slow, corrupt-prone and required in-person visits. Digital delivery of services was essential for transparent, fast and accessible government. Key Features TNeGA (Tamil Nadu e-Governance Agency): nodal body for e-governance implementation Key portals: TN e-Seva (citizen services), Kizhangu (land records), CM Dashboard Smart Governance: real-time dashboards for scheme monitoring by CM's office P

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: ELCOT (Electronics Corporation of TN) provides hardware/infrastructure; TNeGA provides software/portal solutions. Both are different organisations under the IT Dept. 💡 TIP Tamil Nadu topped the National e-Governance Award multiple years. TNPASS (population registry with biometrics) integrates with ration cards, health cards and scheme databases — unique to TN. Smart Card Ration Cards were pioneered by TN. 💡 MNEMONIC: "TNeGA = e-Governance hub; ELCOT = Hardware/infra — same IT Dept, different roles" TNeGA handles digital services; ELCOT handle

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-78 ELCOT — Electronics Corporation of Tamil Nadu Information Technology Dept. , TN 1977 All Parties ▼ Key Features Established 1977 as IT PSU of TN government; procures, maintains government IT hardware Manages NOFN (National Optical Fibre Network) for broadband in TN's rural areas Runs ITES parks, IT SEZs and manages data centres for TN government ELCOT Cloud: Government cloud infrastructure for hosting state applications Budget / Outlay Annual procurement of ₹500–800 crore in IT hardware for government departments ⚠ TRAP ELCOT ≠ TIDCO (TN I

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: ELCOT = IT/electronics PSU. TIDCO = industrial investment promotion PSU. Both are TN government companies under different departments. 💡 TIP ELCOT manages connectivity for 12,500+ CSCs (Common Service Centres) in TN through BharatNet. ELCOT's IT parks house major companies in Coimbatore, Madurai, Tirunelveli — decentralising IT beyond Chennai. 💡 MNEMONIC: "ELCOT = Electronics Corp = IT infra PSU (1977) — hardware backbone of TN govt" Oldest IT PSU in TN; handles procurement, cloud, connectivity, IT parks

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-79 Amma Unavagam (Amma Canteens) Municipal Administration & Water Supply Dept. , TN FEB 2013 AIADMK ▼ Why Launched Urban poor and daily wage workers could not afford hygienic meals in city areas. Subsidised nutritious food was needed to prevent hunger and malnutrition among the urban working class. Key Features Subsidised meals: Idli at ₹1, rice meals (Sadam) at ₹5 — heavily subsidised by TN Govt Initially 407 canteens in Chennai; expanded to all corporations and municipalities Run by Self-Help Groups (women entrepreneurs) — dual welfare: foo

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: It was LAUNCHED by AIADMK (J. Jayalalithaa, 2013) but is now continued by DMK. Both parties claim credit — a common TNPSC political neutrality trap. 💡 TIP Amma Unavagam inspired similar schemes across India: Indira Canteens (Karnataka), Anna Canteens (AP), etc. TN model is considered the pioneer. Rs 1 Idli is one of India's most recognized welfare scheme symbols. 💡 MNEMONIC: "Amma Canteen = ₹1 Idli + ₹5 Sadam + SHG women operators (2013, AIADMK)" Price points (₹1/₹5) + SHG model + 2013 launch = three key Amma Canteen facts

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-80 Amma Mini Clinic (Urban Health Outposts) Health & Family Welfare Dept. , TN 2011 AIADMK ▼ Key Features Small urban health centres in corporation wards — first-contact healthcare for urban poor OPD services: consultation, basic diagnostics, free medicines dispensed Connected to District Government Hospitals via referral system Aligned with Makkalai Thedi Maruthuvam (doorstep health services) ⚠ TRAP Amma Mini Clinic = urban health outposts (ward-level).

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Makkalai Thedi Maruthuvam = mobile health teams going to homes (doorstep). Both are health access schemes but different delivery models. Do not confuse. 💡 TIP Urban health in India faces a paradox — cities have medical colleges but slum residents cannot access them. Amma Mini Clinics bridge this gap by placing first-contact healthcare within walking distance in urban slums and corporation wards. 💡 MNEMONIC: "Amma Mini Clinic = Ward-level urban OPD → referral to GH → urban health ladder" Ward clinic → referral → district hospital = three-tier

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-81 Amma Pharmacy (Generic Medicine Outlets) Health & Family Welfare Dept. / TNMSC 2015 AIADMK ▼ Key Features Generic medicines sold at 50–90% below MRP through TNMSC (TN Medical Services Corp) Outlets at government hospitals, district headquarters and high-footfall areas Tamil Nadu Medical Services Corporation (TNMSC) procures centrally — quality assured Aligned with National Essential Medicines List; over 900 generic drugs available ⚠ TRAP Amma Pharmacy (TN) ≠ Pradhan Mantri Bhartiya Janaushadhi Pariyojana (PMBJP) — the central government's

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: PMBJP has 10,000+ Janaushadhi Kendras pan-India. Amma Pharmacy is TN-specific through TNMSC. Both serve same purpose — different implementing bodies. 💡 TIP TNMSC (est. 1994) is one of India's best-managed drug procurement agencies — ranked top by WHO. TN's central drug procurement model (bulk buying) reduces costs by 50–80% and is replicated by Rajasthan, Chhattisgarh. A key TNPSC health governance fact. 💡 MNEMONIC: "Amma Pharmacy = TNMSC bulk buy + 50–90% cheaper + 900+ generics" Central procurement (TNMSC) + deep discount + wide product ran

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-82 TN Police Modernisation Programme Home Dept. , TN / TN Police 2012 onwards All Parties ▼ Key Features Innuyir Kaaval (life protection) integrated command and control: 1100 emergency number upgrade CCTNS (Crime and Criminal Tracking Network & System) — national project implemented in TN ITMS (Intelligent Traffic Management Systems) in Chennai, Coimbatore Body cameras, drones for crowd control; upgraded armoury and forensic labs ⚠ TRAP CCTNS = Central Government scheme (MHA) — implemented at state level.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: It is NOT a state scheme but is being executed through TN Police. TN has 100% CCTNS implementation — top performer nationally. 💡 TIP Tamil Nadu Police has 1,12,000+ personnel (2024). Dial 112 = emergency (police+ambulance+fire merged). TN Police is known for transparent online FIR registration and SMART policing indices. 💡 MNEMONIC: "TN Police = Dial 112, CCTNS, Innuyir Kaaval = Modern + Digital + Quick response" Three pillars: unified emergency (112), digital records (CCTNS), coordinated response (Innuyir Kaaval)

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-83 Perasiriyar Anbazhagan Educational Scholarship School Education / Higher Education Dept. , TN 2022 DMK ▼ Why Launched To honour K. Anbazhagan (Perasiriyar — great teacher), veteran DMK ideologue, educator and Tamil scholar, who served Tamil Nadu's education system for decades. Key Features Scholarships for meritorious government school students from economically weaker sections Covers tuition, books, uniform and transport — holistic educational support Targets first-generation college-goers from SC/ST/OBC families Linked with Naan Mudhalva

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Anbazhagan (1924–2015). Not to confuse with Perarignar Anna (Annadurai's title). "Perasiriyar" is Anbazhagan; "Perarignar" is Anna — frequent TNPSC exam trap. Anbazhagan: President of DMK from 1991 till his death; champion of Tamil language and Dravidian educational philosophy. His life work in school education is the thematic basis of this scholarship scheme. 💡 MNEMONIC: "Perasiriyar (Great Teacher) Anbazhagan → scholarship for first-gen college students" Teacher's legacy = education scholarship; first-generation students = breaking education

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-84 School of Excellence Programme (TN) School Education Dept. , TN 2022 DMK ▼ Key Features Select government schools upgraded to "School of Excellence" — one per assembly constituency (234 schools) State-of-the-art labs, smart classrooms, libraries, sports facilities, coding labs Additional qualified teachers, career guidance counsellors stationed at each school Replicate private school standards in government sector to arrest migration to private schools Budget / Outlay ₹7,450 crore over 3 years (2022–25); one school per each of 234 assembly

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Model Schools (centrally) = a different national scheme. Don't confuse numbers: 234 = assembly constituencies in TN; 32 = Lok Sabha seats in TN. "One per constituency" is the unique TN anchor. 💡 TIP Schools of Excellence in TN have NCC and NSS units, language labs (English + Tamil + German/French), and industry-linked vocational labs. The vision: ensure a world-class government school within 5 km of any child in TN. 💡 MNEMONIC: "School of Excellence = 234 schools, 234 constituencies, ₹7,450Cr — one world-class school per MLA constituency" 234

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-85 Vaan Vannam (Natural Colour Sky) / Zero Budget Natural Farming TN Agriculture Dept. , TN 2022 DMK ▼ Why Launched Chemical-intensive farming caused soil degradation, groundwater pollution and high input costs for Tamil Nadu farmers. Natural farming offered a path to reduce costs and restore soil health. Key Features Promote Zero Budget Natural Farming (ZBNF) across TN — inspired by Subhash Palekar's model Jeevamirtham (natural liquid fertiliser from cow products), Beejamirtham (seed treatment) promoted Target: 1 lakh farmers in natural farm

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Andhra Pradesh's ZBNF model (under Rythu Sadhikara Samstha) is more comprehensive. TN's model is still scaling up. 💡 TIP ZBNF concept: Subhash Palekar (Padma Shri 2016). The "Zero Budget" means no external chemical input cost — farming with indigenous cow breeds' products (ghanajeevamirtham, jeevamirtham). Sustainable farming = key TNPSC environment + agriculture overlap topic. 💡 MNEMONIC: "Vaan Vannam = Natural colours → Chemical-free farming → Jeevamirtham + Beejamirtham" Natural sky colours = chemical-free farming; Jeeva (life) + Beeeja (s

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-86 Tamil Nadu Wetland Conservation Programme Environment & Climate Change Dept. , TN 2021 DMK ▼ Key Features TN has 1,22,000+ wetlands — second highest in India; major floodwater regulators Restoration of 2,000+ tanks (ooranies) under Anaithu Grama Oorani scheme Pallikaranai Marsh (Chennai) declared wetland conservation zone; restoration ongoing IWMP (Integrated Watershed Management Programme) convergence for wetland restoration ⚠ TRAP Pallikaranai Marsh — Tamil Nadu's only surviving natural wetland within Chennai — is classified as a "Degrad

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: It is NOT a Ramsar site yet (though proposed). Don't confuse with Vedanthangal Bird Sanctuary (a different ecosystem in Kanchipuram district). 💡 TIP TN Ramsar sites (2024): Chitrangudi Bird Sanctuary, Kazhuveli Bird Sanctuary, Gulf of Mannar, Koonthankulam Bird Sanctuary, Pallikaranai Marsh (partial), Point Calimere — 6 Ramsar sites in TN. Frequently asked in TNPSC environment section. 💡 MNEMONIC: "TN Wetlands: 1,22,000+ tanks + 6 Ramsar sites + Pallikaranai restoration = water security ecosystem" Scale (1.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: 22 lakh) + international recognition (6 Ramsar) + urban flagship (Pallikaranai) = TN wetland facts

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-87 Nilavembu Kudineer Scheme (Vector Disease Prevention) Health & Family Welfare Dept. , TN / AYUSH 2017 AIADMK ▼ Why Launched Dengue and chikungunya outbreaks caused mass morbidity in Tamil Nadu. A traditional Siddha herbal formulation (Nilavembu Kudineer) was found effective in managing fever and preventing complications. Key Features Free distribution of Nilavembu Kudineer (Siddha herbal drink) during dengue/chikungunya season Proven effective in Phase 2/3 clinical studies; CCRS recommended Distribution at PHCs, government hospitals, Amma

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Tamil Nadu has a strong Siddha medicine tradition; TNMSC produces and distributes it. The Council for Research in Siddha (CRS, Chennai) endorsed it — not AYUSH HQ in Delhi. 💡 TIP Nilavembu (Andrographis paniculata) is the key herb. TN became the first state to institutionally use Siddha medicine in mass disease prevention. This was cited as a success case in COVID-19 era for AYUSH medicine promotion. 💡 MNEMONIC: "Nilavembu = Siddha herb + Free dengue prevention + TN's traditional medicine pride" Siddha (not Ayurveda) + dengue/chikungunya prev

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-88 Tamil Nadu EV Policy 2023 (100% EV Transition) Industries Dept. / TANGEDCO / Transport Dept. , TN 2023 DMK ▼ Key Features Target: 100% government fleet electrification by 2030; 1 crore EVs on TN roads by 2030 EV Manufacturing Hub: attract ₹50,000 crore EV investment; Ola Electric, TVS, Ather HQ in TN Charging infrastructure: 2,000+ fast-charging stations in highways by 2025 TANGEDCO EV tariff: ₹4/unit at charging stations — lowest in South India Budget / Outlay ₹50,000 crore investment target; ₹500 crore for charging infrastructure by TN g

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: EV Policy aligns with FAME II and national EV30@30 target. TN already manufactures 35% of India's automobiles. 💡 TIP Chennai is India's "Detroit" — automotive hub. With EV Policy 2023, TN aims to transition from ICE (Internal Combustion Engine) to EV manufacturing. Ola Electric IPO (2024) — headquartered in Bengaluru but manufacturing in Krishnagiri, TN (Futurefactory). 💡 MNEMONIC: "TN EV = Auto capital + EV policy + 1 crore EVs by 2030 = TN reinventing its auto sector" From India's ICE auto capital to EV manufacturing hub — policy-driven tra

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-89 Kalaignar Arasu Palli Sappadu (CM Breakfast Scheme) School Education Dept. , TN SEP 2022 DMK ▼ Why Launched Many government school children came to school without eating breakfast, affecting their concentration and learning outcomes. Hunger was identified as a key barrier to school attendance and performance. Key Features Free nutritious breakfast (upma, pongal, semiya, idli) for Class 1–5 government school students Covers 14. 5 lakh students in 30,000+ government primary schools SHG women prepare and serve meals — employment + welfare dua

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Tamil Nadu is the ONLY state providing BOTH breakfast AND lunch free in government schools. This dual scheme is a key TNPSC fact. 💡 TIP TN was the FIRST state in India to implement CM Breakfast Scheme (Sep 15, 2022 — launched on CM M. Stalin's birthday). School dropout rate fell by 15% in pilot schools after breakfast scheme. Attendance improved significantly. 💡 MNEMONIC: "TN Schools = Breakfast (CM scheme) + Lunch (PM POSHAN) = India's only dual free meal state" Unique double: morning breakfast + afternoon lunch = two free meals for TN gover

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-90 Urban Farming Initiative (Namma Chennai Urban Farming) Municipal Administration / Greater Chennai Corporation 2021 DMK ▼ Key Features Rooftop and balcony farming promoted in Chennai; free training, seeds and soil kits distributed 1,000 households trained in first phase; rooftop farms in corporation properties demonstrated Reduces urban heat island effect; community gardens in parks and vacant corporation land Linked with TN's Agri-food system framework and green city goals ⚠ TRAP Urban farming is part of GCC (Greater Chennai Corporation) G

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: It is a policy/programme within broader TN environmental and food security goals. Do not treat it as a standalone funded welfare scheme. 💡 TIP Chennai is India's 6th largest city (population ~10 million metro). Urban heat islands, food miles and garden space deficit are urban challenges addressed by rooftop farming. Singapore, Tokyo models inspire Chennai's urban agriculture push. 💡 MNEMONIC: "Urban Farming = GCC initiative + rooftop + heat reduction + food security" City-level programme: cool roofs + fresh vegetables + community gardens = ur

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-91 Cauvery Delta Protected Agriculture Zone Policy Agriculture / Revenue Dept. , TN 2020 AIADMK ▼ Why Launched Cauvery delta districts (Thanjavur, Tiruvarur, Nagapattinam, Ariyalur, Cuddalore) — India's "rice granary" — faced threats from hydrocarbon exploration (oil/gas) which would damage agricultural land and groundwater. Key Features 5 delta districts declared "Protected Agriculture Zone" — no hydrocarbon exploration permitted Over 8 lakh hectares of agricultural land protected; 40+ lakh farmers benefited Hydrocarbon project (ONGC concess

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: It legally prevents Centre from granting hydrocarbon exploration licences in these 5 districts. Central government challenged this; legal battle ongoing. TN's first use of state authority to override central energy policy is constitutionally significant. 💡 TIP Cauvery Delta = India's "Paddy Bowl" (also called "Granary of South India"). Thanjavur alone produces 30+ lakh MT of rice annually. The protected zone policy is studied as an environmental federalism case study.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Key TNPSC Polity + Agriculture intersection topic. 💡 MNEMONIC: "Cauvery Delta = 5 districts + No oil exploration + India's first agri-protected zone" 5 delta districts locked from hydrocarbon drilling — first such state protection of agricultural land

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-92 TN Integrated Vector Control Programme Directorate of Public Health & Preventive Medicine, TN 2010 onwards All Parties ▼ Key Features Targeted control of dengue, malaria, filariasis, chikungunya, Japanese encephalitis through vector elimination Larval source reduction, IRS (Indoor Residual Spraying), insecticide-treated bed nets for malaria zones Pulse polio-style door-to-door fever surveillance with community health workers Integrated with NVBDCP (National Vector Borne Disease Control Programme) + NHM ⚠ TRAP NVBDCP = National Vector Borne

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: TN's state-level programme implements NVBDCP + adds state-specific interventions. Dengue is Class A notifiable disease — mandatory reporting. Malaria has been nearly eliminated in most TN districts. 💡 TIP Tamil Nadu achieved "Malaria Elimination" status in several districts. Filariasis elimination programme covers 35 districts. Mass Drug Administration (MDA) for filariasis — albendazole + DEC — is TN's annual health campaign, often confused with polio vaccination.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: 💡 MNEMONIC: "Vector Control = Dengue + Malaria + Filaria + Chikungunya = 4-disease comprehensive mosquito battle" Four vector-borne diseases controlled under one integrated programme = IVCP scope

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-93 Namadhu MGR Scheme (Health Insurance Legacy) Health & Family Welfare Dept. , TN 2012 AIADMK ▼ Key Features Universal health insurance scheme for all TN residents — not limited to BPL Precursor/parallel to CMCHIS; named in memory of M. Ramachandran (MGR), founder of AIADMK Cover for specified illnesses and surgical procedures up to ₹1 lakh per family Later merged/upgraded into Chief Minister's Comprehensive Health Insurance Scheme (CMCHIS) ⚠ TRAP MGR (Maruthur Gopalan Ramachandran) = CM 1977–1987; launched free noon meal scheme (MDM pioneer

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Namadhu MGR scheme = tribute to his welfare legacy. Don't confuse MGR the CM with MGR the actor — same person but exam questions separate the roles. 💡 TIP MGR's welfare legacy: Free noon meal scheme (1982), free bus pass for school children, prohibition enforcement, film industry worker welfare — all pioneered concepts later replicated nationally. TNPSC regularly asks about MGR's policy contributions. 💡 MNEMONIC: "MGR legacy: Noon meal (1982) + Namadhu MGR health (2012) = TN welfare lineage" MGR's 1982 noon meal scheme + 2012 health insurance

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-94 TNAU Farmer Support Schemes (Tamil Nadu Agricultural University) Agriculture Dept. / TNAU, Coimbatore 1971 (TNAU est. ) All Parties ▼ Key Features TNAU (est. 1971, Coimbatore) — India's top agricultural university; develops TN-specific varieties ADT 45, ADT 53 (high-yield paddy); CO 51 (sugarcane); PKM 1, PKM 2 (moringa) varieties famous Farm Science Centres (KVKs) in all 38 districts for extension services Agri Tech Labs (ATMA), Farmer Producer Organisations (FPOs) support through TNAU Budget / Outlay TNAU annual budget ₹800+ crore; 10 co

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: TNAU is a state agricultural university. IARI (Indian Agricultural Research Institute, New Delhi) is the central premier agri institute. TNAU varieties (ADT, CO, PKM series) are specifically developed for TN's agro-climatic conditions. 💡 TIP TNAU's CO 1 (sugarcane variety) and ADT 45 (paddy) revolutionised TN agriculture in 1970s–80s. PKM 1 moringa (drumstick) variety by TNAU is exported globally — "Moringa capital of the world" is Krishnagiri, TN.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Key TNPSC agriculture + science intersect topic. 💡 MNEMONIC: "TNAU Coimbatore = ADT paddy + CO sugarcane + PKM moringa — state agri innovation hub" Three flagship TN crops with TNAU-developed varieties: paddy, sugarcane, moringa

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-95 Chief Minister's Trophy Sports Scheme (TN Sports Development) Sports Dev. Authority of Tamil Nadu (SDAT) 2012 onwards All Parties ▼ Key Features Annual inter-district sports competition across 44 disciplines for school and college youth Cash prizes and government job recruitment linkage for medal winners SDAT (Sports Development Authority of TN) manages infrastructure: stadiums, swimming pools, indoor halls Talent identification programme: promising athletes selected for state-sponsored advanced training ⚠ TRAP SDAT ≠ SAI (Sports Authority

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: SDAT is TN's state sports authority. SAI runs NIS (National Institute of Sports), Patiala and national sports infrastructure. Both coexist and collaborate but are separate bodies. 💡 TIP Tamil Nadu athletes at Paris Olympics 2024: Neeraj Chopra (Haryana) won gold; P. Sreejesh (Kerala) retired as hockey goalkeeper. From TN: focus on wrestling, boxing, chess. Praggnanandhaa (chess), Viswanathan Anand — TN's chess legacy is globally recognised. 💡 MNEMONIC: "CM Trophy = SDAT + 44 disciplines + inter-district + talent to state team pipeline" Distri

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-96 Model Schools Scheme (Central + TN State) School Education Dept. , TN / Ministry of Education (Central) 2009 (Central) UPA/INC then BJP ▼ Key Features Centrally Sponsored Scheme: one Model School per educationally backward block (EBB) TN has 206 EBB blocks; 204 model schools sanctioned for TN under central scheme Schools provide quality secondary education with residential facility for rural students Equivalent to Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya (JNV) model but at block level ⚠ TRAP Model Schools (central CSS) ≠ Schools of Excellence (TN state

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Model Schools target Educationally Backward Blocks; Schools of Excellence target all 234 constituencies. Two different criteria, two different scales. 💡 TIP JNV (Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya) = one per district, residential, Class 6 entry (central scheme). Model Schools = one per EBB block, less selective. Kendriya Vidyalaya (KV) = for central government employees' children. Three central school types — frequently confused in TNPSC. 💡 MNEMONIC: "JNV=district level, Model School=block level, KV=government employee — three tiers of central school

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-97 Repair Café Initiative (Circular Economy TN) Environment / Municipal Administration Dept. , TN 2022 DMK ▼ Key Features Community spaces where citizens can get electronic/home items repaired for free or low cost Reduces e-waste and solid waste by extending product life — circular economy model Inspired by global Repair Café movement; piloted in Chennai and Coimbatore Women SHG members trained as repair technicians — skill + circular economy dual benefit ⚠ TRAP Repair Café is a PILOT/PROGRAMME — not a standalone government scheme with large

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: It is part of TN's waste management and circular economy strategy under the Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) framework for e-waste. Don't overstate its scale. 💡 TIP India generated 16 lakh MT of e-waste in 2022 (3rd globally). Tamil Nadu is a top e-waste generating state. E-waste (Management) Rules 2022 mandate EPR. Repair Café addresses the consumer end of the e-waste challenge by preventing premature disposal. 💡 MNEMONIC: "Repair Café = Fix before Throw = Circular Economy + SHG skills + e-waste reduction" Circular over linear economy:

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-98 Zero Budget Natural Farming — TN Implementation Agriculture Dept. / TNAU 2020 AIADMK/DMK ▼ Key Features No external chemical fertiliser or pesticide; uses indigenous cow products (Jeevamirtham, Bijamirtham) Mulching, Whapasa (soil aeration principle), Acchadana (covering) — four pillars of ZBNF TN trained 50,000+ farmers in ZBNF techniques through KVKs (Krishi Vigyan Kendras) PM Pranam scheme (Central, 2023) incentivises states cutting chemical fertiliser use — TN eligible ⚠ TRAP ZBNF ≠ Organic Farming (though both avoid chemicals).

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: ZBNF specifically uses COW-derived inputs (Jeevamirtham uses 1 cow's urine+dung per acre). Organic farming uses various certified bio-inputs. Also: ZBNF's scientific validity is debated — AP saw yield drops; Andhra-model is larger than TN's. 💡 TIP ZBNF was mentioned in the Economic Survey 2019–20. Subhash Palekar (ZBNF founder) received Padma Shri 2016. AP's ZBNF is world's largest — 6 lakh farmers. TN's model is smaller but significant. Key intersection: agriculture + environment + economy.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: 💡 MNEMONIC: "ZBNF = 4J: Jeevamirtham + Bijamirtham + Jivatu + Jivacha (cow-based inputs) + zero chemical cost" All four inputs cow-derived; zero external purchase = zero budget farming concept

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-99 TN MSME Support Schemes (TIDCO/SIDCO/TIIC) Industries Dept. , TN Various All Parties ▼ Key Features TIDCO (TN Industrial Development Corp): promotes large industries and FDI; manages industrial estates SIDCO (Small Industries Dev Corp): 61 industrial estates with 30,000+ MSME units TIIC (Tamil Nadu Industrial Investment Corp): term loans at concessional rates for MSMEs TN MSME Policy 2021: capital subsidy, power tariff rebate, ESI/EPF support for new units Budget / Outlay TIIC disburses ₹3,000+ crore annually; SIDCO manages ₹2,000 crore in

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: TIDCO = large industries/FDI; SIDCO = small industry estates; TIIC = industrial finance/loans. All three under Industries Department but distinct mandates. Exam often tests which body does what. 💡 TIP Tamil Nadu is India's #1 state in MSME units (37+ lakh registered MSMEs as of 2023). TN receives highest FDI in manufacturing. "Global Investors Meet" (GIM) — TN's biennial investment summit attracts $250 billion+ investment pledges. GIM 2024 was held in Chennai.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: 💡 MNEMONIC: "TIDCO=Large, SIDCO=Small estates, TIIC=Finance — TN's industrial trinity" Three TN industrial bodies: T-S-T (TIDCO-SIDCO-TIIC) = Large-Small-Finance trinity

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: T-100 Kalaignar Magalir Mattrum Malar Tholil Thittam (Women's Flower Business Scheme) Horticulture Dept. / Social Welfare Dept. , TN 2022 DMK ▼ Why Launched Women flower vendors in TN's temple towns had no formal livelihood support, licensing or social protection. The scheme formalises and supports this traditional women-dominated vocation. Key Features Free licence and identity card for women flower vendors in temple towns and markets Working capital support through SHG linkage; insurance for accidental death/disability TN is India's #1 jasmin

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Madurai jasmine ("Madurai Malli") has GI Tag (Geographical Indication). GI tag is under the Intellectual Property India (IPI) framework — often asked in TNPSC alongside the scheme. Don't confuse GI tag with patent or trademark. 💡 TIP TN's flower economy: ₹5,000 crore annually. Jasmine, chrysanthemum, rose, tube rose are TN specialties. GI Tags from TN: Madurai Malli (jasmine), Kancheepuram silk, Thanjavur Doll, Nilgiri Tea — each represents a geographic craft/product identity.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: 💡 MNEMONIC: "Malar Tholil = Flower (Malar) Business (Tholil) = Madurai Malli GI + women vendor welfare" Jasmine (Malar) + livelihood scheme (Tholil) + GI Tag recognition = TN flower economy support

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: 20 HIGH-YIELD QUESTIONS · TNPSC GROUP 1 PATTERN Expand All Answers Collapse All Answers

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: PYQ Q01 PM-KISAN scheme provides financial assistance of _______ per year to eligible farmer families. (TNPSC 2021) ▼ ✅ Answer: ₹6,000 per year (paid in 3 instalments of ₹2,000 each via Direct Benefit Transfer) 📌 Explanation: Launched February 24, 2019 (backdated to December 1, 2018). 100% Central Sector Scheme under Ministry of Agriculture. Covers all landholding farmer families (~14 crore). Excludes institutional landholders, government employees, income tax payers.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: 💎 GEM PM-KISAN is often the starting point of "KISAN ecosystem" questions: PM-KISAN (income) → KCC (credit) → PM Kisan Maan Dhan (pension). All three linked through the same farmer beneficiary database. Aadhaar-seeding is mandatory since 2021.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: PYQ Q02 Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY was launched from which city? What is the coverage amount per family per year? (TNPSC 2019) ▼ ✅ Answer: Launched from Ranchi, Jharkhand on September 23, 2018. Coverage: ₹5 lakh per family per year. 📌 Explanation: World's largest government-funded health insurance scheme. 74 crore families (50 crore beneficiaries) identified via SECC 2011. Cashless at any empanelled hospital across India. Tamil Nadu initially opted out (has CMCHIS) but later integrated.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: 💎 GEM Ayushman Bharat has TWO components: (1) PM-JAY (health insurance ₹5 lakh) + (2) Health & Wellness Centres (upgraded sub-centres). TNPSC asks about both. Also note: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) is a THIRD component added 2021 for digital health IDs (ABHA).

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: IMP Q03 Match the following Central Government Schemes with their respective Ministries: A. Jal Jeevan Mission C. Ministry of Rural Development 2. Ministry of Housing & Urban Affairs 3. Ministry of Agriculture 4. Ministry of Jal Shakti ▼ ✅ Answer: A-3, B-4, C-2, D-1 📌 Explanation: PM-KISAN → Agriculture & Farmers' Welfare. Jal Jeevan Mission → Jal Shakti (Ministry of Drinking Water). PM SVANidhi → Housing & Urban Affairs (for urban street vendors).

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: DDU-GKY → Rural Development (rural youth employment). 💎 GEM Ministry matching is a high-frequency TNPSC trap. Key confusions: PMKVY under MSDE (not HRD/Education). Swachh Bharat Mission — SBM-G under Jal Shakti; SBM-U under Housing & Urban Affairs. NHM under Health, not AYUSH. Memorise these ministry-scheme pairs as a priority list.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: IMP Q04 Assertion (A): Kalaignar Magalir Urimai Thittam is a pioneering women's income support scheme in Tamil Nadu providing ₹1,000 per month directly to women heads of households. Reason (R): Tamil Nadu is the first state in India to recognise women as household heads for direct cash transfer purposes. ▼ ✅ Answer: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A. 📌 Explanation: KGMUT launched September 15, 2022 provides ₹1,000/month to women heads of poor households — first entitlement (not charity) framing for women's unpaid wor

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: TN indeed pioneered this "Woman as Head of Household" concept for DBT targeting. Amount increased to ₹1,000/month from September 2023. 💎 GEM KGMUT has 1. 07 crore beneficiaries — one of India's largest direct cash transfer schemes for women. The scheme is now widely cited in policy circles as a model for recognising unpaid domestic and care work in national accounts. Other states (Telangana's Rythu Bharosa, Rajasthan's Indira Gandhi Smartphone) have followed similar women-centric DBT models.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: PYQ Q05 Tamil Nadu was the first state in India to implement the school mid-day meal scheme. Who launched it and in which year? (TNPSC multiple years) ▼ ✅ Answer: K. Kamaraj (Chief Minister of Madras State) launched it in 1925 for the first time, and expanded it systematically in 1956. Ramachandran) launched the free Noon Meal Scheme (state-wide, free of cost) in 1982. 📌 Explanation: The national Mid-Day Meal Scheme (now PM POSHAN) was inspired by Tamil Nadu's initiative.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: The Supreme Court (PUCL case, 2001) ordered all states to implement MDM. Tamil Nadu is credited with pioneering the concept. 💎 GEM TNPSC regularly asks: "Who launched Noon Meal Scheme? " — technically Kamaraj pioneered it (1925/1956) but MGR made it universal and free (1982). If question asks for "free Noon Meal Scheme" → MGR 1982. If "first school lunch programme" → Kamaraj. Read question carefully — this distinction appears in almost every TNPSC cycle.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: IMP Q06 Find the incorrect statement about Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM): A. JJM was launched on Independence Day 2019 by PM Modi from the Red Fort B. The target norm under JJM is 55 litres per capita per day (lpcd) C. JJM replaced the National Urban Drinking Water Programme (NUDWP) D. JJM is funded 90:10 for Himalayan and North-Eastern states ▼ ✅ Answer: Statement C is INCORRECT. JJM replaced the National Rural Drinking Water Programme (NRDWP), not NUDWP.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: A, B, D are correct. 📌 Explanation: JJM (Har Ghar Jal) targets rural households only. NRDWP was the predecessor. JJM Urban (under MoHUA) is a separate scheme for cities. The 55 lpcd norm, 90:10 funding for NE/Himalayan states, and Independence Day 2019 launch are all correct facts. 💎 GEM JJM is the world's largest drinking water scheme by coverage (19 crore households). By 2024, India achieved 14+ crore Functional Household Tap Connections (FHTCs).

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: JJM dashboard shows real-time village-wise progress — cited as a model for public scheme monitoring.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: PYQ Q07 Match the following Tamil Nadu schemes with their launch years: A. Kalaignar Magalir Urimai Thittam B. CM Breakfast Scheme C. Naan Mudhalvan D. Pudhumai Penn 1. 2022 (Sep 15) 3. 2022 (Sep 15) 4. 2023 ▼ ✅ Answer: A-3 (Sep 15, 2022), B-2 (Sep 15, 2022), C-1 (2021), D-4 (2023) 📌 Explanation: Interestingly, both Kalaignar Magalir Urimai Thittam AND CM Breakfast Scheme were launched on the SAME day — September 15, 2022 (CM M. Stalin's birthday).

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Naan Mudhalvan launched 2021. Pudhumai Penn launched 2023 providing ₹1,000/month to girls in Class 6–12 continuing education. 💎 GEM September 15 is CM M. Stalin's birthday — DMK government consistently launches flagship schemes on this date. For TNPSC, remember "CM's birthday schemes": Urimai Thittam + Breakfast Scheme (Sep 15, 2022), and School of Excellence (announced on same occasion). Birthday-linked launches are a pattern.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: IMP Q08 Which of the following is NOT a component of PM Awas Yojana - Urban (PMAY-U)? In-Situ Slum Rehabilitation (ISSR) B. Credit Linked Subsidy Scheme (CLSS) C. Pradhan Mantri Gramin Awas (PMGA) D. Beneficiary-Led Construction (BLC) ▼ ✅ Answer: C — Pradhan Mantri Gramin Awas (PMGA) is NOT a component of PMAY-U. The 4 components of PMAY-U are: ISSR, CLSS, AHP (Affordable Housing in Partnership), BLC. 📌 Explanation: PMAY-Urban has 4 verticals (I-CAB mnemonic).

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: "Gramin" (rural) component belongs to PMAY-G (Gramin), not PMAY-U. This is a classic confusion trap in TNPSC. 💎 GEM PMAY-U 2. 0 announced in Union Budget 2024-25 for 1 crore additional urban families; CLSS extended to middle-income group. PMAY-G was extended to 2026 with new targets. Update: "Housing for All" original 2022 deadline was missed; new deadline is 2024 for G and 2026 for both.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: IMP Q09 Assertion (A): Tamil Nadu's Makkalai Thedi Maruthuvam scheme is a unique doorstep health delivery model providing medical services at citizens' homes. Reason (R): Tamil Nadu has a shortage of government hospitals which necessitates doorstep medical care. ▼ ✅ Answer: A is true but R is false. (Tamil Nadu actually has a relatively good primary healthcare network — the real reason is equity and access for elderly, disabled and bedridden patients.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: ) 📌 Explanation: MTM was launched to reach vulnerable populations (elderly, disabled, bed-ridden) who cannot easily visit hospitals — not because hospitals are scarce. TN has ~1,800 PHCs and 450+ government hospitals. MTM complements, not replaces, this network with mobile health teams. 💎 GEM MTM's innovation: mobile medical teams (doctor + nurse + lab technician + pharmacist) visit homes on fixed schedules. Over 1 crore people reached by 2024.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Blood sugar, BP, ECG done at home. Connected to CMCHIS for referral. This is a replicable model — cited by WHO and UN as best practice for primary healthcare equity.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: PYQ Q10 The MGNREGS (Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Scheme) guarantees employment of how many days per year to rural households? What is the wage for Tamil Nadu under this scheme? (TNPSC 2018) ▼ ✅ Answer: 100 days guaranteed per household per year (extendable to 150 days for drought-affected areas). Tamil Nadu wage rate: ₹294/day (2024 revised rate). 📌 Explanation: MGNREGS enacted under the Mahatma Gandhi NREG Act, 2005; implemented from Feb 2, 2006.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: If employment not provided within 15 days, unemployment allowance (1/4th wage for first 30 days, 1/2 thereafter) is payable. 60:40 ratio of unskilled:skilled work mandatory. 💎 GEM MGNREGS key legal provisions: Right-based (enforceable entitlement); employment must be within 5 km radius; work must be 33% women; social audit is mandatory annually. Tamil Nadu is one of the best performers in MGNREGS — 100% payment through bank/PO accounts. Women workers form 70%+ of TN's MGNREGS workforce.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: IMP Q11 Find the incorrect statement about Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY): A. SSY was launched on January 22, 2015 along with Beti Bachao Beti Padhao B. Account can be opened for a girl child below 10 years of age C. SSY has EEE (Exempt-Exempt-Exempt) tax status D. The scheme matures after 18 years from the date of account opening ▼ ✅ Answer: D is INCORRECT. SSY matures 21 years from account opening (not 18 years). Early closure allowed when girl turns 18 and marries; 50% withdrawal for education after 18 years of age of the girl.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: 📌 Explanation: The 21-year maturity (not 18) is the most common exam trap. A, B, C are all correct. Interest rate (currently 8. ) is among the highest for small savings. EEE status: deposit is deductible under 80C, interest is tax-free, maturity is tax-free. 💎 GEM SSY vs PPF comparison: Both have EEE status. PPF: 15 years + extendable; SSY: 21 years fixed. SSY interest usually higher than PPF. Both under Government of India — safe investments. For TNPSC: remember SSY is ONLY for girl child (under 10), maximum 2 accounts per family (exception

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: PYQ Q12 Which scheme provides micro-credit to urban street vendors who lost livelihoods due to COVID-19? What is the initial loan amount? (TNPSC 2022) ▼ ✅ Answer: PM SVANidhi (PM Street Vendor's AtmaNirbhar Nidhi). Initial loan: ₹10,000 (collateral-free). Enhanced loans: ₹20,000 (2nd tranche), ₹50,000 (3rd tranche). Launched: June 2020. 📌 Explanation: Ministry of Housing & Urban Affairs. Beneficiaries must be registered under Street Vendors Act 2014.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Digital payment incentive (cashback) for using UPI. Over 50 lakh vendors benefited. Tamil Nadu: 3+ lakh street vendors covered. 💎 GEM PM SVANidhi is a COVID-19 economic relief scheme that became permanent. It's the first microcredit scheme specifically for URBAN street vendors (not rural). Credit history building for unorganised sector workers is a side-benefit — vendors can access formal credit after SVANidhi track record. Integrated with PM Svanidhi app for real-time monitoring.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: IMP Q13 Assertion (A): The National Education Policy 2020 replaces the 10+2 school structure with a 5+3+3+4 structure. Reason (R): This restructuring aligns with the neurological development stages of children and includes early childhood care education (ages 3–8) within the school framework for the first time. ▼ ✅ Answer: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A. 📌 Explanation: The 5+3+3+4 structure: Foundational (3–8 yrs, classes 1–5 + 2 pre-school years), Preparatory (8–11, classes 3–5), Middle (11–14, classes 6–8), Seco

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Early Childhood Care & Education (ECCE) ages 3–6 are now part of school framework under NEP — first time in India. 💎 GEM NEP 2020 abolished the M. Phil degree (significant change). Introduces Academic Bank of Credits (ABC) allowing students to accumulate credits across universities. Multiple Entry/Exit: 1 year = certificate, 2 years = diploma, 3 years = degree, 4 years = degree with research. Tamil Nadu opposed the 3-language formula (wants to retain 2-language policy).

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: IMP Q14 Match the Tamil Nadu schemes with their key features: A. Naan Mudhalvan B. Pudhumai Penn C. Illam Thedi Kalvi D. Innuyir Kaaval 1. ₹1,000/month for school girls continuing education 2. Doorstep education for COVID-affected students 3. Career skilling and internship for youth 4. Police emergency integrated command centre (Dial 112) ▼ ✅ Answer: A-3, B-1, C-2, D-4 📌 Explanation: Naan Mudhalvan = youth career skills + internships (2021). Pudhumai Penn = ₹1,000/month for girl students in government schools continuing Class 6–12 (2023).

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Illam Thedi Kalvi = volunteer-based doorstep education launched during COVID (2021). Innuyir Kaaval = TN Police's integrated emergency response system with Dial 112. 💎 GEM TN Education schemes comparison: Naan Mudhalvan (skills/employment) vs Pudhumai Penn (girl retention/cash transfer) vs Illam Thedi Kalvi (learning gap bridging). All three are 2021-2023 DMK launches. A common error is confusing Pudhumai Penn (₹1,000 for continuing education) with Magalir Urimai Thittam (₹1,000 for women household heads).

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: PYQ Q15 Under PMAY-G (PM Awas Yojana Gramin), what is the unit assistance provided to beneficiaries in plain areas? How is the beneficiary list prepared? (TNPSC 2020) ▼ ✅ Answer: Unit assistance: ₹1. 20 lakh (plain areas), ₹1. 30 lakh (hilly/NE/difficult areas). Beneficiary list: Socio-Economic Caste Census (SECC) 2011 data, verified by Gram Sabha. 📌 Explanation: PMAY-G launched November 20, 2016. Funding: 60:40 (Centre:State); 90:10 for NE and hilly states.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Convergence with MGNREGS provides ₹18,000–19,000 for 90/95 days of unskilled labour for house construction. Total cost per house with convergence: ~₹1. 💎 GEM SECC 2011 (Socio-Economic Caste Census) is the data backbone for multiple welfare schemes: PMAY-G, PM-JAY/Ayushman Bharat, DDU-GKY. SECC was conducted alongside Census 2011 — first time socio-economic data was systematically collected. Its data remains the primary source for BPL identification in rural schemes — a key governance fact for TNPSC Mains.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: IMP Q16 Find the INCORRECT statement about Tamil Nadu's Chief Minister's Comprehensive Health Insurance Scheme (CMCHIS): A. CMCHIS provides health cover of ₹5 lakh per family per year B. CMCHIS was launched in 2009 and is funded entirely by the state government C. Tamil Nadu joined Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY completely and discontinued CMCHIS D. CMCHIS covers families with annual income below ₹72,000 ▼ ✅ Answer: C is INCORRECT. Tamil Nadu did NOT completely discontinue CMCHIS.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: TN initially opted out of PM-JAY; later partially integrated both schemes. CMCHIS continues to cover more TN-specific conditions and is state-funded. TN runs a hybrid model. 📌 Explanation: CMCHIS launched 2009 under AIADMK (expanded to ₹5 lakh by 2016). Funded by TN state budget. PM-JAY is a central scheme (60:40 funding). TN chose to maintain its own scheme alongside. This "opt-out" of PM-JAY by TN is frequently asked in TNPSC current affairs questions.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: 💎 GEM States that didn't join PM-JAY initially: Tamil Nadu (has CMCHIS), Telangana (has Aarogyasri), West Bengal (has Swasthya Sathi), Odisha (has Biju Swasthya Kalyan). This reflects Indian federalism in health — states can run parallel schemes. As of 2024, most states have integrated to some extent. TN-Centre health scheme relations = important TNPSC Polity + Welfare question.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: IMP Q17 Assertion (A): PM Mudra Yojana provides loans without collateral through three categories — Shishu, Kishore, and Tarun. Reason (R): PM Mudra Yojana is directly implemented by NABARD (National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development) for rural enterprises. ▼ ✅ Answer: A is true but R is false. PM Mudra is implemented through Commercial Banks, RRBs, MFIs, Small Finance Banks — NOT by NABARD. NABARD handles agricultural credit; MUDRA handles non-farm micro-enterprise credit.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: 📌 Explanation: Shishu (up to ₹50,000), Kishore (₹50,001–₹5 lakh), Tarun (₹5–10 lakh). MUDRA stands for Micro Units Development and Refinance Agency Ltd. Launched April 8, 2015. 40 crore+ loans worth ₹22 lakh crore disbursed since launch (2024). 💎 GEM Mudra loans do NOT require collateral or guarantor — key feature. MUDRA Bank itself refinances banks/NBFCs/MFIs; it doesn't directly give loans to borrowers. Tarun Plus (₹10–20 lakh) category added 2024 for established micro-entrepreneurs.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Women borrowers form 68% of MUDRA loan accounts — highest women participation in any loan scheme.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: PYQ Q18 Which of the following statements about the Swachh Bharat Mission (SBM) is CORRECT? SBM-Gramin is under Ministry of Housing & Urban Affairs B. India was declared ODF (Open Defecation Free) on October 2, 2019 C. Swachh Survekshan is conducted annually for rural areas only D. SBM Phase 2 (2020-25) targets ODF status (same as Phase 1) ▼ ✅ Answer: B is CORRECT. India was declared ODF on October 2, 2019 (Gandhi Jayanti) — 150th birth anniversary of Mahatma Gandhi.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: A is wrong (SBM-G is under Jal Shakti, not Housing). C is wrong (Swachh Survekshan is for URBAN areas). D is wrong (Phase 2 targets ODF+ and ODF++, not just ODF). 📌 Explanation: ODF+ = functional, clean and maintained toilets. ODF++ = ODF+ with faecal sludge management. Phase 2 also focuses on solid and liquid waste management (SLWM) in villages. Indore has consistently topped Swachh Survekshan (urban) rankings. 💎 GEM SBM is cited as one of the world's largest public hygiene behavior change campaigns.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Academic studies showed SBM reduced child mortality by 5. 3% in villages that achieved ODF status. Tamil Nadu was among the first states to achieve ODF status (2018). Swachh Bharat Day = Oct 2 (Gandhi Jayanti) — annual compliance review.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: IMP Q19 Match the following Central Schemes with their unique distinctions: A. PM Vishwakarma 1. Only scheme with mandatory job placement (75%) post-training 2. First scheme specifically for 18 traditional craft trades 3. First microcredit scheme for urban street vendors 4. Credit-linked subsidy merged from REGP + PMRY — implemented by KVIC ▼ ✅ Answer: A-3, B-1, C-4, D-2 📌 Explanation: PM SVANidhi = first urban vendor microcredit (2020, Covid response).

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: DDU-GKY = mandatory 75% job placement distinguishes it from all other skilling schemes. PMEGP = credit subsidy scheme merging REGP+PMRY, implemented through KVIC. PM Vishwakarma = Sep 2023, first scheme for 18 specific traditional artisan trades. 💎 GEM Employment scheme ecosystem: MGNREGS (rural unskilled employment guarantee) → DDU-GKY (rural skilled formal jobs) → PMKVY (urban/rural skilling) → PM Mudra (self-employment loan) → PMEGP (micro-enterprise creation) → Stand Up India (SC/ST + women enterprise loans).

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: PYQ Q20 The Cauvery Delta Protected Agriculture Zone was declared by Tamil Nadu in 2020. Which 5 districts were included, and what activity was specifically prohibited? Also, who pioneered the School Noon Meal Scheme in Tamil Nadu? (TNPSC Mains type — multi-part) ▼ ✅ Answer: Five districts: Thanjavur, Tiruvarur, Nagapattinam, Ariyalur, Cuddalore. Prohibited activity: Hydrocarbon (oil and gas) exploration. School Noon Meal: K. Kamaraj pioneered (1956 expanded); MGR made it universal and free (1982).

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: 📌 Explanation: The Cauvery delta protection was specifically against ONGC's Block CY-DWN-2001/2 hydrocarbon exploration licence in Kaveri Basin. TN passed a resolution citing threat to 40 lakh farmer livelihoods. This is first-of-its-kind use of state legislative power to block central energy licensing. 💎 GEM Constitutional angle: Under Schedule VII, Mines and Minerals (including Oil) is in the Union List (Entry 53, 54). States cannot legally prevent central government mineral licensing — hence TN's declaration has been challenged.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: This raises the question of concurrent jurisdiction over land, agriculture (State List) vs. minerals (Union List). The Cauvery Delta issue represents Centre-State federal conflict over natural resources — a high-value TNPSC Polity Mains topic.
