chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.text: SSC Nouns — High-Yield Crack Sheet 1){event. preventDefault();history. back();}" title="Back" style="position:fixed;bottom:24px;left:24px;z-index:9998;width:42px;height:42px;border-radius:50%;background:rgba(13,15,20,0. 92);border:1px solid rgba(245,200,66,0. 35);color:#f5c842;font-size:20px;line-height:1;text-decoration:none;backdrop-filter:blur(8px);box-shadow:0 2px 14px rgba(0,0,0,0. 45);display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;transition:all .

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.text: 2s;" onmouseover="this. background='rgba(245,200,66,0. 12)';" onmouseout="this. background='rgba(13,15,20,0. 92)';"> CRITICAL RULE SSC TRAP TIP PYQ ALERT EXCEPTION RULE DEFINITION

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.section: Definition & Overview — What Is a Noun?
chunks.text: A noun is one of the two primary building blocks of English (the other being verbs). It is the name given to a person, place, thing, animal, idea, feeling, or concept . Every sentence requires at least one noun to convey meaning. ✅ TIP — MEMORY HOOK Everything around you right now is a noun: pen , board , chair , table . Even abstract ideas like fear , democracy , luck are nouns. If you can name it, it's a noun. Noun Represents Examples Type Hint Person / Profession teacher, singer, manager, gardener, doctor Common Noun Specific Named Person Ra

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.section: Six Types of Nouns — Complete Reference
chunks.text: # Type Definition Examples SSC Relevance 1 Common Noun General name for any person, place, or thing in a class city, chair, dog, teacher, pen, student Basis for all plural rules; error spotting 2 Proper Noun Specific name of a particular person, place, or thing. Always capitalised. Chennai, Ravi, Diwali, Tajmahal, India, Monday, January Capitalisation errors; article use before proper nouns 3 Collective Noun Name for a group of persons, animals, or things treated as one unit family, class, committee, flock, herd, choir, gang, jury S-V agreement

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.section: Six Types of Nouns — Complete Reference
chunks.text: Written as one word, hyphenated, or two words. toothpaste, fruit juice, ice cream, crossroad, stepmother, mother-in-law Pluralisation rule — only the HEAD noun is pluralised ⚡ SSC TRAP — COMMON vs PROPER city = Common Noun (lowercase) | Chennai = Proper Noun (uppercase) library = Common | Anna Centenary Library = Proper park = Common | Lal Bagh Botanical Garden = Proper Rule: Once you give a specific name to something, it becomes Proper.

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.text: Collective Noun Used For Collective Noun Used For family people flock birds / sheep class / school students herd cattle / cows committee members pride / troop lions jury / panel judges pack wolves choir singers shoal / school fish gang boys/thieves fleet ships / aircraft company actors bunch / bouquet flowers regiment soldiers string beads crew sailors grove / row trees cabinet ministers flight stairs / steps 🏆 PYQ ALERT — COLLECTIVE NOUNS IN FILL-THE-BLANK SSC frequently tests: "A ___ of lions is called a pride / troop.

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.text: " or "A ___ of actors is called a company. " Memorise at minimum: flock (birds) , pride (lions) , herd (cattle) , fleet (ships) , pack (wolves) , string (beads) .

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.text: Category Examples Feelings / Emotions fear, anxiety, happiness, stress, sorrow, joy Qualities / Traits honesty, generosity, bravery, determination, patience, wisdom States / Conditions luxury, comfort, freedom, chaos, democracy, poverty Concepts / Ideas opportunity, democracy, justice, chance, faith Events / Moments birthday, childhood, marriage, career, death

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.text: RULE 01 General Rule — Add -s For most nouns, simply add -s to form the plural. pen → pens | mountain → mountains | river → rivers | computer → computers | player → players RULE 02 Nouns ending in -s / -ss / -sh / -ch / -x / -z → Add -es Singular Plural Singular Plural bus buses class classes brush brushes branch branches church churches beach beaches box boxes sandwich sandwiches witch witches flash flashes RULE 03 Nouns ending in consonant + y → Change y to i , add -es baby→babies | family→families | story→stories | fairy→fairies | puppy→pupp

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.text: Use an before vowel sounds (not letters — sounds ). Word First Sound Article Example artist vowel /æ/ an an artist insect vowel /ɪ/ an an insect umbrella vowel /ʌ/ an an umbrella hour vowel /aʊ/ (h is silent) an an hour honour vowel /ɒ/ (h is silent) an an honour heir vowel /eə/ (h is silent) an an heir uniform consonant /juː/ (y-sound) a a uniform unit consonant /juː/ (y-sound) a a unit university consonant /juː/ (y-sound) a a university 🔴 CRITICAL RULE The article is determined by the SOUND , not the letter.

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.text: hour starts with 'h' (consonant letter) but is pronounced /aʊər/ → use an hour . uniform starts with 'u' (vowel letter) but is pronounced /juː/ → use a uniform . RULE 07 Irregular Plurals — Spelling Change Singular Plural Singular Plural man men woman women child children person people mouse mice louse lice tooth teeth foot feet goose geese ox oxen 🏆 PYQ ALERT tooth→teeth , foot→feet and child→children are frequently tested. Using childs or tooths = guaranteed error.

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.text: RULE 08 Same Form — Singular = Plural (Zero Plurals) These nouns do NOT change form in the plural. Noun Singular Use Plural Use Exception Note sheep one sheep many sheep Always sheep deer a deer several deer Always deer fish a fish many fish fishes = different species aircraft an aircraft two aircraft Always aircraft salmon a salmon 100 salmon Always salmon series a series two series Always series species a species many species Always species 🔴 CRITICAL RULE — fish vs.

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.text: fishes fish = plural of ONE species (I bought 10 fish). fishes = multiple different species (The ocean has many fishes). This distinction is a favourite SSC trap. RULE 09 Always Plural (Pluralia Tantum) — Paired / Symmetric Objects These nouns exist only in the plural. They refer to objects with two matching parts or are always used in pairs. Group Nouns Correct Usage Clothing (paired legs) trousers, jeans, shorts, tights, pyjamas, pants a pair of trousers Eyewear glasses, spectacles, goggles, binoculars a pair of glasses Tools (two handles) sc

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.text: Always use a pair of scissors / trousers . These take a plural verb : "My trousers are torn. " RULE 10 Compound Noun Pluralisation — Pluralise the HEAD Noun Only Compound Noun Head Noun Correct Plural Wrong Form ❌ bedroom room bedrooms bedrooms✓ but bedsroom✗ commander-in-chief commander commanders-in-chief commander-in-chiefs brother-in-law brother brothers-in-law brother-in-laws mother-in-law mother mothers-in-law mother-in-laws step-daughter daughter step-daughters steps-daughter maid-servant servant maid-servants maids-servant member of par

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.text: " → members (not member). "He met both his brothers-in-law" is the most frequently tested PYQ compound plural. Using brother-in-laws is the classic error. RULE 11 Nouns ending in -ful → Add -s after -ful cupsful ❌ → cupfuls ✓ spoonful→spoonfuls | handful→handfuls | glassful→glassfuls | bucketful→bucketfuls 🔴 CRITICAL RULE The -s goes AFTER the whole word: cup ful s , never cup s ful . The word is a single unit; the -ful suffix makes it a new noun.

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.text: RULE 12 Hyphenated Nouns with Numbers → Always use Singular Head When a noun phrase with a number is used as a modifier (adjective) before another noun, keep the first noun singular . Wrong ❌ Correct ✓ Rule a 3-days workshop a 3-day workshop Used as adjective → singular a 20-men committee a 20-man committee Used as adjective → singular a 10-pounds note a 10-pound note Used as adjective → singular a 5-years-old child a 5-year-old child Used as adjective → singular a 2-miles walk a 2-mile walk Used as adjective → singular RULE 13 Plural-looking N

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.text: Always use a singular verb with them. Category Nouns Correct Verb Form Academic Subjects mathematics, physics, economics, civics, statistics, linguistics Mathematics is difficult. Diseases mumps, measles, rickets, rabies, diabetes Measles is contagious. Games / Sports billiards, athletics, darts, gymnastics Billiards is played all over. News news The news was good. Other means, series, innings, politics, ethics The series is interesting. 🔴 CRITICAL RULE — The #1 SSC Error Spotting Target Mathematics are my favourite subject.

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.text: ❌ Mathematics is my favourite subject. ✓ The news were very depressing. ❌ The news was very depressing. ✓ Linguistics are the study of language. ❌ Linguistics is the study of language.

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.text: Rule # Condition Action Example 01 Most nouns Add -s pen→pens 02 Ends in -s/-ss/-sh/-ch/-x/-z Add -es bus→buses 03 Consonant + y y → i, add -es baby→babies 03e Vowel + y Add -s monkey→monkeys 04 Ends in -f/-fe (most) f/fe → -ves leaf→leaves 04e Ends in -f/-fe (exceptions) Add -s roof→roofs 05 Consonant + o Add -es hero→heroes 05e Vowel + o / short words Add -s radio→radios 07 Irregular Spelling changes man→men, child→children 08 Zero plurals No change sheep→sheep 09 Always plural Use pair of a pair of scissors 10 Compound noun Pluralise head wo

chunks.text: MASCULINE GENDER Masculine Feminine boy girl man woman father mother son daughter brother sister husband wife uncle aunt nephew niece prince princess king queen emperor empress wizard witch actor actress waiter waitress policeman policewoman bachelor spinster sir / lord madam / lady bridegroom bride monk nun hero heroine ANIMAL GENDERS Animal Male Female lion lion lioness tiger tiger tigress horse stallion mare dog dog bitch fox fox vixen rabbit buck doe sheep ram ewe pig boar sow chicken rooster/cock hen duck drake duck cattle bull cow goose g

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.text: Standard SSC English = just cousin . 🏆 PYQ ALERT — Animal Gender MCQs "The female of a horse is called a ___. " → mare "The female of a fox is called a ___. " → vixen "A female pig is called a ___. " → sow These are common fill-in-the-blank PYQs from SSC CGL Tier I.

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.text: The possessive form shows ownership or association. It is formed using an apostrophe + s ( 's ). Case Rule Example Meaning Singular noun Add 's Ravi's pen | Peter's bed | Dad's cooking Belongs to one person Plural noun ending in -s Add ' only (after s) rats' tails | horses' hooves | teachers' books Belongs to many Plural noun NOT ending in -s Add 's children's room | men's voices | women's rights Irregular plural possessive Two owners, shared item Add 's to the LAST name only Katie and Mike's house (one shared house) Joint ownership Two owners,

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.text: This is Susan and Jeni room. ❌ → This is Susan and Jeni's room. ✓ (joint) He gave informations. ❌ → He gave some information. ✓ (uncountable) The horse's tail is surrounded by every insects. ❌ → The horse's tail is surrounded by every insect.

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.text: Feature Countable Uncountable Can be counted? Yes (1, 2, 3…) No Has plural form? Yes: pen → pens No: rice → rice (NOT rices) Use a/an? Yes: a pen, an apple No: ~~a rice~~ | a bowl of rice ✓ Use many/few? Yes: many books, few pens No: ~~many water~~ | much/little water ✓ Use much/little? No (use many/few) Yes: much rain, little sugar Use some/any? Yes (plural): some books Yes: some water, any milk Quantifier needed? Not for plural: I like bananas Yes: a cup of coffee / a bag of sugar

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.text: Category Nouns Liquids/Drinks water, milk, juice, coffee, tea, beer, oil, blood Food substances rice, bread, sugar, salt, flour, butter, meat, pasta Materials wood, glass, metal, cotton, paper, stone, sand, iron Abstract ideas information, advice, news, knowledge, research, evidence Other furniture, luggage/baggage, traffic, weather, progress, work, travel, equipment, money Subject areas music, homework, education, training, accommodation 🔴 CRITICAL RULE — NEVER pluralise these furnitures ❌ → furniture ✓ informations ❌ → information ✓ advices

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.text: Uncountable Noun Correct Quantifier Form bread a loaf of bread / a slice/piece of bread sugar / salt a bag of sugar / a grain of salt / a pinch of salt paper a sheet/piece of paper advice a piece of advice information a piece of information soap a bar of soap chocolate a bar of chocolate / a piece of chocolate cake / cheese a slice/piece of cake rice a bowl/grain/bag of rice water / juice a glass/cup/bottle of water music a piece of music / a song (countable) furniture a piece of furniture

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.section: Dual-Use Nouns (Countable AND Uncountable depending on context)
chunks.text: Noun Uncountable Usage Countable Usage noise I can't hear much noise. I heard a noise just now. (= one particular sound) paper I need some paper to write on. I bought a paper. (= newspaper) | research papers hair She has long hair. (all hair) There's a hair in my soup. (one strand) room There is no room. (= space) There is a spare room. (= physical room) experience I got a lot of experience. (= overall) I had some interesting experiences. (= specific events) time I don't have time.

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.section: Dual-Use Nouns (Countable AND Uncountable depending on context)
chunks.text: (= in general) We had a good time. (= one occasion) coffee/tea I don't like coffee. (general) Two coffees, please. (= cups of coffee) travel Travel broadens the mind. (in general) —use trip/journey for specific travel ⚡ SSC TRAP — little vs. a little I have little water. ❌ (= no water at all — negative meaning) I have a little water. ✓ (= some water — positive meaning) I have little work to do. ❌ (= virtually no work) I have a little work to do. ✓ (= some work) Same pattern: few (negative — nearly none) vs.

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.text: 🏆 NOTE All sentences below are from SSC CGL / CHSL Previous Year Question Papers, discussed in the lecture. Identify the error, then check the rule. # Sentence (with Error) Error Correction Rule Tag 1 There should be furnitures in my room. furnitures furniture UNCOUNTABLE 2 Cyclone is a natural phenomenon and nobody can stop it for sure. No error Correct sentence NO ERROR 3 3 dozens bananas have been sold by me. dozens 3 dozen RULE 12 4 He told me that it was his friend's Ravi's house .

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.text: double possessive his friend Ravi's house POSSESSIVE 5 His hairs are black and attractive. hairs are → hair is His hair is black and attractive. UNCOUNTABLE 6 All sister-in-laws attended the party on the eve of the New Year. sister-in-laws sisters-in-law RULE 10 7 He gave some informations I had been waiting for for the previous 2 months. informations information UNCOUNTABLE 8 He laid to beat the poor and the rich on that auspicious occasion. the poor / the rich with -s the poor / the rich (no -s, they represent a class) THE + ADJ 9 Those piece

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.text: jewelleries jewellery UNCOUNTABLE 10 In this my cousin brother is a cheater and he cheats his family members and friends. cousin brother cousin REDUNDANCY 11 The female horse is called a ___. [A] pony [B] mare [C] doe [D] rooster Fill-in mare (B) GENDER 12 Athletics are good for young people. are → is Athletics is good for young people.

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.text: HIGH PROBABILITY Uncountable noun used in plural form furnitures / informations / advices / luggages / jewelleries — These appear in nearly every SSC paper cycle. Expect at least one uncountable error in Error Spotting. All the furnitures in his room are expensive. ❌ → All the furniture in his room is expensive. ✓ HIGH PROBABILITY Compound noun — wrong pluralisation (in-law series) SSC CGL 2024/25 trend: brothers-in-law / mothers-in-law / commanders-in-chief / members of parliament .

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.text: The error is always adding -s to the preposition or the last word. He invited all his brother-in-laws. ❌ → He invited all his brothers-in-law. ✓ HIGH PROBABILITY Plural-form singular subject (mathematics/news) + wrong verb Classic SVA error. Mathematics are / Physics have / News were — all wrong. These are expected in Sentence Improvement sections. The news were very disturbing. ❌ → The news was very disturbing. ✓ RISING TREND little vs. a little — Cloze Test In fill-in-the-blank, choose between little (almost nothing) and a little (some).

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.text: Rising frequency in recent CHSL papers. She has a little money left. ✓ (= some money) She has little hope. ✓ (= almost no hope) RISING TREND Number + noun modifier — singular form required "a 3-days workshop" type errors increasing in Tier II. The number-noun combo always takes singular form when used as an adjective. MEDIUM Collective Noun — animal group vocabulary Fill-in questions: "A ___ of lions / a ___ of ships / a ___ of fish. " Medium frequency but highly scoreable if prepared.

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.text: 🔴 EXAMINER'S TRAP #1 — The Single Most Likely Mistake Using a/an based on the LETTER (not sound): writing "a hour" instead of "an hour" , or "an university" instead of "a university" . The sound rule for articles catches most students. Similarly, a/an + uncountable noun (e. , "an information" ) is instantly wrong.

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.text: # Sentence (Find the Error) Error Correction Rule Q1 The childrens are playing in the garden. childrens children (already plural) Rule 07 Q2 We need to buy a furniture for the new house. a furniture some furniture / a piece of furniture Uncountable Q3 He is an honest man and works hard. No error Correct — 'honest' sounds /ɒnɪst/ (vowel sound) Article Rule Q4 All the passers-by stopped to watch the accident. No error passers-by is the correct plural Rule 10 Q5 She gave me an useful advice yesterday.

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.text: an useful + advice (two errors) a useful piece of advice Article + Uncountable Q6 I have kept all my luggages in the car. luggages luggage Uncountable Q7 The news were shocking for the entire nation. news were news was Rule 13 Q8 He attended a two-days conference in Delhi. two-days two-day Rule 12 Q9 The flock of sheeps was grazing in the field. sheeps flock of sheep Rule 08 Q10 She has little friends, so she is often lonely. little (for countable noun) few friends (little = uncountable; few = countable) Few / Little

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.text: NOUN TYPES (6) Common | Proper | Collective | Concrete | Abstract | Compound PROPER NOUN RULE Always starts with Capital Letter. , Chennai, Monday, Diwali, Taj Mahal. ARTICLE RULE (SOUND, NOT LETTER) a + consonant sound | an + vowel sound an hour ✓ | a uniform ✓ | an honest ✓ -f/-fe → -ves (but exceptions) leaf→leaves, wife→wives BUT: roof→roofs, chief→chiefs COMPOUND NOUN PLURAL Pluralise the HEAD word only: brothers-in-law ✓ | members of parliament ✓ ZERO PLURAL (NO CHANGE) sheep, deer, fish, aircraft, salmon, species, series ALWAYS PLURAL (P

chunks.title: Nouns
chunks.text: a little little = almost none (negative) | a little = some (positive) few = almost none | a few = some (both for countable) POSSESSIVE RULE Singular: Ravi's pen | Plural (s): boys' bag | Plural (non-s): children's room KEY ANIMAL GENDERS horse: stallion/mare | fox: fox/vixen | pig: boar/sow | sheep: ram/ewe | duck: drake/duck fish vs. fishes fish = plural of one species | fishes = multiple different species COUSIN BROTHER — WRONG Correct English = just "cousin" (Indian English error, tested in SSC)

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: Based on lecture: "Arivoam English — Pronouns (Parts of Speech Series)" · Full technical breakdown 📖 Scope: All 8 Pronoun Types 🎯 Exam Focus: Error Spotting + Sentence Improvement ⚠️ Negative Marking: Tier I −0. 5 | Tier II −1 📊 English Weight: ~35% of question paper Subject Pronoun Object Pronoun Possessive Adjective Possessive Pronoun Reflexive Pronoun Error / Wrong form

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: Foundation · Definition What is a Pronoun? CORE DEFINITION PRO + NOUN = "Instead of a Noun" A pronoun is a word used in place of a noun to avoid repetitive use of the same noun. It is introduced after the noun (antecedent) has been established at least once. ⚠️ Critical Rule — Antecedent Agreement A pronoun must agree with its antecedent (the noun it replaces) in person, number, and gender . This is the #1 source of errors in SSC Error Spotting. Miss Milling stood at Miss Milling's classroom door and greeted Miss Milling's third-period students

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: → All repetitions replaced with pronouns: Miss Milling stood at her classroom door and greeted her third-period students as they walked into the classroom.

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: # Type Key Words SSC Frequency 1 Personal I, you, he, she, it, we, they Very High 2 Possessive mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs Very High 3 Reflexive / Intensive myself, yourself, himself… High 4 Reciprocal each other, one another Medium 5 Relative who, whom, whose, which, that Very High 6 Demonstrative this, that, these, those Medium 7 Distributive each, either, neither High 8 Indefinite anyone, nobody, some, all, both… High

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: Type 1 · Most Tested Personal Pronouns Personal pronouns refer to specific persons or things . They have two positional forms: Subjective (Nominative Case) — used as the subject of a verb — and Objective (Accusative Case) — used as the object of a verb or preposition. Person Number Subject Object Possessive Adj. Possessive Pro. Reflexive 1st Singular I me my mine myself 1st Plural we us our ours ourselves 2nd Sing. you you your yours yourself / yourselves 3rd Sing.

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: (M) he him his his himself 3rd Sing. (F) she her her hers herself 3rd Sing. (N) it it its — itself 3rd Plural they them their theirs themselves ⚠️ Critical Rule — Subject vs. Object Position Use Subject pronouns (I, he, she, we, they) as the doer of the action. Use Object pronouns (me, him, her, us, them) after verbs and prepositions . In comparisons using than/as , always use the subject pronoun . "She is far better than he " (not him ) — because "he [is]" is the implied complete clause.

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: Lisa likes cats. She likes to stroke them . "She" = subject; "them" = object (replaces "cats") My name is Michael. My father works hard. He is a doctor. My sister is older than me . Was it I who had to face the music? After linking verbs (is/was), use Subject pronoun, not object. As a student of literature, she is far better than he . "him" is WRONG here — comparison clause requires subject pronoun. 🪤 SSC Trap — "It is I" vs. "It is me" Formally/grammatically correct: "It is I" (subject pronoun after linking verb).

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: SSC tests this. Students instinctively write "It is me" (colloquially acceptable, but grammatically debated). In SSC Error Spotting, treat "It is me" as an error and replace with "It is I.

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: Type 2 · Critical Distinction Possessive Pronouns vs. Possessive Adjectives ⚠️ Critical Rule — The Most Tested Distinction This distinction appears almost every year in SSC exams. Possessive Adjective (my, your, his, her, its, our, their) — always accompanies a noun . It modifies the noun. " my book" Possessive Pronoun (mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs) — stands alone . It replaces the noun entirely. "This book is mine . " NO apostrophe is ever used with possessive pronouns.

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: your's / their's = always WRONG. Possessive Adjective Usage Possessive Pronoun Usage my my book (+ noun) mine This book is mine. your your pen yours Where is yours? his his car his The car is his. her her cat hers Is this cat hers? its its tail — (no standalone form) our our house ours The house is ours. their their lunch theirs We had lunch but they had theirs.

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: You said it's your problem, but I said it's mine . "mine" = possessive pronoun replacing "my problem" Your problems are the same as mine . → Corrected: Yours are the same problems as mine. Tara has lost her cat. Is this cat hers ? I see our car, but where is yours ? 🪤 SSC Trap — Letter Closing "Yours faithfully" Your's faithfully = WRONG (apostrophe with possessive pronoun is never allowed) Yours faithfully = CORRECT. This is a classic SSC question.

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: "Yours" here is a possessive pronoun, not a contraction. Never use an apostrophe with mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs, its. 🏆 PYQ Alert — Appeared in SSC CGL 2019, 2022 Sentence: "The hotels of Ravi are more luxurious than Rohan . " → Error: comparing hotels with a person. Correction: "…than those of Rohan . " — Use demonstrative pronoun "those of" to compare equivalent nouns (hotels with hotels).

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: Type 3 · Frequently Misused Reflexive & Intensive Pronouns REFLEXIVE Action reflects back on subject The subject and object are the same entity . The reflexive pronoun is essential — removing it changes or destroys the meaning. She hurt herself . Remove "herself" → sentence loses meaning INTENSIVE (EMPHATIC) Used for emphasis only Same -self/-selves form, but used purely for emphasis . Removing it does NOT change the core meaning. Hana herself made the dinner reservation.

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: Remove "herself" → meaning remains clear ⚠️ Critical Rule — "Myself" cannot replace "I" or "me" You and myself will enjoy the party. → WRONG (no antecedent "I" earlier in sentence) You and I will enjoy the party. → CORRECT Reflexive pronouns cannot be used as plain subject or object pronouns . They require the base pronoun to appear earlier.

chunks.title: English
chunks.section: Verbs that REQUIRE Reflexive Pronouns (SSC Hot List)
chunks.text: Verb Correct Use amuse I amused myself by reading. avail He availed himself of the opportunity. blame Don't blame yourself . cut I cut myself with the pen. enjoy I enjoyed myself . help I helped myself by cooking. hurt She hurt herself . absent (oneself) He absented himself from class. apply (oneself) She applied herself to studies. pride (oneself) He prided himself on his work.

chunks.title: English
chunks.section: Verbs that DO NOT use Reflexive Pronouns (Exception List)
chunks.text: 🪤 SSC Trap — Avoid Reflexive with Routine Actions For routine bodily actions, reflexive pronouns are not used in standard English. He washed himself in cold water. → Standard: He washed in cold water. He always shaved himself before going out. → He always shaved before going out. Michael dressed himself for the party. → Michael dressed for the party. Exception: "He is old enough to wash herself " — here the reflexive IS needed because it emphasizes the capability (emphatic use).

chunks.title: English
chunks.section: Verbs that DO NOT use Reflexive Pronouns (Exception List)
chunks.text: 🏆 PYQ Alert Sentence: "He bathed himself in the river. " — The error is himself . Bathing is a routine action; reflexive pronoun is redundant. This pattern appeared in SSC CHSL 2021.

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: Type 4 Reciprocal Pronouns CORE RULE Reciprocate = to return an action. Reciprocal pronouns express a mutual/shared action or relationship . Each other → used when exactly 2 people/things are involved One another → used when more than 2 people/things are involved Situation Use Example 2 people each other John and Kate love each other . 3+ people one another They recited poetry to one another . 2 people one another The two sisters shouted at one another in public.

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: ✗ The two sisters shouted at one another in public. → Correct: shouted at each other . 🪤 SSC Trap — Count the People SSC will give you a sentence with "each other" or "one another" and change the number of participants. Always count the people/things before choosing. Two sisters = 2 people = "each other. " A class of students = 3+ = "one another.

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: Type 5 · Most Complex Relative Pronouns A relative pronoun connects (relates) a clause or phrase to a noun or pronoun . It introduces an adjective clause that gives more information about the antecedent. Pronoun Refers To Function Example who Persons (subject) Subject of the relative clause The musician who wrote the song is French. whom Persons (object) Object of the relative clause I know the boy whom she met. whose Persons / things Possessive relative The boy whose phone rang stood up.

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: which Animals / Things Subject or Object; also in comparisons The car which she bought is red. that Persons, animals, things Universal; used in restrictive clauses 12th Sept is the date that I was born. ⚠️ Critical Rule — WHO vs. WHOM WHO = Subject pronoun (He/She equivalent). Ask: "Who did it? " → answer is the subject. WHOM = Object pronoun (Him/Her equivalent). Ask: "Whom did I meet? " → answer is the object. Test: Replace with he/him. If "he" fits → use WHO.

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: If "him" fits → use WHOM. The person who I met yesterday was my classmate. Test: "I met him" → him fits → use WHOM Correct: The person whom I met yesterday was my classmate.

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: Context Use Restrictive clause (essential info, no commas) that Non-restrictive clause (extra info, with commas) which Comparison between two people/animals which Refers to a person (subject position) who / that When antecedent is both person + thing that The chef did not know who of the servants broke the expensive dinner set. Two servants → comparison → use WHICH Correct: … which of the servants… He was on the phone for hours together, who really irritated his parents.

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: "Who" refers to a thing (the action of being on the phone), not a person → use WHICH Correct: … which really irritated his parents. 🏆 PYQ Alert — SSC CGL 2018, 2020, 2023 "whose" for possessive relative clauses is frequently tested. Pattern: "The boy whose back is red has to stand up. " Students confuse this with "who's" (= who is). "Who's" ≠ "whose. " SSC tests this apostrophe distinction.

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: Type 6 Demonstrative Pronouns Distance Singular Plural Near this these Far that those ⚠️ Critical Rule — Demonstrative Pronoun vs. Demonstrative Adjective Demonstrative Pronoun : Stands alone, replaces a noun. " This is a table. " Demonstrative Adjective : Modifies a noun. " This table is here. " Same words (this/that/these/those) — function determines the category. 🏆 PYQ Alert — "Those of" for Comparison "The hotels of Ravi are more luxurious than Rohan .

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: " → Error: comparing hotels with a person. Correction: "…than those of Rohan. " — "those of" = the hotels of. This appeared in SSC CGL 2022 Tier-II. Rule: When comparing possessions/attributes , use that of (singular) or those of (plural) to avoid faulty comparison.

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: This is my desk. That is my friend's house. [Demonstrative Pronouns] These are my pets. Those are horses. This table is here. [Demonstrative Adjective — "this" modifies "table"]

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: Type 7 · Verb Agreement Trap Distributive Pronouns Distributive pronouns refer to members of a group individually , not collectively. Although addressing a group, each member is considered separately and singularly . Pronoun Meaning Number of People Verb each every one individually 2 or more Singular either one of the two exactly 2 Singular neither not one of the two exactly 2 Singular ⚠️ Critical Rule — Always Singular Verb Each, either, neither → always take a singular verb Each of the players is playing well.

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: (NOT are ) Neither of the brothers has given the correct answer. (NOT have ) For 3 or more people , use none / no one / any (not either/neither) 🪤 SSC Trap — Either/Neither for TWO only "None of the two boys came. "None" is for 3+. "Neither of the two boys came.

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: Each of my friends has made a phone call to me today. Each of the players is playing well. Either Adam or Eve is guilty. Neither of the brothers has given a correct answer. Neither suggestion will be accepted. Neither is suitable for the post.

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: Type 8 Indefinite Pronouns Indefinite pronouns do not refer to a specific person, place, or thing. They begin with words like any-, every-, some-, no- . Category Pronouns Verb Agreement Always Singular no one, nobody, nothing, anyone, anybody, anything, everyone, everybody, everything, someone, somebody, something, each, either, neither, one Singular verb Always Plural several, few, both, many Plural verb Singular OR Plural some, any, more, most, all, none Depends on context ⚠️ Critical Rule — "one" Pronoun Consistency When you start a sentence

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: Never switch to he/his/him midway. One should keep one's promise. (NOT his promise) One should do one's duty. (NOT their duty) ⚠️ Critical Rule — "All is well" vs. "All are here" All is well → "all" refers to a concept/state → singular verb They were all elated → "all" refers to a group of people → plural verb Context determines singular/plural for: some, any, more, most, all, none

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: Everybody who was working in the company gave his or her contribution. NOT "their" — "everybody" = singular Everyone loves this song. [singular] Nobody can do it. [singular verb] None of the scholars of this class has passed.

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: Order of Pronouns · Most SSC-Tested Rule The "231" and "123" Order Rules ⚠️ Critical Rule — Order When Something Negative / Bad / Blameworthy When confessing a mistake, wrongdoing, or negative situation, put yourself first (responsibility): 1st Person → 2nd Person → 3rd Person "I, you, and he are responsible. " I, you and he have committed this mistake. ⚠️ Critical Rule — Order When Something Positive / Praise / Award When talking about something favourable, complimentary, or praiseworthy — put the other person first (politeness/courtesy): 2nd

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: " You, he and I have completed our studies. You, Raj and I have completed our studies. (NOT Raj, I and you ) 🪤 SSC Trap — The Arrangement Question Previous Year Question pattern: "He, I and you shall arrange everything. " — Find the error and rearrange. Since it is a positive/neutral statement — order should be: "You, he and I shall arrange everything. " The error is at part A (He, I and you → wrong order). This appeared in multiple SSC papers.

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: Situation Order Mnemonic Positive / Praise / Good news 2 → 3 → 1 "Young Tigers I-dentify" (You-They-I) Negative / Mistake / Blame 1 → 2 → 3 "I went You went They went" (I confess first) Advanced Rules · Tier II Focused Either…Or / Neither…Nor — Agreement Rules RULE A — Singular + Singular Both nouns singular → Singular verb + Singular pronoun Either Raj or Mohit will invest his money. Neither Raj nor Sam could pass the test. RULE B — Singular + Plural Mixed → Verb agrees with the NEARER noun; Plural pronoun Singular noun comes FIRST, plural nou

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: The verb/pronoun matches the closer (second) noun. Either the leader or the associates were associated with the responsibility of citing the authority — their authorised message. RULE C — 3+ Singular Nouns with Each/Every Each/Every + Noun + and + Noun → Singular verb + Singular pronoun Every learner and every teacher does his or her duty. 🪤 SSC Trap — "Neither of the two" vs "None of them" "Neither" = only for exactly 2 "None" = for 3 or more "None of the two friends has got the money back" → WRONG → "Neither of the two friends has got the mo

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: 2026 Cycle · Expert Prediction The 2026 Prediction Vault HIGH PROBABILITY Pattern 1 — Who vs. Whom Expect 1–2 questions on correct use of who/whom. SSC has been increasing this in Tier-II. "The person [who/whom] I spoke to…" → whom (object of preposition "to"). Tip: If a preposition precedes, always use WHOM. HIGH PROBABILITY Pattern 2 — Possessive Pronoun Apostrophe "Yours faithfully" vs "Your's faithfully" — a perennial trap. Also: its (possessive) vs it's (it is).

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: SSC includes this every 2–3 years. RISING TREND Pattern 3 — Faulty Comparison with "that of / those of" Sentences comparing possessions without using "that of / those of" will have the error. This pattern appeared in CGL 2022 and is trending upward for 2026. VERY HIGH Pattern 4 — Pronoun Order (231/123 Rule) Fill-in-the-blank or rearrangement question testing the positive/negative order rule. 2–3 expected in Tier-I 2026. MEDIUM Pattern 5 — Reflexive Pronoun with Routine Verbs "He bathed himself" type errors.

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: The reflexive with wash/bathe/shave/dress is increasingly appearing in CHSL error-spotting. HIGH PROBABILITY Pattern 6 — Indefinite Pronoun Subject-Verb Agreement Everybody / Nobody / Someone + plural verb = error. SSC Tier-II specifically tests: "Everybody gave their " → should be "his or her.

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: Exam Simulation · Error Spotting Quick Drill — Find the Error # Sentence (with error) Error Location Correction Rule 1 You and myself will enjoy the party. myself I Reflexive can't replace subject pronoun 2 As a student of literature, she is far better than him . him he Subject pronoun in comparisons 3 The two sisters shouted at one another in public. one another each other Each other = exactly 2 people 4 He bathed himself in the river. himself Remove it No reflexive with routine actions 5 None of the two friends has got the money back.

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: None Neither Neither = exactly 2 6 Yours faithfully ( Your's faithfully) Your's Yours No apostrophe in possessive pronouns 7 He, I and you shall arrange everything. He, I and you You, he and I Positive context = 2→3→1 order 8 Everybody gave their contribution. their his or her Everybody = singular antecedent 9 The person who I met yesterday was my classmate. who whom Object of verb "met" → whom 10 The hotels of Ravi are more luxurious than Rohan .

chunks.title: English
chunks.text: Rohan those of Rohan Faulty comparison — use "those of" Revision Card Master Cheat Card — 60-Second Revision Rule Quick Memory Hook Subject pronoun in comparisons "She is better than he [is]" — complete the implied verb Possessive pronouns — no apostrophe mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs, its — NEVER add ' s Reflexive ≠ Subject/Object Myself cannot do it ✗ | I can do it myself ✓ Each other / One another 2 = each other | 3+ = one another Who / Whom He = who | Him = whom Pronoun order (positive) 2 – 3 – 1 (You, He, I) Pronoun order (negative)

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: 2s;" onmouseover="this. background='rgba(245,200,66,0. 12)';" onmouseout="this. background='rgba(13,15,20,0. 92)';"> 🧭 TNPSC Group 1 · Prelims + Mains · History

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: A 360° examination-ready compendium of every major foreign traveller — who they were, where they came from, where they visited, what they wrote, and every TNPSC-relevant fact distilled from their accounts. 20+ Travellers Covered 6th BC to 18th AD Century 50+ MCQs Embedded Tamil Nadu Focus Included 📋 Copy-Ready Study Prompt — Paste in any AI for deeper drill Act as a senior TNPSC Group 1 question setter with 18th-century academic rigor. Quiz me on foreign travellers to India — covering: (1) full name & nationality, (2) century/period of visit,

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Frame questions as MCQs with plausible distractors, match-the-following pairs, assertion-reason, and two-statement true/false sets. Emphasize Sangam-era Greek contacts, Chola/Pandya visitors, and Mughal-era European accounts. Difficulty: TNPSC Group 1 Prelims + Mains combined. textContent='Copy',2000)">Copy Traveller Profiles

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Each profile includes origin, period, places visited, king/ruler met, travelogue name, and TNPSC-targeted exam questions. All Travellers Greek / Roman Chinese Arab / Persian European Tamil Nadu Focus

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Mg Megasthenes 🇬🇷 Greek · Ambassador · Seleucid Empire 302–298 BCE Ambassador Pataliputra Indica The earliest and most important Greek ambassador to India, sent by Seleucus I Nicator to the court of Chandragupta Maurya. His work Indica is the foundational Greek account of India. King Met Chandragupta Maurya (Sandrokottos) Capital Pataliputra (modern Patna, Bihar) Book Indica (preserved in fragments) Key Notes Described 7 castes of India; noted absence of slavery; described the royal Mauryan army with war elephants; described Pataliputra's woo

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Mentioned south Indian kingdoms as "Pandaea" (Pandya) ruled by a queen. TN Link First Greek to mention Pandya kingdom — called it "Pandaea" — and described pearl trade from south India ⚡ TNPSC Exam Questions Who was Megasthenes? He was the Greek ambassador sent to the court of Chandragupta Maurya by Seleucus I Nicator. What is the name of Megasthenes' travelogue? Which south Indian kingdom did Megasthenes refer to as "Pandaea"? — Pandya Kingdom. Megasthenes described India as having how many castes?

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: — Seven castes. St Strabo 🇬🇷 Greek · Geographer · Pontus (modern Turkey) 1st Century BCE Geographer Geographica Did not visit India personally but compiled all existing Greek accounts about India in his monumental Geographica — a crucial secondary source for ancient Indian history. Period 64 BCE – 24 CE Book Geographica (17 volumes) Key Notes Preserved information from Megasthenes, Nearchus, and others; described India's physical geography; accounts of Indian rivers (Indus, Ganges); described trade between India and Rome.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: ⚡ TNPSC Exam Questions Strabo compiled ancient Indian accounts in which book? Strabo's Geographica contains how many volumes? Pl Pliny the Elder 🇮🇹 Roman · Author · Como, Roman Empire 1st Century CE Roman Scholar India (via trade) Naturalis Historia Roman naturalist who wrote Naturalis Historia . Did not visit India but documented Indo-Roman trade extensively. Famously complained that India was draining Rome of its gold. Book Naturalis Historia (Natural History) Key Notes Recorded that Rome paid India 550 million sesterces annually for luxury

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Documented pepper, silk, ivory trade. Mentioned Muzaris (Muchiri/Cranganore) as a major Indian port. TN Link Described Tamil ports, pepper trade from Malabar; mentioned Tamil kings sending embassies to Rome ⚡ TNPSC Exam Questions Who complained that trade with India was draining Rome of gold? — Pliny the Elder. Pliny's major work documenting India trade is called? — Naturalis Historia. Which port did Pliny mention as the busiest Indian trade port?

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: — Muzaris (Cranganore/Muchiri). Pt Claudius Ptolemy 🇪🇬 Greek-Egyptian · Geographer · Alexandria 2nd Century CE Geographer Geographia South India Alexandrian geographer whose Geographia contains the earliest map-like description of South India, identifying Tamil ports and rivers with remarkable accuracy. Book Geographia Key Notes Mentioned "Sopatma" (Mahabalipuram area), "Chaberis" (Kaveri mouth/Kaveripattinam), "Poduca" (Pondicherry/Arikamedu), "Camara" (possibly Kaveripattanam).

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Created early map of India. TN Link Identified Arikamedu as "Poduca" — a major trading post; mentioned Kaveri river and Cauvery delta ports ⚡ TNPSC Exam Questions Ptolemy identified "Poduca" as which modern Tamil Nadu location? — Arikamedu (near Pondicherry). Ptolemy called the Kaveri river by which name? Ptolemy's geographical work is called? Fa Fa-Hien (Faxian) 🇨🇳 Chinese · Buddhist Monk · China 399–414 CE Buddhist Pilgrim North India · Sri Lanka Record of Buddhist Kingdoms First Chinese Buddhist monk to travel to India in search of Buddhis

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: He visited during the reign of Chandragupta II (Vikramaditya) of the Gupta dynasty. King Met Chandragupta II (Vikramaditya) — Gupta Empire Places Peshawar, Taxila, Mathura, Pataliputra, Varanasi, Bodh Gaya, Kapilavastu, Sarnath, Sri Lanka Book Fo-Kwo-Ki (Record of Buddhist Kingdoms) Key Notes Described Gupta society as prosperous and peaceful; said no capital punishment existed; observed that people were honest and vegetarian in the central lands; found Buddhist monasteries flourishing; visited sacred Buddhist sites.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: ⚡ TNPSC Exam Questions During whose reign did Fa-Hien visit India? — Chandragupta II (Vikramaditya) of Gupta dynasty. Fa-Hien's travelogue is called? — Fo-Kwo-Ki / Record of Buddhist Kingdoms. What did Fa-Hien observe about Gupta society? — Prosperous, no capital punishment, people honest and vegetarian. Which Chinese pilgrim visited India between 399–414 CE? HT Hiuen Tsang (Xuanzang) 🇨🇳 Chinese · Buddhist Scholar · Henan, China 629–645 CE Buddhist Pilgrim All India incl.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: South India Si-Yu-Ki The most important Chinese traveller to India. He spent 16 years in India during the reign of Harsha and travelled extensively including South India and Tamil regions. Called the "Prince of Pilgrims". King Met Emperor Harsha of Kanauj; also Pulakesi II (Chalukya); visited Pallava kingdom Places Taxila, Kanauj, Nalanda, Bodh Gaya, Kanchipuram, Mahabalipuram area, Sri Lanka via south India route Book Si-Yu-Ki (Records of the Western Regions) · Also: Datang Xiyu Ji Key Notes Described Nalanda University in detail — 10,000 stud

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: TN Link Visited Kanchipuram — described it as a thriving Buddhist centre; described Pallava king Narasimhavarman; noted Tamil Nadu's wealth and trade ⚡ TNPSC Exam Questions During whose reign did Hiuen Tsang visit India? — Harsha of Kanauj (also Pulakesi II Chalukya). Hiuen Tsang's travelogue is named? — Si-Yu-Ki (Records of the Western Regions). Which Tamil city did Hiuen Tsang describe as "Kanchipuram"? — Kanchipuram (Pallava capital). Hiuen Tsang described which university in detail?

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: — Nalanda University. Which Chinese traveller is called the "Prince of Pilgrims"? IT I-Tsing (Yijing) 🇨🇳 Chinese · Buddhist Monk · Shandong, China 671–695 CE Buddhist Pilgrim South India · Nalanda · Sri Vijaya Buddhist Records Chinese monk who travelled to India by sea route via Sri Vijaya (Sumatra). He studied at Nalanda for 10 years and wrote about South Indian Buddhist practices. Route Sea route via Sri Vijaya (Sumatra) — not overland like Fa-Hien Places Nalanda, South India ports, Sri Lanka; studied at Nalanda 10 years Book Record of Budd

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: TN Link Described Buddhist centres in South India including Kanchipuram region; documented sea trade routes from Tamil ports ⚡ TNPSC Exam Questions Which Chinese pilgrim took the sea route via Sumatra to reach India? — I-Tsing (Yijing). I-Tsing studied at which Indian university for 10 years? — Nalanda University. I-Tsing noted Buddhism was thriving in which region? — South-East Asia (Sri Vijaya). AM Al-Masudi 🇮🇶 Arab · Historian-Geographer · Baghdad, Abbasid Caliphate 10th Century CE (916 CE) Arab Geographer West India · Deccan · South India

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Period 896–956 CE · Visited India c. 916 CE Book Muruj al-Dhahab wa Ma'adin al-Jawhar (Meadows of Gold and Mines of Gems) Key Notes Described the caste system; documented Hindu temples; described the Deccan kingdoms; wrote about Indian Ocean trade; described the western coast of India; mentioned the kingdom of the Rashtrakutas; described Indian customs of sati. TN Link Described Tamil trade along western coast; wrote about pepper, spices, and textile trade from south India ⚡ TNPSC Exam Questions Al-Masudi is called the "Herodotus of the Arabs.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: " His major work is? — Muruj al-Dhahab (Meadows of Gold). Al-Masudi visited India during the reign of which dynasty? — Rashtrakutas (Deccan). What Indian social practice did Al-Masudi describe in his accounts? — The caste system and sati. AB Al-Biruni (Abu Rayhan) 🇺🇿 Persian · Scholar · Khwarezm (modern Uzbekistan) 1017–1030 CE Persian Scholar North India · Punjab · Sindh Kitab al-Hind The greatest medieval Islamic scholar and the first true Indologist.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: He came to India with Mahmud of Ghazni and spent 13 years studying Sanskrit, mathematics, astronomy, and Indian philosophy. Came with Mahmud of Ghazni (after his invasions) Places Multan, Thaneswar, Punjab, Sindh, Kashmir region Book Kitab al-Hind (Book of India / Tahqiq ma lil-Hind) Key Notes Learned Sanskrit; translated Sanskrit texts into Arabic; described Hindu philosophy, mathematics, and astronomy with deep respect; criticized caste discrimination; noted India's intellectual achievements; described India's physical geography and climate a

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: ⚡ TNPSC Exam Questions Al-Biruni's famous book about India is? — Kitab al-Hind (Tahqiq ma lil-Hind). Al-Biruni came to India with which ruler? — Mahmud of Ghazni. Which language did Al-Biruni learn to study Indian texts? Who is considered the first true "Indologist"? IB Ibn Battuta 🇲🇦 Moroccan · Explorer · Tangier, Morocco 1333–1347 CE Explorer Delhi · Deccan · Malabar · Tamil Ports Rihla The greatest medieval Muslim traveller — called the "Marco Polo of the Muslim World.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: " Served as a Qadi (judge) under Muhammad bin Tughluq in Delhi and later travelled to South India and China. King Met Muhammad bin Tughluq (Delhi Sultanate) — served as his ambassador to China Places Delhi, Daulatabad, Malabar Coast, Quilon (Kollam), Calicut, Hormuz, Maldives, China Book Rihla (Travels / A Gift to Those Who Contemplate the Wonders of Cities) Key Notes Described Muhammad bin Tughluq as both a tyrant and generous; described mass transfer of capital from Delhi to Daulatabad; described Malabar pepper trade; described Calicut as a g

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: TN Link Visited Tamil Nadu ports; described Quilon and Calicut as major trade centres; noted Tamil merchants dealing in spices and textiles ⚡ TNPSC Exam Questions Ibn Battuta served as a Qadi under which Delhi Sultanate ruler? — Muhammad bin Tughluq. Ibn Battuta's famous travelogue is called? Which South Indian port did Ibn Battuta describe as the greatest? — Calicut (Kozhikode). Ibn Battuta is from which country? — Morocco (Tangier). As whose ambassador was Ibn Battuta sent to China?

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: — Muhammad bin Tughluq's ambassador. MP Marco Polo 🇮🇹 Italian · Merchant-Explorer · Venice, Italy 1288–1294 CE Merchant Explorer South India · Pandya Kingdom · Coromandel The Travels of Marco Polo The Venetian merchant-explorer who passed through South India on his return journey from China. He described the Pandya kingdom of South India and Tamil Nadu's pearl fisheries in detail. 1288–1294 CE (returning from China via sea) Places Malabar Coast, Pandya territory (Tamil Nadu), Coromandel Coast, Cape Comorin, Ceylon (Sri Lanka) Book Il Milione

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: TN Link "Ma'abar" = Coromandel Coast (Tamil Nadu). Described Pandya king's court, pearl fisheries, idolatry and Hindu temple practices in Tamil regions ⚡ TNPSC Exam Questions Marco Polo called the Coromandel Coast of Tamil Nadu by which name? Which pearl fishery location in Tamil Nadu did Marco Polo describe? — Mannar (between Tamil Nadu and Sri Lanka). Marco Polo's travelogue is called? — Il Milione (The Travels of Marco Polo). Which South Indian dynasty's kingdom did Marco Polo describe as "Ma'abar"?

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: — Pandya Kingdom. Marco Polo was from which city in Italy? NC Nicolo de Conti 🇮🇹 Italian · Merchant · Venice, Italy 1420–1421 CE Merchant Vijayanagara · South India Account (via Poggio Bracciolini) Venetian merchant who visited the Vijayanagara Empire and described it in detail. His account was recorded by the Papal secretary Poggio Bracciolini in the work De Varietate Fortunae . King Met Vijayanagara ruler (Deva Raya I era) Places Vijayanagara (Hampi), Malabar, South India Key Notes Described Vijayanagara city as very large, with a circumfer

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: TN Link Described Vijayanagara's influence over Tamil regions; documented Telugu and Tamil cultural practices ⚡ TNPSC Exam Questions Nicolo de Conti described which empire's capital in South India? — Vijayanagara Empire (Hampi). Nicolo de Conti's account was recorded by whom? — Poggio Bracciolini (in De Varietate Fortunae). AR Abdur Razzaq 🇮🇷 Persian · Ambassador · Herat, Timurid Empire (Persia) 1443–1444 CE Persian Ambassador Vijayanagara · Calicut · Mangalore Matla-us-Sadain Persian ambassador sent by Shah Rukh of the Timurid dynasty to Cal

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: His account is one of the most important descriptions of the Vijayanagara Empire at its peak. King Met Deva Raya II — Vijayanagara Empire Places Calicut (Kozhikode), Mangalore, Vijayanagara (Hampi city) Book Matla-us-Sadain wa Majma-ul-Bahrain Key Notes Described Vijayanagara city in extraordinary detail — its markets, bazaars, temples; said the city was as large as a great metropolis; described Calicut as a great port city with merchants from all over the world; described the prosperity of Deva Raya II's reign; noted Hindu temples with preciou

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: TN Link Described Tamil traders in Calicut; documented Vijayanagara's rule over Tamil regions; noted the prosperity of south Indian trade routes ⚡ TNPSC Exam Questions Abdur Razzaq visited Vijayanagara during the reign of which king? — Deva Raya II. Abdur Razzaq's travelogue is named? — Matla-us-Sadain. Abdur Razzaq was sent as ambassador by which ruler? — Shah Rukh (Timurid/Persian ruler). Which festival at Vijayanagara did Abdur Razzaq famously describe?

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: — Dasara (Mahanavami) festival. DB Duarte Barbosa 🇵🇹 Portuguese · Official · Lisbon, Portugal 1500–1516 CE Portuguese Official Malabar · Vijayanagara · Coromandel Book of Duarte Barbosa Portuguese official who spent years in South India as an interpreter and administrator. His book provides one of the best accounts of South India in the early 16th century. Period Served in Portuguese India c. 1500–1516 Places Malabar Coast, Coromandel Coast, Vijayanagara territory, Tamil ports Book Livro de Duarte Barbosa (Book of Duarte Barbosa) Key Notes De

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: TN Link Described Tamil ports (Pulicat, Nagapattinam, Tuticorin); documented pearl fisheries; described the Nayaka administration in Tamil regions ⚡ TNPSC Exam Questions Duarte Barbosa was a Portuguese official who described which empire as the "greatest Hindu empire"? — Vijayanagara Empire. Which Tamil Nadu ports did Duarte Barbosa describe? — Pulicat, Nagapattinam, Tuticorin. Duarte Barbosa's book on South India is called? — Livro de Duarte Barbosa (Book of Duarte Barbosa).

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: DP Domingo Paes 🇵🇹 Portuguese · Merchant-Chronicler · Portugal 1520–1522 CE Portuguese Merchant Vijayanagara Empire Chronica dos reis de Bisnaga Portuguese horse-trader and chronicler who visited Vijayanagara during its golden age under Krishna Deva Raya. His account is considered the most vivid description of Vijayanagara at its peak glory. King Met Krishna Deva Raya — Vijayanagara Empire (greatest king) Places Vijayanagara (Hampi), Goa, Malabar Coast Book Chronica dos reis de Bisnaga (Chronicle of the Kings of Bisnaga) Key Notes Called Vija

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: TN Link Krishna Deva Raya's empire included Tamil regions; described Vijayanagara's Telugu and Tamil subjects; noted Nayak governors in Tamil territory ⚡ TNPSC Exam Questions Domingo Paes visited Vijayanagara during whose reign? — Krishna Deva Raya. Domingo Paes compared Vijayanagara city to which European city? Domingo Paes compared Krishna Deva Raya to which conqueror? — Alexander the Great. Which festival at Vijayanagara did Domingo Paes describe in extraordinary detail?

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: — Mahanavami (Dasara) festival. What is the title of Domingo Paes' chronicle? — Chronica dos reis de Bisnaga. FN Fernão Nunes (Nuniz) 🇵🇹 Portuguese · Horse Merchant · Portugal 1535–1537 CE Portuguese Merchant Vijayanagara Chronicle of Vijayanagara Portuguese horse merchant who wrote a historical chronicle of Vijayanagara kings. His account provides detailed information about the succession of Vijayanagara rulers. Period Visited c. 1535–1537 CE (post Krishna Deva Raya era) Key Notes Wrote a chronicle of Vijayanagara rulers from 1336 to his tim

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: ⚡ TNPSC Exam Questions Fernão Nunes wrote a chronicle covering Vijayanagara from which year? — 1336 CE (founding of Vijayanagara). Fernão Nunes described the role of which administrative officers in Tamil territory? — Nayaks (governors). TR Sir Thomas Roe 🇬🇧 British · Ambassador · England 1615–1618 CE British Ambassador Mughal Court · Ajmer · Agra Embassy of Sir Thomas Roe First official British ambassador to the Mughal court of Jahangir. He came seeking trading rights for the East India Company.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: His journal is one of the best accounts of Jahangir's court. King Met Emperor Jahangir (Mughal Empire) — at Ajmer and Agra Places Surat, Ajmer, Agra — all within the Mughal Empire Book Embassy of Sir Thomas Roe to the Court of the Great Mogul (Journal) Key Notes Described Jahangir's personality and drinking habits; noted Mughal court's love of painting; described the political rivalry among Jahangir's sons; noted Nur Jahan's enormous influence over the emperor; described the East India Company's early trading struggles; provided insights into M

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: ⚡ TNPSC Exam Questions Thomas Roe was the first British ambassador to the court of which Mughal emperor? Thomas Roe came to India representing which organization? — British East India Company. Thomas Roe described the enormous influence of which Mughal queen? In which cities did Thomas Roe meet Jahangir? — Ajmer and Agra. JT Jean-Baptiste Tavernier 🇫🇷 French · Gem Merchant · Paris, France 1638–1668 CE (6 voyages) French Gem Merchant Mughal India · Golconda · South India Travels in India French gem merchant who made six voyages to India over 3

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: He was the first European to describe the famous Kohinoor diamond and gave detailed accounts of Golconda diamond mines and South Indian trade. King Met Shah Jahan, Aurangzeb (Mughal); Golconda Sultans Places Surat, Golconda, Bijapur, Agra, Delhi, Dacca, Masulipatnam (Tamil/AP coast) Book Les Six Voyages de J. Tavernier (Six Voyages in Turkey, Persia and India) Key Notes First European to describe the Kohinoor diamond; described Golconda diamond mines; described the Taj Mahal under construction; compared Indian and European textile trade; gave d

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: TN Link Visited Masulipatnam (Machilipatnam) and Coromandel Coast; described Tamil and Telugu textile industries; documented diamond trade from Golconda affecting South Indian economy ⚡ TNPSC Exam Questions Which French traveller was the first European to describe the Kohinoor diamond? — Jean-Baptiste Tavernier. Tavernier made how many voyages to India? — Six voyages (over 30 years). Tavernier's book about his Indian travels is called? — Les Six Voyages (Six Voyages).

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Which diamond mines did Tavernier describe in detail? — Golconda diamond mines. Tavernier described the Taj Mahal in which state? — Under construction (during Shah Jahan's reign). FB François Bernier 🇫🇷 French · Physician-Philosopher · Anjou, France 1656–1668 CE French Physician Mughal India · Kashmir · Delhi Travels in the Mogul Empire French physician who served in the Mughal court as physician to Dara Shikoh (heir of Shah Jahan). He witnessed the Mughal war of succession and Aurangzeb's rise to power.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: King Met Shah Jahan, Dara Shikoh (served as physician), Aurangzeb Places Delhi, Agra, Kashmir, Surat, Bengal, Bihar Book Travels in the Mogul Empire (Histoire de la dernière révolution des états du Grand Mogol) Key Notes Provided eyewitness account of Shah Jahan's illness and succession war; described the execution of Dara Shikoh; described Kashmir's beauty extensively; criticized Mughal land ownership (no private property in land); described Bengal's rich agriculture; wrote about Indian philosophy and compared it with European thought.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: ⚡ TNPSC Exam Questions François Bernier served as physician to which Mughal prince? — Dara Shikoh (son of Shah Jahan). Bernier's book about the Mughal Empire is called? — Travels in the Mogul Empire. Which Mughal emperor's succession war did Bernier witness firsthand? — Aurangzeb's war against Dara Shikoh. Bernier criticized Mughal land ownership on what grounds? — Absence of private property in land (all land belonged to the Emperor). WH William Hawkins 🇬🇧 British · Captain · England 1608–1611 CE English Captain Mughal Court · Agra Purchas H

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: He could speak Turkish which greatly pleased Jahangir, who initially gave him a royal welcome. King Met Emperor Jahangir (at Agra) Places Surat, Agra — Mughal capital Book Account published in Purchas His Pilgrimage (compiled by Samuel Purchas) Key Notes First Englishman at Mughal court; Jahangir liked him because he spoke Turkish; Hawkins even married an Armenian woman in India; described Jahangir's alcoholism; noted that Portuguese influence blocked English trading rights; ultimately failed to secure trading concessions; described Surat's tra

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: ⚡ TNPSC Exam Questions William Hawkins was the first Englishman to visit the court of which Mughal emperor? Why did Jahangir initially favour William Hawkins? — Hawkins could speak Turkish, which pleased Jahangir. William Hawkins' account was published in which compilation? — Purchas His Pilgrimage (by Samuel Purchas). Quick Reference

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Traveller Period Country King/Ruler Met Travelogue Key TNPSC Fact Megasthenes 302–298 BCE Greece Chandragupta Maurya Indica Called Pandya kingdom "Pandaea"; described 7 castes Fa-Hien 399–414 CE China Chandragupta II Fo-Kwo-Ki Described Gupta golden age; no capital punishment Hiuen Tsang 629–645 CE China Harsha / Pulakesi II / Pallava king Si-Yu-Ki "Prince of Pilgrims"; described Kanchipuram; Nalanda I-Tsing 671–695 CE China — Buddhist Records Came by sea via Sumatra; studied at Nalanda 10 yrs Al-Masudi 916 CE Arab (Baghdad) Rashtrakuta rulers

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: 01 · Prelims Level Which Chinese traveller visited India during the reign of Chandragupta II (Vikramaditya) of the Gupta dynasty? A Hiuen Tsang B I-Tsing C Fa-Hien D Ma Huan Show Answer ✓ Answer: C — Fa-Hien. He visited India in 399–414 CE during Chandragupta II's reign, described as the Gupta golden age with prosperity, no capital punishment, and thriving Buddhism. His book is Fo-Kwo-Ki. 02 · Prelims Level Megasthenes referred to the Pandya kingdom of South India as: A Panduranga B Pandaea C Ma'abar D Tamilakam Show Answer ✓ Answer: B — Pandae

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Megasthenes in his Indica referred to the Pandya kingdom as "Pandaea," described as ruled by a queen, and noted for its pearl trade. "Ma'abar" was Marco Polo's term for the Coromandel Coast. 03 · Prelims Level Which traveller is called the "Prince of Pilgrims" and described Kanchipuram as a great Buddhist centre? A Fa-Hien B I-Tsing C Hiuen Tsang D Al-Biruni Show Answer ✓ Answer: C — Hiuen Tsang. Called the "Prince of Pilgrims," he visited Kanchipuram (Pallava capital) during 629–645 CE, described it as a thriving city and Buddhist centre.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: His travelogue Si-Yu-Ki is the most important account of 7th century India. 04 · Mains Level Match the travelogue with its author: 1. Kitab al-Hind A 1-Hiuen Tsang, 2-Ibn Battuta, 3-Megasthenes, 4-Al-Biruni B 1-Megasthenes, 2-Ibn Battuta, 3-Hiuen Tsang, 4-Al-Biruni C 1-Al-Biruni, 2-Megasthenes, 3-Ibn Battuta, 4-Hiuen Tsang D 1-Marco Polo, 2-Fa-Hien, 3-Ibn Battuta, 4-Al-Masudi Show Answer ✓ Answer: B — Indica=Megasthenes (Greek ambassador, 302 BCE); Rihla=Ibn Battuta (Moroccan traveller, 1333 CE); Si-Yu-Ki=Hiuen Tsang (Chinese pilgrim, 629 CE);

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: 05 · Prelims Level The Portuguese traveller who compared Vijayanagara to Rome and Krishna Deva Raya to Alexander the Great was: A Duarte Barbosa B Abdur Razzaq C Domingo Paes D Fernão Nunes Show Answer ✓ Answer: C — Domingo Paes (1520–1522). He visited Vijayanagara during Krishna Deva Raya's reign (the golden age) and compared the city to Rome and the king to Alexander. His chronicle: Chronica dos reis de Bisnaga. 06 · Prelims Level Which French gem merchant was the first European to describe the Kohinoor diamond?

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: A Jean-Baptiste Tavernier B François Bernier C Thomas Roe D Domingo Paes Show Answer ✓ Answer: A — Jean-Baptiste Tavernier. He made 6 voyages to India (1638–1668), described the Kohinoor diamond and Golconda mines, and also described the Taj Mahal under construction. His book: Les Six Voyages. 07 · Mains Level Consider these statements about Al-Biruni: 1. He came to India with Mahmud of Ghazni. He wrote Kitab al-Hind after learning Sanskrit. He described Vijayanagara empire in detail.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Which statements are correct? A 1 and 3 only B 1 and 2 only C 2 and 3 only D 1, 2 and 3 Show Answer ✓ Answer: B — Statements 1 and 2. Al-Biruni came with Mahmud of Ghazni (1017 CE) and wrote Kitab al-Hind after learning Sanskrit. Statement 3 is WRONG — Al-Biruni preceded Vijayanagara (founded 1336 CE) by 300 years. Vijayanagara was described by Abdur Razzaq, Domingo Paes, and Nicolo de Conti. 08 · Prelims Level Marco Polo called the Coromandel Coast (Tamil Nadu) by which name in his travelogue?

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: A Pandaea B Tamilakam C Ma'abar D Indica Show Answer ✓ Answer: C — Ma'abar. Marco Polo called the Coromandel Coast (Tamil Nadu) "Ma'abar" in his travelogue Il Milione. He described the Pandya kingdom's pearl fisheries at Mannar and noted the king's jewel-covered body. 09 · Mains Level Which traveller described Muhammad bin Tughluq as both a generous and tyrannical ruler, and served as his ambassador to China? A Al-Biruni B Abdur Razzaq C Ibn Battuta D Al-Masudi Show Answer ✓ Answer: C — Ibn Battuta (Moroccan, from Tangier).

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: He served as Qadi (judge) under Muhammad bin Tughluq and was later sent as his ambassador to China. His travelogue Rihla is one of the most important medieval accounts of the Delhi Sultanate. 10 · Prelims Level The Persian ambassador who visited Vijayanagara during Deva Raya II's reign and described the Dasara festival was: A Al-Masudi B Ibn Battuta C Abdur Razzaq D Al-Biruni Show Answer ✓ Answer: C — Abdur Razzaq. Persian ambassador from Herat (Timurid Empire), sent by Shah Rukh.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Visited Calicut and Vijayanagara in 1443–1444 during Deva Raya II's reign. His Matla-us-Sadain described the Dasara festival in detail.

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Chinese Pilgrims Order " F irst H appy I ndia" — Fa-Hien → Hiuen Tsang → I-Tsing (in chronological order, all Chinese Buddhist pilgrims) F-H-I = Fa-Hien, Hiuen Tsang, I-Tsing Vijayanagara Visitors " N ice A rmies D efeat D aring F oes" — Nicolo Conti → Abdur Razzaq → Domingo Paes → Duarte Barbosa → Fernão Nunes N-A-D-D-F = 5 Vijayanagara travellers Rulers & Their Visitors Chandragupta Maurya = Megasthenes | Chandragupta II = Fa-Hien | Harsha = Hiuen Tsang | Muhammad bin Tughluq = Ibn Battuta | Jahangir = Hawkins + Roe King → Traveller pair Tami

chunks.title: Study Material
chunks.text: Both French, both 17th century Mughal India. T = Tavernier (gems) | B = Bernier (doctor) Built for TNPSC Group 1 aspirants · All travellers up to 18th century covered · Exam-pattern questions embedded · Tamil Nadu focus highlighted throughout ↑
